chapters.title: Chapter 301: Daisan High School
chapters.body:
Chapter 301: Daisan High School

Chapter 301: Daisan High School

“However, Basilisk was still continuously morphing...”

Fujiwara Aoki continued recalling the events. His face was nearly void of any trace of emotion, appearing to have sunken into a state of numbness. “Until one day, people started noticing that it was no longer limited to just images. Even a simple passage, a string of spoken sentences, even mere contact with people could trigger a response similar to Basilisk!

“By that point, there was no saving the world anymore!”

Upon hearing this, Chen Chen was visibly shocked. “When you said triggering a response similar to Basilisk, what are you referring to?”

“It’s just something I’ve heard other people saying.”

Fujiwara Aoki snapped back to his senses. He seemed reluctant to discuss this but still gritted his teeth and resumed. “Before most people could realize what was going on, they were seeing that the number of people around them was quickly dwindling and the death toll was only going up. It started with the government disappearing, then their neighbors, even their own relatives... The world was rapidly deteriorating. We survivors began to group together and exchange our survival experience...”

Fujiwara Aoki pondered briefly before going on. “Some people started saying that the Basilisk image was slowly starting to evolve. It had broken free of the limitations of an image and adapted into messages of different shapes and forms. These messages had also surpassed the capability of Gödel’s shock and they made their victims lose their minds, sometimes their memories as well. In the end, we even received word that Basilisk was causing biological changes!”

Chen Chen began ruminating on everything he had heard from this man. These forms of transmission had surpassed his understanding. If not for the fact that Fujiwara Aoki was expressing himself in a lucid manner, Chen Chen may have believed that he was driven to insanity due to fear and solitary.

“These past five years, Basilisk is continuously evolving every day.”

Fujiwara Aoki muttered, “At first, despite the missing government and rapidly deteriorating social order, my group and I continued heading out to scavenge every day. We still met other people, various conflicts would break out...

“However, with each passing day, we started to notice the number of other people in the city was declining as well. It was like those people just vanished into thin air. Some of their remains can be found inside the buildings while some barely left behind their remains...”

A wipe of fear appeared in Fujiwara Aoki’s eyes. “On several occasions, I’ve even witnessed Basilisk morphing into a new form and killing someone right before my very eyes. This one time, I was sleeping in a dormitory and it was midnight. Just as I was about to get up to head to the toilet, I suddenly saw a woman slowly emerging from the chest of my partner sleeping on the upper bunk. It was just the upper part of the woman appearing, I remembered her hair was a complete mess. She reached out with her hands and strangled my partner...”

Chen Chen’s shrunken eyes instinctively darted toward the diary in his hand, seemingly realizing something.

“After that, I heard from another partner that the woman appeared in your dreams.”

Fujiwara Aoki explained, “There appeared to be some sort of rule. Once you have triggered it, the woman in white will begin appearing in your dreams and she’d draw closer and closer each time you dreamt of her until she appeared right in front of your bed...

“There are plenty of similar cases. Can you imagine? That all kinds of strange occurrences had stemmed from that Basilisk image pattern?”

Capping his tale, Fujiwara Aoki spread his hands out helplessly. “This is truly a d*mned world. This is why I said that reading the diary of a deceased is extremely dangerous because nobody could say for sure that this diary wouldn’t contain any variation of those messages that triggers instant death...”

At this point, Fujiwara Aoki had nearly told Chen Chen everything that he needed to know. Chen Chen also gained an impression of the truth of this world. Chen Chen nodded and sighed. “If not for the current state of this world, I might not have believed everything you just told me.”

“I understand.”

Fujiwara Aoki smiled with a crooked expression. “But it is the truth, pressed under the imposing power of Basilisk, the entirety of human civilization has collapsed. Until today, I’ve only met two groups of outsiders, that includes you guys.”

“In that case, please bring me to your group.”

Chen Chen nodded. “I’d like to meet your group. This way, I can gain a better, complete understanding of the situation regarding Basilisk.”

“I’m afraid that’s not possible.”

Fujiwara Aoki shook his head somewhat reluctantly. “Your weapons are extremely advanced. There might not be many of you, but if a fight were to break out, our group is no match for you at all. Besides, I’ll be kicked out of the group by the leader if I brought you guys back before getting the green light from the group.”

Chen Chen waved his hand. “If you’re worried that we harbor ill intentions, then just take me with you. I can come with you unarmed.”

As Chen Chen said this, he began to take off the GS Combat Suit. He handed the suit to the Black Knight behind him before swapping out into another set of common clothes. “Aoki-san, you must understand that I have no reason to harm you or your group for that matter. Besides, if I had any ill intentions, I’d simply capture and interrogate you with more effective means. I’d be able to obtain answers far easier that way, isn’t it?”

Fujiwara Aoki immediately fell silent, seemingly still debating with himself.

“It must be rather difficult scavenging for food with things the way they are now.”

After mentioning this, Chen Chen gave a signal. The Black Knights behind him quickly produced a large box of energy bars from the spaceship. “I can give your group these in exchange for information.”

Upon seeing this, Fujiwara Aoki seemed to be tempted. In the end, he still gritted his teeth and held his ground. “I believe in Chen Chen-san’s sincerity. However, this is no trivial matter. This directly concerns the lives of more than thirty people in my group, please forgive me for this difficult decision!”

“Why so?”

Chen Chen shook his head and smiled faintly. “Actually, you won’t have to lead me there, I can already roughly pinpoint the location of your group. When I found you, you were walking from west to east. The city center is on the east while the suburbs lie on the west. This suggests that you were coming from the suburbs area.

“Also, I can see that your backpack is nearly full, you also have more than half a bottle of water remaining, meaning that you haven’t exhausted many resources yet. This way, I can conclude that you mustn’t be more than 10 hours away from your group’s position.”

With that, Chen Chen snapped his fingers lightly. Immediately, the spaceship shot out a ray of light and projected a top-down view of Tokyo and quickly zoomed inward.

“This is our current position and the suburbs are down there. There aren’t many suitable places to hide in the suburb, but a location that can fit more than thirty people must be a large venue.”

Upon noting this, Chen Chen pointed at a high school on the edges of the suburbs. “Based on your mention of ‘dormitory’ and ‘upper-bunk’, I can conclude that...

“The base where your group is located is the Tokyo Daisan High School.”

chapters.title: Chapter 302: Betrayal
chapters.body:
Chapter 302: Betrayal

Chapter 302: Betrayal

Half an hour later, four drones stealthily approached and surrounded a middle school in the Tokyo suburb district.

At the same time, the Bugatti Spaceship began approaching from afar. It quickly landed on a rooftop near the abandoned school.

When Fujiwara Aoki stepped out of the spaceship, his expression was full of powerlessness. “Chen Chen-kun, since you’ve already had an idea of the location of our group, why not come here directly? Why must you bring me along?”

“It’s easier to gain your group’s trust if I bring you along with me, otherwise your friends may think that you were met with misfortune.”

Chen Chen answered.

“But if you do it this way, the leader might think that I led you to them...”

Fujiwara Aoki refuted in a low voice.

Chen Chen did not bother with giving further explanations. After all, he came to figure out the situation so he would do whatever it took for him to obtain the information he was after. If these people were not going to tell him what he needed, he did not mind resorting to more violent methods and inject them with the truth serum.

With this in mind, Chen Chen had the three Black Knights watch over the ship while he took Fujiwara Aoki with him and approached the main entrance of the school.

From the outside, this appeared to be a completely deserted school. Even the iron fence gates had long collapsed due to lack of maintenance. Dense vines grew all over the compound and the buildings like a thick rug laying over them.

Fujiwara and his group of survivors were well aware of the rules of survival in the end times. Nobody would be able to figure out at first glance that this abandoned school was the gathering point of a small group of survivors.

Right after stepping through the main entrance, Chen Chen could faintly feel peering eyes surrounding him from all sides. However, since he took off the PK Armor, he did not have access to thermal imaging vision to spot where the sentries were posted.

“Chen Chen-kun, please come with me.”

Fujiwara Aoki decided that his words were not of much use. He let out a sigh before leading the way to the largest school building.

This building was probably the main lecture area of Daisan High School. It was the largest and also tallest of them all and functioned as the main sector of the compound. Based on the competency that this surviving group had displayed, they had good reason to not use this building as a primary meeting spot.

Since Fujiwara Aoki was leading him to this building first, this further proved that this building was designated as a defensive building. The group must have strategized ahead of time that in the event where one of their members was captured, the captured member could lead the enemy here for the group to ambush.

When Chen Chen considered this, he only flicked an eyebrow gently without saying a word. He continued to silently trail behind Fujiwara Aoki.

It was only when they arrived outside the building when Fujiwara Aoki finally stole a calculative glance at Chen Chen. He gently pushed open the entrance. “Chen Chen-kun, please come in with me.”

At this point, Chen Chen had a sharp and clear impression of someone stalking him from the darkness. Apart from that, he also sensed a slight sting in his temples. Chen Chen had attained a form of bioelectricity sensitivity ever since acquiring “Field”.

The faint pangs of pain he felt in his temple implied that whoever was hiding in the darkness harbored ominous intent toward him.

“I believe that you may have been mistaken.”

Chen Chen knitted his brows together. Instead of following Fujiwara Aoki into the building, he snickered and said, “I came here to speak with your group, not to be butchered. What’s the meaning of this?”

“What, what do you mean?”

Fujiwara Aoki chuckled uncomfortably. “I’m only following the procedures of our group. If you want to join our group, this is the required process...”

“Is that so?”

Chen Chen’s expression turned grim. “This is what I get in return for the great generosity I have displayed? In that case, have your leader come out here and talk with me right here. Just know that my patience is limited.”

“This...”

Fujiwara Aoki’s expression changed. He was well aware that Chen Chen was not alone since there were the three accomplices wearing the GS Combat Suit as well as the small-sized spacecraft. If a battle were to break out, there was no way his small group could put up a fight against their opponents.

“Still not willing to come out?”

Chen Chen turned in the direction of the school building and looked into the pitch-black windows, he beckoned, “If you guys aren’t going to come out, then allow me to have someone escort you out.”

The color went out of Fujiwara Aoki’s face when he heard this. Before he could get a word in, four rapid swishes slicing across the air could be heard. At the same time, the parrot on his shoulders began chirping endlessly. Something fast seemed to be approaching!

“Ah!”

“Idiot!”

Following the abrupt commotion, Fujiwara Aoki could hear several shrieks coming from the windows in the building. It was only then when he finally realized that four nearly completely transparent drones had appeared above him without him noticing!

That was not the end of it. The four drones appeared to be the signal for the small spaceship that took him here. He saw that the spaceship was also approaching from afar!

At the same time, there was a blue flash of light as Chen Chen’s figure instantly vanished into thin air. From where Fujiwara Aoki stood, he only caught a glimpse of a figure dashing into the school building.

Fujiwara Aoki’s expression became twisted as he gritted his teeth and made a resolution. He took out a gun and aimed it at the distorted air. “Chen Chen-kun, stop this immediately or else I will... Ah!”

Before he could finish his sentence, he felt a flash of pain coursing along his arm. When he instinctively looked down to inspect the pain, he saw his right wrist still holding the gun had been sliced off. The cut was so clean it was as if it had been polished!

Chen Chen hardly paid a second thought to Fujiwara Aoki. After entering the building under the guise of invisibility, he immediately went toward the classroom where the drone fired a bullet into. Just as he stepped into the classroom, he met a tough-looking, large, and stocky man who was howling miserably and clutching his right hand as he dashed out of the classroom.

Unlike Fujiwara Aoki, the right hand of this large man had turned into an indistinguishable mash of meat.

Survivors in the end times were few and far between. If not for Chen Chen needing to keep them alive to obtain information, it would not just be the right hand of this man that was turned into lumps of flesh.

The large man did not notice Chen Chen who was invisible. He continued charging for the door with his unwounded hand clutching his bleeding hand. Chen Chen instantly grabbed the man by his neck while at the same time forming a fist with his left hand and viciously jabbed it into the man’s stomach!

More than half of Chen Chen’s arm instantly sunk into the large beer belly of the charging man...

“Oof!”

Suddenly ambushed, the brawny man barely had the strength to whimper. His eyes turned into giant globes as he clutched at his stomach and slid into the ground, temporarily losing his mobility.

There was a scarcely-used medical term for such a scenario – stomach coma.

chapters.title: Chapter 303: First Confrontation With Basilisk
chapters.body:
Chapter 303: First Confrontation With Basilisk

Chapter 303: First Confrontation with Basilisk

After subduing the first enemy, the door to another classroom in front of Chen Chen suddenly snapped open. Another bearded man clutching at his arm rushed out of the door just in time to see Chen Chen taking down his partner.

Despite being invisible, Chen Chen’s large movement made his silhouette instantly noticeable to the other party.

The bearded man’s expression quickly changed as he instinctively reached for the gun before remembering that his arm was wounded. He immediately used his healthy arm to reach for a flashlight and charged at Chen Chen!

Enhanced flashlight?

The moment the flashlight emitted the first ray of light, Chen Chen quickly turned away from the source of light. It took a second before he realized that the bearded man did not aim the flashlight at his eyes, but instead at the wall right beside him!

A strange image appeared before Chen Chen!

It was an extremely bizarre image where hundreds of polygons seemed to be arranged in a predetermined pattern. They seemed to form the shape of an eye, but the eye was a combination of countless eyes drawn on top of each other. Some of them were laughing and some of them were crying. Some of them were like the squinting, smiling eyes of a lover while others were full of fear. It was like staring into a depthless abyss...

Joy, anger, sorrow, resentment, fear...

Countless eyes stacked on top of each other to produce this distinctive pattern. They were etched with senseless ink, but upon closer inspection, there was nothing left...

Upon seeing this image, Chen Chen felt his heart skipping a beat as he quickly twisted his neck away from the source. However, it was too late as the image had been carved into Chen Chen’s mind.

“Blasted!”

Chen Chen immediately made the connection and realized what he was looking at. His expression turned sharply while at the same time, countless graphene poker cards appeared and shot toward the bearded man!

“Swoosh swoosh swoosh!”

The light emitted from the flashlight in the bearded man’s hand was extinguished instantly. His eyes remained as large as they had been and he appeared as if he was frozen in place.

The very next second, his eyes, facial features, neck, body... Faint traces of blood appeared all over his body.

After that, the bearded man crumbled onto the ground like a tower of crumbling building blocks. Hundreds of chunks of broken flesh scattered all over the floor.

The hallway was immediately filled with a misty haze of blood.

At this moment, Chen Chen kept his eyes completely shut and his jaw was clenched together as tightly as he could. He felt as if there was a gigantic hand wrapping itself around his brain, the world appeared to be spinning out of control. The image illuminated by the flashlight was still burning in his mind, refusing to fade away...

“Sir Commander? Sir Commander!”

Inside the Bluetooth earpiece, the voice of X-112 sounded like it came from another dimension. Chen Chen tried to elicit a response but his voice was caught in his throat. He focused on taking repeated gasp, forcing air into his lungs even when his lungs were already inflated with air.

From the moment of Chen Chen’s betrayal until now, barely half a minute had passed. At the same time, the three Black Knights that were invisible and posted a short distance away had also arrived at the scene. They rushed into the building and surrounded Chen Chen to cover for him.

After what felt like another thirty seconds had elapsed, Chen Chen felt himself recovering slightly from the previous dizzy sensation. He felt a surge of joy as he mustered the strength to produce a sentence, “Don’t... Don’t mind me...

“Capture them alive if possible!”

Chen Chen let himself collapse onto the floor and clasped the floor with both hands to a point where even his fingernails were cracking and he remained unbothered. He felt like there was a terrible storm raging inside his mind. The height of the storm had only just passed and the roar of the wind was slowly fading out.

One second, two seconds, three seconds...

One minute, two minutes, three minutes...

The storm came out of nowhere but took its time dissipating. Although it only took three to five minutes, Chen Chen felt like he had been suffering for ages.

After recollecting himself from the slowly fading dizziness, Chen Chen picked himself back up from the ground. It was then when he noticed that his shirt was drenched in sweat and there was a pile of bile where he laid which he must have vomited without realizing it.

The image had somehow caused Chen Chen to throw up without even remembering it.

“Is this Basilisk?”

A curtain of shadow glimpsed past Chen Chen’s vision. He was sure it was the NZT-48 that saved him.

According to Fujiwara Aoki, the Basilisk triggered a “Gödel’s shock” phenomenon on whoever met its eyes and effectively shut down the brain. Although it was still unknown whether the effect was only temporary, the heart ceased to function along with the brain, which essentially meant that anyone who saw the Basilisk would die instantly.

An extremely potent brain was required to alleviate the major damage caused by Basilisk. This meant that only young people with strong, developing brains and agile thinking had a chance to survive from Basilisk.

However, even people like Fujiwara Aoki who was resistant to its effects had fainted for half an hour upon making eye contact with Basilisk. After ingesting the NZT-48, Chen Chen did not faint but instead experienced a violent reaction for several minutes after.

“I was careless.”

Chen Chen shook his head. Although he was confident that he could negate the effects of Basilisk, this would still mark one instance where he was closest to death over the course of all his intricate planning and strategizing so far.

With this in mind, Chen Chen approached the corpse that had been mutilated into numerous pieces. With a gentle wave of his hand, several poker cards so transparent they were nearly invisible proceeded to appear in Chen Chen’s hands.

After putting away the graphene poker cards, Chen Chen bent down and picked up the flashlight.

“Weaponizing the Basilisk pattern, what a brilliant idea...”

Chen Chen looked at the flashlight in hand and directed his attention to what was illuminated in front of the flashlight. Before doing this, he made sure to cover half of the flashlight’s bulb with his hand and proceed to look down.

In front of the flashlight was a mini copy of the Basilisk pattern carved on the hardwood.

Just now, the flashlight was used to project the Basilisk pattern located right on the wall beside Chen Chen. Completely unguarded, Chen Chen had his first encounter with the dangerous image right away.

It was largely unknown how the bearded man was able to pull this off and carve the Basilisk pattern in its entirety. Could it be that by covering one half of it while carving the other half, “Gödel’s shock” would not be triggered since the image was not displayed in full?

Chen Chen had a mild epiphany.

At the same time, the voice of X-112 came from the Bluetooth earpiece again. “Sir Commander, are you feeling better now?”

“Feeling much better now.”

Chen Chen nodded. “How’s the situation up there?”

“All the members in the school building have been captured alive. There are 18 of them in total, 12 of them are women. All of them were hiding in the cellar of the cafeteria located at the back of the building.”

X-112 answered faithfully.

A gleam of cold light flashed past Chen Chen’s eyes. It was true that some people were truly despicable. Regardless of how well you treat them, you would only receive hostility in return. Violence was the optimal solution to shatter the fantasies of such people.

It was after you conquer them when you would see them turning into loyal dogs, bowing respectfully to you like clockwork...

chapters.title: Chapter 304: Another Type Of Virus?
chapters.body:
Chapter 304: Another Type Of Virus?

Chapter 304: Another Type of Virus?

Inside the abandoned middle school, there was a burst of crying and pleading from a group of women.

The Black Knights and drones herded about a dozen women to the hall of the school’s teaching building. At this time, more than ten men who were bloodied lay sprawling across the hall.

Among these people, the injured ones sat where they were, quietly whimpering in pain while the uninjured or less injured ones were busy with medical care and treatment.

As the two groups of people met, many trembling women threw themselves into the arms of their male partners, crying and wailing loudly.

“Chen Chen-kun, no, Your Excellency Chen Chen, these women are innocent!”

At this time, Fujiwara Aoki, who had bandaged his wrists, knelt in front of Chen Chen with his body supported by one hand and begged. “All of this is our fault. If Your Excellency can’t quell your anger, I’m willing to commit seppuku[1] as a form of apology. I only ask that Your Excellency spare these people!”

With that, he lowered his head to the ground. “I beseech you!”

Currently, Chen Chen was sitting on his knees in front of the group. Even now, his head was still buzzing, and he did not know how many of his brain cells had died. If he was an ordinary person, it was likely that he would be rendered immobile by now.

The function of NZT-48, besides allowing people to obtain extraordinary mental capabilities, also suppressed any side effects in the body.

At this point, Chen Chen had put on the PK Armor again. He slowly raised his head and looked at Fujiwara. His voice was low and hoarse. “Fujiwara-kun, you’ve misunderstood. I’ve never said I’d do anything to you guys. My purpose in coming here is very simple — I just want to obtain information.”

“I can tell Your Excellency everything I know, and I’ll leave nothing out!”

Fujiwara Aoki suddenly looked up. “Not only that, but we also have a special file on ‘it’. Those Basilisk patterns and all kinds of mysterious phenomena are recorded in the file. Although I dare not say that it’s extremely detailed, it records all the mysterious events we’ve experienced so far!”

“Mysterious phenomena — do you mean something like the ‘woman in the dream’?”

Chen Chen’s heart leaped and he asked immediately.

“Yes!”

Fujiwara Aoki replied, “We can give you all this information. As long as Your Excellency spares us, that’s all we ask for!”

“Very well. Go and get the file then.”

Chen Chen nodded and then warned again, “After I receive the file, I’ll question each of you individually, so don’t try to deceive me with fake files. As long as I’m satisfied, I can even perform surgery to transplant your right hand back.”

As Chen Chen spoke, a Black Knight walked over with Fujiwara Aoki’s severed hand which had been placed in a metal box filled with ice cubes.

“Really?”

Fujiwara Aoki was overjoyed. He quickly stood up. “Then I’ll get it now!”

“Go.” Chen Chen waved his hand and watched Fujiwara Aoki leave promptly.

“Sir Commander, should we send a drone to stalk him?” X-112’s voice came from the headset.

“No, if he wants to run, just let him run.”

Chen Chen laughed icily and turned his gaze to the people in the hall. “Anyway, there are so many people here. We won’t miss him.”

However, Fujiwara Aoki did not intend to escape. Within ten minutes, he walked over with a thick hardcover book, which seemed to have a lot of materials stuffed between its pages. The reason they chose a hardcover book should be for preservation purposes.

Before Fujiwara Aoki could approach Chen Chen again, he was stopped by a Black Knight on the side. The Black Knight snatched the hardcover book and flipped through it page by page.

The Black Knight had no brain, so things like Basilisk patterns were completely ineffective against it. There was nothing to worry about even if Fujiwara Aoki had set a trap.

“In this volume, all Basilisk patterns have been placed inside the slots of the album. As long as you don’t pull them out, you won’t see the whole picture, so you can be guaranteed of its safety.”

At this time, Fujiwara Aoki explained, “As for the mysterious phenomena evolving from the Basilisk patterns, they can all be proven to not be infectious through text, because if the text is infectious, no one will record it. Because anyone who wrote down the text or read it will die from it.”

Infectious text...

Hearing this, Chen Chen could not help feeling horrified. Although in their previous conversation, Fujiwara Aoki had mentioned that the “it” evolving from the Basilisk patterns had taken many forms, it now appeared that the situation was more serious than he imagined.

Even words were able to kill?

As he thought of this, Chen Chen became even warier.

After the Black Knight had checked the information, X-112 said, “Sir Commander, it’s true that all the Basilisk patterns within are half-hidden and not fully exposed, but it can’t be determined if what he said about the text-recorded ‘mysterious phenomena’ is true.”

“It’s alright. Make him look through it himself,” Chen Chen ordered.

Following Chen Chen’s order, the Black Knight once again pushed the book back into Fujiwara Aoki’s hands.

At this, Fujiwara Aoki did not say much. He simply flipped through the entire hardcover book.

Although the hardcover book looked very thick, it was like a photo album. Each page was made with thick leather, so there was not much content. Fujiwara Aoki only took two or three minutes to flip through the book.

“Good.”

After he saw this, Chen Chen nodded and another Black Knight immediately stepped forward and led Fujiwara Aoki to the next room for surgery.

The God chips of these Black Knights all contained the full range of medical information. As long as there were enough tools, there was no difficulty for them in performing a limb surgery like this.

As for Chen Chen, he took the material and began to flip through, page by page.

In this collection of information, the pages were each laminated in plastic. It seemed that to preserve this data, this group of people had invested a lot of consideration.

Following that, Chen Chen turned to the first page.

The first page contained the Basilisk pattern named “Eye” that Chen Chen saw before, but this image was placed in the plastic frame of the book, so the margins were covered up. Thus when Chen Chen looked at this picture, despite feeling a little dazed, there were no symptoms.

Then, Chen Chen turned to the second page, only to see a short explanation written with a gel pen. The words in the book were in Japanese and they looked graceful and neat as if they were written by a woman.

[Basilisk pattern: Eye.]

[Symptoms: When the observer sees the “Eye”, it will induce Gödel’s shock, causing the death of the observer. The survival rate is not more than 10%.]

[Danger level: Level 3, extremely dangerous.]

[Method of infection: None, unable to spread by itself.]

Method of infection, unable to spread by itself...

As he read the final line, Chen Chen immediately realized, some Basilisk patterns could spread by themselves?

In that case, these Basilisk patterns were not only able to spread and infect humans, but they could also mutate during transmission. Was not this characteristic very similar to viruses?

As he considered this, Chen Chen seemed to have thought of something.

[1] A form of Japanese ritual suicide. See: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Seppuku

chapters.title: Chapter 305: Woman In Dreams
chapters.body:
Chapter 305: Woman In Dreams

Chapter 305: Woman in Dreams

Was this a virus that used information as its carrier?

This was Chen Chen’s most intuitive impression of these Basilisk patterns so far.

Nonetheless, this statement was still inaccurate because no matter how powerful a virus was, it could not conjure a woman out of thin air to strangle an infected person.

This completely went against the laws of physics.

With this in mind, Chen Chen continued turning to the next page.

The second picture was very similar to the first. It was in black and white and consisted of countless blocks. These blocks were laid out in a complicated, repetitive pattern, and the effect was dizzying. Even when Chen Chen saw it, he felt that his thoughts were becoming sluggish.

When these color blocks were pieced together, Chen Chen vaguely made out the figure of a reaper holding a sickle.

Fujiwara Aoki had mentioned this image to Chen Chen before. Its name was Reaper, and its ability was...

[Basilisk pattern: Reaper.]

[Symptoms: The observer will not react when seeing the “Reaper”. After the observer looks away, they will forget everything about this pattern until someone mentions this pattern to the observer. Then the observer will die instantly. The survival rate is not more than 5%.]

[Danger level: Level 3, extremely dangerous.]

[Method of infection: None, unable to spread by itself.]

When he saw this, Chen Chen could not help shaking his head. This Reaper was truly peculiar. Who knew how many people died, how many lives were stuffed within this image, before the survivors were able to sum up these rules?

It was likely that even a dozen lives were worth less than a single punctuation mark in this book.

After that, Chen Chen flipped the page again. There were seven or eight patterns behind. One of the patterns was called “Shaker”, which meant that after seeing that picture, the viewer would shudder all over until their cerebrospinal fluid and blood had been completely shaken out before they died.

One pattern was called “Flame”, indicating that after seeing that image, the person would suffer from spontaneous combustion out of thin air, burning from the inside until they were reduced to a pile of ashes.

There was even a pattern called “Filth”. Anyone who saw this pattern would suffer from dark blotches all over their body and this pattern could be transmitted to other people.

It was like the dark spots were alive. They first appeared on a random part and then started to spread across the entire body. As they spread, the human body began to become continuously carbonized and it finally reached the point where taking one step would break one’s hands and feet.

When the carbonization spread to the internal organs and the head, the infected person would then die...

After the series of patterns, the book also later noted that there were still a few pictures that had not been included and the nature of those patterns was not clear. One must be cautious and so on when encountering unknown patterns.

Afterward, Chen Chen continued to flip to the back and immediately saw the next paragraph, but these texts no longer had any patterns. Rather, they were phenomena that had evolved in bizarre manners.

[Mysterious phenomenon: Woman in Dreams.]

[Symptoms: The infected person will start to dream and it will be a terrifying nightmare. The scene in the dream is always astonishingly similar to the place where the infected person sleeps, that is to say — the place where the infected person sleeps will also be the setting of their dreams, whenever they dream. The only difference from reality is that they will see a woman in white in the dream.]

[They will not be able to clearly see the face of the woman in white, only a pair of white eyes without any pupils. When they see the woman, the infected person will always experience intense terror and wake up quickly from the fright.]

[However, after being infected by the strange phenomenon of the “Woman in Dreams”, each time they sleep, they will dream of her again. Moreover, the more they dream, she will get closer and closer to the infected person, and eventually reach the infected person.]

[After corresponding this to reality, bystanders will see the “Woman in Dreams” emerge from the chest of the infected person out of thin air, and choke the infected person to death, upon which the “Woman in Dreams” will also vanish.]

[Danger level: Level 2, somewhat dangerous.]

[Method of infection: Temporarily unidentified. It is known that physical contact with an infected person, sexual relations, sharing the content of the nightmare, and so on, will not cause infection.]

After reading this, Chen Chen immediately looked pensive. Was this the mysterious phenomenon that evolved from the Basilisk patterns? It was truly spooky.

Chen Chen suddenly thought, what should he do if he was infected with this stuff?

Although Chen Chen was still in big-brain-mode under the effects of NZT-48 at this time, when faced with this strange phenomenon, he could only come up with two solutions.

The first one: suicide.

Of course, this did not mean shooting oneself in the head. Rather, one would inject an anesthetic to keep one’s brain in a blank state where one would not dream. Upon waking up, NZT-48 would be quickly ingested. By allowing one’s brain to rest in this manner, one might be able to evade the killing method of the “Woman in Dreams”.

The second method was less punishing but riskier. Namely, to return to reality.

This was because Chen Chen had speculated that there was a great deal more to the USB drive. Was it possible that the creator of this USB drive had considered this situation beforehand and thus the portal was able to isolate such mysterious phenomena?

Chen Chen’s conjecture was not unreasonable, because in the USB flash drive’s first form, life forms could not be extracted. At one time Chen Chen thought that the USB drive really could not allow life to pass through.

However, it was not until the USB drive’s second form that Chen Chen understood, the first form seemed to be a protection for the weak.

The inability to extract life forms was to prevent the user from being killed, such as directly ingesting the T-virus, ALZ-113, and so on. A careless move could destroy the entire world or open a direct channel for alien pathogens to be brought into reality, triggering a catastrophe for humanity.

It was only when the user obtained a certain level of capability with the help of the USB drive, which was akin to the user having passed the test of the USB drive, that the USB drive would activate its second form and open a passage to another world.

In other words, the tests that the USB drive put the user through were gentle and gradual.

Therefore, could it be that the portal would isolate such strange phenomena? That, when Chen Chen crossed the channel and returned to reality, he would only return by himself while the mysterious phenomena would be unable to pass through the channel?

Still, this solution had its risks.

As the USB flash drive had never come with a manual since the beginning, Chen Chen had to rely on incremental discoveries. Consequently, if Chen Chen’s judgment was mistaken, would it not be equivalent to bringing this mysterious phenomenon into reality?

If this was the case, Chen Chen was still going to die and he might also take down the entire human civilization along with him.

“Still, it’s not as if that situation would be unavoidable.”

Chen Chen pondered, then mumbled softly, “I can use the Rubik’s cube to send back a message and ask Little X to close all exits near the passage or even build a Faraday cage. If I still can’t avoid the mysterious phenomenon’s fatal pursuit even after I’ve returned, I’ll just die by myself in the Faraday cage...

“After my death, Little X shall continue to charge the USB drive. When there’s enough power, stuff the entire room in which I died into Gantz’s dimension...”

An involuntary sneer crept across Chen Chen’s face as he thought about this. Indeed, this was basically the best solution. He would not have to sit around waiting for his own death nor would he spread the strange phenomenon into reality.

Following that, Chen Chen continued turning the pages, reading about one mysterious phenomenon after another.

chapters.title: Chapter 306: Yoru Kamidera
chapters.body:
Chapter 306: Yoru Kamidera

Chapter 306: Yoru Kamidera

[Mysterious phenomenon: Stalker.]

[Mysterious phenomenon: Shadow.]

[Mysterious phenomenon: Call of the Ocean.]

...

Chen Chen looked through them one by one. Even he could not help but feel a little uneasy. According to this information, this sort of mysterious phenomena seemed to be everywhere — sea, land, and air.

Even if human beings were to take flight, hide underground or go out to sea, it was difficult to escape the tightening noose of these phenomena. Even if human beings stopped listening, seeing, and speaking, sealing off their five senses, they could still be plagued by mysterious phenomena like the “Woman in Dreams” whose rules were still unknown.

Living in this world was like being in the deepest pit of despair. No wonder the humans in this world only persisted for less than five years before they had almost gone extinct.

Moreover, such mysterious phenomena were completely beyond the framework of the existing scientific system. In other words, these strange existences had gone beyond the scope of physics, chemistry, and biology, and were more like a part of a brand new discipline that humans had never discovered...

As he reflected on this, Chen Chen continued to turn the pages and immediately saw the last mysterious phenomenon.

[Mysterious phenomenon: Ghost at the Window.]

[Symptoms: A mysterious phenomenon resembling the “Eye” pattern. The mysterious phenomenon begins with a “person” floating in mid-air. It has the appearance of a human being, but its body is ceaselessly contorted, like a ghost. Appearing mostly between high-rise buildings, it is extremely lethal to survivors living in such buildings. Anyone who sees it outside the window will undergo symptoms similar to the “Eye” pattern, and the survival rate is not more than ten percent.]

[Danger level: Level 3, extremely dangerous.]

[Method of infection: Visual infection, mostly between high-rise buildings.]

When he read about this mysterious phenomenon, Chen Chen immediately thought of the white-collar corpses who had died suddenly, which he found when he was exploring the office building and the woman who hanged herself in a high-rise apartment.

Before they died, the white-collar workers had turned their heads to the floor-to-ceiling window, apparently seeing something outside the window.

As for the woman who hanged herself in the high-rise apartment, she had covered the glass of the window completely before she died. She had been clearly frightened of something outside the window.

The woman was afraid of the “Ghost at the Window” because she had seen it with her own eyes, yet she had not died. This meant that she was immune to that kind of damage, just like how some people were immune to the “Eye” pattern. However, she did not realize this.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen sighed to himself. He continued turning the pages but found that the pages behind were all blank. The mysterious phenomenon that Chen Chen had first encountered at the apartment when he came to Gantz’s dimension was not in these records.

“Are there only these five Basilisk patterns and these few mysterious phenomena?”

Chen Chen raised his head and questioned the group of captives who were still bustling about in the hall.

At once, everyone hurriedly lowered their heads to avoid the sharp glint in Chen Chen’s eyes.

“These are all the weird incidents that have been heard from the members of each team since the establishment of this team.”

At this point, a fifteen or sixteen-year-old youth with long hair suddenly spoke. The boy had delicate features and pale skin. At first glance, Chen Chen had thought he was a woman.

When Chen Chen heard this, he shot the youth a look. Unlike the others, the youth’s eyes were not the panicked eyes of a captive, but were rather calm instead. At first glance, he seemed out of place in this world.

Even so, behind this calm surface, there was a dead expression, which came from an indifference to life and death. This young man had some unusual experiences that caused this to happen.

“Yeah, yeah!”

It was not until the youth spoke first that some of the captives stammered and explained, “All of our known mysterious phenomena have been recorded in there. This volume of information is also the most precious item in our team. To survive in this world, this information is indispensable...”

Chen Chen lifted his chin, handed the information to the Black Knight beside him, and said, “X-112, record all the patterns inside.”

“Understood.”

The voice of X-112 came from the earphones. Chen Chen thought for a while and glanced at the somewhat unusual youth again.

The youth was wearing a T-shirt that was clearly becoming thin. It was rather faded and dirty, but it could not mask his androgynously attractive face.

At this time, the youth was swiftly bandaging a wounded person with skillful movements. However, unlike the others, he looked indifferent even when facing the wounded person as if he did not care about the injury of his companion.

It was not just this young man. Chen Chen noticed that this numb, detached look was also present in the eyes of several other women. Except for those women who were in a relationship with the men in the team, the women’s affiliation to the team was apparently not that simple.

These women were much more haggard than the others. Their bodies were thin and their clothes were ragged...

At once Chen Chen suddenly understood the role of these women.

Nonetheless, Chen Chen was not too surprised in this regard. After all, this was the tradition of the East China Sea Continent. Even as early as World War II, this country already had a similar social configuration.

The only thing that astonished Chen Chen was that this group of people did not even spare young boys. It was unscrupulous.

However, this matter had nothing to do with Chen Chen. Currently, he still had in mind his original mission in coming to Gantz. After X-112 had recorded the information provided by the team, Chen Chen stood up and directed a question at this group of people.

“Do any of you know the location of the nearest North American military base in the East China Sea Continent?”

Everyone stared at him, but no one answered Chen Chen.

Faced with this silence, Chen Chen raised his eyebrows in response and was about to continue to inquire, but at this moment, the youth suddenly spoke, “I know where. But the place has also been abandoned.”

Chen Chen looked at the youth. “How do you know?”

“I went there to seek asylum three years ago.”

The youth replied, “Not just there — I’ve also been to the military base where the East China Sea Self-Defense Force is located, but there’s nothing there besides corpses and ruins.”

Chen Chen just nodded. He must admit that this young man looked as if he was only eleven or twelve years old three years ago. It was admirable that he was able to survive alone in this peculiar apocalypse at that age.

“What’s your name?” Seeing this, Chen Chen instinctively asked with some interest.

“Yoru Kamidera.”

The young man lowered his head slightly as if to indicate respect for someone stronger. “Your Excellency, my name is Yoru Kamidera.”

“Very well, are you interested in coming with me?”

After Chen Chen’s question, the young man showed an emotion other than indifference for the first time. He seemed to be stunned, yet Chen Chen clearly observed that, among the group of captives who were asked to sit in the hall, two or three of them who seemed to be the ones in command had an abrupt shift in their expressions as if there was an unwilling glint in their eyes.

Of course, this unwillingness was not necessarily because they were exploiting the youth in that way. Rather, maybe this youth played another part in improving the lives of these people during this doomsday.

Seeing this, a smile curled up at the corner of Chen Chen’s mouth. He knew that he had guessed correctly.

chapters.title: Chapter 307: Thuds In The Night
chapters.body:
Chapter 307: Thuds In The Night

Chapter 307: Thuds in the Night

After Chen Chen’s invitation, the youth was silent for a while.

He seemed to be considering whether he would receive a different treatment after following Chen Chen and whether he could get a chance to turn his current life around.

In the eyes of the teenager, although it appeared that Chen Chen’s side was stronger, it was evident that going with Chen Chen was risky. If he stayed with the original team, he might live on in adversity for a while longer. Whereas with Chen Chen’s group, they seemed intent on confronting the mysterious phenomena.

“I need to think about it, Your Excellency.”

In the end, the youth lowered his head slightly and said this.

Chen Chen was not disappointed, but nodded and agreed.

He looked at the sky. It was already evening in the dimension of Gantz, so he said again, “Then give me an answer tomorrow.”

After saying that, Chen Chen left the group of people for X-112 to deal with while he led a Black Knight to the room where the team leader was.

After all, he had just undergone violent mental turmoil. If it were not for the support of NZT-48, Chen Chen would have passed out. Even if he was not in a coma, Chen Chen was at his limit by now and urgently needed a place to rest and rejuvenate.

In that case, the school occupied by this group of people was naturally the safest place so far. After all, if there were any danger here, it would not have become a gathering place for this group.

The room belonging to the leader of this doomsday survival team was located in the first dormitory of the teachers’ apartment. The windows here were also covered with newspapers and curtains. In addition, the room had thick bedding and complete living facilities. It was even on par with the average starred hotel.

When he saw this, Chen Chen immediately took off his PK Armor, ate a few energy bars to replenish his body’s water and nutrition, then collapsed directly onto the bed.

A strong wave of exhaustion struck him and Chen Chen fell asleep promptly before he could think about anything else.

Elsewhere, Fujiwara Aoki had his wrist attached, then he was made to join the rest in the hall of the teaching building to be monitored. The Black Knights who had no feelings and would never tire were fully adequate to deal with this group of people.

As the sun set, the brightly burning ball of fire slowly descended over the skyline of Tokyo. It was as if some unnamable shadow was slowly spreading. The originally well-lit metropolis was gradually replaced by an expanse of darkness.

After even the fire was put out, nighttime Tokyo became even eerier...

Chen Chen slept fitfully the entire night.

Perhaps it was because Chen Chen had looked directly into the “Eye” during the day. Even after he fell asleep, his brain was still aching faintly as if a blunt knife was stabbing in bit by bit. In just a few hours, Chen Chen was sweating profusely in his sleep.

In a thick haze, Chen Chen seemed to be dreaming...

“Thud...”

“Thud...”

“Thud...”

In the dream, something seemed to be ceaselessly, quietly thudding. The sound was muffled and soft. If one listened carefully, the sound was gone again.

Even so, as more time passed, the thudding seemed to become a little louder, rising from an insignificant volume to an increasingly distinct sound.

The Woman in Dreams?

Chen Chen suddenly woke up in the dream at an unknown point in time. This was very common for Chen Chen. After all, with his control over his brain, he could easily regain consciousness in his dream.

In other words, as long as Chen Chen wanted to, he could transform any dream into a lucid dream. This ability came from Chen Chen’s extremely powerful self-control.

Moreover, he had no sense of fear in his dream. At this time, in a sober state of mind, Chen Chen subconsciously looked to the door, but he did not see the figure of the mythical woman in white.

Could it be that this was not a mysterious phenomenon?

Seeing this, Chen Chen simply started to ponder in his dream. Following that, he once again heard a certain sound by his ear.

It was a sound of dull impact with an inexplicable rhythm, like a clock, constantly thudding in rhythm.

“Thud...”

“Thud...”

“Thud...”

This sound could not be made by the Black Knight. He was guarding the room and he would not make any meaningless sound.

Furthermore, based on the direction of the sound, it might not be in this room, but the next one...

Oh no.

Suddenly, Chen Chen seemed to realize something. He left the dream at once and slowly opened his eyes, only to realize that the room was pitch dark.

“Sir Commander, the room next door...”

Just as Chen Chen was awake, the voice of X-112 came from the Bluetooth headset that had not been taken off.

“Shh. I know.”

Chen Chen raised his index finger and made the gesture for silence, then immediately put on the PK Armor and adjusted his vision to thermal imaging.

Although thermal imaging could not penetrate a wall that exceeded a certain thickness, it was at least still very convenient in this dark night when he could not even see his own hands.

At this time, the dull thudding sound continued. The sound was not loud and it might even be ignored in normal times. However, in the deep night of doomsday, when it was so quiet that even one’s heartbeat could be heard, it was as clear as a lamp in the night.

“Thud...”

“Thud...”

The sound came again and again as if it would never stop. It was very peculiar.

“Impossible. When I asked the Black Knights to escort the group out, I’ve already checked every room. There should be no one next door.”

X-112 was a little confused. “Could someone have slipped through the cracks?”

“There’s no point in guessing about this now.”

Chen Chen whispered, “I’m already awake. Let the Black Knight go next door to check again. Plus, I suspect that...”

At this point, Chen Chen’s eyes were incomparably serious. The image of the hanged woman quickly flashed through his mind.

Seeing this, X-112 did not hesitate anymore but immediately controlled the Black Knight to open the door and walk toward the room next door where the sound was ceaselessly coming from.

“Click...”

With the sharp sound of a door lock being opened, the door of the next room was opened.

It seemed that this disruption had broken a certain balance and the thudding noise stopped abruptly at the same moment.

“Sir Commander...”

It felt as if eternity had passed or perhaps it was only two or three minutes. The voice of X-112 came from the headset again. “The next room has been explored and no signs of life have been found here, just...”

“I got it. I’ll go over now.”

Chen Chen did not ask any more questions but walked directly out of the room and came to the next door.

This must also be a room for high-level members of the team. Although the facilities in the room were not as comprehensive as the team leader’s room, it was also considered luxurious in a doomsday where resources were scarce.

Chen Chen just quickly glanced around the layout, then paid it no mind. After that, he followed the Black Knight’s gaze, only to see that in the wall before the Black Knight, there was an extra dent that had appeared out of thin air.

Chen Chen stepped forward and touched the dent, only to get white dust on his hand.

This dent was newly hammered in and the white plaster on the wall had not even fallen off. At this time, as Chen Chen touched it, a piece of white plaster finally fell off, exposing the red brick and concrete within the wall...

At this sight, Chen Chen fell completely silent.

chapters.title: Chapter 308: The Usb Drive’S Backlash
chapters.body:
Chapter 308: The Usb Drive’S Backlash

Chapter 308: The USB Drive’s Backlash

Of course, Chen Chen was no stranger to this scene.

In the daytime, he had just seen this scene where a female corpse was hanged in the room. There were a lot of dented marks on the wall. When he had gone to the neighboring room, he saw that the public wall had a deep mark from a significant impact.

That mark was about 20 cm in diameter and about 5 cm deep, which was much deeper than the mark in front of him now. Still, Chen Chen was certain that the source of these two marks was the same.

This was because the deeper dent he had come across appeared to be formed under a long-term impact whereas this mark seemed to be recently made.

‘Coincidence?

‘Or...’

At this moment, he seemed to understand why there were signs that the woman had pressed her ear to the wall in the room where her corpse was hanging and the traces of the wall being hit with a baseball bat.

Perhaps when that woman was alive, on a night filled with despair and darkness, she had also noticed this hollow and strange thudding sound...

A dark light flashed in Chen Chen’s eyes.

He looked around. If there were any people or animals hidden in the room, they would have been noticed when the Black Knight came in earlier. This was because Chen Chen was sure that the thudding had stopped when the Black Knight opened the door.

The teachers’ dormitories were all single rooms. When the door was opened, the entire room could be seen in a glance. If it had been a human or an animal, it was impossible that the Black Knight would fail to notice it.

In other words, that thing was so fast that it could vanish right in this room as soon as the Black Knight opened the door.

With this in mind, Chen Chen came to the rear window of the dormitory again. He touched the window lightly. The window was shrouded in newspaper and it was also locked from the inside. There was no way a person or an animal could have escaped from the window.

Since there was no possibility of a getaway, what was it that could avoid the Black Knight, the thermal imaging vision of the PK Armor or even the detection of an artificial intelligence near the level of Little X?

What was it that would beat against the wall deep in the night?

All of this was almost completely inconsistent with the common understanding of physics. Chen Chen narrowed his eyes and had a vague notion.

“So, I’ve been targeted by a ‘mysterious phenomenon’.”

Chen Chen frowned. He did not expect that, despite the precautions he had taken beforehand, and being so cautious when looking at the information of the doomsday survival squad, he would still be infected with a “mysterious phenomenon”.

What was even more troublesome was that this particular mysterious phenomenon was not in the records of the data. That was to say, Chen Chen needed to personally obtain an understanding of this mysterious phenomenon, sum up its rules, then seek a way out within those rules.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen glanced around the dormitory one last time, then turned to leave and return to the team leader’s bedroom.

He lay down on the bed tiredly again. Although Chen Chen’s brain was still aching slightly, he did not continue to sleep but began to analyze silently.

“X-112, help me record a new mysterious phenomenon.”

Chen Chen said softly, “The name of the mysterious phenomenon is called: Thuds.”

X-112 immediately responded, “Recording has already started.”

Chen Chen nodded. “The information produced by this group is still too general. I think that for a more comprehensive understanding of the mysterious phenomena, at least four categories are needed, namely: phenomenon characteristics, infection symptoms, infection conditions, and danger assessment.

“The characteristic of the ‘Thuds’ phenomenon is that after a human is infected, there will be rounds of thudding noises next door. And if one heads to the next room at this point, one will not see anyone, only the traces of the physical impact on the wall.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and added. “It’s not yet clear what triggers the thudding sounds, whether it’s triggered by the infected person staying in a room for a long time or by the late night time? Plus, how long will this thudding sound continue and if it lasts too long, and a hole is knocked through the wall, what will happen...”

“What if you change rooms?” X-112 asked suddenly.

“There’s no use changing rooms.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “It can be seen from the room where the female corpse was hanged that the woman had two bedrooms when she was alive. She heard the thuds against the wall of one bedroom. She noticed something was wrong and apparently changed to the other bedroom. However, the thuds began again on the other side of the bedroom wall.”

“No wonder she has dents in the walls of the two adjacent bedrooms...”

X-112 understood at once. “If changing rooms can’t solve the problem, then how should we counter this?”

“I don’t know.”

Chen Chen shook his head, displaying a solemn expression for the first time. “This is something I have never encountered before. In truth, since just now, I have been prepared for death.”

“Sir Commander...”

X-112 had nothing to say.

“It’s okay. There’s nothing much to say to that.”

Chen Chen waved his hand. “I have long understood the truth that the one who wants to wear the crown must bear its weight. If I want to obtain power from the USB flash drive, sooner or later I’ll experience a backlash from it. I just didn’t expect that this day would come so quickly.”

“Maybe this mysterious phenomenon won’t kill you?”

X-112 said persuasively, “After all, that woman died not because she was killed by the thudding noises, but because she hanged herself. That is to say, it’s very likely that the lethality of this mysterious phenomenon isn’t that formidable.”

“That is just wishful thinking.”

Chen Chen retorted, “Perhaps the thuds will last for several nights and the longer it lasts, the more it will shatter the emotions of the infected person, just like the Basilisk pattern. Who knows — the sound itself may be the lethal thing.

“Maybe the power of the thudding sound hasn’t been shown for now, but it doesn’t mean that it’s not a threat. In fact, in my opinion, there’s no logic at all with a mysterious phenomenon like this, so we must not assess it with common sense. Otherwise, I’ll definitely die a miserable death...”

After listening to Chen Chen’s rhetorical statements, X-112 was completely silent.

“At present, it seems that the characteristics of the mysterious phenomenon ‘Thuds’ have yet to be explored. The symptoms and conditions of infection are even more unknown. We don’t even have leads for the danger assessment as we lack sufficient clues.”

Chen Chen said, “So, after this, I’ll lie on the bed first to see if there’ll be more thudding noises tonight. If the thuds occur again, it means that they’re related to how long I stay in a room. If not, it means that there’s no connection there.

“I also recall that during the day, other than myself, the three Black Knights also saw the hanged female corpse, so would they also be infected with this mysterious phenomenon?”

After thinking for a while, Chen Chen added again. “X-112, let the Black Knight who is in charge of guarding me go to another dormitory. Just find any room that’s not the one next door. I want to see if the same thuds will occur for him.”

“Got it.”

After X-112 replied, the Black Knight turned around and left.

After watching the Black Knight leave, Chen Chen nodded and asked again, “Didn’t you notice? This kind of mysterious phenomenon is quite interesting and there are almost limitless possibilities we can try.

“For example, if I install a camera next door, what do you think I’ll see? Or if I move to a room made entirely of glass, what will happen then? And if I stay in a detached room that has no other rooms around it, will the thuds still become knocks on the door?”

As Chen Chen said this, there was a cheerful glimmer in his eyes. “In a nutshell, with this mysterious phenomenon, I can carry out many, many attempts, just like a rigorous experiment. And this is the kind of experiment that I’m best at...”

chapters.title: Chapter 309: Hyperthymesia
chapters.body:
Chapter 309: Hyperthymesia

Chapter 309: Hyperthymesia

In this manner, Chen Chen conversed with X-112 while waiting for the sound of the thudding to begin again.

However, as time ticked by the minute, the sound never resumed. It was not until four o’clock in the morning the next day that Chen Chen sank into sleep again.

When Chen Chen opened his eyes again, it was already noon the next day.

After a night of sleep, Chen Chen’s head finally did not hurt as much as it did yesterday. Although there was still a slight ache, at least it was within a tolerable range.

“Sir Commander, after you fell asleep last night, the sound didn’t start up again and the Black Knight in the other room didn’t experience any strange phenomena.”

X-112 reported.

“Then what about the two Black Knights guarding the group, have they encountered anything unusual?”

Chen Chen sat up, rubbed his temples, and asked.

“No. Everything was as usual.”

“In that case, it’s likely that the problem is with me...”

Upon hearing this, Chen Chen’s expression suddenly sharpened. “Although the Black Knights don’t have a brain, no matter what, they are still living beings. If even I were infected by a mysterious phenomenon, it doesn’t make sense that the Black Knights wouldn’t be infected, unless these mysterious phenomena can distinguish between normal humans and controlled humans?

“So, it must be because I did something in that room before that the Black Knights didn’t, which led to the mysterious phenomenon only infecting me, but not the Black Knights.”

Thinking of this, Chen Chen began to recall everything that happened yesterday. What he had done was simple — he had gone to the room next door and touched the dent of the impact. At that time, he had gotten white plaster all over his hand.

“Is it because I touched the marks left by the thuds?”

Chen Chen was not sure about this.

After putting on the PK Armor again, Chen Chen led the Black Knight out of the room and came to the hall of the teaching building again. After a night of being supervised in a group, the expressions of these survivors were a little haggard. After the group was captured, their mentality had changed.

“How did they behave last night? Is the situation still stable?”

Chen Chen observed everyone and suddenly asked.

“It’s fairly stable. In truth, several of these people had plans to escape or even fight back. After all, they are people living in a doomsday, so they’re not averse to taking risks. However, the Black Knights are too good at keeping watch and this group of people never found the opportunity to do so.”

X-112 replied, “But these people definitely don’t know that, even if they are whispering, every word can be clearly heard by us. Last night, I heard the team leader making quite a few offers to Yoru Kamidera such as promising that he’ll no longer be their slave and will instead, become a full member, and other arrangements with food and living facilities.

“Again, Yoru Kamidera didn’t agree right away, but replied that he had to think about it.”

Hearing this news, Chen Chen nodded. Sure enough, Yoru Kamidera must have some specialty that was extremely beneficial to the team. Otherwise, the team leader would not possibly risk offending Chen Chen by trying to get Yoru Kamidera to stay.

“Yoru Kamidera, what have you decided?”

As soon as he entered the hall, Chen Chen immediately asked Yoru Kamidera.

“Your Excellency, I’m willing to go with you.”

Yoru Kamidera immediately replied upon hearing this.

“Very good.”

Chen Chen nodded. “In this case, come with me to locate the military base now.”

Although Yoru Kamidera was a little puzzled about Chen Chen’s objective in finding the military base, he did not say anything, only stood up and walked to Chen Chen’s side.

At this moment, the Bugatti Spaceship arrived, driven by X-112, and landed in front of Chen Chen.

Following Chen Chen’s directive, Yoru Kamidera boarded the spaceship with a lively expression. Then, Chen Chen and the three Black Knights went in after him. When the gull-wing door closed and the Bugatti Spaceship took off, the group in the teaching building seemed to awaken from a stupor and sighed in relief.

Both Chen Chen’s arrival and departure had happened so quickly without any warning signs. They felt as if they had just woken up from a dream.

Fujiwara Aoki tapped his plaster cast with his right hand as he stared after the departing spaceship with a complicated expression. If it were not for the hostility of his team yesterday, they would not have suffered a double loss.

Of course, Chen Chen would not be concerned about the thoughts of a group of bugs. After only half an hour of journey, under Yoru Kamidera’s guidance, Chen Chen finally located the North American military base stationed in the East China Sea Continent.

As a country that was defeated in World War II, the East China Sea Continent did not have the right to form an army, only the right to self-defense. Therefore, there was a large number of North American military bases in the East China Sea Continent.

The reason Chen Chen wanted to find this place was for the sake of tracking down Gantz’s technological information.

In Chen Chen’s view, it was very likely that humans had not fully grasped the technology given to them by the alien civilization and could only create machines far beyond human cognition through blind imitation, such as the Gantz sphere. Nonetheless, this was also enough.

This was because if humans could replicate the Gantz black sphere, then as long as the methods were not too unreasonable, perhaps in reality, Chen Chen could also achieve mass production of the Gantz sphere.

If this was the case, then Chen Chen would essentially have a super army that could shuttle around the world at any time, coming and going like phantoms. It would not be like before when they had to rely on international flights to carry out activities all over the world.

Although this kind of military base might not necessarily have the technology of the Gantz black sphere, Chen Chen could find more clues about the black spheres through the military base, thereby resuming the investigation and greatly reducing the time taken to find the needle in the haystack.

Perhaps because it was Yoru Kamidera’s first time in a flying vehicle like this, for once his eyes were shimmering with wonder throughout the entire journey. Only then was there a trace of naivety, unlike his previous self — evidently a youth, yet with dull, dejected eyes as if he cared for nothing in this world.

“This is it. This is the port of Yokohama, on the west coast of Tokyo Bay in central Honshū. It’s also the largest seaport in the East China Sea.”

Currently, Yoru Kamidera pointed to a position on the holographic screen and said softly, “Because this is the largest port in the East China Sea, it was regulated by North America. The Yokohama North American military base in the East China Sea was established here.”

“You seem to know all this very well.”

Chen Chen asked reflexively, “Is that your specialty?”

“No, my specialty is very simple, it’s just a kind of hyperthymesia.”

Yoru Kamidera shook his head. “It’s what people also call a photographic memory. I can remember everything that happens each year, month or day, every hour, minute, and second. The scene of any day can be rewound like a cassette tape and reconstructed in my mind.”

“Hyperthymesia?”

Chen Chen was startled. Did this not mean that Yoru Kamidera could even remember things like the Basilisk patterns?

chapters.title: Chapter 310: Yygq
chapters.body:
Chapter 310: Yygq

Chapter 310: yygq

Chen Chen had also once looked up this neurological disease that seemed to be a superpower.

In the past, the scientific community believed that forgetting was a passive process and those memories that were used less often would disappear slowly and automatically.

However, with more in-depth research, an increasing amount of evidence showed that the loss of memory was not a passive process. On the contrary, forgetting was more of an active process.

Human brains were constantly forgetting. This was a preset function of the brain, like a program that was continuously running in the background. One could not see it, but it truly existed.

It was not until 2017 that scientists verified this process. This was a mechanism of active forgetting, called “essential forgetting”. It involved a specific subset of cells inside the brain. These cells were called “forgetting cells,” which broke down the imprints of the memory cells.

The prefrontal cortex of the brains in hyperthymesia patients was very different from that of ordinary people. Whenever this region tried to compress information, it became abnormally active, preventing the compression of information. Therefore, they could not forget certain memories.

Nonetheless, although patients with hyperthymesia had an exceptional, unique memory, not all of them would reach the apex of life as ordinary people might assume. On the contrary, they could not even become the top student in their class.

This was because, despite hyperthymesia patients having powerful storage and memory capabilities, there was usually a problem with the function that halted memory retrieval.

In other words, the brains of patients with hyperthymesia were often in a state of nonstop retrieval. This caused them to be in perpetual anguish and their work and study were often affected by recollections of the past. In severe cases, this could lead to insomnia and excessive dreaming, even resulting in mental illness.

“Of course, I haven’t always had this ability.”

At this time, Yoru Kamidera continued. “Strictly speaking, I must’ve obtained it when I woke up after seeing the ‘Eye’ and falling into a coma for three hours.”

Chen Chen’s expression sharpened after hearing this. It was true that most patients with hyperthymesia were not born with this ability, but rather, they only acquired it after certain incidents. However, this must be the first time someone had obtained this ability through a Basilisk pattern.

“Doesn’t this mean that you can even recall the Basilisk pattern?”

Chen Chen looked at the military base that was getting closer and asked with interest, “So, do you remember the image of the ‘Eye’?”

“I do. I can even draw it out by hand.”

Yoru Kamidera said, “Also I’ve found that my resistance to Basilisk patterns is getting stronger and stronger. I even have a certain degree of resistance against patterns such as ‘Reaper’, ‘Flame’, and ‘Filth’.”

After some thought, Yoru Kamidera added. “Your Excellency, the information you saw was written by me and the Basilisk patterns in it were all drawn by me.”

Chen Chen could not refrain from applauding. “It seems that your role is even greater than I thought. What about against ‘mysterious phenomena’?”

“I’m sorry to say that I’m useless against ‘mysterious phenomena’.”

Yoru Kamidera shook his head. “The leader of the team once asked me to make a similar attempt but it failed. Fortunately, the mysterious phenomenon was not that lethal, otherwise, I’d have died.”

Chen Chen nodded and said no more.

At this time, the spaceship had reached the top of a building near the Yokohama military base.

The three Black Knights got off the spaceship with hard drives and data cables, heading toward the military base. There should be a server room belonging to the military base in there. As long as the diesel generator was located, the server room could be re-operated.

Chen Chen and Yoru Kamidera stayed on the spaceship, waiting for the mission to progress.

To prevent encountering Basilisk patterns, Chen Chen did not even turn on the video, but just used X-112 as a hub that would report the situation encountered by the Black Knights in the military base at all times.

“At present, it looks like there are no guards in the military base.

“The Black Knights found some human traces in the weaponry. It seems that doomsday survivors occasionally come here to replenish ammunition and other supplies.

“It’s certain that the warehouse has been emptied out a very long time ago. This must’ve happened soon after doomsday occurred...

“Still searching. We have found the diesel generators, but the server room hasn’t yet been found...”

A string of updates came from X-112.

Chen Chen was bored out of his mind, so he simply continued to look through the black diary, hoping to find other clues from it.

Seeing that Chen Chen was riffling through the diary, Yoru Kamidera beside him could not help but say, “The diaries of the dead are very dangerous because many of the mysterious phenomena that have evolved from the Basilisk patterns are highly contagious. There are even some that are simply a piece of text.”

“I know.” Chen Chen nodded. “But are there any specific examples for reference? I have only heard of other types of mysterious phenomena, but never any that are textually based.”

“I’ve only heard people mentioning highly fatal mysterious phenomena that are based in text, but they didn’t go into detail because if anyone hears those words, they’re a goner.”

Yoru Kamidera looked quite somber. “But I know of a textual mysterious phenomenon that isn’t very contagious and with relatively milder effects.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Tell me about it.”

“This was about the time when the ‘Eye’ had just appeared on the Internet.”

Yoru Kamidera was caught up in his memory. “I had just turned ten that year and I encountered the ‘Eye’. In that disaster, my parents died and only I survived...

“Later, I heard someone mention that they’ve found a strange network domain name, which is a website address. The full website address is www.yygq.com. You can log in to that website whether with a mobile phone or a computer.

“The name of that website is just four letters — yygq, so this website is also called the yygq website.

“However, this URL doesn’t exist. It’s not in the URL registration records of any country, yet it still can be easily accessed. After entering the site, the page will display a forum with a black background and green text. The functionality of this forum is extremely crude. It’s as simple as it can get.

“Nevertheless, I heard people say at the time that, as long as anyone has logged on to that website and registered to leave a comment, whatever words they type on the forum, in whatever language, will be automatically translated into traditional Chinese. Moreover, it will be in a specific format.”

“Traditional Chinese?”

Chen Chen was taken aback at once.

“Yes. For example, if you type ‘How are you’ in Japanese, then after typing it out, the words will become “Your ashes have not been scattered[1] yet, right?”, or if you type in Japanese, ‘I am fine too’, it will automatically become ‘I’m feeling much better’.”

[1] “I’ll scatter your ashes” is a Chinese catchphrase that originated from Douyin, meant to express extreme fury.

chapters.title: Chapter 311: Pale White Palm
chapters.body:
Chapter 311: Pale White Palm

Chapter 311: Pale White Palm

Yoru Kamidera continued. “Or perhaps the sentence you type will become ‘Is it possible that other people think I’m not doing well, it can’t be, can it?’. By the time you tried to explain that ‘I’m not trying to judge anyone’, the sentence turns into ‘He’s triggered, he’s triggered!’ or variations of such sentences that upset people in general.

“Furthermore, when you’re proved by people saying such things and try to reply, ‘Please be civilized’, what you type will instead be sent as ‘Anything else?’, ‘Are you done?’, ‘Quit being so loud, what’s your problem!’, you get the idea...”

“...”

Chen Chen became speechless and shot a look at Yoru Kamidera. “That’s all?”

“This is only the prelude.”

Yoru Kamidera flashed a bitter smile. “What’s stranger was what came after that. Since then, strange things happened to the people who had logged onto the forum. Everything they say in real life, write on paper or just any form of communication as a whole turns into Chinese pronunciations. The tone of the translation is aggressive as well. This effect only slowly fades away after one week.”

“You can’t escape it even in reality?”

Chen Chen was stunned upon hearing that. It was then when he made the connection that it must have been related to the mysterious text phenomenon.

“Yes.”

Yoru Kamidera added. “This may seem comedic compared to the other mysterious phenomenon, but on a fundamental level it was no different from the other cases. It also has a transmission method of its own.

“Take this current observation with the mysterious text, for example. It’s transmitted through the yygg website and the symptom of the infection is that one will speak in a specific pattern.
