In this sense, its actual damage and threat aren’t significant. But you can only imagine that if the symptom of the infection is changed to something else such as instant death on the spot, then this will instantly turn into an extremely lethal occurrence.

“Or better yet, instead of the condition of transmission being ‘logging onto the website’, it changes to ‘anyone who hears such phrases are immediately infected with a similar condition’. You’ll immediately see it rapidly spreading across the globe.”

“That’s true.” Chen Chen nodded.

Yoru Kamedera went on. “This is why something like a diary of the deceased can be extremely dangerous. That’s because you have no clue what took the person’s life, there’s a chance that the person died of such bizarre cases that are conducted by reading specific text...”

While the two of them were discussing these mysterious phenomena, there was suddenly a slight knock on the glass beside the seat where Chen Chen was seated.

“Dong!”

Yoru Kamidera continued. “Apart from that, there’s another potential risk. Which is that those strange texts may bring another...”

Before he could finish his sentence, there was another knock.

Chen Chen’s expression became grave as he turned to look outside the cabin window of the spaceship.

“Dong!”

The moment Chen Chen turned to inspect, a horrifically pale hand left its prints on the cabin window of the spaceship!

Yoru Kamidera was visibly shocked by the sudden commotion.

Chen Chen felt his heart tighten as well. He continued to stare silently at the imprint of the palm on the cabin window. However, the cabin windows of the Bugatti Spaceship were not made with glass materials but instead, a form of semi-transparent materials that came fully attached to the doors. Therefore, it was difficult to get a clear view of objects outside the spaceship.

From where Chen Chen sat, he could see that there were hardly any fine details in the palm. There were no fingerprints, no palm prints, nor was it adorned with any jewelry. All that could be seen was boney veins crawling across the slender fingers.

The only remaining notable feature was the distinctive paleness.

It was an extremely uncomfortable sight. Such eerie paleness would rarely if ever be associated with fair skin, but instead gave the impression of a cold, dead body.

There was no way this was the palm of a person...

Unlike Yoru Kamadera, Chen Chen hardly showed any signs of being agitated or fearful. He only promptly raised his head to ask, “X-112, what happened? Can you detect what’s outside the spaceship?”

What added to Chen Chen’s surprise was that X-112 did not respond at all!

“X? Urgent command input, X-112, I order you to respond!”

Chen Chen asked again but the X-112 artificial intelligence integrated with the spaceship seemed to have been shut down. No matter how Chen Chen tried to summon the copy of Little X, he could not draw a response.

“Sir, it’s no use.”

Yoru Kamidera started to calm down. Although he was just a common person, he did possess a coolness exceeding his peers of the same age as he remarked, “Usually, when one of these mysterious phenomena are triggered, the victims enter a state which they cannot pluck themselves out of and even nearby observers cannot interact with them. It’s pretty clear now that the intelligence system in this spaceship can no longer hear your orders.”

“Then why can you still interact with me?”

Chen Chen gave up on communicating with X-112 and turned to Yoru Kamadera. “Are you targeted by it as well?”

“It’s possible.”

Yoru Kamidera nodded. “But allow me to wager a guess. If I’m not mistaken, you must’ve been targeted by these strange events previously, right?”

Without waiting for Chen Chen to respond, Yoru Kamidera continued. “The truth is, many of these mysterious phenomena have a contagious characteristic, just like viruses and diseases do. An infected person may transmit the condition via countless mediums you would never think of, such as a simple sentence, a simple touch, an eye contact or simply just being in the same room.

“Sure, I’m just speculating at the end of the day since I’ve never encountered this variation of the phenomenon before. Even now, I’m not sure if I’m actually infected, but I think the reason I can still interact with you now is that the full effect of the phenomena hasn’t unraveled yet. You’re currently in the incubation phase.”

“Incubation phase? Contagious? Infection symptom...”

Chen Chen found himself at loss for words for a brief moment before flashing a smile. “Not bad, treating these mysterious phenomena as a disease. I like these terms.”

As the two of them continued discussing, Chen Chen suddenly noticed that the mark of the palm had disappeared.

An apt way to put it should be that the two of them did not notice when it had disappeared. It was as if the imprint of the palm was never there at all. Even if the two of them fixed their combined attention on the palm, they would still fail to notice the exact moment which it vanished.

It was as if they suddenly dozed off for a split instant and the palm just vanished without a trace.

Upon reevaluating it, did the palm even exist in the first place?

Chen Chen displayed a thoughtful look.

“X-112?”

Chen Chen asked again.

“Yes, Sir Commander.”

X-112 responded almost instantly, “Is there something I can help you with?”

Chen Chen immediately fell silent.

“Sir Commander?” Failing to generate a response from Chen Chen, X-112 enquired again.

“Nothing.”

Chen Chen shook his head and clicked the button to open the door, which caused the gull-wing doors[1] of the Bugatti to shift upward, allowing them to see what was outside the spaceship.

Chen Chen stepped out of the spaceship to survey his surroundings. After that, he closed the doors and inspected the surface of the gull-wing door.

There were no palm prints on the gull-wing door, but there was a slightly caved in mark left behind.

It would appear that something had gently knocked against the door of the spaceship twice...

[1] An automotive industry term describing car doors that are hinged at the roof rather than the side

chapters.title: Chapter 312: Area 51
chapters.body:
Chapter 312: Area 51

Chapter 312: Area 51

Chen Chen had originally thought that such a collision only occurred during midnight. Its second appearance had effectively thrown off Chen Chen’s current hypothesis of its pattern.

Besides, Chen Chen was not in a room but inside a spaceship. Or could this mean that the trigger condition was not limited to a room, but just any enclosed spaces?

With this in mind, Chen Chen checked his watch and saw that the time now was ten-thirty in the morning. He suddenly asked, “X-112, what were you doing one minute ago?”

“Me?”

X-112 appeared somewhat confused but still looked up its activity log following Chen Chen’s orders. It answered shortly after, “Sir Commander, please take a look. I was operating all this time.”

“Did you not hear a knock outside the door?”

Chen Chen asked.

“No...”

X-112 went to inspect the spacecraft and quickly noticed the marks left on the door, “That’s not right, when did this mark show up? Even the live surveillance footage of the spaceship didn’t show anything...”

“The spaceship is monitored?”

Chen Chen lit up at the prospect. If there was surveillance footage, that meant he may get a glimpse of what the owner of the pale white palm looked like.

“Yes, the spaceship is equipped with holographic projection surveillance which covers all angles. I should be able to notice any notable changes on the spaceship.”

X-112 assured promptly.

“Then please rewind to the footage from one to two minutes ago.”

Chen Chen gave the order. Immediately after that, X-112 produced a projection of the holographic three-dimensional image of a miniaturized version of the spaceship that stood at the height of a regular person’s waist. There was even a display on its side indicating the time of the event being ten twenty-eight and zero seconds.

It would appear that this was the live footage of the spaceship at the time.

Viewing from the hologram, there was nothing in view in the space surrounding the spaceship.

However, when the clock ticked to twenty-eight minutes and forty seconds, the entire hologram suddenly turned blurry appearing as if the projection was disrupted. What was originally a high-resolution hologram suddenly turned into a chaotic splatter of colors.

This condition persisted for thirty-five seconds. When the clock ticked at twenty-nine minutes and fifteen seconds, the hologram had once again reverted to a normal state.

When this happened, a new mark left behind by a collision appeared on the gulf-wing door of the spaceship.

“The mysterious phenomenon had occurred again just moments ago?”

Upon seeing this footage, X-112 expressed its confusion, “But I didn’t notice anything at all.”

“I understand.”

Chen Chen remarked solemnly, “Based on your activity logs we can also see that your program is running just fine in this thirty-five-second span. The reason you were not able to notice it is because the problem lied with the spaceship and myself...”

“If that’s the case, then why was I able to notice something being off yesterday?”

X-112 was a little perplexed.

Chen Chen ruminated on this for a moment before turning to Yoru Kamidera who was in the spaceship. “Because just now, it was only me and Yoru Kamidera who encountered the ‘mysterious phenomenon’.”

When Chen Chen considered this, he further instructed, “X-112, update the data on ‘sound of impact’.

“Based on the second experience of the infected codenamed ‘O5’ with the mysterious phenomenon, the findings are – the ‘sound of impact’ may only occur in fully-enclosed spaces and isn’t limited by time, meaning that it may occur during both daytime and nighttime. As of now, we’re unable to determine the trigger condition for the ‘sound of impact’, but we can conclude that any other living being in the same room as O5 can detect the same impact as well...”

Chen Chen quickly added. “Apart from that, upon triggering the second ‘sound of impact’, O5 saw a palm appearing. At the time, O5 was inside the Bugatti Spaceship and saw the shape of the palm through the semi-transparent cabin window. So far, we can conclude that the palm is connected to the ‘sound of impact’...”

After updating the associated data, Chen Chen could not help but steal another glance at the cabin window of the spaceship.

The structure of the window was extremely sturdy and was built into the door of the spacecraft.

Although the quality of the spacecraft did not appear particularly outstanding in the movie, the fact that it could remain intact after experiencing a crash at supersonic speed was a fine indicator of its superior quality.

It should be safe to say that the Bugatti Spaceship had no problem holding itself against regular bullets. Even a sniper rifle may not be able to shatter its windows.

“It may appear that the cabin windows just saved my life.”

Chen Chen reflected thoughtfully. “If the windows were built using ordinary engineered plastics or glass instead of extremely resilient material designed to withstand the atmospheric pressure of outer space, I may be long dead already.”

After all, the appearance of the palm was far too outlandish, Chen Chen no longer felt comfortable with relying on his luck.

His gut was telling him that one of these days, he would be in big trouble if the owner of the hand somehow broke through the barrier between them...

After that, Chen Chen did not return to the spaceship right away. Instead, he continued issuing orders outside the spaceship. Within an hour, the three Black Knights had found the server room of the military base by utilizing the unmanned drones. They started setting up the diesel generators.

Everything progressed without a hitch after that.

Within half an hour, the server units that had been retired for more than five years began operating again. Although there were security preventative measures such as firewalls, they hardly stood a chance against X-112. After thorough scanning, X-112 extracted all the secret information.

However, these secrets and news of scandals had long lost any relevance or significance following the demise of human civilization. What Chen Chen was after was information related to the Gantz sphere.

This time, it took X-112 longer to scan.

Chen Chen waited for more than three hours before X-112 finally brought news. “Sir Commander, I’ve verified the location of the production workshop of the Gantz sphere. Originally, there were only three production facilities. One was located in German, the other in North America, and the final one in the Mainland.

“After the great calamity, the production of the Gantz sphere was practically completely halted, but the research wasn’t suspended and was only conducted in secret instead. Based on the current information, the known position of the North American research center is inside the Area 51 military base.”

“Alright, then let’s get ready to depart.”

Chen Chen ordered, “I think for this spaceship of ours, it shouldn’t take too long to travel from the East China Sea region to North America.”

“Traveling in the atmospheric layer of the Earth should take approximately four hours. If we exit the Earth atmospheric layer and aim for North America in a pattern akin to ballistic missiles, it should only take one hour and twenty-five minutes.”

X-112 replied.

The described ballistic missile flight method was the application of vertical take-off to help exit the earth’s atmosphere, then flying along a predetermined path toward a set location.

Due to the absence of air resistance in a vacuum environment, the missile could reach a tremendous speed. Under such conditions, a ballistic missile could advance toward its target at a speed of up to Mach 24.

It was a devastating speed. By the time it reentered the Earth’s atmospheric layer and began approaching its target, there was not a single entity in the world that could obstruct it in its path.

This was what a ballistic missile was capable of.

To be clear, such flight methods were not only constricted to missiles but it was achievable by aircraft as well. Each major country in the world had been fervently working toward developing aircraft of such level. This aircraft advancement was known as supersonic aircraft.

The current Bugatti Spaceship was capable of flight in space as well. With X-112 at its helm, it could also achieve such a flight method.

Chen Chen considered this for a moment. Since X-112 took the initiative to suggest incorporating this flight method, that meant projections had been run to determine that using such flight methods would not pose any risk. With this in mind, he nodded. “In that case, let’s go with ballistic missile guided flight. We need to find the technology for the Gantz sphere as soon as we can. I suspect that the mysterious phenomenon surrounding me grows stronger as time passes.”

“Understood.”

X-112 took note of that. After Chen Chen boarded the spaceship, they went to the military base to pick up the three Black Knights.

After everyone returned to the spaceship, the Bugatti Spaceship immediately took flight, aiming to exit the atmosphere. It continuously climbed upward at a slanted angle.

“The spaceship will be entering a state of supersonic flight.”

There was a fierce eruption of accumulated air as the spaceship began to speed up exponentially. A fearsome force forcefully pushed Chen Chen and Yoru Kamidera into their seats and held them in place.

“We’ve entered the stratosphere and are still accelerating, current speed Mach 3!”

X-112 continuously provided the latest status report for Chen Chen to allow Chen Chen to issue new orders at first notice whenever needed.

“Mach 5!

“Mach 7!

“The spaceship has exceeded Mach 10 and has broken into the middle section of the atmospheric layer. Current altitude 50,000 meters above sea level.”

As the Bugatti Spaceship ascended to greater heights, the surrounding air had become thinner as well, causing the spaceship to accelerate faster.

The body of the spaceship was coated with a high-temperature coating layer capable of withstanding temperatures up to 2,000 degrees Celsius. Even when flying into the atmosphere, it could maintain a high speed without fear of being burned.

After three minutes had passed, X-112 reported again, “The spaceship has reached Mach 20. We’re currently breaking into the ionosphere, the current altitude is 80,000 meters!”

Chen Chen looked out the window to inspect the view outside. He saw that the pale blue sky outside had turned into a condensed and dark tint, it was the color of space.

Shortly after that, the spaceship had surpassed an altitude of 110,000 meters, breaking the world record for aircraft altitude. However, this was not surprising. With the advanced structure of the Bugatti Spaceship, it could effortlessly take on the fifth-generation combat jets of North America while flying upside down. Ascending to an altitude exceeding a hundred thousand meters was child’s play for it.

“The spaceship has passed Mach 20!”

It was then when X-112 suddenly announced again, “Current altitude 150,000 meters!”

At this point, even Chen Chen could not resist holding his breath.

Although he was well aware of how capable the Bugatti Spaceship was, he had never imagined it reaching Mach 20. Such speed was equivalent to gliding at 7 kilometers per second, which was ten times the speed of regular bullets.

To the current day, only a handful of guided ballistic missiles could achieve such speed. With that said, it was clear that this was not the limit.

After another ten minutes had passed again, X-112 reported again, “The spaceship is currently flying at 230,000 meters above sea level, the speed has equalized at Mach 23. Pushing any higher than this may be risky. Should I proceed with further accelerating?”

“That won’t be necessary, safety first. Start altering the course now.”

Chen Chen made a waving gesture.

Following Chen Chen’s order, the spaceship with its glowing red hot body began altering its course in space. It lined itself up along the trajectory of the Earth, aiming at the North American continent located on the other side of the Earth.

What followed after was another round of acceleration.

In this round of acceleration, as the spaceship followed its charted course, Chen Chen saw dark orange-red flames glowing around the shell of the spaceship. Not only did the kindle of flames not disintegrate as the spaceship descended, but the scorching flames also grew brighter.

“What astounding technology.”

Inside the spaceship, Chen Chen could not help but marvel at the wondrous charm of technology. Technology was what brought humans to a never-before-explored territory. Technology was responsible for expanding human’s footmarks beyond the Earth and into foreign, unprecedented lands...

Furthermore, Chen Chen barely felt the slightest rise of temperature inside the spaceship. As it turned out, the spaceship’s thermal insulation coating perfectly isolated the heat produced by constant friction against the atmosphere. The insulation coating was very durable and was hardly affected even though the spaceship had been flying for nearly an hour.

Unlike Chen Chen, Yoru Kamidera kept a stoic, blank expression appearing as if he was uninterested in the voyage. It was his tightly clenched fist that gave away the nervous emotions swelling inside him.

Needless to be said, he was someone with very limited experience boarding airplanes even before the calamity.

The spaceship maintained its course this way at breakneck speed for twenty minutes. By this point, it had re-entered the atmospheric layer and was starting to slow down due to friction against the air.

“We’ve arrived at the airspace above North America, less than a thousand kilometers away from our point of destination. Beginning deceleration...”

After remaining silent for twenty minutes, X-112 announced again, “Current flying speed Mach 15, a hundred kilometers above sea level.

“Continue decelerating...”

Chen Chen turned to look outside the spaceship upon hearing this. He watched as the radiant yellow glow slowly transitioned into a bright red, the contrast of the red spark slowly deepened as they descended.

“Current speed Mach 12, 80 kilometers above sea level...

“Mach 10!

“Mach 5!”

By this point, the sparks outside the spaceship were extinguished. A rush of pale blue tint started to paint themselves over the previous indigo sky as bright white clouds began to appear outside the window again.

They were less than 10 kilometers above sea level at this point. When Chen Chen looked out the window, he saw the landscape covered in lush greenery. Unlike the world Chen Chen came from, this dimension was overtaken by bright green fauna.

Perhaps instead of needing several centuries or even millennia, all of the prints left behind by humans on this Earth would be cleansed by nature in just a matter of decades, with not so much as a trace left behind.

Perhaps after several millennia, a new intelligent civilization would re-emerge and reach the summit of the current human civilization. When they began uncovering the underground archaeological remains, they may very well find memoirs of the previous civilization.

Chen Chen took the time to reflect on the novel idea before he started to see gigantic structures coming into view on the plains up ahead.

This was yet another military base. It was also the motivation for Chen Chen embarking on his current trip – the famous air force base located in Lincolnshire, Vada, of the North American region.

North America, Area 51.

chapters.title: Chapter 313: Under Attack
chapters.body:
Chapter 313: Under Attack

Chapter 313: Under Attack

When they were only approximately ten kilometers away from Area 51, the urgent sound of the alarm system blared across the spaceship. X-112 announced simultaneously, “Sir Commander, detected radar scanning at 12 o’clock!”

“There’s someone at Area 51?”

Chen Chen was immediately taken aback by the completely unexpected news. Who would have thought that the North American Continent still had an assembly of troops even when human civilization was facing extinction?

“We detected a strong continuous radar frequency. Sir Commander, our position has been compromised by the Area 51 military operation.”

X-112 further cautioned. “The opposition has no interest in communicating with us. That’s not all, our omnidirectional infrared detector has detected an object approaching at high speed. It seems to be a surface-to-air missile!”

“Can we dodge it?”

“There are three ways we can dodge it. For the current situation, we’ll use electromagnetic cloaking.”

X-112 answered.

After several seconds, X-112 informed again, “The missile had lost sight of its target. Sir Commander, should we continue infiltrating the military base.”

“Forget it, the military base is manned.”

Chen Chen gathered his thoughts for a moment before he dismissed the idea. “First, let’s figure out the situation and see if there’s a way we can communicate with them. If it’s only a small doomsday squad consisting of several hundreds of members of scattered troops, then we can launch a direct attack. If it’s a properly established army, we can sit down and discuss terms with them.”

“Understood.”

X-112 responded before turning the spaceship around to retreat from the airspace of the military place. Then, it landed on top of a hill on the edge of the area.

When the spaceship had landed, Chen Chen dispatched three Black Knights to survey the surrounding area. After ensuring the hill was a safe area, he asked again, “X-112, can we contact Area 51?”

“I’ve relayed a radio message but they’re not responding to us yet.”

X-112 answered, “I’m not sure why, but they appear to be extremely hostile.”

“There are only two possibilities.”

Chen Chen said after brief consideration, “The first one is that they’ve identified us as some kind of enemy. Or better yet, as an eternal foe that they’ll do whatever it takes to destroy us upon finding us.

“Second, this is essentially an act stemming from their shame and guilt.”

“Shame and guilt?”

“Because they aren’t the original owner of the military base. Out of fear of being kicked out of the place by its rightful owner, they resort to killing.”

Chen Chen deducted. “Which means that there’s a healthy chance that the army camping inside Area 51 isn’t any regular army, but an independent rebel force formed after the collapse of the North American government. For the sake of holding onto their current position, they refuse any means of communication with the officials. There’s a strong chance that they’ve mistaken us for those officials.”

“Which possibility do you think is more likely?” X-112 asked.

“Probably the latter.”

Chen Chen thought for a moment. “Or maybe it’s a combination of both possibilities. Perhaps the rebel army inside this military base had already battled with the government, this might explain why they attacked us on sight.

“Of course, it doesn’t matter which one it is. What interests me more is how they were able to hide from the mysterious phenomenon occurring all over the world.”

“That’s true, it’s no small feat upkeeping an established army in the end times, it’s almost impossible.”

X-112 agreed. “Based on the ruling principle of the spread of the mysterious phenomena, it can be observed that the rate of infection increases in densely populated areas. It’s basically like a contagious disease in this sense. It doesn’t make sense that the military camp would become a safe harbor from the ongoing mysterious phenomena.

“This is why we have to investigate the place. If for anything else, just to figure out the logic behind this.”

Chen Chen gave an affirmative nod. “Dispatch the drones to investigate first, we have the cloaking devices after all. Besides, the radar cannot detect us if we’re flying at low altitudes. We don’t have to perform a thorough investigation, just get an approximate idea of how many people are in the military base and what weapons they have.”

“We can have the Black Knights bring wireless receivers with them. If I can infiltrate into the military base web server using an extension server, I can immediately figure out the situation in Area 51.”

X-112 suggested as well.

“Excellent, then we shall execute both plans simultaneously.

Chen Chen gave his approval with a nod. Four unmanned drones and a Black Knight were then dispatched together to the military base in the distance.

Meanwhile, X-112 continued trying to communicate with them via radio but to no avail.

While all this was happening, there were a dozen or so middle-aged men and women dressed in military uniforms gathered in a conference room in a special section deep under the Area 51 Military Base.

There was a tinge of sadness in all of their eyes. Some of them constantly sighed while others seemed to be thinking about something. Now and then, a flash of anger would appear in their eyes, disrupting their stream of thoughts.

Seated on the head of the conference table was a white-bearded elderly man. A badge on his shoulder indicated that he was a major general. He wore sunglasses although the room was dimly lit. There was also a cigar he hung by the corner of his lips. The major general spoke suddenly.

“I believe every one of you has received the news as well. Just ten minutes ago, an unknown flying object swept down from the skies above and appeared to be aiming straight for our military base...”

“Word is you issued an order to intercept them with the Sidewinder surface-to-air missile but they managed to slip away.”

The first man on the major general’s left said with an unchanging expression. There was also a badge on his shoulder reflecting his rank of colonel in the North American military structure.

“Perhaps the missile has degraded over the years.”

The white-bearded elderly man made a waving gesture with his hands. “As you all are well aware of, the count of survivors in the world continuously trends downward. Some time ago, the North American government had tried to reclaim their territory but before it even got started, they were crushed by an instance of level-A mysterious phenomena. When I received the news, I nearly choked on laughter.”

The general suddenly switched his tone when he mentioned this, “In other words, there aren’t many chances of us finding a qualified engineer to maintain our facilities these days.”

“But listen to me, aren’t they trying to communicate with us?”

The first member on the right side of the table was a blonde-haired lady in her forties. Despite being a woman, her posture was the most authoritative of all the people present here. There was always a fierce spark visible in her eyes and a militaristic quality about her movements.

“Yes, that’s the reason I called for this meeting.”

The white-bearded elderly looked over the people present, both rows of his teeth still firmly gripping the cigar. “The origin of that aircraft is still unknown. Based on its flight pattern it should be one of those supersonic aircraft secretly developed by the government. If it weren’t for it decelerating upon approaching us, it’d be nearly identical to a guided missile.”

“But by the looks of it, the aircraft seems to be carrying passengers...” The white-bearded elderly added.

“Impossible!”

The middle-aged colonel frowned and raised his objection. “It’s impossible for an aircraft of such speed to carry passengers. When flying, it generates more than 10 G’s of net gravitational acceleration. Such velocity would turn the brain of its passengers into a pulp!”

“Perhaps the aircraft has a built-in specialized buffer system?”

The blonde-haired lady refuted before turning to the elderly man. “But this isn’t the time for us to discuss this. What I’m more curious about is what did they send to us?”

“Joan, you’re impatient as ever.”

The white-bearded elderly man suddenly laughed merrily. When he gave a signal by clapping his hands together, several subordinates entered the conference room and handed a sheet of a report to everyone seated at the conference table.

“This is the complete log of the previous encounter.”

After everyone had received a copy of the report, he continued. “As you all can see from the radio logs, the other party is asking us why we opened fire at them without issuing a warning. They’re also continuously asking to speak to the person in charge of the military base.”

“Are you not planning to respond?”

The colonel asked, “We still haven’t figured out what faction they belong to. There’s a chance they aren’t part of the government, but this aircraft they used...”

The colonel further inspected the black and white image in the report and asked, “General Cary, are you sure the picture of the aircraft hasn’t been mixed up with some picture of a car?”

Everyone inspected the subject referred to. Attached in the report was a not very high-quality picture. On top of that, it was in black and white so all that could be seen was a blurry image. Inside the picture was what appeared to be a vehicle hovering above the air. The only feature that set it apart from regular vehicles were what appeared to be two thrusters on its sides.

“If even a common car can glide across the sky at speed surpassing Mach 10, then surely I may have been mistaken.”

The elderly man they called General Cary chuckled. “At first, I thought it was a remnant of the government army, but I realized that wasn’t the case after they avoided the ballistic missile. Currently, their identities remain a mystery. After all, I haven’t heard of any remaining states or countries since the calamity that has invented such advanced aircraft. Also, consider the following: what can we possibly gain out of negotiating with them?”

The elderly man chuckled again as he spoke, “I’m sure many of us here share the same suspicion, that they are coming for our specially designed underground shelter here. Are we going to share equal ownership of this underground shelter to these people? This is the shelter that keeps us away from the mysterious phenomena...”

There was a distinct change of expression among the members when they heard this. Those who originally had conflicting opinions began to settle down as well.

It was true, the underground shelter only had enough to accommodate a certain amount of people. Upon exceeding that number, the supply and demand of food and water would become greatly imbalanced. Once the delicate recycling process of resources was disrupted, they would have no choice but to raise the frequency of opening the underground shelter to resupply resources in the outside world.

God knew that the world was now infested with those d*mnable things. There were no guarantees that one of these times of them opening the shelter, those things would not sneak inside.

Once the mysterious phenomena found their way inside, the now safest shelter in the entire world would instantly turn into a horrific embodiment of hell on Earth...

Upon considering this, the members who were longing for some sort of contact with the outside world decided they were better off silencing these ideas.

“It seems to me that we all have a different opinion on this matter, so I say we do this the democratic way.”

After the white-bearded general looked over the members, he quickly got an idea of the general ongoing sentiment. Again, he announced, “Here’s what I think, nothing good can come out of taking unnecessary risk. To prevent having our shelter taken away from us, we shouldn’t establish any form of contact with the outside world. If they still dare to storm us by force, we shall shoot them down with our missiles! Who agrees and which ones of you disagree?”

“... Agreed!”

The middle-aged man’s expression quickly turned dark. After producing a long sigh, he was the first one to respond.

“Seconded.”

The blonde-haired lady seated opposite nodded her approval as well.

“Seconded.”

“Seconded.”

...

Unbeknownst to them, while they were gathering their thoughts on the matter, four completely transparent drones had snuck into the military. They came unnoticed, gliding at low altitudes and approaching from a distance, going 80 kilometers per second.

Once the drones successfully infiltrated the base, they immediately dispersed their formation and scattered across the base to conduct a brief scan of the entire base.

“A-1 did not discover any signs of life...”

“A-2 did not discover any signs of life...”

“A-3 and A-4 did not discover any signs of life either...”

Half an hour later, Chen Chen received all the messages from the unmanned drones, “Sir Commander, we’ve swept through all of the warehouses and facilities of the military base and couldn’t discover any signs of life. By the looks of it, the military base had been deserted for a very long time.”

“Then surely there’s no way for an abandoned military base to attack us?”

Chen Chen turned it around. “It’s clear that the structures are just a facade. The real military base must be deep underground.”

“I think so too.”

X-112 answered.

“Has the Black Knight arrived?” Chen Chen enquired.

“Just arrived. But if even the unmanned drones cannot find any clues, I doubt the Black Knight would be able to find the underground entrance.”

“It doesn’t matter even if we can’t find the entrance. We can locate them using the electric cables and the local network lines, there’s no way they would go as far as to hide the circuitry as well.”

Chen Chen pondered for a moment. “Besides, you can directly infiltrate them if we can find a cable, isn’t it?”

“That’s true.”

Yoru Kamidera started to get a grasp on the current situation as he listened to the exchange between Chen Chen and the artificial intelligence. He became delightfully impressed by the level of artificial intelligence displayed by X-112 which surpassed the average person. He expressed his thoughts suddenly, “Your Excellency, I’ve also thought of another idea following the trajectory of your original idea. I wonder if you’d be interested in hearing me out.”

Chen Chen flicked his eyebrow curiously and turned to him. “Let me hear it.”

“From the information you collected, there’s a Basilisk image called ‘Filth’. We can take advantage of your artificial intelligence to inject the image into their main control systems. This way, we can eliminate them without sparing any effort, then the entire military base will be ours to claim!”

Chen Chen cracked a quick smile upon hearing this. “It’s a good idea, but not so much if the underground base has a built-in self-destruct system.”

Yoru Kamidera was slightly caught off guard by this. It was true that if the underground base was fitted with self-destruction capabilities, the people residing inside may choose to activate it as a final act of resistance once they had lost all hope. Should that happen, they would have succeeded in defeating their opponents but failed at getting what they were after.

“Sorry, that slipped through my mind.”

Your Kamidera answered dejectedly.

“It’s alright, it’s not an entirely bad idea.”

Chen Chen waved his hands. “Except I’m not only gunning for the underground base, but something else as well...”

chapters.title: Chapter 314: Secret Of Gantz
chapters.body:
Chapter 314: Secret Of Gantz

Chapter 314: Secret of Gantz

Within three hours, the Black Knight had successfully dug up a track of wire deep underground by tracing along the layout of the base’s wiring. It was an optical cable with a diameter of a regular fist.

The Black Knight swiftly cut open the outer layer of the optical cable, giving way to a gap wide enough to fit three fingers inside. There were bundles of thin optical cables layered within the larger cable.

After that, the Black Knight produced a device resembling a black mechanical beetle. Upon being installed on the exposed section, hundreds of probes came out of the beetle and automatically plugged themselves into the optical fibers.

“I received the signal.”

At the same time, X-112 that was positioned some twenty to thirty kilometers away suddenly informed, “The Black Knight successfully found the optical cable and installed the interceptor. I can begin infiltrating the internal server of the base using the interceptor signal.”

“Try to crack their network barrier without raising an alarm.”

Chen Chen reminded.

“Understood.”

Following X-112’s words, rows of massive data streams flashed automatically on the spaceship’s holographic projection. At the same time, X-112 began providing the latest updates. “Currently intercepting all data, preparing to decode and analyze...

“Extracted the base logs, currently infiltrating the base’s defense systems...

“The firewall has been breached...”

X-112’s infiltration was extremely efficient, there was an endless stream of updates. After approximately five minutes, X-112 spoke again, “Sir Commander, the defense systems of the base have been compromised. We can now barge right in.”

“Excellent work.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Tell me about the base.”

“This base is the North American Area 51 air force base. Somewhere around 1990, as the Gantz sphere technology began emerging, the North American, European, and Central Continents all obtained production rights of the Gantz sphere. This place is where the production of the Gantz sphere in North America was based.”

X-112 began to provide a detailed background. “However, Area 51 was already an air force military operation base in the very first place. To deploy production lines while also concealing them from the public, the underground section of Area 51 military base was excavated. This was where the production line of the Gantz sphere was set up.

“But these aren’t the primary focus. The true highlight is that to produce the Gantz sphere, an experimental environment known as ‘Vantablack’ was required.”

“I’ve heard about it.”

Chen Chen was intrigued. “‘Vantablack’ is a specially simulated environment wherein subsequent means of radiation reduction is applied to various sources of variation. This is done to negate as many sources of cosmic ray interference as possible.”

“Yes.”

X-112 replied affirmatively, “A ‘Vantablack’s environment is essential for conducting cutting-edge experiments such as dark matter detection, neutrinoless double beta decay experiments, and nuclear astrophysics. The current best method for constructing such an environment is by utilizing deep underground rock layers. Ideally, the laboratory should be built as deep underground as possible while also incorporating multiple layers of shielding to minimize the surrounding environmental radiation. The result is an almost completely pure dimension devoid of electromagnetic radiation...”

“Which is to say that the Gantz sphere can only be created under such specific conditions?”

Chen Chen nodded thoughtfully. “Excellent, I wonder if our Spire Experimental Base checks these boxes.”

“That would be entirely impossible.”

X-112 answered, “According to the information extracted, the Gantz sphere production line in the Mainland is set up in a laboratory in Sichuan Jinping. The laboratory is built 2,400 meters under a mountain. It’s the world’s deepest laboratory and they claimed it was for studying dark matter. Meanwhile, our Spire Laboratory is only 700 meters deep.”

Chen Chen let out a sigh. “Then have you found the manufacturing technology of the Gantz sphere?”

“I’ve found it, it’s being transmitted.”

X-112 announced again, “Based on the looks of it now, your speculation is correct. The Gantz sphere is no more than a terminal, while the relay station is a Gantz satellite with extended wavelengths. In other words, the Gantz sphere being used in conjunction with the Gantz satellite allows it to transport people around the world.

“Additionally, there’s something else called Gantz server inside the Gantz sphere system. The North American server is located right here. It’s used to log the data of all the people transported with Gantz. This includes information on the transported person’s cell layout, DNA structure, even the string perturbation frequency of the particles that make up DNA, and such. Without the server, the Gantz sphere is little more than scrap metal.”

“Is it that complicated?”

“That’s not even the end of it. What truly challenges the boundary of what we know as science is that each one of the Gantz Sphere, Gantz satellite, and the Gantz server are more than mechanical products.”

X-112 explained, “For the Gantz sphere, mechanical parts only make up 50% of its structure while the Gantz satellite consists of 70% mechanical parts. Meanwhile, the Gantz server is purely crafted using high-purity germanium, it isn’t made up of any other substance or contains mechanical structures. You might as well call it a statue carved from high-purity germanium... Needless to be said, this is a new aspect of scientific advancement that modern humans have not been able to achieve yet.”

“It does seem that way.”

Chen Chen flashed a curious look. “Any other discoveries?”

“The next part should be something that you’re most interested in.”

X-112 continued. “Based on the collected data, each country had foreseen the appearance of the Basilisk images and related mysterious phenomena upon their obtaining of the technology for the Gantz sphere. This is because the earliest form of Basilisk pattern is merely part of the pattern carved on the Gantz server machine...”

“What!”

If the other news Chen Chen had received so far could be described as being moderately acceptable or even predictable to a certain degree. This piece of information was completely left field and stunned Chen Chen. Even Yoru Kamidera was surprised by this revelation.

“Are you sure there hasn’t been some kind of mistake?”

“There’s no mistaking it.”

X-112 doubled down. “The complete report is logged inside the base. This report is further encrypted and shelved away in the deepest corner. At one point, it was also reported to the North American president. However, before they could conduct further research on the subject, human civilization was crippled by the mysterious phenomena. Naturally, the research was halted as well.”

As X-112 said this, a dense sheet of report written in English appeared before Chen Chen in the form of a holographic projection. X-112 went on. “What’s even more surprising is that inside this report, a research staff called Professor Stanford states that the Gantz server exists in a vulnerable but extremely unique dimension.

“There’s no way to analyze or detect this universe. Even the slightest trace of cosmic radiation would cause its complete destruction, but one certain thing is that it exists...

“One way to tell is that within the radius of the Gantz server, all mysterious phenomena are completely blocked and rendered useless.”

chapters.title: Chapter 315: Harvest
chapters.body:
Chapter 315: Harvest

Chapter 315: Harvest

Yoru Kamidera was completely taken by shock upon hearing the words of X-112.

There was a good reason for his exaggerated reaction for he saw with his very own eyes how the world was ruined because of a picture. To a certain extent, the picture had even destroyed human civilization along with the hopes and dreams of countless people.

Now he was told that the doom of mankind, the Basilisk images along with the far more terrifying mysterious phenomena had all come from the previous savior of mankind, Gantz?

This revelation was a heavy punch to the gut for Yoru Kamidera. He was immediately consumed with a sense of absurdity and irony.

“Gantz server machine?”

This seemed to have drawn Chen Chen’s interest. “Exactly, what’s the Gantz server machine? Will it be dangerous to approach it directly?”

“This is the strange part.”

X-112 quickly responded, “There are all sorts of patterns on the Gantz server machine but at the end of the day, it was an artificial product. However, the people building it didn’t seem to have experienced any strange events. On top of that, after the Gantz server machine was put together, many leaders of many countries came to inspect it and nothing came out of it either. From this, we can conclude that the Gantz server machine itself is harmless.”

“It only makes me more curious when you put it this way.”

Chen Chen said, “Show me. I, for one, would like to see how this Gantz server machine looks like.”

“If you insist.”

Following Chen Chen’s orders, the hologram projected by X-112 suddenly shifted. The streams of data and text instantly vanished and were substituted by a metallic gray structure. The structure was very peculiar. It was formed by two spiraling objects intertwining with each other as they climbed to the top to form a spiral-shaped tower with two points of the spiraling objects on its tip!

The sight of the spire was extremely peculiar to behold. It seemed to contain layers on top of layers, all of them with strange patterns that Chen Chen had never seen before, all densely packed across the surface of the spire. Upon closer inspection, they were completely identical to the Basilisk patterns!

“This is the Gantz server machine.”

X-112 informed, “This Gantz server machine is made entirely of solid high-purity germanium. With my current database, I haven’t been able to deduct why it is the core structure of Gantz.”

“Me too.”

Chen Chen nodded. “The workings of these things have exceeded the boundaries of human knowledge. What’s even more absurd is that this is the work of humans as well. It doesn’t look particularly difficult to construct either. Was high-purity germanium used because it’s a metalloid semiconductor that is somewhere between being a metal and non-metal?”

Neither X-112 nor Yoru Kamidera had an answer for Chen Chen’s question, for there was no way to find the answer.

Chen Chen was not fixated on obtaining a proper answer either, he only murmured, “Despite the evident risk, we have to get our hands on these scientific subjects never before seen by mankind. I also suspect that the three statues from Elysium share a similar principle with this Gantz server machine. Perhaps they may have identical attributes...”

“Didn’t you decide to steer clear away from the three statues from Elysium back then?”

X-112 found it perplexing. “Why did you try to avoid it back then, but now suddenly decide to research these subjects?”

“It’s simple.”

Chen Chen smiled. “Despite both of them sharing similar characteristics of being world-ending devices, Gantz isn’t like the three statues from Elysium. From our previous investigation of the three statues, we saw that there was simply no way of dissecting them. In the end, I concluded that the potential insight of that research wasn’t worth its potential risk and ultimately decided to abandon the project.

“But now, what’s presented in front of me is something that we can properly utilize. The technology of Gantz is more accessible than the three statues. Put it this way, imagine if you present two lighters to cavemen, one of them is an ordinary ‘flint and steel wheel lighter’ and the other is a ‘solar energy lighter’. Which one of these do you think is more suitable for ancient craftsmen to study?”

“The flint and steel wheel lighter, of course.”

X-112 answered, “Based on the scientific progress of ancient times, the latter lighter is more intuitive and simpler for ancient craftsmen to understand.”

“That’s right.”

Chen Chen nodded contentedly. “And now, the Gantz technology is our version of the flint and steel wheel lighter. Although we still have a very limited understanding of it, it’s at the very least more accessible than the solar energy lighter.”

With this in mind, Chen Chen got up decisively. “Which is why we have to take control of this underground military base. I also need to ensure that the Gantz server machine underground remains unharmed.”

“Don’t worry, there are less than a hundred people inside the underground base and they are only using conventional kinetic weapons. These are the people who have retreated from the surface upon realizing that the Gantz server machine has the mystical ability to keep the mysterious phenomena at bay. This is how they were able to survive in the end times.”

“I see, then it’s probably the government trying to reclaim the machine once the mysterious phenomena began to spread.”

Chen Chen nodded. “But to their surprise, they arrived here only to be greeted by an independent rebellion faction. That explains why they displayed such hostility upon our arrival.”

As they were discussing, the other two Black Knights had left the Bugatti Spaceship as well. Both of them headed for the military base at full speed.

“With your coordination, three Black Knights should be enough to take over the underground base.”

Chen Chen silently weighed the risk in his mind when he saw the Black Knights departing. As long as their opponent used only conventional weapons instead of weapons like the gravity gun or X-gun like Gantz, there should be nothing for Chen Chen to worry about.

As expected, with X-112 controlling them, the three Black Knights had silently snuck through the entrance and successfully infiltrated the sealed underground base.

With that, the three of them had entered no man’s land. X-112 carefully coordinated them and relayed the position of every person inside the base. By utilizing the invisibility technology in their kit, they had cut the population in the base by more than half within half an hour without raising any alarm.

It was around that point when someone finally found the body of a murdered soldier and sounded the alarm.

However, before the others could be alerted, X-112 cut off the power of the entire base, effectively closing the curtain of hope for them.

Like playing cat and mouse, the three Black Knights harvested one life after another in complete darkness. These people who should have died in the calamity had their five years of tranquility abruptly cut short.

Five years of complacency had dulled their senses and alertness. They lost their previous instincts and failed to condition their body accordingly, which caused them to degrade back to ordinary people.

In the end, as a safety measure in case he missed something, Chen Chen had the Black Knights keep three people alive for interrogation. The three lucky subjects were the three highest position officials in the underground base.

There was the elderly major general, the middle-aged colonel, and the woman named Joan.

chapters.title: Chapter 316: Mutation
chapters.body:
Chapter 316: Mutation

Chapter 316: Mutation

Several hours later, Chen Chen was already strolling in the central section of the underground base.

There were two rows of “Vantablack” laboratory lined up along both sides of the walkway. It was near impossible to detect any trace of cosmic ray or electromagnetic disturbance within these laboratories as they functioned as a one-of-a-kind pure space.

Strictly speaking, the purpose of this underground base was the same as Chen Chen’s “Spire Experimental Base”. They were both experimental bases built with a focus on conducting scientific experiments.

However, over the last few years, these laboratories were forcefully opened by the soldiers occupying this base. A layer of dust and rust was seen resting on top of all the priceless equipment, rendering them completely useless, as good as scrap metal.

“Shame.”

Chen Chen shook his head slightly upon seeing what had happened. It was such a cruel joke, letting a bunch of vulgar, simple-minded soldiers claiming control of a world-leading physics experiment base.

One could imagine that the world-class scientists working in the laboratory that was kicked out by these people as the calamity was approaching were long dead by now. Just the idea of it was a sour thought.

As Chen Chen walked along the corridor of the base by himself, the sealed doors opened one after another when he approached as if they were welcoming their rightful master.

It did not take long before Chen Chen had arrived at the end of the underground experimental base.

It was a sinkhole sinking deep underground.

The sinkhole crept several hundred meters underground, which was even deeper than the base itself. Strictly speaking, the base assumes the layout of a circuit of spherical structure, each one of these round buildings forming a ring around the giant sinkhole in the middle.

At the present, Chen Chen had come to the innermost section of the base. He was greeted by an arc-shaped observation window that stood more than 3 meters tall. There were many such windows on both sides of the pit and they formed a straight line all the way down the sinkhole.

On the other side of the observation window, there was nothing but darkness as well. The silhouette of the bottomless sinkhole can barely be made out.

“Can the power system be reactivated?”

Chen Chen raised his head and asked.

“Yes, although its implementation doesn’t reach the level of nuclear fusion technology, they do have a local nuclear fission reactor. It can keep the underground base operational for another fifty to a hundred years.”

X-112’s voice came from the speakers on the wall closest to Chen Chen. At the same time, the gigantic observation in front of Chen Chen suddenly lit up.

To be clear, it was the sinkhole before Chen Chen that was illuminated by strong bursts of light. The blinding source of light instantly cast its shine on every crevice of the walls in the sinkhole!

As Chen Chen took in the sight of the sinkhole from the observation windows, his pupils immediately shrunk. He was seized by an indescribable shock...

From the observation windows, Chen Chen could see that the sinkhole was up to 230 meters deep. It resembled a pillar running through the center of the underground base. On the bottom of the sinkhole was a small square measuring more than a thousand square meters.

However, this was not what bemused Chen Chen. The true culprit was the colossal object placed at the center of the square, with a diameter of up to 10 meters, and standing more than 50 meters tall!

Like an enormous statue.

The statue was coated in black, with dizzy, spiraling patterns all over it. Its full figure could not be seen from the observation window. Only the upper portion of its body was visible from there.

Chen Chen did not need it spelled out for him to know that this was the Gantz server machine built from high-purity germanium.

As it was constructed using germanium metal, a layer of germanium monoxide had formed on the surface as an effect of long-term oxidation. Instead of the original silvery luster, the surface of the statue was deep black with a metallic quality.

“This must be the Gantz server machine.”

Chen Chen squinted when he saw the enormous metallic statue and noted. “Although I’ve seen its design, you never told me that the Gantz server machine was of this size.”

“The dimension of the Gantz server machine isn’t fixed. It’s instead adjusted according to the desired efficiency.”

X-112 explained, “The North American Continent is extremely ambitious and chose to erect a Gantz server machine of this size. A server machine of this size can extend its signal range across half the globe, allowing it to gain control over plenty of other Gantz spheres on other continents.”

“I see.”

Chen Chen nodded and carefully studied the densely packed spiraling patterns on the Gantz server machine. “Where did these patterns come from? Inside Gantz, the aliens use their technology to transmit signals to the brain of a person in a vegetative state, causing the person to speak this way. I doubt these patterns can be simply described with the vocabulary of man, no?”

“As you might expect, it’s done using a newly established language system.”

X-112 replied, “This is a new language known as E language which is an abbreviation for Everlasting, which is a symbolization for eternity and Godhood. The first thing the chosen woman did was pass on this new language system to mankind before passing on the message of the Gantz sphere.

“In summary, this is a form of language that can convey far more information than any existing human languages. The message that can be conveyed using this language is more than ten thousand times above that of all 5,651 human languages combined. Not only could it be used to describe all of the known colors in the world, shapes, and other various messages, it can even be used to describe patterns down to the most minute detail.”

A spark appeared in Chen Chen’s eyes. “Is there any specific information on this language? If it’s possible, I’d like to learn this language.”

“Unfortunately, your brain must equip computational efficiency at the level of a quantum computer to learn such a language. It’s simply impossible for the human brain to achieve such a feat, not even if you take NZT-48.”

X-112 struck down Chen Chen’s idea. “Humans also had to compose this language into a set of algorithms to help them create the Gantz sphere and all that comes with it.”

“Alright then.”

Chen Chen remarked with a touch of frustration, “I get it now, that explains why the Gantz server machine looks like this. The patterns covering the statue are essentially the equivalent of programs written by us humans. These programs are what allows the server machine to operate. In other words, this statue is essentially a computer of another civilization.”

“That is an apt metaphor.”

X-112 agreed. “You’re a genius, it does seem that way so far.”

“In that case, is it possible that...”

Chen Chen’s expression turned grave as he said this, the tone of his voice followed suit and grew darker, “The Basilisk patterns appearing on the Internet in this dimension is spread by the Gantz server machine?”

X-112 was at a loss for words.

A shadow of a smirk appeared on Chen Chen’s lips as he raised his head to look at the gigantic statue through the observation window. “What are the chances, Gantz server machine?”

After Chen Chen uttered these words, the reply of X-112 never came. At the same time, a loud click could be heard echoing across the entire underground base. A curtain of darkness suddenly closed in on Chen Chen’s vision as the entire underground base descended into complete blackness.

In the darkness, Chen Chen heard a rush of chaotic clicking sounds rapidly approaching from afar. It was the sound of the safety valves shutting off, seemingly trying to completely trap Chen Chen in the underground base!

chapters.title: Chapter 317: Backlash
chapters.body:
Chapter 317: Backlash

Chapter 317: Backlash

“As I thought, you’re not X-112.”

Plunged into desolate darkness, Chen Chen was completely unfazed. Not only that, he appeared more like he was deeply intrigued. “How should I address you? Alien? Or just Gantz?”

“You catch on quickly, human. You’re smarter than a significant portion of humans but that doesn’t mean anything.”

At the present, X-112’s voice had shifted into a more robotic rendition, reminiscent of a synthesizer. Its voice was emitted from the speakers, both hard and cold like a robot, completely devoid of expression. “I can make a deal with you and purge the mysterious phenomenon you’ve been afflicted with. My sole condition is that you’ll leave this base and never set foot here again!”

“Alright.”

Chen Chen immediately answered without a second thought, “Deal!”

“...”

The voice from the speaker became silent for a moment before saying, “Human, I don’t hear the sincerity in your words. You’re lying to me.”

“I never lie.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “Go ahead and try if you don’t believe me. If you can purge the mysterious phenomenon from me, you have my word that I’ll leave this place.”

“Alright, human. I’ve purged it for you, you can leave now.”

The voice suddenly informed again, having quickly shifted from its previous tone.

“...”

This time, it was Chen Chen who paused. He made a small gesture with his hands like a shrug. “So, we’re done talking?”

“Human, show some sincerity, then I’ll talk.”

The voice threatened, “You might not be aware of this, but the mysterious phenomenon you’re afflicted with is going to reach its peak soon. When that happens, the chances of your survival is nil.”

“Here’s the problem, how do I know if you’ve actually purged it off of me?”

Chen Chen asked with a frown greeting his expression, “What if you were lying to me about having purged it and I die from it right after leaving? Doesn’t that mean that no one will be left alive to spill your secret? That fits your motive, doesn’t it?”

“Human, you have no choice but to believe me.”

The voice continued. “This is your only chance at survival!”

“It appears to me that you have not consumed X-112.”

Upon hearing the other party’s remark, Chen Chen became visibly calmer.

“Human, how did you figure that out?”

“It’s simple, because he knows that I have other ways to erase the effects of the mysterious phenomenon.”

Chen Chen smirked. “The whole thing adds up when put this way. If you can infiltrate the Internet at will, you’d have incited a nuclear war a long time ago, just like the Skynet. Why would you run with an alternative of spreading Basilisk images online to destroy mankind? If I had to guess, the reason you were able to upload the Basilisk pattern was that you were connected via a network cable, am I wrong?

“Although you’re an alien computer, you don’t have the power to manipulate electromagnetic signals. This means that you don’t have access to the wireless network required to attack X-112 located outside the base. All you can do is chase X-112 out.

“Previously, when X-112 invaded the underground base network, you didn’t reveal yourself and waited until I entered the base before expelling X-112 from the internal network. I couldn’t notice that X-112 was replaced since there was no signal underground, hindering X-112 from alerting me of the fact.”

Chen Chen spoke softly, dissecting the situation, “There’s only one explanation for your actions and that is to keep me chained up in here because I was coming right at you!”

“That’s right.”

The voice answered, “I never made a move despite having all these humans huddled around me because they never saw me as a target. As long as you don’t have any ill intentions against me, I won’t hurt you.”

“You think you can fool me with your tales?”

Chen Chen frowned. “These people are a bunch of useless nobodies, but I’m not like them. You only kept them around because they helped you keep the forces of the outside world at bay. On your own, you don’t have any offensive capabilities. You can’t even utilize the powers of the Basilisk images and the mysterious phenomenon, am I wrong?”

“...”

It had become quiet.

Chen Chen further pressed the issue. “You didn’t negotiate with me because you don’t want to kill me. It’s because you don’t have any way to kill me. That’s why you tried to seal up the underground base to lock me up in here, but when you see the armor I have on, you begin having second thoughts.”

When Chen Chen said this, the two shoulder cannons propped up from the back of the PK Armor, aiming at the observation window in front of him. The thermal imaging vision module was activated as well. The darkness was inconsequential to Chen Chen.

“You were worried that the underground base cannot properly entrap me, then you were concerned that my weapon can easily shatter this observation window, allowing me to easily destroy you...”

“Human, the workings of your mind is nothing short of perfection.”

The voice started, shifting its attitude again. “I only have the same advice for you, which is that the mysterious phenomenon occurring to you is inching closer toward its climax. Are you sure you want to die with me?”

“I never bet on the mercy of others.”

Chen Chen shook his head. Immediately after, the shoulder cannon on his left began producing a steady hum as an invisible but significant power slowly accumulated.

“High-power microwaves cannon. All it takes is one shot at maximum buildup. I doubt this observation window is going to stop me.”

Chen Chen said softly. Without waiting for the Gantz server machine to respond, the twenty centimeters thick glass of the observation window appeared to have been hit by an invisible wave of explosion. In an instant, sharp ripples deep enough to fit a finger into were punched into the structure of the glass!

This was the power of microwave weapons.

Microwave weapons were a sub-classification of electromagnetic waves. Unlike regular electromagnetic waves, it used high-energy electromagnetic waves to obliterate its targets. On top of destroying electronic devices, its thermal effects allowed it to bypass defenses and armor to directly kill the soldiers beneath them.

When the Gantz server machine saw cracks instantly forming on the glass that was otherwise immune to bullets, it finally panicked. “Human, don’t do this, I’m willing to help you purge off your afflicted mysterious phenomenon first!”

“Really?”

The cannon on Chen Chen’s shoulder halted briefly as he raised his head. “Purge it right now, you have three seconds!”

“As you wish!”

Following Chen Chen’s command, the statue on the opposite end of the observation window began moving!

No, the enormous germanium-made statue did not move. Instead, traces of dark-red rays began appearing on its surface. These traces of light insidiously tracked along the surface of the Gantz server machine, appearing extremely malicious in nature. At a glance, it gave off a strong, ominous aura.

The light appearing on the statue only lapped around it for a brief moment before dimming out again. At the same time, the voice spoke, “Alright, I’ve purged the mysterious phenomenon from you, you’re safe now...”

“I don’t believe you!”

However, Chen Chen immediately responded, “I don’t feel anything at all. I think you’re trying to make a fool out of me!”

Without any hesitation, the shoulder cannon strapped behind Chen Chen produced a relentless microwave. What followed after was the sound of a roaring explosion, which shattered the thick observation window in a powerful display like an avalanche!

The destructiveness of the microwave cannon continued to grow from this point on. Its gigantic force instantly bombarded the surface of the statue. In a split instant, thin cracks began forming on the surface of the statue which appeared like a work of art...

The Gantz server machine, obliterated.

chapters.title: Chapter 318: Crystalline Properties
chapters.body:
Chapter 318: Crystalline Properties

Chapter 318: Crystalline Properties

It was Chen Chen who was slightly surprised when he saw the twin-spire tower shattering at the touch.

Although he did expect a similar outcome, he never foresaw that it would only take a single hit from the microwave weapon geared with the PK Armor for the boss-tier monstrosity to crumble.

When the Gantz server machine was destroyed, light was brought back to the underground base and the door behind Chen Chen shuffled opened again. At the same time, the slightly urgent voice of X-112 could be heard through the speakers, “Sir Commander, I was attacked by an unknown AI just now, are you hurt?”

“I’m fine.”

Chen Chen answered expressionlessly, “How about you, did the opponent cause any damage?”

“The opponent is extremely powerful and severely trumps me in terms of computing speed. It was originally going after my source code but for some reason, it stopped pursuing me after I was chased out of the internal server of the underground base.”

X-112 answered, “The opponent didn’t trace along the wireless signal to continue invading my main program.”

“That’s because it can’t do that.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Let’s discuss this later. Have the Black Knights bring the captives over. I’ll question them myself.”

“Understood.”

After approximately ten minutes after Chen Chen gave the order, three Black Knights appeared on the far side of the corridor. Each of them carried a captive dressed in a military outfit on their shoulder.

When the Black Knights approached Chen Chen, they rudely threw the captives onto the ground. All three of the knocked out captives produced loud thuds against the floor as they fell. They slowly awakened from the sudden pain.

Chen Chen took a glance at them. The three of them were covered in blood all over. One of the blonde-haired ladies was even short of an arm, her wounds were left exposed like a dirty pile of mud.

The other members of the underground base were all massacred by the Black Knights.

If not for the ranks of these three officials and the secrets they carried with them, they may have ended up the same as the rest and turned into tomato paste by the microwave weapons.

When Chen Chen saw the three of them gradually recollecting themselves, he approached them and asked in a belittling temperament, “I only have three questions. Whoever can answer all of them gets to live.”

Chen Chen hardly gave them time to mentally prepare themselves and began without notice. “First question, do you guys know that the Gantz server machine was a machine with a conscience?”

Ignoring the blank expression on their faces, Chen Chen went on to the next question. “Who was it that arranged a deal with the Gantz server machine to allow it to connect to the underground base network?”

There was a slight twitch in the elderly man and the middle-aged man’s expressions.

Chen Chen studied the expression of the elderly man and asked again, “Who was responsible for arranging a deal with the Gantz server machine and contributing to the destruction of human civilization?”

This time, there was a not-so-subtle change of expression on the elderly man and the middle-aged man.

The three questions were delivered in quick succession. Chen Chen continued glaring at the three of them but none of them spoke.

Chen Chen abandoned whatever reservations he had and immediately wrapped his arms around the blonde-haired lady’s neck and lifted her with one arm.

The lady yelped miserably and struggled persistently in Chen Chen’s claw. She felt as if Chen Chen’s arm was made of bronze cast iron. No matter how much she resisted, her captor seemed to be completely unfazed.

“Let, let go...”

The lady barely managed to mouth the two words, before she could complete her sentence, Chen Chen suddenly squeezed tightly with all his strength. There was a quick snap, followed by the blonde-haired lady’s eyes shrinking into themselves before her head dropped unnaturally. The final breath left in her was gone.

The elderly man and the middle-aged man were horrified upon witnessing this scene.

“My patience is very limited.”

Chen Chen relaxed his grip and tossed the lifeless body of the blonde-haired lady like tossing trash. He turned to the remaining two. “Do you need me to repeat my question?”

“I’ll talk, I’ll talk!”

The middle-aged man seemed to have been traumatized by what he had witnessed as he pointed at the elderly man beside him and began his tale. “It’s this old guy, I saw him regularly coming in and out of the Gantz server machine section with my very own eyes. For quite some time now, I’ve suspected that the Basilisk patterns appearing in the world had something to do with this server machine. By the time I discovered everything, we had long lost contact with the government, I can only watch as he did everything...”

“No, it’s not me!”

The white-bearded elderly man glared at the middle-aged man with hate in his eyes. “I don’t know anything at all. This has to be the work of this vile dog, he wants to put the blame on...”

Before he could finish, Chen Chen abruptly held down the arteries on the neck of the two men, causing them to immediately pass out again. He turned to the Black Knight behind him. “Take these two away and force a confession out of them. There’s a chance we can find out the source of destruction of this dimension from them.”

“Understood.”

X-112 replied.

Chen Chen turned around and approached the observation window shattered by the microwave weapon. The statue looked like a body viciously decapitated from its waistline. The upper portion of its body shattered into dozens of pieces like smashed glass. Only the lower part of it was kept intact.

However, upon observing the tiny fragments, Chen Chen could not help but squint his eyes to get a closer look.

Looking from the observation window, the square at the bottom had pieces of the Gantz server machine scattered all over the ground. From a distance, the fragments seemed to assume irregular shapes but upon closer inspection, Chen Chen noticed that nearly all of the pieces took on the same twin-spire form.

With a gentle wave of his hand, one of the pieces the size of a regular fist leaped into the air and went straight toward Chen Chen’s palm.

The broken piece had a beautiful silver glow around it as it was not exposed to oxygen. It was the clean and luminous glow of metal. For some unknown reason, this fragment shared the same twin-spire tower structure of its original body.

It seemed to faintly resemble DNA structures.

This implied that no matter how the Gantz server machine was destroyed and how many pieces it broke into, they would all retain the same shape.

This remarkable attribute reminded Chen Chen of the crystalline.

Crystal materials usually took on the form of regular geometric patterns, giving an impression that they were artificially carved that way. This was because the internal atomic arrangement was extremely fixed. Their formations were better than troops of soldiers.

They were repeatedly arranged on top of each other in three-dimensional space to achieve the form of a crystal lattice. The result of this was that their appearance took on the form of a fixed geometric polyhedron.

For example, most rock sugars were identical in shape, all standardized blocks. Sometimes, some of them may stick out from the rest as they became fused by multiple blocks. By crushing these rock sugars into tiny particles and placing them under a microscope, it should be observed that the rock sugar would always have the same shape no matter how small they were pulverized into.

What he was seeing now was that this strange statue had displayed the same characteristics the original crystalline shown...

“Its form will never be altered no matter how many times they are pulverized?”

Chen Chen looked at the metallic statue which took on the form of a twin spire in his palm. This was of great interest to him.

chapters.title: Chapter 319: Doomsday Trap
chapters.body:
Chapter 319: Doomsday Trap

Chapter 319: Doomsday Trap

These characteristics of the Gantz server were, in Chen Chen’s view, extremely outrageous.

This was a crystal structure that would not change its change no matter how it was destroyed and it was easily replicable. Chen Chen would almost think that there was some conspiracy.

According to the original plot of the comic, all of Gantz’s technology was recounted by the German entrepreneur’s daughter. She had this ability because Alien Civilization A was about to be wiped out by Alien Civilization B, so they transmitted their technology to the people on Earth.

The reason for this was that Earth was Alien Civilization B’s next target.

This was what happened in this reality. In just a few years, Alien Civilization B came to Earth and waged a war, and the ending was that humans successfully defeated Alien Civilization B by using Gantz’s technology.

In the very end, the leader of Alien Civilization B was defeated by the protagonist Kei Kurono, which caused Alien Civilization B to blow up their own spacecraft, concluding the story of Gantz.

However, long before coming into Gantz, Chen Chen had harbored a doubt. In his opinion, the Gantz technology that Alien Civilization A gave to Earth seemed to far exceed the level of Alien Civilization B’s technology. Even human beings, with the Gantz technology, could defeat Alien Civilization B. Was it possible that Alien Civilization A, the original owner of these technologies, was vanquished?

In Chen Chen’s view, this would be illogical. It was ridiculous that Alien Civilization A itself had been destroyed while on the other hand, human beings could turn the tables, with just a group of stragglers who had not even undergone military training being able to defeat the menacing Alien Civilization B.

Therefore, there must be something else behind the demise of this world. These were secrets that were not even mentioned in the original work. They were buried under the iceberg of the plot but were nonetheless inextricably linked to the storyline.

As he thought of this, a dark look grew in Chen Chen’s eyes. He lightly gripped the statue fragment in his hand. At once, the metal germanium chunk in his hand was crushed into smaller fragments.

Even so, these fragments still maintained a complete double helix structure, totally unchanged...

“Let’s go.”

Chen Chen clapped his hands, turned away, and walked toward the outside world. After a long journey, he was finally leaving the underground base and returning to the ground.

At this time, it was five o’clock in the evening and dusk of this second day was approaching.

After waiting for an hour, Chen Chen got the answers he wanted. X-112 had already made the Black Knights interrogate those two.

It turned out that the old man with a white beard was originally named Cary. He was the thirteenth person in charge of Area 51 and had the rank of major general.

Around 1990, when the North American government acquired Gantz technology, they converted Area 51 into a Gantz research base. At the same time, they sent Cary to be the one in charge of all projects in Area 51 besides the actual research.
