This was how Major General Cary came into contact with the Gantz server for the first time, this grand statue of astonishing shape.

When Major General Cary saw the statue for the first time, he was transformed from a forceful, military man to someone who only felt unparalleled shock. It was as if he was looking at the ninth wonder of the world. Whether in terms of artistry or style, this statue appeared to be flawless, almost like it should not exist in the world...

As if possessed, Major General Cary repeatedly looked for excuses to enter the underground base and visited the Gantz server over the next few days.

Even stranger was, as Major General Cary’s visits increased in number, it was like this statue had gradually implanted its roots into Major General Cary’s heart, making him progressively obsessed and crazy.

It was like taking drugs.

Of course, this was a madness that could still be suppressed by reason, so Major General Cary would only constantly find reasons to come and observe the statue, but he would never neglect everything else just to do so.

Until one day, this statue bursting with artistic beauty suddenly spoke.

Of course, this was not a speech, but rather a sort of voice that resounded directly in Major General Cary’s mind. That voice kept bewitching him, causing his subconscious to feel like obeying the voice of the statue.

It seemed that there was a human personality hidden in the statue.

Moreover, initially, the statue only had heart-to-heart chats with Major General Cary, like a little girl next door. To Major General Cary, who had lost his daughter in his early years, it was as if something invisible had hit the softest part of his soul.

In his eyes, the statue full of artistic beauty before him was no longer a cold Gantz server, but an elf-like lively girl.

In this way, Major General Cary began to meet with the statue every day behind the backs of the group of researchers in the underground base, even to the point where one day apart felt like three years.

Until one day, this statue made a request for the first time, a request that did not seem excessive — namely, to find a network cable that could connect to the Internet.

The reason given by the personality in the statue was that it was so dark and cold and lonely there, so it wanted to observe the world through the Internet and not be lonely anymore.

At this time, Major General Cary, who had been bewitched for a long time, barely hesitated. He found an excuse to send away the scientists who researched the Gantz server and personally ordered the engineers to secretly install a network cable that could connect to the external network in the laboratory.

Thus, just a few days later, the first Basilisk pattern event in history broke out...

When this major turn of events first occurred, Major General Cary knew he would not be able to escape the military court sooner or later. Plus, the matter was so serious that he had completely come to his senses. He was already prepared to spend the second half of his life in prison.

Nevertheless, before his involvement was even revealed, the North American government and military had already collapsed...

Not only that, the little girl in the statue even told him that within a 100-meter radius of the server, all mysterious phenomena would be suppressed. Within this radius, there would be no mysterious phenomena at all.

Thus, Major General Cary relied on this information to win over a group of officers. Then, under the guidance of the statue, Major General Cary swiftly and decisively eliminated all the opposing officers and occupied this underground base by himself.

Therefore, this radius was called the Death Radius and everyone in the base also called it —

Dead Space.

All of this was the story of Major General Cary.

According to him, it was not only this Gantz server, but the other two Gantz servers located in Germany, Europe, and Jinping, Mainland China had also beguiled the highest person in charge of the base and launched the first cyber-attack on the same day.

In other words, all the Gantz servers were connected. The Gantz server could emit a signal that humans could not detect. This signal could be sent out at a speed with almost no delay and received by other Gantz servers.

It was precisely because of this that these servers could act as the terminals of Gantz spheres all over the world.

After silently listening to the story of Major General Cary, Chen Chen could not help frowning.

If what the old white-bearded man said was true, did this not mean that the Gantz server not only had the Basilisk patterns as a trump card, but it also had powerful intelligence and the ability to bewitch people?

It was just not known whether it seduced people’s hearts through words or some other enigmatic power...

chapters.title: Chapter 320: Message
chapters.body:
Chapter 320: Message

Chapter 320: Message

Having obtained the information, Chen Chen returned to the Bugatti Spaceship.

As he looked at the ruins of the city flashing by the window, Chen Chen could not help wearing a rueful look.

To think that one day he would be able to solve the mystery of a civilization’s demise. Although all of this would happen sooner or later, as he got closer to the truth, Chen Chen felt that a crisis was gradually closing in.

This feeling did not arise from “Thuds”, but rather, it was as if he was on a chessboard, observing a giant hand that controlled all of fate.

Why did the USB drive send him to these worlds of destroyed civilizations? Did it want to tell him something or was it all just a game of chess?

Chen Chen shook his head. This was not the first time he had thought about this issue and it would not be the last time. It was just that as Chen Chen continued to grow stronger, he was gradually approaching that truth.

This time, the mystery of a human dimension’s destruction was successfully solved, which was further proof.

“And who knows if that Gantz server had cured me of that ‘Thuds’ mysterious phenomenon...”

Chen Chen murmured in a low voice. If the Gantz server had cured him, that would be the best, but if not, then it was hard to tell what would happen.

The reason Chen Chen did not believe that it could resolve the strange phenomenon for him was simply an issue of logic.

According to the data, the Gantz server had no ability to utilize the mysterious phenomena. In other words, its only power was to bewitch people.

Chen Chen did not know if this was a deliberate restriction put in place by Alien Civilization A or a flaw in its manufacturing. Regardless, since the Gantz server could not use this power, how could it erase this for Chen Chen?

After all, there was a saying that an ability would go both ways.

If the Gantz server did not erase the mysterious phenomenon of “Thuds” for Chen Chen, then he could only resort to other solutions.

This was also why Chen Chen was certain that the Gantz server had not absorbed X-112 because it did not know about the conversation Chen Chen had with X-112, nor did it know that Chen Chen still had one last loophole to rely on, and that was the USB drive.

The USB drive’s portal might be able to wipe out this mysterious phenomenon for Chen Chen.

These thoughts flashed across his mind. At this time, the Bugatti Spaceship that Chen Chen was in had flown out of the atmosphere again, returning to its previous position using the flight path of a ballistic missile.

However, when Chen Chen inadvertently looked at the porthole at his side, something white suddenly darted past the window.

It seemed to be a thin, pale arm...

Chen Chen instinctively looked at the altitude gauge on the spacecraft.

At this time, the Bugatti Spaceship had entered the exosphere, with an altitude of more than 500 kilometers. The atmosphere here was extremely thin and the air density was only one hundred millionth that of sea level.

Not only that, but at this time the Bugatti Spaceship was also flying at a speed exceeding Mach 25. Chen Chen believed that no one in the world could have been lying across the spaceship to follow Chen Chen up until now.

Since it was not a human being, then the thing outside the window would soon be revealed...

Chen Chen turned gloomy. Sure enough, the Gantz server’s words were not wrong. The mysterious phenomenon plaguing him was about to reach the outbreak phase. As the deadline drew closer, the mysterious phenomenon would only become more malicious and absurd...

Still, fortunately, it was just an arm flashing past and it did not do anything unpleasant to Chen Chen. After all, considering the environment outside the spacecraft at this time, if something really happened, even the protagonist of Chronicle would not survive, let alone Chen Chen.

After half an hour, the spacecraft reappeared in the sky above Tokyo and successfully found the passage that had been opened.

At this time, the black portal was still suspended in thin air atop a ruin. From the front, it looked like a black vortex, but the more one went closer, the more one would notice the peculiarity of this black vortex.

This was because this black vortex would not be illuminated by any light and at the same time, from a certain angle sideways, one would find that it had no thickness at all.

This was a phenomenon beyond the scope of science known to humankind.

The spaceship slowly landed beside the portal. Chen Chen deliberately glanced at the hull of the spaceship after getting off, but this glance caused his pupils to contract.

He saw that there were a few five-finger palm prints on the hull of the spaceship...

Yoru Kamidera who got off the spaceship also noticed this, but he just looked at Chen Chen without saying anything.

After that, Chen Chen turned his head and looked at the passage in front of him, but he did not return at once. Instead, he said, “X-112, compile all the information here and send it over.”

As he spoke, Chen Chen took out a Rubik’s Cube again, pressed a few buttons with familiar gestures, then spoke into the Rubik’s Cube, “X, this is Chen Chen.”

“By the time you’re seeing this holographic recording, I’d already be caught up in something strange.

“I can’t say what kind of thing it is, but you can understand it as a virus. However, unlike regular viruses, it’s a virus that spreads using ‘information’ as a carrier.”

As he talked, Chen Chen silently placed the Rubik’s Cube on the ground. “This is something that doesn’t exist in our dimension. No, it may also exist, but it has never appeared on Earth. At the same time, it spreads in so many different ways. X-112 will tell you all about this. I won’t repeat those points. I’ll only talk about two things now.”

Here, Chen Chen turned completely serious. He raised an index finger. “First, close the passage and create a Faraday cage. This magnetic cage must not only cut off all information signals, but also all substances on the material plane.

“It must be completely sealed and extremely strong. At the very least, it must not be opened from the inside. There mustn’t be a single crack and no sound must pass through from within. After it’s built, put the computer and the USB drive in, then use a Black Knight to open the portal again in there.

“At last, I’ll come back through the passage and seal myself inside.

“I’ll keep myself there for seven days. During these seven days, no matter what happens in the Faraday cage, you shouldn’t open it. If nothing happens in these seven days, then I’ll give you the order to open the Faraday cage.”

Having said that, Chen Chen paused for a while and then raised two fingers. “Secondly, if I die in the Faraday cage, destroy all the information I passed to you, then clone me and use that clone to donate everything under my name to the Mainland authorities. After that, build a rocket and cast the USB drive out of the Earth, the farther the better...”

“Lastly... Take care.”

Having said all this, Chen Chen squatted again, pressed the end button on the silver Rubik’s Cube, and gently tossed the Rubik’s Cube into the black channel in front of him.

At this point, the message would be received by Little X on the other side of the portal.

chapters.title: Chapter 321: Yoru Kamidera’S Analysis
chapters.body:
Chapter 321: Yoru Kamidera’S Analysis

Chapter 321: Yoru Kamidera’s Analysis

Ten minutes after Chen Chen threw the Rubik’s Cube in, he watched as the black vortex in front of him began to shrink rapidly. In the blink of an eye, it had collapsed into a ball.

With a puff, just like an extinguished flame, the portal immediately vanished from Chen Chen’s sight without a trace.

Chen Chen knew that this was Little X who had already responded to him.

Chen Chen did not allow these Black Knights or Yoru Kamidera to return to reality because he was not even sure whether these brainless clones would be infected with any mysterious phenomena. If they were infected, once they returned to reality, they would only unleash a special “infectious disease” that had no cure.

Once this kind of “infectious disease” broke out, it would only send the world down a path of annihilation, just like the world in Gantz.

Therefore, the only thing Chen Chen could do was to completely isolate himself and others from reality.

Of course, Chen Chen would not sit around waiting to die. Otherwise, he would not have asked Little X to prepare a Faraday cage.

He only hoped that Little X would hurry up.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen raised his head. Currently, the sun had already begun to set in the west, dyeing red the boundless dusk over the horizon. At first glance, it looked like an endless cloud of fire, burning and bruising the entire sky.

At this moment, the deserted city, the fallen civilization, and the flaming clouds on the horizon seemed to mirror each other for an instant, producing a special kind of loneliness in Chen Chen’s heart.

It felt like being abandoned by the world.

As the visibility of his surroundings decreased, Chen Chen’s sense of unease began to weigh him down more heavily.

He could not describe what it felt like. It was like a death row prisoner facing their trial. Any sound of footsteps would trigger an intense burst of terror because they had realized that these footsteps belonged to Death itself.

At this moment, Chen Chen was feeling the same way.

“Yoru Kamidera.”

At this point, Chen Chen suddenly spoke and posed a question to the youth beside him, “Have you heard about thudding noises that come from the next door?”

“Is that... A sort of mysterious phenomenon?”

Beside Chen Chen, Yoru Kamidera, who was feeling a little restless, looked up at once. He seemed to understand something. “Please go on.”

“I went into a strange room yesterday during the daytime. There was a woman in the room, but she was dead, having hanged herself.”

Chen Chen said quietly, “But the strange thing was that there were signs of impact from a baseball bat on the wall of her bedroom. It seemed that she had used the baseball bat to hit the wall back when she was alive.”

“What’s on the other side of the wall?”

When Chen Chen paused, Yoru Kamidera asked quickly.

A glint of approval shone in Chen Chen’s eyes. As expected of a young man who could survive the doomsday until now. Chen Chen had only mentioned the beginning, yet Yoru Kamidera could already acutely pinpoint the crux of the matter?

With this in mind, Chen Chen immediately replied, “Across the bedroom wall is another residence. I went there as well, but it was filled with dust. It’d appear that it has been uninhabited ever since the doomsday, but...”

Chen Chen’s voice softened. “But I saw a dent made by an impact on the wall of this residence. This dent had deepened into a part of the wall. In other words, something was incessantly hitting the wall as if it would only stop once it broke through.”

“What if you change rooms?” Yoru Kamidera asked again.

“The woman did change bedrooms at that time, but the same still happened in the new bedroom. There was still the same never-ending thuds on the opposite side of the wall.”

Chen Chen answered, “Not only that, but I also found some oil stains on the wall of that bedroom. It seems that the woman had frequently assumed the same position against the wall when she was alive, placing an ear on the wall to listen to the other side.”

When Yoru Kamidera heard this, his expression flickered. “Got it. I think this was what happened.

“When she was alive, the woman had lived alone in that room, but one day, she suddenly heard faint thuds on the other side of the wall. At first, she thought there was someone in the opposite room, but because this was doomsday without any law or morality, she couldn’t determine whether the other person was a friend or a foe. Therefore, she could only press her ears to the wall over and over, trying to hear the conversation on the other side.

“However, gradually, she realized something was wrong.”

Yoru Kamidera paced slowly and said as he walked, “It may be that the thuds had something unique about them such as a particularly long duration or a particularly uniform rhythm. Gradually, the woman began to suspect the thuds on the other side of the wall — are they human? Or something else...

“As time passed, the thuds continued to the point where they kept ringing day and night, until finally, the woman snapped.”

Yoru Kamidera continued. “The noise seriously affected her sleep and at the same time, horrified her. At this time, she had vaguely understood that the sound was not made by a human being but by the thing that was her deepest fear...

“So she finally snapped and she began to use a baseball bat to hit the wall there, continuously venting her inner terror. It was as if as long as she displayed an aggressive side, the other party would retreat. This is a common psychological response in times of fear.

“Later on, she tried countless possibilities but in the end, she couldn’t find a solution or she already guessed what would happen after the wall was broken down.”

At this point, Yoru Kamidera looked toward Chen Chen again. “I’m guessing that this is the logic behind the woman’s actions as well as her mental journey. In the end, she thought, ‘Instead of dying at the hands of a mysterious phenomenon or dying out of fear and torture, why don’t I stop it myself?’ Am I correct?”

After listening to Yoru Kamidera’s analysis, Chen Chen immediately applauded. “Very good. It’s exactly what I’ve guessed, but I can’t guarantee whether it’s right or not.”

“I get it. You’re infected with this kind of mysterious phenomenon.”

Yoru Kamidera nodded. “I just don’t know how you got infected with this mysterious phenomenon. If there were more clues, maybe we can find a countermeasure.”

“I don’t know myself, but I have some theories.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and pointed to the three Black Knights. “Perhaps the whole room is cursed. As long as you enter, you’ll be infected with this strange phenomenon of ‘Thuds’. The reason I’m the only one infected is that those three aren’t complete human beings.”

“Not complete human beings?”

Upon hearing Chen Chen’s words, Yoru Kamidera was instantly taken aback, then said, “I’ve long thought that these three people are a bit too rigid, but I don’t understand what it means when you say they’re not complete humans. Are they clones?”

“They aren’t just clones, but because they have been specially processed since the embryonic stage, these people also don’t have brains.”

Chen Chen explained, “The reason they can behave so ordinarily is only because they are controlled by the X-112 artificial intelligence I’ve been talking to.”

“But this still doesn’t quite make sense. After all, the mysterious phenomena can’t distinguish whether a person has a brain...”

When he was told of the Black Knights’ identity, Yoru Kamidera displayed a surprised look. Still, after some thought, he shook his head again. “Thus, I think it’s more likely that there is some other infection condition and only you triggered this condition, thus becoming infected with this mysterious phenomenon called ‘Thuds’.”

chapters.title: Chapter 322: The Mysterious Phenomenon’S Outbreak
chapters.body:
Chapter 322: The Mysterious Phenomenon’S Outbreak

Chapter 322: The Mysterious Phenomenon’s Outbreak

After listening to Yoru Kamidera’s analysis, Chen Chen could not help nodding.

Although Chen Chen was capable of meticulous thoughts after taking NZT-48, he was still relying on his own baseless theories. How could this be compared to inquiring a veteran who had survived in this world for more than five years?

Chen Chen would not feel embarrassed or anything just because the other guy was younger than him. He was too wise for that.

Presently, Chen Chen said thoughtfully, “I think I get what you mean. When I was reading the material before, I also noticed that the so-called mysterious phenomena may be very similar to the ghostly incidents and supernatural events that are common in folklore. But the mysterious phenomena always have their unique regularities. There aren’t so many ghosts and monsters, but rather a special kind of phenomenon...”

“Yes, that’s what I meant.”

Yoru Kamidera nodded quickly. “Whether it’s the Basilisk patterns or the strange phenomena, they have their unique regularities. These regularities are like wind, thunder or rain. They are no different from natural phenomena.”

“I’m relieved to hear you say that.”

Chen Chen heard this and laughed. “Whether it is wind, rain or thunder, the occurrence of these natural phenomena requires certain conditions such as an alternation of hot and cold air currents, an increase in the air’s electric charge, and so on. This is to say, as long as these conditions are disrupted, we can stop a phenomenon?”

With that said, Chen Chen took out a wireless headset and tossed it to Yoru Kamidera. Then he boarded the spacecraft while saying, “In that case, Yoru-kun, please do me a favor. Please stand with the three Black Knights outside the spaceship and keep watch. If anything abnormal happens, you must alert me immediately.”

“I understand...”

Yoru Kamidera’s expression shifted. “You want to see if someone is observing from outside the spaceship, will the thuds still appear?”

“That’s right. So far, when the thuds occurred, they happened without anyone else outside. Therefore, I want to try this — if I stay alone in an enclosed environment, yet there are others outside, will it be triggered again with this condition?”

As he spoke, Chen Chen shot a deep meaningful look at Yoru Kamidera. “Yoru-kun, you’ll help me, right?”

Yoru Kamidera’s face became somber and he said solemnly, “Rest assured, I’ll keep watch outside for Your Excellency!”

Seeing this, Chen Chen nodded.

Both of them had a tacit understanding. Namely, what if the rules changed during the outbreak of this mysterious phenomenon? For instance, would the mysterious phenomenon to kill the infected in the enclosed space, first kill the observer outside?

If that happened, then not only would Chen Chen die, but even Yoru Kamidera was most likely a goner.

In other words, Yoru Kamidera, who was acting as a counter against the conditions of the mysterious phenomenon, was not necessarily free of danger.

Even so, Yoru Kamidera had no power to refuse.

Chen Chen glanced at the darkening sky for the last time, then pressed the closing button. Immediately, the gull-wing doors on both sides of him closed slowly, turning the spacecraft into a closed space again.

After doing this, Chen Chen exhaled in the dark and closed his eyes, entering a state of restive meditation.

The day slowly turned to night.

Soon, the last ray of twilight had disappeared into the boundless sky. Without humanity’s industrial pollution, the sky had become clear again.

As the sun set, the sky changed from navy blue to indigo. Meanwhile, a silver-white ribbon emerged above everyone’s heads.

It was the resplendent Milky Way!

Yoru Kamidera silently wore the wireless headset over his ears, then walked to the front of the spacecraft and sat down as did the other three Black Knights.

The four of them were split into four directions, with the spacecraft in the middle. The advantage of this was that, in the event of any abnormality, they would notice it at once.

Time ticked by, minute by minute...

In no time, it had turned from early night to deep night.

Without their realizing it, night had grown denser, permeating the air like a fog. Perhaps because of their exploits during the day, Yoru Kamidera felt a hint of oncoming sleepiness in the darkness.

At first, he could not refrain from yawning a few times. Then, he shook himself, startled, quickly picked up the water before him and took a big gulp, recovering his spirits.

Even so, Yoru Kamidera could not last long and soon the sleepiness washed over him again. Without realizing it, Yoru Kamidera had closed his eyes while his head was slowly drooping...

However, just as he was drifting between sleep and wakefulness, an indescribable chill crept up to him.

In his sleep, Yoru Kamidera only felt that the temperature had dropped a few degrees. Puzzled, he opened his bleary eyes, but in the next second, his pupils shrank furiously!

At an unknown time, a white figure was sprawled on its stomach upon the spaceship!

It seemed to be a human figure, all in white, with both arms holding onto the spaceship. Its head was turned sideways, one ear pressed closely to the porthole.

This action...

At this moment, Yoru Kamidera seemed to understand something.

[Not only that, but I also found some oil stains on the wall of that bedroom. It seems that the woman had frequently assumed the same position against the wall when she was alive, placing an ear on the wall to listen to the other side...]

Chen Chen’s description flashed in his mind. In this instant, Yoru Kamidera fully understood why Chen Chen was infected with this mysterious phenomenon.

That was, Chen Chen must have assumed the same position as this white figure did, based on the oil stains on the wall!

It was even very possible that while Chen Chen was performing this action, on the other side of the wall, this white figure was also leaning against the wall, performing the same action as Chen Chen...

Was the mysterious phenomenon happening now?

Yoru Kamidera’s heart was pounding wildly. Although he had many years of experience, being someone well-informed even among the survivors, he was only sixteen years old after all. His mind was far from reaching an adult’s maturity.

Now, he was forced to face a mysterious phenomenon again and he was no more than three meters away!

He subconsciously looked at the Black Knights on the left and right sides of the spaceship. It stood to reason that the three computer-controlled clones should have taken action when faced with this situation, but at this time they were sitting in place, motionless as a block of wood.

Even the computer AI in the spaceship did not respond just as before...

“Sure enough, AIs and other artificial existences can’t come into contact with mysterious phenomena? No, it must be that certain manifestations of mysterious phenomena cannot be observed by artificial intelligence life.

“In that case, His Excellency Chen Chen is in danger...”

With this in mind, Yoru Kamidera was caught in a dilemma.

If he notified Chen Chen, he was likely to attract the attention of the white figure and this might even lead to him getting involved in the mysterious phenomenon.

However, if Chen Chen was not alerted, then it was very likely that Chen Chen would die in this mysterious phenomenon’s outbreak...

chapters.title: Chapter 323: Counterattack
chapters.body:
Chapter 323: Counterattack

Chapter 323: Counterattack

In truth, Yoru Kamidera did not want to care whether Chen Chen lived or died.

If Chen Chen died in the midst of a mysterious phenomenon, Yoru Kamidera would have a chance to obtain that incredible armor. He had personally seen how exceptional the Black Knights were with this armor.

If he could get his hands on that suit of armor, Yoru Kamidera was confident that he could survive this doomsday for more than ten additional years...

However, if he did not alert Chen Chen and Chen Chen survived this mysterious outbreak, what would happen?

It was obvious. He must not be fooled by how nicely Chen Chen had treated him this whole time. If he pulled something like this, he would be the next one to die...

From this man’s every word and deed, Yoru Kamidera had already discerned that Chen Chen was basically like a ruthless hero. He had treated Yoru Kamidera with respect, but on second thought, it was not as if Chen Chen had given him any choice. Chen Chen’s word was the law.

It was just that Chen Chen did not present his brutal side on the surface.

All these thoughts flashed through Yoru Kamidera’s mind. Meanwhile, the white figure was still propped up against the door with both hands. However, its back suddenly arched while its head and body were pulled back.

This was...

Yoru Kamidera vaguely thought of something.

In the next moment, the white figure suddenly lurched forward violently. It bashed its head against the gull-wing door of the spacecraft, making a loud thud!

“Thud!”

This sound...

At this moment, Yoru Kamidera finally understood that the thuds were nothing else but the sound of the white figure hitting its head against the spaceship!

However, even at this time, the spaceship and the three Black Knights remained silent as if their program had crashed.

When he saw this, Yoru Kamidera finally mustered up the courage. Fine, he would take a gamble!

Thinking of this, Yoru Kamidera softly turned on the button of the wireless headset while he lowered his voice and said, “Your Excellency Chen Chen, there is...”

In the instant he turned on the wireless headset, before he could even finish the phrase “there’s something”, there was an inexplicable rustling sound from the headset.

It was as if the communication signal had been interfered with in some way!

At the same time that Yoru Kamidera spoke, the white figure suddenly halted, then turned its head at lightning speed and looked toward him!

At present, Yoru Kamidera felt that the blood in his body was about to freeze...

By the dim moonlight, Yoru Kamidera saw at last that it was a blank face with no facial features, no contours, just like a monstrous picture scroll, upon which the painter had not painted any details...

However, just as Yoru Kamidera’s heart seized in horror, the gull-wing door suddenly swung out into the air. An arm covered in black armor stretched out and viciously struck the white figure in the face.

Suddenly, from Yoru Kamidera’s perspective, he only saw that arm plunging directly into the head of the white figure like it was a block of tofu. The white head was instantly deflated and its entire face caved in!

Even so, in the next moment, the white figure had vanished into thin air...

Only then did Chen Chen walked out of the spaceship with a somber expression.

He looked down, gripped his fist, then glanced at Yoru Kamidera at one side. “You did well.”

“Your Excellency...”

Seeing this scene, Yoru Kamidera was completely stupefied. He said incredulously, “You hit that white figure?”

“No, I didn’t hit anything.”

Chen Chen shook his head. He looked extremely disgruntled because just now, he had not only used the PK Armor to attack the opponent but had also secretly used Field energy.

The weird thing was that, whether it was physical force or Field energy, it was impossible to make any contact with the white figure. Chen Chen only felt his vision momentarily going out of focus. When he recovered, he found that he was just punching the air.

One should recall that everything in the world, as long as it was made of matter, would have electromagnetic force. If even Field energy could not touch the white figure, did it mean that the white figure was not made of matter at all, without even atoms and electrons, but something else completely...

In other words, the white figure did not exist.

As for how Yoru Kamidera performed outside just now, Chen Chen also saw it clearly from the surveillance. Although Yoru Kamidera struggled visibly with his hesitation, in any case, ultimately he kept his promise and assisted Chen Chen when the mysterious phenomenon occurred.

Although this assistance did not do sh*t, at least it showed that Yoru Kamidera could be tentatively trusted.

At this time, looking at the youth’s dumbfounded eyes, Chen Chen sighed. Then he looked at the gull-wing door of the Bugatti Spaceship, only to see that the door was now dented and the mark was much deeper than before.

Before this, the figure had only hit it once before it was distracted by Yoru Kamidera. If it had hit it a few times, the gull-wing door might be smashed through.

This was a preposterous matter. After all, although the Bugatti Spaceship was not a military spacecraft, the hull could still withstand the vacuum of space, but now it had crumpled like tofu.

It was clear that the white figure did not use much force as if it had not eaten for a long time. Even so, the actual mark of impact far exceeded one’s common understanding of physics.

“Could this be the outbreak phase of the mysterious phenomenon just like what the Gantz server said?”

Chen Chen murmured.

“No, it’s not...”

However, Yoru Kamidera quickly interjected, “I’ve also heard about the outbreak phase, but if the mysterious phenomenon is approaching that phase, you won’t be able to drive away the opponent with just one punch.”

“How do you know that?” Chen Chen raised his eyebrows upon hearing this.

“Now that I think about it, the reason that monster disappeared is probably because a condition was broken.”

Yoru Kamidera did not directly answer, but earnestly explained, “It’s just that because you dared to open the hatch to fight back, it might give the impression that you drove it away with a punch, but in fact, that may not be the case...”

“You mean that because I left the spaceship, the enclosed environment was nullified at that moment, so the condition of the strange event was disrupted and it disappeared?”

Chen Chen interrupted with a question.

“Yes.”

Yoru Kamidera nodded and said, “It’s not necessarily that you left the spaceship. It may also be that I saw it, which caused it to vanish. You said that when the strange phenomenon of ‘Thuds’ occurred for the first time, the sound stopped when the door of the next room was opened by the Black Knight. From this, it can be seen that an observer should be the key to breaking the condition.”

“But just now you’ve already noticed it, yet it didn’t vanish. How can that be explained?”

Chen Chen frowned.

“I suspect this is an issue at a more complex level of detail.”

Yoru Kamidera thought for a while. “If possible, I think this mysterious phenomenon called ‘Thuds’ may have the rules of ‘active sighting’ and ‘passive sighting’. When the Black Knight pushed open the door of the next room, it was an active sighting, so the rules were broken and the white figure disappeared.

“But just now, I first noticed the white figure in a half-asleep state. At this point, it might still be counted as a ‘passive sighting’. Therefore, the white figure didn’t vanish until I acted to alert you. This turned the ‘passive’ into the ‘active’, so the white figure disappeared...”

chapters.title: Chapter 324: Back To Reality
chapters.body:
Chapter 324: Back To Reality

Chapter 324: Back to Reality

“Passive and active?”

Chen Chen muttered in a low voice. Yoru Kamidera’s analysis had given him a lot of food for thought.

“As for why I said this isn’t an outbreak phase, it’s because I’ve seen many strange phenomena that have become increasingly malevolent over time.”

Yoru Kamidera went on to explain, “For example, in the ‘Woman in Dreams’ phenomenon, every time the infected person dreams, they’ll see that woman. She’ll get closer and closer as time goes by and eventually reach the bed of the infected person and strangle them to death.

“As a result, some people tried setting an alarm clock to target this condition and only allow themselves to sleep for five minutes at a time. After all, dreams take time to generate. If you only sleep for five minutes at a time, you may be able to avoid dreaming, thereby avoiding the woman.”

“So, this method failed?”

Chen Chen asked.

“That’s right. This method did have a certain effect on the infected person at the beginning. With this method, he succeeded in surviving a week, but he also failed several times during this period, which allowed the woman in white to reach his bed. The next time he dreamed, he would die.

“At this point, he was beyond cautious. He even found a companion to stay by his side, in case the alarm clock could not wake him.

“However, after falling asleep for the last time, he never woke up. Neither the alarm clock nor his companion could wake him up. His companion could see that he had begun to struggle with himself, but ultimately he couldn’t escape that dream, until the woman in white emerged out of his chest.”

Yoru Kamidera concluded, “This is the outbreak phase. During the outbreak phase, mysterious phenomena will become more and more aggressive and even completely irreversible. This time, you could still restrict the opponent through the rules, and even escape. This means that you’re not in the outbreak phase for now.”

After thinking about it, Yoru Kamidera added. “Of course, based on the situation just now, your current state is very close to the outbreak phase.”

Chen Chen wore a pensive look after listening to all this. “Your analysis is good, but there are still some points that are far-fetched. Since you’ve mentioned the outbreak phase, is it possible that the change in rules this time is because I’m close to the outbreak phase, rather than due to any active or passive sighting?”

“I’m not sure about that. After all, I’m just guessing based on my experience,” Yoru Kamidera answered with some embarrassment.

Hearing this, Chen Chen did not continue to question him but looked at the Bugatti Spaceship at the side. At this time, X-112 in the spacecraft spoke up, “Sir Commander, I’m sorry that I failed at my job again...”

Chen Chen waved his hand. “This isn’t your fault. Don’t blame yourself because even the surveillance on the spaceship was disturbed at that moment, showing only static on the screen. Electronic products seem unable to detect this kind of existence.”

Since even X-112 could not observe the white figure, naturally the three Black Knights were the same. Chen Chen’s strongest force had completely lost its effectiveness in the face of this mysterious phenomenon.

With this in mind, Chen Chen returned to the spacecraft. He had also considered whether he could escape catastrophe if he slept in the open field or if he kept the spacecraft’s hatch open the entire time. Still, this was just an idea.

After all, although the white figure appeared every time the infected person was in a closed space, one must not forget, its purpose was to break into the closed space of the infected person. Who could guarantee that, if the infected person slept in the open wild or left the cabin hatch open, it would not directly trigger a fatal kill?

Therefore, Chen Chen would never dare to try this.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen closed the hatch again and placed himself in an enclosed space while Yoru Kamidera remained outside the spaceship, waiting silently.

Fortunately, after the thuds were disturbed, they would not reappear for a period of time. It seemed that this rule was still valid. Everyone struggled to stay up until the next morning, and nothing happened again.

Not only that, but as midday approached, the black vortex that had vanished before the spaceship appeared again!

“Sir Commander, my progenitor seems to have fulfilled your request.”

X-112 notified.

Seeing this scene, Chen Chen nodded while secretly heaving a sigh of relief. “X-112, transfer yourself back to the hard drive and I’ll take you back.”

“Understood.”

After X-112 agreed, the entire spacecraft was fully turned off. Five minutes later, a hard drive bounced out of the spacecraft and was immediately caught by Chen Chen.

Following that, Chen Chen patted his chest. In the next second, in a burst of blue plasma, the PK Armor resumed its form as an armguard, then slid off Chen Chen’s arm.

Not only that, but Chen Chen also took out a small knife, then walked toward the three Black Knights. After making them take off their PK Armor and GS Combat Suits, he made direct incisions in their necks with the blade, from which he took out three bloody microchips.

Yoru Kamidera watched this with mouth agape.

“I can’t guarantee whether they were also infected with any mysterious phenomena, so the best thing to do is to keep their bodies here.”

Chen Chen looked at Yoru Kamidera and explained, “Of course, you’re different. Both your experiences and your hyperthymesia have high research value, so you can rest assured that I’ll bring you with me.”

“Your Excellency, you haven’t told me what’s on the other side of this passage.”

Yoru Kamidera was a little apprehensive. “Are you from the future?”

“No.”

Chen Chen moved toward Yoru Kamidera, step by step. Although Chen Chen had taken off the PK Armor, his mere presence was still overwhelming to Yoru Kamidera. “The place to which I’m taking you, strictly speaking, is a parallel universe.

“In this parallel universe, there are no mysterious phenomena on Earth, nor the first calamity, not even Gantz. It’s a completely peaceful world.”

“Really?”

Upon hearing Chen Chen’s words, Yoru Kamidera’s eyes gleamed with shock. “Is there such a thing as a parallel universe?”

“I never lie to anyone.”

Chen Chen was standing next to Yoru Kamidera now. He promptly gripped Yoru Kamidera by the shoulders and in a half-coercive manner, walked them both toward the dark vortex in front.

Yoru Kamidera was so scared that he closed his eyes involuntarily.

The moment they passed through the passage, the both of them only saw darkness before their eyes. A sense of weightlessness took hold. By the time that sensation had passed, Chen Chen realized that he had arrived in a dim room.

The walls and floor of this room were entirely made of pure metal. The entire room was only four or five square meters in size and he could not make out any doors or windows. Only a laptop, and a USB flash drive inserted in it, were placed in the corner.

Currently, the laptop emitted a faint light, barely illuminating the items in the room.

There were only two small metal beds in the room and a toilet in the corner.

There was nothing else besides that.

It appeared that this was the Faraday cage that Little X had prepared for him. It might also very well be Chen Chen’s final coffin.

Just as Chen Chen was sighing wordlessly to himself, the familiar, warm voice of a little girl rang out in the same instant.

“Sir Godfather, welcome home...”

chapters.title: Chapter 325: The Memetic Effect
chapters.body:
Chapter 325: The Memetic Effect

Chapter 325: The Memetic Effect

When Chen Chen heard Little X’s voice, he could not hold back a thin smile. “X, you shouldn’t install communication facilities in this room. It’s likely that mysterious phenomena will leak out and cause a worldwide disaster.”

“It doesn’t matter because I’ve analyzed that sound isn’t the infection condition of this mysterious phenomenon you’ve encountered.”

Little X’s childish voice replied, “According to the information brought back by my clone X-112, I think the infection condition should be related to the female corpse you saw.”

“It’s the action!”

At this moment, Yoru Kamidera had adapted to the environment here and he quickly brought up his previous guess. “When I was outside the spaceship I saw the movements of the white figure. It put an ear on the wall, exactly in the same manner as the oil stain you described!”

Chen Chen was startled when he heard this. He recalled that when he entered that room, he did imitate the woman’s previous actions by placing his ear against the wall based on the traces she left.

In other words, this action triggered the infection condition?

“This makes sense.”

Little X’s voice was approving. “So to prevent the mysterious phenomenon from spreading out, I ordered the industrial park to work overnight, manufacturing a Faraday cage made of special tungsten steel. The thickness of the walls is more than half a meter and there are buffer layers installed. Even a tank’s armor-piercing projectiles can forget about breaking through this metal lump.”

“I don’t know if a tank’s armor-piercing projectile can break through, but if it’s hit by a projectile, Yoru Kamidera and I will be shaken to death in here.”

Chen Chen spread his hands.

“It doesn’t matter. This is just the first layer of the isolation room.”

Little X quickly added. “Now our factory is still working overtime to manufacture the second protective wall of the isolation room. The thickness of the second protective wall will be up to one meter. Once completed, it can be directly placed over the isolation room. At that point, the thickness of the isolation room’s outer wall will be more than 1.5 meters.

“After that, they’re rushing to complete the third and fourth layers too. My ultimate goal is to create an isolation room that is more than ten meters thick and capable of resisting nuclear bombs. No matter what mysterious phenomenon it is, it can forget about breaking through this wall!”

“It sounds splendid indeed.”

Chen Chen could not help laughing. “But how about our air and food supply as well as our excretions?”

“Please rest assured, I’ll install a circulation device and a discharge device on each layer. There’s no problem with air circulation.”

Little X explained, “As for the food supply, don’t worry. I’ve set up a special energy bar dispenser. This dispenser is usually closed. Only when the energy bars and drinking water are placed inside, will the mechanism be triggered, and the food will enter, layer by layer. For example, when food is entering the second layer of protective wall, the first layer of protective wall will automatically close. In any case, the sealed state of the room will always be maintained.”

After hearing Little X’s explanation, Chen Chen did not know whether to laugh or cry. Still, on second thought, it seemed that there was no alternative.

It could even be said that this was a contest between modern technology and mysterious phenomena. They would find out whether the products of human technology was more secure or if the mysterious phenomenon would still win out.

“Not only that.”

Little X continued. “I’ve controlled the Black Knights to work overtime to come up with a T-virus that matches your DNA. We’re just one step away from success. I want to know — once you officially merge with the T-virus and become a new lifeform with different DNA from normal humans, will these mysterious phenomena still be effective?”

After listening to Little X’s words, Yoru Kamidera was also completely astounded. Although he did not know what T-virus was, he still understood that viruses could transform human DNA. This direct manipulation of DNA to evade mysterious phenomena — it was the first time he had heard of such a method. Still, when he thought about it, it was worth trying.

Even he was stunned by this little girl’s remarks.

In this manner, the pair stayed in this impenetrable room. Chen Chen did not shy away from the topic, constantly discussing the Basilisk patterns and the mysterious phenomena with Little X while Yoru Kamidera listened silently.

“I’ve carried out the relevant research based on the information you brought back, and it seems that the principle of the original Basilisk pattern isn’t that complicated.”

Little X said compliantly, “I should know. For example, there’s a computer virus called a ‘fork bomb’. This tiny character set can define a meaningless function, then ask the computer to execute it repeatedly. The computer has no common sense to rely on and will only obey. What it is given becomes what it will execute, finally causing the program to crash.

“The picture named ‘Eye’ is like a virus targeting the human brain. The moment the human brain receives, through vision, the information from this picture, it will suffer brain death due to a program crash.”

“I know this, it’s called ‘Gödel’s shock’.”

Chen Chen nodded. “But how do you explain the other Basilisk patterns?”

“Similarly, they are all a type of information.”

Little X then replied, “You can think of it as an information virus. Information can be transmitted and this picture is the carrier of information. Human vision is the channel for information transmission and the target of transmission is the human brain. There’s already an existing concept of information of this nature, we call it — memes.”

“Memes?”

Chen Chen’s expression flickered. His knowledge about memetics flashed in his mind automatically — that was a kind of information dissemination unit, evolved from the word “gene” from biology, meaning “the gene of information”.

According to this concept, it was an existence whose evolution did not depend on the specific chemical basis of genetics, but only depended on the self-replicating transmission unit. This was what they called a meme.

The dissemination of memes was the process of disseminating information. Unlike genes that could only be disseminated across generations, memes could spread from person to person at any time through communication and other media.

To give a simple example, in the cesspool of the Internet, someone might learn about the joke of using “Righteous Light”[1] and occasionally crack the joke themselves. Then, someone uninformed would ask: “What’s the joke?”

After the first person’s explanation, the second person understood the meaning of “Righteous Light”. Thus, when the latter encountered something that reeked a little of positivity, they would hum the tune subconsciously — this was the spread of memes.

In this case, the song “Righteous Light” was the carrier of the meme. It was transmitted through communication between people. Human beings were the infectors or hosts of the meme, and the transmission channel was the entire Internet.

After the host was infected, there would be an intense desire to spread the meme around. This desire could also be seen as a form of “bragging”. In short, driven by this desire, the meme would be spread through the Internet to an increasingly larger audience, eventually forming a kind of network trend.

“However, this is different from jokes. Generally speaking, jokes have no influence on human beings. Even globally banned music such as “Gloomy Sunday”[2] uses psychological suggestions and other methods to impact people’s spirits. They contain no inherent influence on humans and will not interfere with the state of human life.”

Little X explained, “But the memetic is different. This is a special kind of information that has the ability to directly interfere. Its impact on humans is on both physical and mental levels. I think this impact and interference can be called —

“The memetic effect!”

[1] A lyric from a Chinese song that later became used to satirize superficially “positive” sentiments.

[2] A song purported in urban legend to have caused many suicides.

chapters.title: Chapter 326: Transformative Characteristic Of Memes
chapters.body:
Chapter 326: Transformative Characteristic Of Memes

Chapter 326: Transformative Characteristic of Memes

“Meme is an independent noun. To put it in simple terms, it can be described as viruses in the form of messages which continuously mutates as it spreads. It has similarities to the MD5 code in transcoders or the DNA fragment in ribosomes. The spread of memes is essentially the spread of information.

“For example, someone who sees something funny will naturally share it with the person. This is the transmission process of memes. The meme begins to spread when the next person to hear the joke shares it again with someone new.

“Humans have a natural incentive to spread the information they collect. This is otherwise known as a ‘self-reward mechanism’. Simply put, spreading information brings people joy.

“To make themselves happier, people will gladly share something that may be entirely unrelated to themselves. Sometimes, they may even do so at their own expense, for example...”

“Celebrity worship.”

Little X’s voice could be heard passing through the speakers. With the research results in the Gantz dimension combined with applicable knowledge in the real world, Little X had almost figured out the mechanisms of the mysterious phenomenon.

She unloaded a stream of information to Chen Chen in hopes of providing more options for him when dealing with the mysterious phenomenon.

“Of course, the ‘self-reward mechanism’ of humans isn’t a product of the digital era. Even in villages where there’s simple gossip circulating around, local opinions also greatly differ from one another during ancient wartime. In the Yanhuang era, clans and tribes would exchange their agriculture and weapon craftsmanship knowledge. This dates as far back as the stone age when our ancestors preserved knowledge of building fire and crafting tools... This self-reward mechanism is deeply ingrained into human DNA. It was already present back when humans were still primates.

“Memes are viruses. They’re extremely contagious and prone to mutations while also being extremely hard to control. In this current era of rapidly developing internet platforms, memes spread almost effortlessly.

“In this era dominated by the Internet connecting people around the globe, memes can sustain themselves longer while also spreading at a larger scale. Combine this factor with the mutational characteristics of memes that closely resemble viruses, I wouldn’t be surprised if the Basilisk pattern eventually shows up in the real world in the future.”

Little X explained, “Because the concept of memes encompasses a multitude of things. It can be a simple word, a sentence, a line of thought, it could even be a color. Memes are messages.”

Chen Chen reflected thoughtfully on Little X’s summary. “In other words, to a certain extent, the Basilisk images are an extreme rendition of mutated memes. It’s this mutation that allows them to become agents of death, is that it?”

“That’s an apt summary, but it’s not completely true either.”

Little X explained, “Common messages by themselves shouldn’t be able to manifest itself physically to kill people. There should be another layer of mechanism that we’re unaware of that is working behind these messages and directly causing the death of its recipients. If we can find out the working theory behind it, we may even be able to manufacture memes that fit our specified purposes.”

“Are you suggesting memetic technology?”

This was an enthralling idea to Chen Chen. If he was able to somehow weaponize memetic technology, even nuclear weapons would have to take a backseat. Such technology could potentially wipe out entire civilizations effortlessly, even the technologically superior aliens could not withstand its might.

Unless the aliens possessed memetic technology in their arsenal as well.

Upon further contemplating it, Chen Chen shook his head, deciding that he was getting ahead of himself. If memetic technology was an essential aspect of modern civilization, then it made no sense that humans would equip it while aliens do not.

As it was, the reason that mankind had not been able to harness the potential of such technology was that such entities that posed a direct effect in the physical dimension had yet to appear in the real world.

Chen Chen frowned upon considering this. “That’s not right, if physical manifestations of memes didn’t exist in the past, then why are there so many ghost stories? Why is it that the products of the meme phenomenon are so similar to the ‘ghosts’ that humans have such a strong impression of?

“No, a better way to put it would be that it is these ghost stories that brought the memetic effect to fruition.”

Little X explained, “You can think of it as a back-and-forth interaction between the message mediums. In the battle between humans and viruses, humans continuously develop antibodies to combat the viruses, don’t viruses do the same thing as well? The motive of viruses isn’t to kill humans, they’re only doing it for reproduction and survival. Powerful viruses aren’t able to continuously spread. It’s the mild viruses that parasitize the human body and slowly fuse into one with humans to evolve together.

“It is the same for memetic effect. Perhaps it’s the idea of ghosts in olden times that pushes meme mediums in the direction of the ghost stories shared by people. Still, this is only a small sample as this doesn’t occur in most cases. For example, memetic effects such as ‘Ghost in the Window’ don’t shape itself to reflect the ancient ghost stories.

“After you exit the isolation zone, we can start conducting experiments on the earliest form of memes, which are the Basilisk patterns.”

Little X encouraged Chen Chen. “I believe that the secret of the memetic messages will be unraveled by us.”

“Hold on!”

It was then when Yoru Kamidera became startled and cut into their conversation. “Your Excellency, previously you mentioned that the memetic effect shouldn’t occur in this parallel dimension. In that case, are you not worried that researching these cursed things in this world will eventually plunge it into the same predicament as the other world?”

“There’s no need to worry about this.”

Little X explained, “I’ve already mentioned this before, the memetic effect could be said to be an essential part of technological development. Even if we don’t research it, it’ll eventually be discovered one way or another as we continue to advance scientifically. Besides, their mutation doesn’t occur spontaneously. Just like regular viruses, it can only mutate continuously when it spreads outward.

“In other words, the emergence of various variations of memes in your previous world is caused by them spiraling out of control on the Internet. As long as we contain the range of its spread, we can get a handle on the meme itself.”

“Isn’t there always the possibility of something going wrong?”

Yoru Kamidera appeared to be terrified of this thing. After finally getting away from the d*mnable world, he was about to witness Chen Chen and his crew researching the very thing responsible for his world’s destruction. He asked in a quaking voice, “What if something went wrong? Does that mean this world is doomed as well?”

“It’s unavoidable.”

Little X explained sheepishly, “Nuclear energy can lead to the destruction of mankind, but why does each country still insist on researching it? Antimatter is more dangerous than nuclear energy, has that ever stopped the major forces of the world from researching it? This is because they don’t only have the potential to destroy the world, they can also change the world and help mankind take the next step. It’s the same for memes, it’s not just a weapon. If utilized properly, it just might change our lives, just like the Gantz server machine.”

“Little X is right.”

Chen Chen nodded in agreement. “The journey toward the scientific progression of mankind had never been a smooth sail. Just because you choose to avoid these things doesn’t mean they will cease to exist. Yoru-kun, it’s time to change your attitude. You’re the first person I’ve ever brought back from an alternate dimension, do you know why I chose you?”

“Because of my profound experience in memetic subjects...”

Yoru Kamidera was drenched in sweat at this point. He quickly lowered his head as if he was surrendering. “I understand now. Please forgive me for my previous comments. Your Excellency has nothing to worry about, I’ll dedicate all my efforts to fulfilling your great vision!”

chapters.title: Chapter 327: Escape
chapters.body:
Chapter 327: Escape

Chapter 327: Escape

Chen Chen dedicated his next few days looking for information regarding memetic research to a point where he almost forgot that he was still afflicted by a memetic curse.

The consequence of that was rather resounding. It had been seven days since Chen Chen returned to the real world and he never heard any sudden thuds.

This was why Chen Chen requested Little X to shed several layers of reinforcement off the quarantine room, leaving all but a single layer. He remained inside for another three days.

Even after that, he did not hear any thuds.

With that, Chen Chen verified that the memetic effect known as “thuds” was truly purged. What purged it was none other than the dark passage of the USB drive.

This suggested that the USB drive did possess the ability to negate memetic effects, just like the previous observation of it obstructing the entry of living beings.

This was a sort of insurance the USB drive provided for its user.

Finally, a potential threat that may lead to Chen Chen’s death was averted. This was very likely the greatest predicament Chen Chen had been in ever since acquiring the USB drive.

Luck may have played a large part in the process, but at the end of the day, it was safe to say that Chen Chen’s risky venture was worthwhile.

Ten days after returning to the real world, both Chen Chen and Yoru Kamidera finally stepped out of the quarantine room.

Chen Chen filled his lungs with the fresh air of the Blacklight nuclear power plant and gazed at the warm sun above. He felt the tenseness exiting his sore muscles.

Chen Chen glanced at Yoru Kamidera who came out with him and nodded toward his direction. “Yoru-kun, welcome to my world. This Earth is completely identical to the Earth which you resided in!”

With that, Chen Chen turned around. “X, have a Black Knight prepare a room for him in the Spire laboratory. After implanting him with the ship, give him access privileges of a grade B research staff. While we’re at that, give him a week off as well.”

Chen Chen turned to Yoru Kamidera again and advised, “Use this week to get yourself some good rest. This is also a chance for you to experience the modern life which you have been away from for so long. After that, I’ll need you to start helping me.”

“Please don’t say that, Your Excellency Chen Chen. You were the one who saved my life and I’ve sworn my fealty to you. Also, if it pleases you, please address me directly by my first name,” Yoru Kamidera responded quickly.

“Excellent.”

Chen Chen nodded before turning to take his leave.

chapters.title: Chapter 328: Experiment
chapters.body:
Chapter 328: Experiment

Chapter 328: Experiment

The Black Knight stepped out after stashing the image into the drawer of the table.

After that, another four Basilisk patterns were printed. They were the “Reaper”, “Shaker”, “Filth”, and “Flame”.

Each one of these images was put away into their respective drawers in the table, each drawer contained only one image.

“X, we can begin the experiment now.”

Inside the laboratory, Chen Chen stood outside the quarantine zone with his arms folded and silently gazed inside the observation window.

“Copy that, dispatching research staff now. Grade Delta researcher codename ‘D00189’ has arrived at the experimental base.”

X-111’s (Spire Experimental Base artificial intelligence, one of the copies of Little X) voice was that of a little girl that carried an icy and mechanical quality. Immediately after X-111’s quick announcement, the side entrance to the quarantine zone suddenly shuffled open. A black man in his mid-thirties stepped in with a puzzled expression on his face.

As he was one of the Delta level researchers of God’s Forbidden Zone, he wore a research uniform of Delta level researchers. It was overall made with special-crafted fiber which kept the wearer warm and absorbed sweat. Its structure was also extremely resilient.

It was a gray uniform with many white stripes laid out horizontally across it and a large alphabet “D” was placed on the chest section of the uniform.

There was a metal hoop crafted with tungsten alloy strapped around the man’s neck. The width of the hoop was less than two centimeters, but its toughness and hardness were several times higher than that of titanium alloy.

The hoop was wrapped with a layer of velvet, which allowed the researchers to make any movements without feeling hindered by the device.

These metal hoops were the rightful badge of Delta level researchers. There was a liquid-crystal display on the ring displaying the codename of its wearer. It was also fitted with an electric shock device and anesthesia device. If the researcher disobeyed orders given, the electric shock device would force the researcher.

The anesthesia device made it possible to easily subdue researchers without hurting them.

There was also a GPS built into the metal hoop so even if a researcher went missing from the Spire laboratory, it would be easy to locate the missing member.

Finally, there was a self-destruct mechanism in the metal hoop. It would be triggered in the event that the metal hoop became damaged, whether it was done at high temperature, with a water jet or other forced methods. Its self-destruction would effectively kill the researcher wearing it as well.

At the present, the Ovambo[1] man surveyed the laboratory curiously. He was slightly restless, but not to the point of being in a state of panic. After all, members like him had been well treated for their entire stay here. In addition to the routine physical examinations, there was also a gym set up for them.

Compared to the outside world, this place was almost heaven on Earth, only minus the access to women and freedom.

At last, the man fixed his eyes onto the observation window toward Chen Chen’s direction.

This was not his first time being questioned. He approached the observation window and gently grazed it with his hand. From where he stood, he could only see the reflection on the clear mirror, unable to see Chen Chen standing behind the window.

“Excuse me, can I know what department this is? Am I going to be questioned here?”

After inspecting the mirror for a brief moment, the black man suddenly spoke.

“It’s not any department. Our company signed a Convict Reallocation Act with the Namibian government, so you’re now participating as an experiment subject.”

Chen Chen held the voice button to transmit his voice into the quarantine zone through the microphone. “It’s the contract that you signed previously. If you cooperate with us in this experiment, you have a chance to receive a reduced sentence.”

When the man heard this, he nodded half-suspiciously and moved to the only table in the quarantine zone. “In that case, officer, in what way can I cooperate with you guys?”

“There’s a picture in the rightmost drawer. I need you to take it out.”

Chen Chen gave a short reminder. “Please note that the picture is positioned upside down. You must make sure it stays that way when you pick it up, no peeking. I’ll give you further instructions after you do this.”

“Got it.”

The man shrugged and proceeded to yank open the drawer. After he placed the upside-down picture on the table, he put his hands in the air. “Officer, I’ve done as you asked.”

“Great job.”

Chen Chen said contentedly, “Next, I need you to answer several questions. First question, 189, has there been any genetic history of epilepsy or mental illness in your family?”

“No.”

“Have you ever experienced brain trauma or performed craniotomy?”

“Never.”

“Do you have any heart, liver or other kidney-related diseases?”

“None at all.”

The man frowned a little and stretched his hands out. “I swear, my body is very healthy and I have no underlying illnesses!”

“Alright. Next, I need you to flip over the picture on the table.”

Upon hearing this, the man flipped it over with mild irritation while also instinctively stealing a glance at its contents.

With just a glance, his pupils suddenly expanded rapidly like he had a spell cast on him, holding him in place.

“189?”

Chen Chen asked, “Can you hear me?”

However, this did not generate a response from the man. His head slowly dropped downward until he eventually lost his balance and fell on his back on the cold hard floor.

“His pulse has stopped.”

Little X informed. At the same time, a Black Knight stepped into the quarantine zone and squatted to feel for the person’s breath. The Black Knight only felt a tiny whiff of air flowing out of the man’s nasal cavity, which was followed by what was eternal stillness.

There was only air pouring out of the Ovambo man, but none flowing the other way around.

“He’s dead.”

After performing a series of routine inspections, Little X informed, “The brain wave activity in D00189 has been terminated along with his brain functionality. His heart has ceased to function as well. Even if we try to rescue him now, he can only be sustained in a vegetative state at best. In medical terms, he can be proclaimed as dead.”

“It seems like there’s no problem with the Basilisk picture itself.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Then, let’s begin the next experiment. Get the fire extinguishers ready. We’re going to try with ‘Flame’...”

After that, the Black Knight promptly got rid of the body of the first Delta grade research member before bringing in the next one.

Chen Chen performed experiments on each Basilisk picture.

The outcome of the experiments went just as they were expected to. After the research member looked at the picture named “Flame”, he immediately burst into flames. Furthermore, Chen Chen realized that the flames stemmed from inside the body of the subject. No matter how quickly they tried to put out the flames, there was no way for them to save the subject.

“Shaker” went just about as he expected as well. The subject who looked at the shaker image immediately suffered a symptom similar to being electrocuted. His body was trembling all over and he would shriek horribly in between his trembling.

The rate of this trembling effect was so volatile that it caused the subject to quickly experience symptoms of dehydration. Even after he was strapped onto a stretcher and had tranquilizers injected, the trembling still refused to stop. The nature of this symptom was a completely physical one. The subject died in the end due to exhaustion.

The subject who saw the “Reaper” image did not display any visible symptoms initially. It was only after Chen Chen asked what it was that he saw when the effect was triggered, which resulted in the subject dying immediately. It was an effect similar to the “Eye”.

The strangest one had to be the picture known as “Filth”.

[1] A South-African ethnic group

chapters.title: Chapter 329: Experiment Concluded
chapters.body:
Chapter 329: Experiment Concluded

Chapter 329: Experiment Concluded

The reason that “Filth” was regarded as the strangest mysterious phenomenon was that after the misfortunate research staff saw the picture, a black spot appeared around his ankle.

The black spot was only around the size of a fingernail. It may have gone unnoticed if not for Chen Chen performing a full body check on the research staff.

It was only after Chen Chen ordered the researcher to strip naked when the member complied unwillingly and discovered the inconspicuous black mark around his ankle.

The reason it was difficult to notice was due to the contrast of skin color. After all, 90% of the research staff Chen Chen hired in Namibia were black people whose skin tones were considered darkest among their peers.

This was not an issue of racism on Chen Chen’s part. In truth, Chen Chen could not care less about skin color. As far as he was considered, he just wanted to hire any researchers available on the market whether they were Caucasian, Asian or whatnot as it was all the same to him. It just happened to be that Namibia was a country largely populated by black people.

Back to the main topic at hand. Initially, after discovering the black spot on the Delta grade research staff codenamed D00253, the afflicted did not report any notable sensation. The black spot looked like powdery soot and upon touching it, it felt like a layer of black ash.

As time went on, the surface area of the black spot slowly expanded. At the same time, black spots began appearing on other parts of the subject’s body. Furthermore, the black spots seemed to seep through the body. After an hour, the total surface area of black spots on the subject’s body had expanded from the original one square centimeter to ten square centimeters.

There were three afflicted locations as well. They were the left ankle, right elbow, and left cheek.

It was around this point when something abnormal started to happen to the research member.

The skin around his ankle was already undergoing a state of carbonization, where the gentlest touch would yield a shallow layer of black ash. Simply by poking its surface with a finger, the subject could produce a piece of black ash from it.

After scooping out the black ash, a half-centimeter pit could be observed appearing in the region of the member’s ankles, leaving the flesh and bones underneath that have not yet been carbonized exposed.

However, there was no end to the carbonization effect. The process could not be contained even by washing away all the black ash around the afflicted region. Its spread could only be hindered slightly by carving out the flesh around the afflicted carbonized region, which would result in black spots eventually appearing on other parts of the body.

What was rather unusual was that the subject afflicted with this carbonization effect did not feel anything, not even the slightest tinge of pain.

At last, after fifteen long hours, the carbonization effect had spread all over the subject’s body. All of his limbs had broken off. The black spot had invaded into the deepest crevice of his brain as well. By then, the research member was pronounced dead and his life came to waste.

Yet, the carbonization effect did not cease just because the researcher was dead.

Twenty-four hours later, every single part of the corpse was completely carbonized. The entire body had turned into a loose pile of black ashes. If this was moved outside, the slightest breeze of wind would reduce it to nothingness.

Chen Chen did not stop even after concluding the experiment on the “Filth” Basilisk image. Based on the information compiled by Yoru Kamidera earlier, this “Filth” was different from the other Basilisk patterns. The symptoms it produced appeared to be contagious.

Therefore, Chen Chen had another research member A look at the “Filth” picture. After the member was infected, he had another research member B enter the quarantine zone to be in close proximity with research member A.

Chen Chen noticed that under the circumstance where the two do not make any physical contact, the black spot phenomenon did not seem to infect the other member.

The contagious effect could not be transmitted even if research member B touched the black ashes falling off of research member A’s body. It would appear that the only proper method of infection is by direct physical contact.

Additionally, this effect appeared to be sporadic. This meant that there was no guarantee that the “Filth” could be transmitted even if the healthy member made direct contact with the infected. There was what seemed to be a certain chance to negate the corrosive effects of “Filth”.

As the frequency of direct contact between the two people went up, the chances of being infected increased proportionally as well.

Little X conducted several experiments to tabulate the rate of infection caused by direct contact with “Filth” and concluded the infection rate was approximately 30%.

“Unlike the other patterns, the ‘Filth’ Basilisk pattern has a determined rate of infection. The characteristics it displays is closer to that of a ‘mysterious phenomenon’ than that of a Basilisk pattern.”

At the moment, Chen Chen was seated on the sofa, quietly observing everything going on behind the observation window. His expression was rather remote. “If we interpret the Basilisk patterns as the earliest form of manifestation of the memetic effect and the ‘mysterious phenomenon’ as the later stages of its manifestation, this places “Filth” right in the middle of this process. It has both the characteristics of the Basilisk pattern and the ‘mysterious phenomenon’.”

“That’s right, that’s what I think too.”

Little X responded, “Sir Godfather, how do you suppose we should break down these memetic effects? I can’t seem to find any relevant information in my database of human civilization that may help provide an insight into the memetic effect. This appears to be a completely unexplored field for humanity.”

“I wouldn’t go as far as to say it is completely unexplored, doesn’t the book The Selfish Gene written by the famous American scientist Richard Dawkins describe memetic concepts?”

Chen Chen smiled. “For now, we can only form surface-level hypotheses concerning memetic technology as mankind’s current understanding of this field is almost a completely blank slate. Still, we wouldn’t have to start from zero if we intend to study it, don’t forget we brought back the blueprint of the Gantz server machine as well as a language known as Everlasting, simply known as E language. Couldn’t we gain control over the Gantz server machine if you mastered the E language?”

“We can give it a shot!”

Little X became enthused upon hearing this. “You’re certainly right. I’ve gone through the data you returned with. The E language is far more sophisticated than any other programming languages while also being more refined. If I can rewrite my program in E language, I might even be able to make the Gantz server machine my new home!”

“That’s right, that’s what I was thinking as well.”

Chen Chen nodded. “If you can get a handle on the operational procedures of the Gantz to sever machine, our research of memetic effects will be made that much easier. At the very least, we won’t have to wander around cluelessly in the dark like this trying to find the right path.”

This conversation between Chen Chen and Little X marked the end of the first Basilisk pattern experiment. As for the more volatile experiments on the mysterious phenomenon, Chen Chen decided he was better off keeping his distance for now.

There had been no mysterious phenomenon occurring in the real world yet. Considering Chen Chen’s current understanding of memetic technology, he would prefer making sure things remain that way for now.

For Chen Chen’s current situation, there was no chance of him successfully combating any memetic effect. Should any memetic effect outbreak occur in the world, it would be as good as a death sentence for all of the world.

chapters.title: Chapter 330: Ambition And Transformation
chapters.body:
Chapter 330: Ambition And Transformation

Chapter 330: Ambition and Transformation

Instead of leaving the Spire Experimental Base after wrapping up the Basilisk pattern experiment, Chen Chen went into another laboratory.

It was a secret laboratory. The Spire Experimental Base by itself was already a secret laboratory, which made the existence of this current secret laboratory an utmost secret.

Inside the laboratory, more than a hundred life incubators were lined up in rows. Many of these life incubators were empty and unused, but a notable amount of them also contained a completely naked young man suspended inside.
