Both the appearance and physique of all these young men were remarkably identical and all of them looked like a direct copy of Chen Chen. The only difference was that they were basked inside the life incubator and devoid of any form of consciousness.

Such was the spectacle Chen Chen beheld in this very moment.

To achieve the result displayed by Alice in the movie, Chen Chen launched his absolute secret project here — the “T-virus customization” project.

“To prevent running out of experiment subjects, I made another hundred cloned copies of you. We only used six of them before producing this final complete product... Sir Godfather, please appraise it.”

Little X’s voice could be heard through the earpiece. At the same time, a Black Knight approached with a spiral test tube in hand. The solution inside the test tube was a translucent azure blue like a shining crystal. It was a captivating sight to behold.

“This is the final product, T-009.”

“Which means that this is the ninth version?”

Chen Chen received the solution and squinted his eyes to inspect it. “This tube of T-virus can make me superhuman?”

“Theoretically, yes.”

Chen Chen was not completely certain of it but proceeded to explain anyway, “Compared to the previous half-finished products, the T-009 virus is refined to its utmost limit. A total of 15 RNA errors have been rectified and it should have perfect compatibility with your genes.”

As Little X said this, another Black Knight wearing a large lab coat approached with a stretcher. “Please take a look, this is clone 009, the latest successful subject.”

Chen Chen turned around to approach it upon hearing this. He saw a young man who was a direct copy of himself bound to the stretcher with belts. This young man seemed to be in a dazed state. He was glancing around all directions and smiled at Chen Chen when they made eye contact.

There was a sense of innocence in that smile, one that Chen Chen never remembered having himself.

“His current stage of brain development is equivalent to that of a completely newborn baby.”

Little X explained, “To verify that the T-virus doesn’t negatively affect the host’s consciousness, we kept the brain of the clones intact during the process. We made sure to chart down their electroencephalogram before injecting them with the T-009. Based on our previous findings, we concluded that their thoughts and consciousness are completely unaltered by the injection.”

“That’s good news.”

Chen Chen nodded. “What about the results of the experiment?”

“The T-009 virus fused perfectly with the DNA of clone number 9. Without having his physique directly altered, his strength, agility, never response speed, muscle tissue strength, and immunity strength are all doubled. If he goes through systemic training, there’s no chance of him losing to any T-version Black Knights!”

Chen Chen picked up the experiment statistical report beside him and went through it briefly. “In that case, let’s begin the final experiment.”

With that, he raised his head and looked at the arrangement of hundreds of life incubators. “Extract all of these clones and inject the T-009 into them. I’d like to see what the success rate of T-009 looks like.”

“That’s a wise move.”

Little X agreed. After that, the Black Knights inside the laboratory got to work.

After that, hundreds of “himself” were taken out of the life incubators one after another and put onto stretchers.

These clones were already injected with immunosuppressive drugs like sirolimus, cyclosporine A, and FK506 half a month ago. Therefore, the current immunity efficiency of the cloned bodies had long been suppressed.

The cloned bodies with compromised immunity structure were perfect research subjects for the T-virus.

Before the clones had time to regain consciousness, they were promptly strapped onto stretchers. Little X began making records of the electroencephalogram of the cloned bodies so that they could be compared against the electroencephalogram readings after injecting the T-virus.

The chances of this occurring were low, but if an alteration of recorded brain waves was noted, then it would imply that this drug designed to revamp the condition of the human body carried a certain degree of side effects. Chen Chen would have to tread carefully if he intended to use it for himself.

With there being hundreds of subjects, the experiment progress went by extremely slowly. Little X went ahead and reassigned 25 additional Black Knights as laboratory assistants. With this, the laboratory was operating at full capacity. With Little X’s given orders, all of the clones of Chen Chen were separated into three batches and all of them were injected with the latest variant of the T-0009 virus.

Similar to the previous conduct, a large amount of supplement glucose and carbohydrates were injected along with the virus. This was done to prevent the clones from dying from malnutrition before they could even succumb to the effects of T-virus during the process of genetic modification.

All that was left to do now was to wait.

One hour, two hours, three hours...

The clock began to tick away. Soon, the clones had survived for 24 hours. Not only were all of them still alive, but there were also no abnormalities in their vitals detected either.

The vitality of each of the clones began improving rapidly.

From where Chen Chen stood, he could see the skin on the clones beginning to shed. As layers of skin began falling off, they were replaced by a new layer of smoother, delicate skin.

At the same time, the muscles of the clones that were originally loose in structure began tensing up at such a rate that it was visible to the naked eye. As more nutrition was injected, the energy was subsequently extracted from these nutritions by the new genes inside the cloned bodies. The microscopic stature of their bodies was constantly morphing...

Although there was no visible change in their physique, the weight of the clones was steadily increasing. This was caused by an increase in density in muscle and bone structure.

After 36 hours had passed, the first batch of clones had successfully braced the infection progress of the T-0009 virus and allowed themselves to be completely fused into one with the T-virus.

48 hours later, the second batch of clones was reported to have successfully fused with the T-009 virus as well.

After 60 hours, the final batch of clones yielded successful results as well. In all of the 93 clones, not a single failure was reported.

“100 percent success rate...”

A brilliant spark of joy appeared in Chen Chen’s eyes when he saw the results. “X, you deserve all the credit for the unqualified success of this project!”

“Sir Godfather, this is all a part of my due responsibility...”

Little X sounded a little embarrassed, even her voice trailed off as she said this.

“Initially, I figured it was worth a shot if there was at least a 90 percent success rate, I never expected that you’d bring me such a welcome surprise!”

Chen Chen laughed. “By the way, could you show me how much the total expense for developing a T-virus compatible with my genes is?”

“It has been ten months, five days, and eight hours since the launch of the project. Factoring in general cost and material cost, the total expenses come to 350 million U.S. dollars will be spent according to the Namibian market price.”

“That doesn’t sound half bad at all. What about after factoring in labor cost and time-cost?” Chen Chen asked.

Little X replied, “That’s difficult to calculate and we can only go by rough estimations. To research the T-009 virus, we assumed priority over a lot of resources originally designated for other projects. This caused some of the project’s progress to be delayed or result in a failure. Including labor, time, and the losses of some of the other projects, we probably invested somewhere in the range of 10 billion to 15 billion U.S. dollars...”

Chen Chen nodded. He pensively picked up a test tube containing T-009 inside it and stared at the glowing blue liquid inside and could not help but chuckle sensibly.

“Tens of billions of dollars in capital investment is enough to help you trump over any moderately-sized pharmaceutical organization. Only the giants like Pfizer and Johnson & Johnson can try to put together the funds to tackle the creation of a new drug.

“Sadly, there are countless board members behind these pharmaceutical giants. It wouldn’t be sensible to exhaust all of the manpower and resources of a company just for the benefit of one person. This means that so far, I’m the only one in the entire world who has the access and rights to do such a thing. This privilege of having it all to myself...”

Chen Chen could not hold himself back from taking in a large breath with a smile slowly surfacing on the corner of his mouth. “It’s pretty good!”

With that, Chen Chen turned around. “Let’s begin, X. I cannot be any more thrilled to have more power soon!”

...

Half a month later.

Chen Chen stood inside a laboratory, naked from the waist up. This was a completely disinfected laboratory where other than Chen Chen, all of the Black Knights were wearing protective suits to avoid polluting the laboratory environment.

Chen Chen’s skin was a pale white sheet. His expression frail and soulless, even his hair had lost some of its shine and turned yellow. He had lost a considerable amount of weight over the past two weeks.

This was the consequence of an extended period of injection of various immunosuppressive drugs.

The drugs had heavily damaged Chen Chen’s body. His trained physique developed from him taking the NZT-48 had completely wasted away. He had lost a significant amount of mass.

However, Chen Chen did not care about any of that.

“Sir Godfather, the immune system of your body had reached the desired potency, but your body itself...”

“It’s alright, my body can still hold on.”

Chen Chen waved the suggestion away before asking at the end, “Are the facial surgeries on my clones complete yet?”

“Yes, all of the clones have gone through the appropriate facial surgeries. I can guarantee that appearance-wise, they don’t hold any resemblance to you.”

Little X answered, “Also, these clones display phenomenal learning ability. After their faces have developed properly, we can begin hiring expert-level teachers to start teaching them the fundamentals so that they can grow into the elite research team desired.”

Chen Chen nodded faintly.

This was the solution Chen Chen came up with after giving the matter heavy consideration.

At the end of the day, these are his contemporaries. If they turn out to be unintelligent Black Knights then so be it. The chip could still be implanted to them and they could pass as Black Knights.

However, things became more complicated if they turned out to be intelligent clones.

This was why Chen Chen came up with this idea.

First, have these clones go through facial reconstruction surgery to change their appearance. After that, tutors would be enlisted to pass on all sorts of knowledge to them so that they could become fine researchers which Chen Chen was in short supply of.

Of course, Chen Chen would not allow them to be involved in matters of the military concerning civilians where Chen Chen drew the line.

After the short conversation with Little X, Chen Chen approached one of the life incubators and began climbing the ladder beside it.

Chen Chen stood on the edge of the life incubator and took a deep breath before hopping into it.

The inside of the incubator was empty. There was no water or any other substances. After Chen Chen had hopped inside, a mechanical arm quickly swooped down from the ceiling and fitted the hatch over the life incubator.

There were several slots built into the hatch with various pipes passing through them. These pipes included respirators as thick as a fist as well as gigantic nutrition injection needles. In one swift motion, Chen Chen put the respirator around his mouth and took several breaths before signaling a thumbs up for Little X outside the life incubator.

After that, Chen Chen picked up several needle heads and inserted them into the veins on both of his arms.

After completing all these steps, large volumes of faint yellow liquid began flowing into the life incubator. The solution was maintained at an average temperature of 36.5 degrees which was ideal for the human body.

The faint yellow growth medium slowly submerged Chen Chen over time. By the time it was done, Chen Chen had his eyes shut and was completely submerged in the growth medium. His body soon began to float automatically inside the growth medium until he was suspended in the middle of it.

Chen Chen subconsciously curled up with his hands around his knees, a posture similar to that of a fetus in a mother’s womb....

“Sir Godfather...”

Little X seemed to have said something in a low tone but as it is, all Chen Chen could hear was muffled buzzing sounds.

However, Chen Chen seemed to understand what Little X was troubled about. First, he shook his head and soon followed it up with a gentle nod before finally becoming idle.

With that, Little X stopped hesitating. Chen Chen soon felt a chilling sensation coursing through his left arm. It seemed as if something was flowing along the tubes and into his blood.

Chen Chen knew that it was the T-virus. Only a couple millimeters of it was enough to drive human civilization into the ground.

From here on, time slowly passed him by...

Several hours later, the Black Knights slowly went out of the laboratory, completely sealing it afterward. All of the lights inside the laboratory slowly dimmed out as it was sealed as if afraid of disturbing the slumber of the man inside.

There was nothing but dead silence inside the laboratory Chen Chen resided in. The faint glow of the electronic display of various machines seemed to magnify the darkness around it and added to the desolate ambient.

Amid all this, the silhouette of Chen Chen could be made out through the glass of the life incubator. He was curled up into a ball and his eyes were closed peacefully, resembling the form of a fetus inside the life incubator. Occasionally, his chest would rise following the tempo of his breathing.

The atmosphere outside the laboratory was the direct opposite of the quiet ambience inside as several hundreds of Black Knights were posted around the Spire Experimental Base. The orders they received were direct — stop any members of the Spire Experimental Base who disobeyed the warnings and attempted to approach the laboratory Chen Chen was residing in. In drastic situations, the use of lethal forces was permitted.

Meanwhile, Little X already had all of the entrances to the Spire Experimental Base sealed tightly. All of the research team personnel inside the Spire Experimental Base were closely monitored at all times as well. Should there be any abnormal activity detected, a crew of Black Knights would be immediately dispatched to apprehend the culprit.

The scientists inside the Spire Experimental Base were oblivious of what was going on, but they sensed the atmosphere inside the laboratory growing thicker and heavier as if there was a dark cloud hanging over their heads. No matter what they did, they made sure to pay extra caution...

chapters.title: Chapter 331: I Saw You
chapters.body:
Chapter 331: I Saw You

Chapter 331: I Saw You

“The human lifespan has its limits. Everyone dies of old age or diseases eventually. This is a part of natural law that humans cannot escape from, but does anyone know why humans experience this phenomenon of aging and dying of diseases?”

On the podium, the youthful-looking Professor Wang Xi spoke persuasively, his voice echoing in the empty large classroom.

Below the stage, Chen Chen suddenly jolted back to his senses. He realized that he may have accidentally dozed off.

It was an extremely long nap that seemed to have lasted ages.

Only, he could no longer recall the dream he had during the nap.

“As we all know, the measuring unit in life is a cell. All of us here are the product of the fission of a tiny zygote. This effect splits it into two, then four, then eight...”

Professor Wang Xi endlessly went on with his presentation on the podium. Meanwhile, the disinterested Chen Chen simply held his chin with one hand and spun a gel pen on the other, all while gazing at the projection screen with his mind wandering.

“Clack!”

It was then when Chen Chen’s finger must have suddenly slipped and sent the gel pen in his darting outward.

Seeing this, Chen Chen naturally bent down and reached for the ground.

“Hm?”

When he craned his head below the table, he suddenly noticed that there was nothing but a sheet of pitch-black darkness under the table.

All of the light had been drained from this darkness. There was not even the slightest hint of reflection nor was there the presence of any other substance. It was something darker than the night itself.

An unplaceable feeling quickly came to Chen Chen, he somehow felt that this picture was both familiar and a stranger to him all at the same time.

As Chen Chen looked at the darkness beneath the table, his mind began wandering to places.

Just as Chen Chen was stuck in this trance-like state of confusion, a bloody eyeball suddenly floated to the surface of the darkness, immediately making eye contact with Chen Chen.

This startled Chen Chen. He subconsciously stretched his arms out to do something but stopped as soon as he extended his arms outward.

What was he trying to do again,

He simply could not recall...

Chen Chen stared back into the thick mist of darkness. It was then when he realized there was no such thing as a bloody eye in the first place. There was nothing at all under the table, nothing but emptiness...

The cloud of confusion inside Chen Chen’s mind was growing thicker. If there was nothing at all under the table, why did he bend down?

Oh, right. To pick up his pen.

Just as Chen Chen made to get down to pick up his pen, the clear voice of Professor Wang Xi suddenly came from the direction of the podium. “That student over there digging under the table, why don’t you answer...”

“Pfft!”

Chen Chen’s dorm buddy beside him produced a loud snicker.

Chen Chen jumped and immediately shot up from under the table. When he looked to the podium, he saw a pair of bloodshot eyes growing out of Professor Wang Xi’s face, staring right at him...

Those eyes were both cold and hollow, the sort of expression that simply could not be produced by any living person. With just a momentary glance, a strange sort of numbness akin to an electric shock surged through Chen Chen’s body. He felt as if all the blood flowing through his body had frozen.

Just as Chen Chen felt this sharp pang in his heart and tried to focus on what was in front of him again, he saw Professor Wang Xi and his always kind expression. It seemed like what he saw just a moment ago was all in his head.

“This student over here, can you tell me what is the complete name of this theory?”

Professor Wang Xi asked again with the patience of a saint.

“It’s... That...”

Chen Chen could not control his stutter as he spoke.

“Lil Chen, Lil Chen, over here!”

Right then, his buddy beside him whispered to him in a low voice. He sneakily shifted his book to Chen Chen’s table and tapped on one of the sentences printed in the book.

Chen Chen silently heaved a sigh of relief when he saw this and shot a gratuitous look at his friend. When he turned his attention to the book, he saw the text on the book packed together densely and squirmed altogether like a pack of ants moving around ever so slowly.

[The American microbiologist Leonardo Hayflick published the Hayflick limit theory in 1965. This theory states that after 56 divisions, cells in the human body will break down and die due to self-produced toxins. “In essence, this ’56 times’ interval is the limit of human cell division and of the body’s metabolic cycle...]

“Ha- hay... flick...”

Chen Chen instinctively craned his neck lower to try to get a better look at the book. He was surprised to see that when he had done that, the passage seemed to have a few more strange words mixed into it...

[The American microbiologist Leonardo Hayflick published the Hayflick I Saw You in 1965. This theory states that after 56 divisions, cells in the human body will break down and die due to self-produced toxins. “In essence, this ’56 times’ interval is the limit I Saw You and of the body’s metabolic cycle...]

“What the hell?”

Chen Chen frowned. He began to think there was something wrong with his eyes and squinted as hard as he could to read the text properly. That was when the text started to become stranger.

[The American micro I Saw You Hayflick published the Hayflick I Saw You in 1965. This theory states that after 56 divisions, I Saw You human body will I Saw You and die due to self-produced toxins. “In essence, this ’56 I Saw You I Saw You I Saw You, I Saw You I Saw You I Saw You...]

Before he knew it, the entire textbook had the five words pasted all over the pages —

[I Saw You I Saw You I Saw You I Saw You I Saw You...]

Chen Chen felt an indescribable ominous feeling rapidly crawling up from the bottom of his spine. It made its quick, continuous ascend along every inch of his spine until it seeped into his head at last. That was when Chen Chen felt as if his head was going to burst open!

I saw you?

Who, who saw me?

Chen Chen wanted to howl but when he opened his mouth. He found that his voice could not come out as if something was caught in his throat. When he slowly raised his head, he saw everyone in the class including his friend and Professor Wang Xi were staring right at him.

They all wore a pair of blood-red eyes...

[I Saw You!]

“Pfft!”

Chen Chen snapped his eyes open without warning. He immediately spat out the respirator in his mouth. As soon as he did that, large volumes of salty amniotic fluid poured into his mouth!

Chen Chen let out a muffled scream as if he was trying to unleash all of the pent-up rage boiling inside him. He raised his right hand and viciously hammered the tempered glass separating himself from the world outside the life incubator!

“Crack!”

It produced a sharp and harsh cracking sound. In an instant, the five-centimeters-thick tempered glass exploded into countless pieces and scattered into every direction, immediately accompanied by the amniotic fluid inside the incubator bursting out like a flash flood!

An immeasurable amount of amniotic fluid and broken glass shards scattered in all directions. Chen Chen emerged out of it and collapsed on the floor in a half-kneeling position. He coughed violently, pumping out a large amount of amniotic fluid from his lungs and stomach with each repeated cough.

“Sir Godfather!”

Little X’s voice full of concern immediately took over the speaker system. All of the lights inside the laboratory were immediately flashed open as several Black Knights charged into the laboratory and wrapped a blanket around Chen Chen.

“Scram!”

The look in Chen Chen’s eyes called for blood, even if just for a split instant. He violently shoved the Black Knight closest to him and in an instant, the 180 centimeters tall, stockily built Black Knight weighing more than 90 kilograms practically flew off!

“Bang!”

After slamming into a life incubator, the Black Knight slid down along its surface. Behind him, several visible radial cracks were left on the incubator,

“Chen Chen, calm down. It’s me, X.”

Little X’s voice came again, this time there was an anxious tone in her voice.

Chen Chen finally stopped when he heard this. As the red in his eyes slowly faded away, he knelt on the ground and looked around with haze in his eyes, seemingly trying to recall something.

Chen Chen remained that way until Little X called out to him several more times. That was when clarity finally returned to his eyes. He raised his head and scanned the laboratory, then he lifted his right palm and looked into it.

It was only then when Chen Chen noticed that the texture of his skin had turned into a fine layer of what seemed like gelatin. There was not a single pore to be seen.

On top of that, he felt as if his body was floating as he moved. There seemed to be an explosive force lurking inside him. The physique of his body had also improved from his previous frail and bony state to being well-proportioned. His firm abdominal muscles and biceps became accentuated without him needing to exert any effort.

“It seems like the experiment was a success.”

Chen Chen had finally completely recollected himself. As he spoke, a smile slowly surfaced on his lips, “Sorry, X, I acted a little too rash just now.”

“Sir Godfather, don’t ever give me a scare like this again!”

Little X expressed her concern. “I even thought that the experiment was a failure and you were going to turn into a zombie!”

“That won’t happen.”

Chen Chen made a waving gesture, his expression turning pensive as he did this. “I think the reason I acted that way was due to a dream I had, inside the dream...”

Arriving at this note, Chen Chen could not help but frown. “I forgot what the dream was about.”

“No need to dwell on it since you already forgot it!”

Little X commented nonchalantly, “Oh, would you like a mirror? What you see may come off as a pleasant surprise for you!”

“Mirror?”

Chen Chen was slightly taken aback by the comment. A Black Knight quickly approached with a large standing mirror and set it in front of Chen Chen.

Chen Chen’s appearance in the mirror did not seem to have taken on a stark transformation from his previous self. However, perhaps as a result of having his genes modified, the general outline of his face became more defined and textured. Complemented by a pair of perfectly dark pupils, there was now an unplaceable aura surrounding Chen Chen who used to have an otherwise average appearance.

On top of that, Chen Chen noticed that he had grown significantly taller. Before this, his measured height was the standard 1.75 meters, but now he had suddenly grown to 1.8 meters.

If something had to be said to appraise the aura the new Chen Chen gave off, it would be — the stone-cold temperament of a powerful president.

Emm...

Upon witnessing himself, Chen Chen could not hold himself back from nodding and commending himself, “I never thought the day would come when the good old loser Chen Chen would become tall, rich, and handsome.”

“What sort of nonsense are you spouting!”

Upon hearing this, Little X suddenly puffed angrily. “After undergoing the genetic modification of the T-virus, you’re no longer purely human. I’d even say there’s now a component of reproduction isolation separating you from common humans now!”

Chen Chen’s expression turned stiff upon hearing this as he asked with shock in his tone, “Are you trying to scare me now? Although I did receive the modification of the T-virus, the difference between my DNA and original human DNA shouldn’t exceed one percent, right? I doubt reproduction isolation would occur from such an inconsequential difference.”

“The same could be said for tigers and lions, orcas and dolphins or horses and donkeys. The genetic difference between these species is less than one percent as well.”

Little X explained, “The results? Reproduction between lions and tigers can only result in abominations like ligers. Orcas and dolphins mating together produce wholphins while donkeys and horses mating together produce mules. It’s widely acknowledged in the scientific field that creatures like ligers, wholphins, and mules are non-fertile. This is due to the chromosomal differences between the two parent species which results in the offspring not being able to form normal gametes. Therefore, their offspring cannot reproduce.”

After saying this, Little X let out a long sigh and expressed regretfully. “Under these circumstances, despite both of the parent genes mutually appearing in their offspring, the lineage cannot be continued from that point on and will simply end there. Nothing good will come out of you reproducing with another human!”

“...”

Chen Chen did not feel like prolonging this debate with Little X. It was not an everyday occurrence that Little X would be so stubborn. He decided to leave it at that and switched the topic. “Let’s do a blood test. I want to see how my DNA has transformed and if it’s the same as the Black Knights. This is the only thing that matters now.”

“Understood.”

Following Chen Chen’s orders, a Black Knight approached with a change of clothes for Chen Chen while another Black Knight came with a disposable syringe. Ten millimeters of blood was promptly drawn from Chen Chen’s wrist.

The needle head of the syringe was a reinforced variant. This was done because the skin of someone who had undergone genetic modification of the T-virus tended to be tougher than that of ordinary humans. Normal needles would easily twist or break upon injection.

After having his blood drawn, Chen Chen paid close attention to his wrist. He could see the mark left behind by the needle closing in on itself at a rate that could be easily observed with the naked eye. Soon, all that was left was a tiny red dot.

“This recovery speed is very similar to the ones displayed in the Black Knights.”

Chen Chen nodded contentedly. This blood test was just a safety precaution, after all. In the less than one percent chance event that something went wrong with the fusing process with the T-virus, the potency of the T-virus would have turned him into a zombie.

The fact that Chen Chen was standing here in one piece was a testament to the success of the experiment.

After that, Chen Chen began evaluating his strength, speed, and overall physique.

The process was the same as when they conducted similar tests on the Black Knights. The results were surprisingly identical to the results yielded from the Black Knights as well.

In terms of speed, Chen Chen’s sprinting speed shot up from 30 kilometers per hour to 38 kilometers per hour, which was 9.55 seconds per 100 meters. This had exceeded the current human world record of 9.58 seconds.

It should be noted that this was before Chen Chen had undergone the appropriate training.

Upon undergoing several months of physical training, Chen Chen should be able to further improve his speed to forty kilometers per hour.

Endurance-wise, Chen Chen could sprint up to 500 meters without any notable decrease in speed. This may not be an astounding amount, but it was still three times the endurance of an average person. Furthermore, this attribute could be further improved with training.

Half a day later, Chen Chen’s DNA profile was complete. Chen Chen’s chromosome number remained unchanged at 23 pairs while the differential between Chen Chen’s DNA and ordinary human DNA has dropped to 99.1%.

This meant that Chen Chen was still considered a human, albeit a genetically mutated human.

Alternatively, a better way to put it would be that Chen Chen belonged to a new subspecies of human beings...

chapters.title: Chapter 332: Preparation For Building Gantz
chapters.body:
Chapter 332: Preparation For Building Gantz

Chapter 332: Preparation for Building Gantz

The success of the T-virus fusion also represented the second step in Chen Chen’s venture to become a supernatural being.

Needless to say, his first step was the crystalline.

Speaking of which, there was a concern of conflict occurring from the two genes interacting with each other that may result in some unknown mutation. To address this issue, Chen Chen specifically had a T-virus fused clone exposed to several crystallines as an experiment.

The experiment yielded successful results. Both the T-virus and crystalline did not pose any effect on each other.

Time passed quickly. It was now the second day since Chen Chen had awakened from his slumber in the life incubator.

Chen Chen was in the Field training ground. There were four almost completely transparent poker cards hovering around him, like four little spirits bouncing up and down, circling and dancing around him adorably.

Chen Chen closed his eyes and used the Field extending outward from his body to detect his surroundings.

It was then when a group of flies was released from their cage by one of the Black Knights. They started patrolling around the Field training around, buzzing everywhere they went. When one of the flies flew nearby Chen Chen, its flight was suddenly cut short!

The fly softly clapped against the cold hard floor. Upon closer inspection, it was noted that it was sliced in half. The cut was extremely clean and delicate as if it had been cut by an extremely sharpened sword in the air!

After that, several other flies approached Chen Chen as well. Before they could realize what was going on, they were sliced in half and dropped onto the floor, their lives ended before they could react.

Chen Chen decided that he was going to start walking around the training grounds to find the flies himself. Whenever the flies passed by a five meters radius around Chen Chen, a near-transparent poker card would glide right by it.

Moments after the poker card swept past the flies, the flies would be split in half.

It was only after all of the flies inside the training ground had met their ill fate when Chen Chen finally opened his eyes again. When he saw all the dead flies on the ground, he smiled.

“The radius of Field has extended to five meters.”

Chen Chen muttered to himself, “Which means that as long as there are no obstacles in the way, I can detect most substances in a five-meter radius around me. This sensory device is sharper and more precise than regular sight. Its characteristics are closer to the sense of touch...

“The only gripe is that the potency of Field hasn’t increased.”

After completing the training session, the four poker cards swept back into the tin box in Chen Chen’s hands without missing it even by an inch, like birds returning to their nest.

After experimenting with Field again, Chen Chen concluded that his potency in Field remained unchanged after fusing with the T-virus. However, there was a significant improvement in his precision of control over Field.

With Field, he could sense the most trivial detail within a five-meter radius, including the finest strand of hair on a person’s body. In turn, he could apply pressure on it using Field.

Chen Chen figured that he should be able to acquire all of the crystallines soon enough.

This process of absorbing crystalline fragments had extended over more than a year at this point. Chen Chen started with extracting several pieces, soon moving on to extracting a couple of dozens of them. Last year, he had extracted five hundred pieces all in one go. To this point, the sum of Chen Chen’s absorbed radiation was roughly a little over half of the amount absorbed by the protagonist in the movie.

Yes, only half.

Previously, when Chen Chen was preparing to film the movie, he had already made the appropriate measurements. The measured volume of the crystallines shaped like sea urchins was in the range of 3.5 to 4 cubic meters while the volume of each crystalline fragment was measured at 0.001 cubic meters.

This meant that for Chen Chen to absorb an entire crystalline, he would need to extract more than three thousand fragment pieces.

Inside the movie, there were three people exposed to the radiation of the crystalline, which meant that the radiation was evenly distributed between the three of them. Following that logic, Chen Chen had only absorbed half of the amount which the protagonist had absorbed since the protagonist would have absorbed roughly a thousand crystalline fragments worth of radiation.

Chen Chen speculated that the body would slowly adapt and build up immunity against this sort of gene-altering radiation. This meant it would be impossible to stack the effect infinitely since that would be too ridiculous.

The only problem was figuring out what the limit threshold was.

With that said, Chen Chen was not going to be able to acquire the remaining half of the crystalline fragments over the coming period.

After entering Gantz, Chen Chen had used up the greater portion of power deposited in the USB drive. He even left behind the PK Armor and Bugatti Spaceship in that dimension.

It was going to take some time to obtain enough power to help him instantiate crystalline fragments.

The good news was that the Spire Experimental Base was now in the process of researching a newer generation inertial fusion reactor. Taking notes from the first generation reactor, the efficiency of the second generation was expected to increase up to seven-folds. Its power generation should reach up to 2 million kWh, which would put it on par with Chen Chen’s nuclear plant.

Though claiming to have unlimited energy would be an exaggeration, at least Chen Chen could instantiate some tools like the PK Armor or crystalline fragments without hurting his reserves as much.

Currently, Chen Chen heavily prioritized the research efforts of the E language as well as the collection of germanium metal.

Germanium metal was very valuable in modern industry. It had extensive and important applications in semiconductors, aerospace measurement and control, nuclear physics detection, optical fiber communications, infrared optics, solar cells, chemical catalysts, biomedicine, and various other fields. Simply put, it was an important strategic resource.

It was also used as a catalyst for alloy pretreatment, in the electronics industry, and the optical industry.

On the international market, germanium metal was priced at approximately 1,000 U.S. dollars per kilogram. It was not incredibly pricey, but it was not particularly cheap either. If Chen Chen wanted to build a Gantz server machine as large as the one he saw in the Gantz dimension, he would need approximately a thousand tons of germanium metal, which came to a billion U.S. dollars.

There were no cutting corners around the construction of the Gantz server machine as it must be a completely solid structure. Its effects could only be exerted if the shape and the texture on its surface were accurate on a nanometer scale.

The only notable challenge to this infrastructure was the Gantz satellite and Gantz sphere serving as relay stations. It was only after the server was set up and connected to the relay station when Chen Chen could transmit humans or other substances between the Gantz sphere and a targeted area marked by the Gantz satellite.

Still, with each of the consortiums across the European and American Continents backing him, it should not pose too much issue for him.

Chen Chen wiped the sweat off his forehead and turned around to leave the Field training ground. Little X continuously reported to him via the earpiece, “We don’t have to acquire germanium metal only by using official commerce channels. After all, this volume of purchase nearly exceeds the total annual production of germanium metal and draws too much attention. If possible, we can try mining for them ourselves.”

Chen Chen stayed silent to let Little X finish.

“Besides, there are two marked germanium deposits right here in Africa. They are located in the Rainbow Nation and Angola respectively. If we can purchase these germanium mines, half of the raw material supply problem is solved...”

Chen Chen nodded thoughtfully upon hearing this advice of Little X. “This is a decent idea. It may take more time, but is the safer choice in the long run. Besides, our crew of Black Knights is increasing...”

“In this case, inform Old Qian about this and have him send someone to discuss this with the two governments.”

chapters.title: Chapter 333: Opposing Organization Of Blacklight Biotechnology
chapters.body:
Chapter 333: Opposing Organization Of Blacklight Biotechnology

Chapter 333: Opposing Organization of Blacklight Biotechnology

“We at Blacklight Biotechnology would like to offer 120 million U.S. dollars to purchase the germanium mining rights in the county of Gaborone.”

A man whose complexion was a poor yellow and seemed utterly exhausted was seated at a negotiation table, looking proud as he spoke. Next to him, there was a professional negotiation team comprised of translators, secretaries, legal consultants, and other professionals.

This man was Wang Liang, one of the original members that came with Chen Chen back when Chen Chen had just arrived in Africa.

Perhaps it was due to how capable he was, Qian Wenhuan would always bury him with a mountain worth of tasks. Now, Wang Liang had finally become one of the senior members of Blacklight Biotechnology. This time, he was dispatched by Qian Wenhuan and put in charge of the acquisition of the germanium mine in Rainbow Nation.

The party on the other end of the negotiation table was none other than the Ministry of Mining and Energy of Rainbow Nation.

Wang Liang kept up a proud front as he waited for the translator to translate his message to the officials. He made sure to emphasize, “This is our highest offer.”

The group on the other end of the table discussed among themselves for a while before responding. Soon, the leading official who was a black man replied in fluent English, “Sorry, I’m afraid we cannot agree to this price.”

“What? Is it because it’s not enough?”

Wang Liang frowned and became slightly flustered. “Do you know what the international market value for germanium is? A thousand U.S. dollars per kilogram. The germanium deposit in Gaborone has 300 tons of proven resources, which is 300 thousand kilograms. Even if we excavate all of them, they are only worth 300 million U.S. dollars.

“Now, doesn’t excavation cost money? What about refining them? Doesn’t transporting them cost money as well? Germanium metals are already a rare resource on their own. They are extremely difficult to excavate and not any easier to refine them. By the time the process is complete, the total cost expended is pretty much on par with the international market price. If you expect us to pay more, we’d rather just head straight to the international market!”

Despite his questionable attitude, Wang Liang was more than competent on the business end of things. He had clearly presented all the positive and negative aspects of germanium ore mining without leaving them room for any nitpicking.

However, there was no telling just what the opposing party was concerned about. After another round of fervent discussion among themselves, the officials still gave the same answer, “Sorry, we still can’t accept this price, we’re going to need at least 150 million U.S. dollars!”

“What!”

Wang Liang became so enraged that he slammed the table with all his might and shot up. “I’m guessing you’re not planning to sell then! In that case, let’s go!”

With that, he left the table and furiously stomped out of the conference room with his crew trailing behind him.

Upon stepping out of the town hall, Wang Liang spat on the floor and exclaimed, “Bunch of uncivilized b*stards!”

“Director Wang, it’s not worth getting upset about these people!”

One of the subordinates quickly stepped forward and produced a high-quality cigar for Wang Liang. This was quickly followed up with him producing a lighter like a magician and lighting the cigar for Wang Liang. “Director, don’t worry about these b*stards, these people are crazy to ask so much for a small scale mine. If it doesn’t work out, we can always go to the next one.”

“The main problem is germanium ores are few and far in between. Also, Qian Wenhuan issued strict orders for us to acquire this germanium mine at all costs...”

Wang Liang shook his head dejectedly. When he mentioned Qian Wenhuan, a hint of gloom met his eyes for a brief instant. He took another strong drag from the cigar before heading for the car. “Alright, let’s not talk about this now. If it comes down to it, we’ll just swing by the houses of these officials later tonight. As long as we can give them something good in return, the price should naturally go down tomorrow...”

With that, Wang Liang entered the car with his secretary diligently escorting him every step of the way.

After closing the door for Wang Liang, the secretary lapped around the vehicle and entered from the other side while the driver got into the driver’s seat.

“Zhou Hang, I want you to find out the address of the minister this afternoon. I need it by evening, got it?”

“Understood, Director Wang. Are we returning to the hotel now?”

“Let’s just head back to...”

As the two men continued their exchange, the driver started the engine. Just as the engine was started, a deafening explosion emerged and blew the car into smithereens!

In an instant, the vehicle along with its three passengers was devoured by a ball of flame that continued to rage on fiercely in front of the city hall...

The remaining members of the negotiation team stood there with their mouths agape, their faces illuminated by the bright flame.

Wang Liang was dead.

After about ten minutes, Chen Chen who had just received the news responded with an immediate twitch of his mouth.

“Who did it?”

Chen Chen was almost speechless. “X, did we not pick up any intel on this?”

“Sorry, Sir Godfather.”

Little X sounded almost ashamed. “I didn’t pick up any intel at all.”

Chen Chen nodded and did not try to put the blame on Little X. He of all people was well aware that Little X had acquired all information related to Blacklight Biotechnology by a large database extracted from the Internet. Unless Little X had very specific search criteria, it would be impossible to thoroughly go through everything ranging from all kinds of chat software, dark web communications or even encrypted sources like radio signals.

It would be false to say that there was no blind spot on the Internet. Besides, this attack was not specifically targeted at Chen Chen, but just an employee of Blacklight Biotechnology instead, making it harder for Little X to notice it.

“Looks like you’re going to need an upgrade to become a truly all-knowing entity on the Internet. In this vein, the E Language and Gantz server machine seem like the ideal options.”

Chen Chen tipped his head at the notion. “Have you been able to find anything by following along the clues that we have now?”

“I’ve already extracted surveillance footage of the Rainbow Nation Gaborone City Hall and found a man in a black shirt.”

As Little X said this, the surveillance footage quickly appeared on the computer display in front of Chen Chen. Shown on the display was a large man wearing a black mask in a black trench coat. When the man walked past Wang Liang’s rental car, he bent down slightly as if he was trying to tie his shoelaces.

Due to the angle of the camera, the man was shielded by the rental car. All Chen Chen could see was the man getting back up after about ten seconds and leaving after that.

Clean execution. The entire process took no longer than twenty seconds.

“This man didn’t reveal his face at all so it’s difficult for me to identify him. I also continued tracking him using surveillance footage but after he took a turn into the slums, he had effectively gone out of my range.”

Little X explained, “I’m not sure if it’s intentional or not. Point is, the target has successfully evaded my investigation efforts.”

Chen Chen’s expression grew darker when he heard this. He pedaled around slowly. “X, looks like somebody has noticed your existence.”

“Honestly, this isn’t all that surprising.”

After giving it some thought, Chen Chen noted in a low voice, “There’s the God’s Forbidden Zone’s Disciple Council, the massacre case in Switzerland, then there’s our routine purge of spies inside Eco Science City. When someone put all of this together, it’s not difficult to figure out that we at Blacklight Biotechnology have access to some sort of nearly all-knowing information channel online.”

Due to the unique nature of Eco Science City, the city never had any shortage of lurking spies.

The demographic of spies was not just limited to the spies of the major pharmaceutical corporations, even the official spies of each major continent were involved. There were spies from the Mainland and spies from North America as well, since everybody was curious just how Eco Science City developed such advanced medical technology.

Most of the time, these spies did not pose any significant threat so Chen Chen just let them be. However, whenever they executed some operation that may endanger the security of the city, someone always erased them without a trace before they could begin.

This was both a way of issuing a warning and also a way of flaunting strength.

After enough occurrences, people would eventually get the message that there was more to the Eco Science City than it let on on its surface.

Chen Chen began to silently dissect the situation they were facing. “So, this means that our lurking enemies intentionally avoid keywords like ‘Blacklight Biotechnology’ and ‘Chen Chen’ when discussing online or perhaps they swapped them out with other codewords instead. There’s also a chance that they used less mainstream communication platforms. This does minimize the chances of us noticing...”

“In that case, how do we deal with them?”

Little X asked almost restlessly.

“Well, let’s just see how things progress from here, then we’ll decide what to do from there on.”

Chen Chen answered. This response of his seemed to have caught Little X slightly off guard.

“Just because they didn’t slip up this time doesn’t mean they can keep it up the next time.”

When Chen Chen saw that Little X still had trouble grasping his meaning, he had to explain, “You still don’t get it? One of these times, they’ll slip up and expose themselves. What are the chances that they’ll never make a mistake? Impossible!”

As Chen Chen said this, the side of his lips tugged upward to form a cunning smile. “X, have you ever played Pac-man? We’re just playing a game of hide and seek with them. We’re playing the seeker while they’re playing the lone Pac-man in an enormous maze...

“I’d like to see just how many pellets of mine they can eat.”

chapters.title: Chapter 334: Kidnap
chapters.body:
Chapter 334: Kidnap

Chapter 334: Kidnap

Zurich, Switzerland.

In the evening, Wynn, who was over 60 years old, put on a black trench coat and a black top hat and walked out of the Blackwatch Research Institute slowly. He headed toward a 90s-style Porsche car on the side of the road.

Wynn was already very old. He originally had several senile diseases and complications such as high blood pressure and diabetes, and his hair was almost all white. However, a few months ago, he had gone to the African branch office, also known as the Eco Science City. When he came back, he had changed.

His hair had regained its black hue and the wrinkles on his face had been reduced by more than half. His dry yellowing eyes were more hydrated and his figure was not rickety like an old man’s, but much more upright.

At first glance, Wynn did not seem to be a 60-year-old man, but a middle-aged man in his early forties.

Everyone knew that this was the result of receiving the age-reversal treatment program. All the elderly who had undergone this treatment were able to miraculously pull away from old age back to middle age.

This was not just about getting younger in one’s face, but rather, the full range of changes in physical fitness. This was enough to make everyone scramble madly for a taste.

Still, the price of this treatment was really expensive. The most basic treatment cost more than 500,000 USD, let alone the more luxurious and refined treatments. At present, there were only a few wealthy and even fewer middle-class people in the world who had the financial resources and qualifications to extend their lives.

Even though the customer base was not large and there were sanatoriums all over the world that secretly carried out the same treatment projects, this was the most authoritative and famous Eco Science City, where an appointment would still be two years away.

At this time, Wynn, the general manager of Blackwatch’s Swiss branch, got into his car while humming a Zurich folk tune. He knew that he had a date in 30 minutes.

Yes, twenty years ago, Wynn had divorced his wife and spent time alone indulging in debauchery. It was only ten years ago that his body gradually became affected and he started to restrain himself.

However, presently Eco Science City had given him a second chance, so as a romantic playboy by nature, he naturally became restless again.

Just as he got into the car and was about to start the engine, he suddenly looked into the rearview mirror of the car inexplicably.

He seemed to have seen someone sitting in the backseat.

Even so, as he looked up, he saw that the backseat was empty and there was nothing there.

“A trick of the eye?”

Wynn shook his head, then laughed at himself and started the car.

After that, Wynn drove to Page Hotel, the place that he had agreed upon with the date in advance.

Zurich was not a busy city. The population density here was extremely low. If one were not in the city’s business district, but some remote trail instead, there was almost no sign of people except the few occasional cars.

At this time, Winn drove the car down a remote trail.

Along the way, Winn was humming the Zurich tune, but just as he was about to cross the small road in front, suddenly, a dark car cut out from behind him, way ahead of the speed limit. This gave Wynn a bad fright.

“D*mn it!”

Winn hurriedly spun his steering wheel to avoid the other vehicle, but what made him a little furious was that the person did not directly pass him by. Instead, they veered toward his side as if they wanted to cut him off and pin down his car.

Helpless, Wynn hurriedly slammed the brakes to decelerate the Porsche.

Even so, the other party still doggedly drove the car in front of Wynn, obstructing the entire one-way street and completely blocking Wynn’s path.

“What are they doing?”

Wynn felt unsettled. He instinctively wanted to reverse the car, but when he looked into the side-view mirror, his expression shifted again.

Another mysterious black car had also appeared behind him, blocking his retreat.

By this point, two people finally got off the black car in front. Both of them were wearing black balaclavas and carrying guns. When they got out of the car, they rushed directly to Wynn’s car. “Get off, Mr. Wynn!”

“What are you doing?”

Wynn’s expression changed. At this moment, there was no way he would not understand that he was being kidnapped!

“We won’t hurt you.”

One of the masked men said, “We just want to take you somewhere else to discuss business. It’s too noisy here and it’ll be quieter there.”

“Do you want money?”

Wynn’s expression darkened and he could not help persuading, “How much do you want? If the amount isn’t large, I can give it to you directly, but if the amount is too large, I won’t be able to make the call at all, and it might attract the police’s attention...”

“We don’t want money. You’d better listen to our orders.”

Seeing that Wynn was nattering on and on, the masked man finally became impatient. He pulled Wynn out of the car, then quickly used a metal detector to uncover all the metal objects on Wynn. After that, he seized Wynn and walked toward the black vehicle.

Another masked man immediately drove Wynn’s car and followed the two black cars toward the suburbs of Zurich.

By now, Wynn was already very anxious. After he got in the car, he was trapped between the two. As he watched the vehicle drive in an increasingly remote direction, he suddenly asked again, “What do you want me to do? Why did you kidnap me? May I know?”

“You’ll know soon enough.”

The masked man sighed, turned his head, and said to Wynn, “Don’t be nervous, Mr. Wynn, we mean you no harm. We’re not targeting you, but the company behind you...”

“You mean, Blackwatch Corporation?”

Wynn’s expression changed and he quickly explained, “Although I’m the Swiss general manager of Blackwatch, this company was originally called Preston. I was one of the shareholders. Later, it was acquired by Blackwatch due to its poor management and I just continued to help them manage the company.

“To be honest, I’m not a core member of Blackwatch at all, and the branch in Zurich has no secrets. If you want to find a way in through me, I’m afraid you’ll be disappointed...”

“Don’t worry, of course, we know all this.”

The masked man snorted coldly. “If you were a core member of the Blackwatch Corporation, we wouldn’t treat you like this.”

“You have a grievance against Blackwatch Corporation?”

Wynn was surprised. “But since you know that I’m not a core member of Blackwatch, why kidnap me? I don’t know anything about its internal workings!”

However, following that, no matter how Wynn questioned them, the masked man stopped talking.

Seeing this, Wynn also knew that he could not persuade the other party with words, so he had to calm down.

The vehicle drove for forty to fifty minutes until it reached a forest close to the Alps, where the asphalt road was no longer visible. Only then did the vehicle stop.

“Get off, Mr. Wynn.”

The masked man took Wynn out of the car half-coercively and then pointed to a concealed, quiet path ahead. “Do you see that, Mr. Wynn? Just follow us and I hope you don’t do anything stupid.”

With that said, he walked ahead in the direction of the path.

Wynn looked around helplessly, only to see the other masked people looking at him coldly, so he shrugged and trailed behind at once.

They walked for nearly an hour. As both his mobile phone and watch were confiscated, Wynn was unable to figure out the specific duration. In short, it was only when the soles of his feet were so sore that he could barely continue walking that a wooden hut appeared in front of the path.

This seemed to be a forest ranger’s residence. Wynn subconsciously looked around, but only saw endless trees in all directions. There were only a few pieces of dried meat hung on the drying rack in front of the house and a large swathe of animal fur and leather under his feet.

Did these people live a primitive life?

This was Wynn’s only thought.

Following the instructions of the masked man in front, Wynn walked into the hut in the forest with a stunned expression. In the hut, he saw two blonde girls sitting on one side and a wireless transceiver in front of them. Both of them had lowered their heads, discussing something in low voices.

As they watched Wynn coming in from outside, the two blonde girls did not say anything, but their faces were cold.

“Well, I believe we should be able to block off all interruptions here, so we can speak freely.”

The masked man also walked in from the outside. He took off the hood over his head, revealing a young and handsome masculine face.

“Mr. Wynn, please allow me to introduce myself. My name is Fisher Oppenheimer. You should’ve heard of my name. After all, there are some intersections between our family and you.”

At this point, Wynn was taken aback in realization. This name was not unfamiliar to him. This was the surname of a large European consortium. As if he could not believe it, he confirmed loudly, “Your father is Sir Oppenheimer?”

“Yes, that’s my father.”

The young man nodded. “I also have an elder brother named Federer, but he’s dead.”

Wynn suddenly recalled the Novartis meeting homicide that shocked the whole of Europe half a year ago. A total of more than 50 Novartis shareholders were killed in the meeting, yet the killers all appeared to come from more than a dozen plutocratic families that were also Novartis shareholders.

Until now, the world had not understood what caused the madness of the dozens of killers and why they initiated this massacre that astounded the world. It was just that those killers disappeared completely after the massacre was launched and they were still missing today.

Federer was one of the missing persons that day.

“The whole world thinks that my brother Federer was one of the masterminds in this affair, but only I know that this isn’t the case.”

The man paced slowly in the room, seeming to fall into the memories of the past. “My brother has a perfect family, amazing talents, and even my father praises his talents and practically acknowledges him as the head of the house. Would such a person destroy his future and kill his colleagues personally?”

Wynn smiled bitterly when he heard the words. “Perhaps it’s true. I’ve seen Federer. An excellent young man. I don’t believe he’d do such a thing, but this matter seems to have nothing to do with me.”

“No, this is deeply connected to you, Mr. Wynn.”

The young man sneered. “Everyone in the world thinks this incident is a terrorist attack or even some kind of cult sacrifice ceremony, but only a few people know, that particular Novartis’ shareholder meeting was aimed at dealing with one company, one person...”

Wynn’s expression changed. “Could it be that...”

“Yes!”

Seeing that Wynn finally reacted, Fisher nodded. “The Novartis shareholders meeting had gathered one-third of the consortiums across the entire European Continent. Their purpose was to deal with the newly emerging Blackwatch Corporation and the man named Chen Chen!”

“I should’ve thought of it long ago.”

Hearing this, Winn gave a wry smile. “Blackwatch almost destroyed the sanatorium business in Europe. No wonder the major European consortiums want to deal with him. But what does this have to do with the massacre last year?”

Fisher said with a cold expression, “Holmes once had a proverb, ‘Once you eliminate the impossible, whatever remains, no matter how improbable, must be the truth.’ Therefore, there’s only one possibility, the Blackwatch Corporation is behind this massacre!”

Wynn was silent when he heard these words and it took a long time before he said uncertainly, “But how did Blackwatch do it?”

“I’m looking for an answer too.”

Fisher shook his head. “However, after making many attempts to go against Blackwatch, we have come up with a frightening speculation that this company has amazing control over the Internet. Anything that uses electronic products or any kind of chat software, is at risk of being eavesdropped on by this company.”

“Is that possible?”

Wynn was a little bewildered. He would not have believed this, but now he had been kidnapped by the group of people in front of him, so he had no choice but to believe.

“There’s a very high chance of that.”

Fisher nodded. “Not only did we discover this, but you should also know that the consortia behind Blackwatch also occasionally leaked out information. They have joined forces to create an organization called ‘God’s Forbidden Zone’. This organization is essentially a consortium mutual aid association. Blackwatch always has access to countless trade secrets, allowing consortia in God’s Forbidden Zone to spend money to buy these trade secrets.

“It can also be seen from this that Blackwatch has some kind of extremely astonishing information channel, which is very similar to our speculation!”

“God’s Forbidden Zone?”

Wynn muttered in a low voice. He had heard of this organization, but he always thought it was just like Freemasonry, a mere Internet rumor. He did not expect it to exist.

“So, if we want to avoid Blackwatch’s eyes, the only way is to come to a haven with no surveillance and no electronic equipment.”

Fisher spread his hands out. “Mr. Wynn, I’m not only avenging my brother but I’ve also united a group of environmentalists and all this is for the Earth and the environment.”

“Yes, for this piece of nature.”

The blond woman who had been silent on the side also spoke. She seemed to be a member of the Environmental Protection Association. “We and Fisher oppose this company for different reasons. Our organization found that this company is destroying Earth. They’ve developed longevity projects and researched drugs for terminal illnesses. This will increase the population on Earth and eventually lead to a population explosion. The mother planet on which we live will be destroyed in the hands of mankind...”

“Not only that, their Project Xingtian is even more ghastly!”

The other blonde woman was filled with indignation. “Can you believe it? Over the past month, millions of people with disabilities have bought Project Xingtian’s bionic prostheses and transformed themselves into androids! This group of trash is destroying human purity and the divinity of mankind. This is a depraved and blasphemous manifestation, they will inevitably go to hell after death!”

“...”

Wynn opened his mouth but did not know what to say.

After that, they both concluded. “We dare to swear that this evil company’s next step will be to specialize in the field of artificial intelligence. It’s very possible that Skynet will be born in their hands. In any case, we must stop this evil company, no matter what price we must pay!”

chapters.title: Chapter 335: To Catch A Big Fish
chapters.body:
Chapter 335: To Catch A Big Fish

Chapter 335: To Catch a Big Fish

After the blonde man, Fisher, finished speaking at the beginning, it was more of the two blonde women’s accusations of Blackwatch Corporation’s crimes. In their description, Blackwatch Corporation had violated the rights of the earth like a heinous criminal.

Although it did not sound quite right to Wynn, he was at their mercy now, so he simply listened and smiled sympathetically. “If my guess is right, the two ladies should be members of the ‘VHEMT’ organization or the ‘Church of Euthanasia’, right?”

“Does Mr. Wynn know of VHEMT too?”

One of the blonde woman’s eyes lit up. “Yes, we’re indeed members of the ‘Voluntary Human Extinction Movement’ organization. Do you want to join us?”

Wynn had only mentioned it casually. He never expected them to be members. He instantly felt awkward, not knowing what to say.

The so-called “Voluntary Human Extinction Organization” was a radical organization founded by a teacher named Knight in North America in 1991. The organizational philosophy was: human beings should exterminate themselves because extinction could prevent environmental degradation and human extinction was also the only way to save the earth and the natural environment.

“Don’t you agree? The world’s population growth rate has far exceeded the earth’s ability to bear it. For the earth’s biosphere, the best thing is that humans voluntarily stop reproducing. After all, humans and the earth’s biosphere are incompatible.”

The blonde woman loudly promoted the organization’s philosophy. “There are tens of thousands of species on Earth that are threatened by humans. These species are constantly being squeezed out of their living space by humanity. Therefore, only the extinction of humans can save these lives!”

“But we’re different from the ‘Church of Euthanasia’. We don’t advocate killing each other to reduce the population. The most fundamental concept of our Voluntary Human Extinction Organization is that humans should stop reproducing. After all, the essence of reproduction is the abuse of children.”

The other woman explained, “Although this is a form of altruism, stopping reproduction can also give humans an idyllic life like in the Garden of Eden. We don’t have to work for our children anymore and we don’t have to worry about what happens after us. When everyone stops reproduction, after a hundred years, the last remaining humans will also be proud of our achievement, and humans will never suffer from abortion, war or hunger...”

“Then I’m sorry, I already have a son...”

Wynn forced a smile. “So, I’ve already gone against the membership requirement of your organization.”

“Not at all!”

The blonde woman shook her head and continued to exhort. “The Voluntary Human Extinction Movement is a loose organization. Anyone who agrees with our thinking is a member of this organization. As long as you agree with us, then you’re a member! And you can also let your son agree with us!”

“I’m willing to head back and try...”

Wynn said awkwardly.

“Okay, that’s enough of that.”

Seeing that they were getting off track, Fisher interrupted them both. “Mr. Wynn, what have you decided?”

“Blackwatch is by no means easy to deal with.”

Wynn could only speak haltingly. “I have to say that your views touched me. As a Catholic, I don’t like the company’s behavior and Chen Chen even publicly declared on the TED platform that humans can be equal to God. This is a blasphemy against God. If you haven’t come to me this time, I’d have also intended to resign from the position of general manager of the Swiss branch...”

“No need for that!”

Fisher and the two blonde women looked at each other and their eyes lit up. “Mr. Wynn, if you continue to work in the Swiss branch and find ways to infiltrate the core of this company, this will help us even more!”

“That is impossible!”

Wynn’s expression changed drastically. “In that case, I’ll encounter great danger because Blackwatch isn’t as harmless and benevolent as they look on the surface. They are the largest private military contractor in Africa. If they want, they can even send assassins to kill off anyone, including me!”

“Then have you thought about the environment and nature? Have you thought about your beliefs?”

Seeing that Wynn was shrinking back, one of the blonde women suddenly said in an awe-inspiring manner, “For the natural environment of the earth, for your faith, what does this danger matter? There are those of us who have already paid so much to overthrow this evil company. Why are you hesitating?”

Another blonde woman also earnestly persuaded, “We’ve abandoned our mobile phones, iPads, and all electronic products. We’re even willing to abandon modern cities and live in the mountains and forests. The reason for this is that we have a common wish — to return nature to the earth!”

“It’s a pity that we can’t help you here.”

Fisher also sighed. “Your son George lives in the canton of Bern, right? Our family happens to be a shareholder of your son’s company. Just today, I’ve arranged for him to go to North America on a business trip so that Blackwatch Corporation wouldn’t notice anything...”

“You guys!”

Wynn’s expression changed completely after hearing this and he could no longer remain calm. “What did you do to my son?”

“He’s fine.”

Fisher shook his head with a pitying look. “As long as you do what we say, I can protect your son from any danger. Of course, if you’re unwilling to cooperate, the moment you divulge any information about us is the moment you’ll receive your son’s death notice.”

“I see, I’ll do what you said...”

Seeing this, Wynn sighed and finally gave in.

“Very good Mr. Wynn, you made a smart choice.”

Fisher seemed to have expected this. Now, he just smiled faintly and at the same time took out a note and handed it to Wynn. “This is your task...”

Wynn took the note in bewilderment. When he saw the dense rows of letters on the note, his expression suddenly shifted. “You guys are going to do this, but this will...”

“This is the only way we’ll have a chance to eliminate Blackwatch!”

Fisher stretched out his hand and said coercively, “Mr. Wynn, we look forward to working with you!”

At this point, Wynn finally shook hands with Fischer unwillingly and said in a soft voice, “I hope you can let my son go after I do this.”

“Don’t worry, we’re not a terrorist organization. As long as you fulfill our requirements, we’ll do what we promised.”

Fisher nodded and agreed at once.

Seeing this, Wynn finally stopped talking. He shot a deep look at the three of them, then turned and walked out of the wooden hut.

At this point, Wynn was taken away by some people outside. After a while, several young people came in, one of whom nodded toward Fisher. “Boss, we’ve also invited Samuel over.”

“What did Samuel say, would he like to cooperate with us?”

Fisher raised his head and asked.

“He asked to see his daughter first.”

“Then, let Annie meet her father. After all, he has such a ‘good’ daughter...”

When he was talking about this, Fisher could not help but break into a suggestive sneer.

...

“Sir Godfather, we’ve gotten hold of the location of the opponent’s three secret strongholds in Switzerland.”

While Fisher continued to capture the Swiss branch of the company, in the African Continent, Little X was also reporting quickly, “These people haven’t found the Black Knights nor are they aware of the existence of the God chip. Should we spring the trap now?”

“Not for now.”

Chen Chen waved his hand. “Our opponent cannot penetrate the Eco Science City and will inevitably attack other weak points. The Swiss branch is one of them. If it were not for the particular nature of the Mainland, it may be possible for an accident to occur at the head office in Shangdu or even with my parents.”

“Please rest assured, your parents are absolutely fine,” Little X said.

Chen Chen nodded and smiled icily. “But I didn’t expect that these extremist environmental protection organizations would also join the fray. The Voluntary Human Extinction Organization? But this group of people was exposed so easily. Is that because we’re too pervasive or they are too confident?”

“This must be human nature. After all, who would’ve thought that something beyond common knowledge like the God chip would exist in the world?”

Little X chuckled lightly.

“In this case, we don’t have to rush to spring the trap. After all, this is just a bunch of small fry. For us presently, the Swiss branch is also something dispensable. Even if that company goes bankrupt tomorrow, I won’t feel an ounce of distress.”

Chen Chen said, “Furthermore, what their group of people is doing now is just a test for us. Behind this group of people, there must lurk the shadows of those European consortia and even the tacit approval of the European government...”

“So, we have to cast a long line to catch a big fish?”

“That’s right.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and could not help but laugh coldly. “And the other party doesn’t seem to be totally ignorant. Samuel, for instance, poses quite a large problem.”

“I’ll continue to monitor the situation,” Little X replied.

Chen Chen nodded. “After this incident, we should be able to get a lot of experimental subjects. In this case, let’s conduct a Gantz experiment.”

“Gantz experiment?”

Little X was a little unclear on Chen Chen’s meaning.

“We’ve purchased a batch of metal germanium. First, we’ll use this batch of metal germanium to make a small Gantz server and then set up an experiment in a hidden place.”

“Are you looking for an uninhabited region to test the memetic effect?”

Little X reacted immediately. She said in surprise, “But where should we go to find such a place?”

“Is it not simple enough to find an island in the Atlantic Ocean?”

Chen Chen asked in return.

“Alright, I got it!”

Little X replied, “I’ll let the Black Knights pay close attention to every movement made by all our targets.”

...

Samuel returned home in a daze.

As soon as he opened the door, a chilly gust wafted out from the room. It was the cold air from the central air conditioner.

However, in the room, there was no longer that crisp and cheerful, lark-like voice to greet him...

Samuel was using all his strength to keep calm but if one looked closely, one would notice that there were tear marks on his face and his mouth was also closed tightly as if he was gnashing his teeth in secret.

It was not until the door was completely closed that Samuel took a deep breath and fell to the ground in anguish.

“Annie, my daughter...”

Samuel kept sobbing and he began to viciously punch the ground with his fists, only stopping when his fists were mangled and bleeding.

Just today, he received a text message from his daughter after he got off work, asking him to go to their favorite Chinese noodle restaurant.

At first, Samuel did not realize anything was wrong. He just went to the restaurant as usual, but before he entered, he was stopped by several men in black.

Then, he was taken to a forest in the Alps.

There, he saw his daughter who had been kidnapped beforehand.

These kidnappers claimed to be members of the Environmental Protection Association and their purpose was to overthrow the Blackwatch Corporation and the organization behind this company — God’s Forbidden Zone.

Of course, these people did not know that Samuel was a member of the God’s Forbidden Zone organization. The reason for this was that he had turned down Chen Chen’s invitation as early as a year ago to go to Africa and was willing to stay away from the core and become a member on the margins in Switzerland.

Nonetheless, because of this, these people thought that he was just like Wynn, employees who were merely retained from the former company.

Secretly, Samuel was a little grateful. He was grateful that he did not tell his daughter too much, otherwise, the fact that he was a member of God’s Forbidden Zone would not be concealed from this group of people. When the time came, if they forced him to divulge more about the things going on behind the scenes, it would cause the God chip behind his neck to detonate.

At that time, it would not matter if he died. He was just afraid that his daughter would also be attacked by these evil people.

However, by now, because of the existence of the God chip, everything he said before should have been overheard by Blackwatch, right?

Would it be a piece of cake for Blackwatch Corporation to rescue his daughter?

Thinking of this, Samuel had a glimmer of hope in his eyes, but then this glimmer of hope turned into a deeper worry — what if the rescue operation failed? Would his daughter die anyway?

Just when he was caught up in these muddled anxieties, in the dimly lit room, several ripples that seemed like currents of water suddenly emerged in the air in front of him.

Samuel was leaning against the door at this time with tears in his eyes. When he saw the bulging ripples in the air, he first rubbed his eyes, then his eyes widened into large rounds and his mouth opened suddenly.

This was because, in his vision, the ripples in front of him turned crystal clear in an instant, and at the same time a blue electric light flared up. After the flare, a man with Asian features in black tights appeared in front of him!

This man had a well-proportioned figure and did not seem to have overly-developed muscles, but there was a special symmetrical beauty to his body, just like those swimmers who often swim. The muscles had unique structural characteristics, whose extraordinary quality was evident at a glance.

At this time, the Asian man did not speak, but instead held a silver-white Rubik’s Cube. He pressed a few buttons on the Rubik’s Cube and then placed the Rubik’s Cube on the ground.

In the next moment, Samuel only saw a flash of light coming from the Rubik’s Cube and this Rubik’s Cube emitted a projection of light and shadow that he had never seen before!

After the flash of light and shadow, Samuel was flabbergasted to find that in front of the Asian man, a translucent figure had appeared out of thin air.

Samuel was well-acquainted with the face of that figure. He subconsciously quivered all over and then he clambered up clumsily and exclaimed, “Your Excellency Chen Chen, is it really you? Are you here?”

Upon seeing Samuel’s amusingly flustered expression, Chen Chen could not help but crack a small grin on the corner of his mouth. “Samuel, long time no see...”

chapters.title: Chapter 336: White Embers
chapters.body:
Chapter 336: White Embers

Chapter 336: White Embers

At the same time that Samuel saw Chen Chen, Chen Chen was reclining lazily in a black seat in the Spire Experimental Base in Africa.
