Seeing this scene, Fisher seemed to recall something. He quickly turned his head and looked at the dozens of patients lying on the ground in the center of the safe zone. At the same time, an unthinkably horrifying possibility came to his mind!

This thought chilled Fisher’s heart. He instinctively wanted to stay away from this young man, but when the young man saw Fisher’s arrival, he rushed forward as if he had seen a savior. “Mr. Fisher, you must save me. I’m also cursed, think of a way to save me!”

“Get away!”

Fisher quickly backed away, but he was still a step too slow. His arm was directly grabbed by the young man. Instantly, Fisher’s wrist was smeared with black ash from the youth!

“D*mn piece of sh*t!”

Fisher’s pupils shrank. He broke away from the other guy’s palm as if he was electrocuted, while kicking him away. “Quickly subdue him, don’t let him approach me. Come on!”

However, at this moment, no one listened to his words. Everyone retreated in unison as if they had seen the plague god.

No one here was a fool. This young man was a member who took care of the volunteers. As soon as he left the safe zone, he displayed the same symptoms as the many volunteers who had lit the signal towers. This scene suddenly reminded everyone of a possibility —

This curse was contagious!

This news was like a stone that caused a thousand ripples. Everyone subconsciously distanced themselves from others and those who took care of the wounded were washing their limbs madly.

In an instant, the camp that was just starting to see some sort of order had become chaotic again!

“Mr. Fisher!”

Several subordinates who were still loyal to Fisher hurried over. “Mr. Fisher, what should we do now?”

Still, at this point, Fisher did not have time to pay attention to this group of people. He just picked up the kettle and frantically cleaned the parts of his arm that were stained by black ash. His face was pale.

“Fisher!”

Just then, a female voice rang out. Fisher subconsciously wanted to run away after hearing this voice, but after hesitating for a moment, he still stopped and looked toward the source of the sound.

He saw Jennifer being supported by another blonde woman, walking slowly over.

Even so, Jennifer’s once beautiful face was now red and swollen like an inflated balloon. There was almost no blood or flesh on the back of her head and only her pale skull was directly exposed to the air.

From a distance, Jennifer was no longer like a young girl in her twenties, but an ugly red troll.

Seeing this peculiar Jennifer at this time, Fisher’s cheek twitched, but he still smiled reluctantly. “Jennifer, you’re injured, you should rest more...”

“In this situation, I can’t rest anymore.”

Jennifer’s voice was hoarse, like metal scratching against glass. She pleaded, “Fisher, I’m beyond help. You don’t need to care about me, but I have a request, please, no matter what, you must take my little sister away from here!”

“Big sister...”

The blonde woman who was holding up Jennifer whispered in tears.

Fisher looked at the blonde woman who was sticking to Jennifer and could not help but twitched his mouth. “Jennifer, what nonsense are you talking about? We’ll all leave this place, please believe me.”

“Fisher, are you still trying to deceive me at this point?”

Jennifer looked even more miserable. “How many things have we done for you, up to now? You can’t even agree to this simple request?”

“When did I not agree to you? We can all leave safely. I’ve always believed in this and I hope you can believe it too.” Fisher frowned. “Now you better lie back down. It’s disorderly enough as it is. I hope you won’t add to the chaos!”

“Add to the chaos? Heh...”

After hearing these words, Jennifer could not help but laugh in response. Then, a deep bone-rumbling voice came from her mouth. “Hahahahahaha...”

The laughter grew louder and louder. Even her sister began to notice something was wrong and subconsciously moved away from Jennifer.

Fisher only felt an uneasy premonition getting stronger and stronger. He inadvertently looked behind Jennifer, where the huge spire tower suddenly flashed with a red light!

This red glow glimmered across the surface of the spire and then disappeared.

In Fisher’s eyes, it was as if the spire tower was in operation at first, but after the red light flashed past, the operation of the spire was suddenly halted...

“What the h*ll...”

Fisher had no time to think about the meaning of the spire tower’s red light flashing and vanishing. He only noticed that Jennifer trembled suddenly at the same time that the spire seemed to be shut down!

It began as only a slight tremor at first, but as time passed, her body’s shaking became more intense. In the end, it was as if she was being electrocuted and even her entire body twitched with afterimages!

At last, before everyone’s horrified eyes, red flesh and blood rapidly grew all over Jennifer’s body. This flesh and blood continued to grow and spread. Finally, Jennifer directly no longer looked like a human being but a monster made of endless flesh and blood!

chapters.title: Chapter 350: The End
chapters.body:
Chapter 350: The End

Chapter 350: The End

It was as if countless blood-red long worms had grown out of Jennifer’s body. Each of these long worms was dozens of centimeters long and it took almost just the blink of an eye to cover Jennifer’s body!

As everyone watched on in terror, Jennifer had turned into a monster made of endless flesh and blood!

With a soft deflating sound, Jennifer seemed to melt and slumped to the ground. At the same time, the flesh and blood all over her body spread out in all directions like tentacles. Fisher’s reaction was the fastest. When she began to mutate, he had backed away. By the time she became a monster, he had withdrawn five or six meters away.

One must say that Fisher had a very high intuition for danger. In the next second, the monster suddenly ejected two tentacles. The two subordinates beside Fisher could not dodge in time and were directly ensnared by the tentacles!

Seeing this scene, Fisher’s heart turned to ice. Without thinking, he turned around and fled. Still, he noticed that several other cursed volunteers behind him also began to shudder and then endless flesh and blood surged from their facial features and wounds. Their entire body was completely transformed into a mass of flesh and blood without a shape!

“Run, quickly run!”

“Save me!”

“Agh, d*mn it, I was caught...”

The once-safe zone seemed to be turned into a hell on earth and screams were ringing in Fisher’s ears. He had exhausted all his strength by the time he crawled into the jungle.

After escaping into the jungle, Fisher looked behind him in shock but saw that on the ground of the safe zone, the infected group had all turned into a pool of flesh and blood. These substances continued to wriggle on the ground, seemingly fused like a fluid as they spread in all directions at the same time...

Those who were caught were incessantly crying for help in the heap of flesh and blood, but this strange substance seemed to have some kind of extreme peculiar power. It directly enveloped them and was still slowly digesting them.

“Could the end of the world be upon us...”

Fisher stared at all of this blankly, but at this moment, he suddenly felt a chill in his back and a heart-piercing agony suddenly struck him!

Fisher turned his head subconsciously, only to see that Jennifer’s sister was standing behind him with a malevolent look. She was holding a sharp flint knife in her hand, the blade of which was red with blood.

“This is for my dear sister...”

Her cold words sounded in his ears but Fisher found that when he was dying, he did not have much fear and instead was overcome by a sense of relief.

Perhaps this world was ending...

Fisher only had time to twitch the corner of his mouth, revealing a wry smile, and then his eyes turned darker. Finally, he had completely lost consciousness...

One day, two days, three days...

Three days later, on the Blacklight which was moored ten kilometers away from the island, Chen Chen looked at the experimental island with a hint of solemnity.

The lush island was once full of emerald green mountains and completely covered by vegetation. However, at this time, most of the island had turned red.

This was a horrifying bright red as if the entire island was covered in a layer of flesh and blood. Looking from a distance, it was an eerie bloody sight.

Even from ten kilometers away, Chen Chen could smell, through the scent of seawater, the stench that was unique to flesh and blood.

Moreover, that patch of flesh and blood was still growing endlessly as if it wanted to cover every inch of the island...

“Sir Godfather.”

When Chen Chen was observing the island, Little X’s voice suddenly came from the speakers behind him. “We’ve sent a Black Knight to the island. After testing, we’ve learned that flames can cause damage to this patch of flesh and blood and this patch also cannot grow in water.”

“That’s good news.”

Chen Chen said without looking back, “If this is the case, then send someone to collect a small sample of the flesh patch. After the collection is completed, the entire island will be burned to prevent this thing from spreading.”

“Understood... By the way, Sir Godfather, there has also been a message from Yoru Kamidera. They’ve thoroughly researched the patch of flesh and blood and now they’re also requesting to return.”

Little X continued to report.

“There are no issues with them?” Chen Chen did not immediately agree but turned to ask.

“No issues. Everything is under the control of X-112. Although X-112 is still unable to control memetic effects, a little interference is still doable. If these people are infected with memes, X-112 will know.”

“I can be rest assured then.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Then bring that group back. By the way, ask Yoru Kamidera and X-112 to jointly prepare a complete report for me.”

“Yes.”

...

When Samuel and the others carefully exited the underground laboratory under the escort of the Black Knights, they immediately saw a dense blanket of flesh and blood on the top of the mountain.

This mass of flesh had turned into a blanket of vegetation-like fungus, covering the ground’s surface and wriggling regularly as if breathing.

When Samuel stepped on this fungal spread, he only felt a softness under his feet, like stepping on an endless stretch of internal organs. It was a very disgusting feeling...

“Don’t worry. Under the control of X-112, these things will not attack us.”

Yoru Kamidera followed out from behind, looking at the blanket of flesh and blood, and could not help looking rueful. “This time, we hit the jackpot. With only 500 lives, we fully observed the mutation process of the memetic effect. Believe me, this experiment will greatly help Blacklight Biotechnology and X-112 in studying memes...”

As he spoke, Yoru Kamidera walked straight to the jungle ahead, only to see trees and branches covered in a shroud of flesh and blood.

In other words, this flesh and blood patch not only captured humans and absorbed nutrients from the soil, but could also digest and absorb plants...

“Is this mutation random? Or is it controlled by this artificial intelligence?”

Samuel asked.

“Of course, it’s random. X-112 is like a human being, it can only learn from scratch.”

Yoru Kamidera walked to the spire and stretched out his hand to pat it. Over the entire island, only its surface was still smooth, and no flesh and blood could attach to the spiral tower.

“If this carpet of flesh and blood was given a few years, wouldn’t the entire Earth be completely swallowed by it?”

Someone suggested this in a soft voice and everyone was secretly shocked at once.

Samuel and the other researchers had nothing to say, but trod cautiously on the endless blanket of flesh and blood, lest it attacked them.

After all, when they were in the laboratory, they could see how these blankets of flesh and blood had attacked humans.

About ten minutes later, the Mi-26 transport helicopter flew over from the Blacklight and stopped at the top of the mountain.

When the helicopter took off again, it carried away everyone on the island and the Gantz server.

After waiting for the helicopter to fly far away, several other helicopters took off from the Blacklight and dumped a large amount of fuel on the island. A few hours later, the entire island began to burn.

In the end, the massive patch of flesh and blood on the island was reduced to a 100-meter-thick plume of smoke in the blaze. Everything was burned to a crisp, completely burying all traces of the experiment here...

chapters.title: Chapter 351: Obliteration Catalyst
chapters.body:
Chapter 351: Obliteration Catalyst

Chapter 351: Obliteration Catalyst

[Memetic Effect: Flesh Carpet.]

[Origin: 1 June 2024, in the temporary observation experiment project on Gantz Island, produced by the memetic mutation of infected person No. 0130 who was infected with “Filth”. Temporarily named “Flesh Carpet”.]

[Description: “Flesh Carpet” is a special memetic with infectious disease properties. From its appearance, it looks like it is formed by laying flesh and blood on the ground. Therefore, the name “Flesh Carpet” is derived from this image. Unlike the conventional type of memetic effect, “Flesh Carpet” has a dual nature resembling “infections” and plants.]

[Symptoms: At present, all known infected persons have been assimilated as part of the “Flesh Carpet”. When someone is infected, they will mutate from their original biological form to a mass of vegetation composed of flesh and blood. It will indiscriminately attack all creatures within a certain range, including but not limited to humans.]

[Mode of transmission: The flesh carpet has extremely terrifying transmission ability. Any life form touching the carpet of flesh and blood, including but not limited to humans, will be 100% infected. At the same time, its transmission is divided into two forms —”violent predation” and “infection transmission”.]

[Violent predation: it stretches out a tentacle to ensnare and capture the target. If the target is a creature, it will be dissolved and digested within twelve hours. If the target is a plant, the carpet of flesh and blood will not attack the opponent, but will directly digest it using its body.]

[Infection transmission: The infected person has no abnormalities at the beginning, but over time, the infected person’s body surface will display a rash, which is an early symptom. At this time, the infected person is already clearly infectious and can spread the rash to any healthy life forms around. The rash will cover the infected person’s body entirely after 30 hours. At the same time, influenced by the memetic effect, the infected person will eat as much as possible and conserve energy. This is a mid-term symptom.]

[About forty to fifty hours later, the infected person will melt into a puddle of flesh and blood without warning and begin to attack all life forms around them. This is a late symptom.]

[It has not been verified whether the infected person can be cured when they are experiencing the first and second stage symptoms.]

[Weakness: Fire can kill the flesh carpet and it also cannot survive in water or an environment without oxygen and soil. Therefore, the difficulty of suppression is low.]

...

After reading the report in his hand, Chen Chen nodded and tossed the report to Yoru Kamidera behind the office desk. “You did a good job. Although it’s not perfect, in this experiment, the timing you chose to shut down X-112 was still appropriate.”

“At that time, X-112 proactively reminded me that abnormal fluctuations in memetic readings were detected. Based on my years of experience, I thought that this should be the phenomenon of memetic mutation, so I asked X-112 to temporarily shut off the Dead Space.”

Yoru Kamidera replied a little shyly, “Sure enough, once the Gantz server’s suppression of memes was lifted, ‘Filth’ immediately mutated and eventually generated a new memetic effect. All of this was recorded by X-112.”

“Very well, you may leave.”

Chen Chen waved his hand. Yoru Kamidera immediately lowered his head and slowly exited the office.

“X, send me a copy of the information recorded by X-112.”

After Yoru Kamidera had left, Chen Chen spoke at once, and suddenly, the printer by his hand started to print out various dazzling, dizzying patterns.

These patterns were all similar to Basilisk, but in fact, this was the E language used by the god-level civilization in the Gantz dimension.

However, when human beings were dealing with this ordinary language, a little improper handling could destroy human civilization more than 10,000 times...

This was the gap between civilization levels.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen flipped one hand over and a transparent NZT-48 appeared in his hand. He swallowed the drug directly and then closed his eyes.

About thirty seconds later, Chen Chen opened his eyes and looked at the intricate patterns in his hand again. This time, his eyes were fixed on the patterns as if he wanted to imprint all of them into his brain.

Nonetheless, within a dozen minutes, faint blood veins appeared in Chen Chen’s eyes as if his brain power was overused.

As Chen Chen gritted his teeth, a trace of blood flowed from the corner of his mouth, which looked sinister.

Just like this, half an hour had passed. When Chen Chen closed his eyes again, his expression finally regained its tranquility.

“I still can’t do it...”

Chen Chen murmured. He sensed that his brain could clearly engrave every detail of the patterns, but within a few seconds, these details were forgotten again as if the hippocampus of the human brain could not store this at all. All the information had merely passed in and out of his mind...

“Sir Godfather, you’ve failed again?”

Little X’s voice came. “Could it be a physiological reason? After all, we can’t determine the life form of another civilization at all. Both sides are likely to have extremely high civilization barriers. Perhaps only their life form can master the knowledge of memes?”

“Impossible.”

However, Chen Chen insisted. “Science isn’t impenetrable. Things that cannot be detected can only mean that they don’t exist. Moreover, I don’t think that the concept of ‘memes’ was created by an alien civilization. Just like the concept of ‘science’, can you say that science didn’t exist before humans discovered ‘science’? Stop kidding me!”

Chen Chen smiled coldly. “In my opinion, the so-called E language is more like a kind of civilized technology, just like the physics formula that we’ve summarized. These Basilisk patterns are using visible text to encapsulate the intangible memes, which means that the Basilisk pattern is the manifestation of the meme. You can say that the Basilisk pattern is a memetic effect, but a memetic effect is by no means a Basilisk pattern.”

“What you said makes sense.”

Little X said thoughtfully, “But doesn’t this conflict with your current state? You’ve already taken NZT-48, but you still can’t memorize this E language. Why is that?”

“The answer is simple.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and said, “When you’ve eliminated the impossible, whatever remains, however improbable, must be the truth — that civilization has set a password for its language.”

“Password?”

“It’s a simple principle. If you want to launch a nuclear bomb, you have to enter a string of keys. If you store your wealth in the safe, you’ll also set a password to prevent others from stealing it. Then this E language that can control memes, would it not have a password too?”

Chen Chen asked, “If you think of it this way, everything can be explained. The memetic effect itself is a new system beyond common sense and existing scientific frameworks. If that is the case, won’t the E language itself also have a memetic effect that prevents other civilizations from learning the E language?”

Little X suddenly saw the light. “I understand. For that god-level civilization, E language is like a weapon. Although they can control this weapon perfectly, they are also afraid that lower civilizations will obtain this weapon and use it as a shortcut to reach the same level as them. Therefore, they added a lock to their weapons. In this way, even if a lower civilization gets this weapon, it cannot be used because it cannot be unlocked!”

“That’s exactly it.”

Chen Chen snapped his fingers. “Thus, X-112 is a very important entry point. The god-level civilization may not have thought that our lower civilization would invent something like artificial intelligence.

“Based on the fact that their black spheres need to borrow human brains to perform calculations, that civilization probably didn’t open the artificial intelligence branch of the technology tree, so X-112 can take advantage of the loophole and come into contact with the E language...”

“In other words, this is a godsend!”

Thinking of this, Chen Chen had a flash of inspiration.

“Since the E language can set a lock to prevent other civilizations from learning it, can we also encrypt our data with memes to prevent leakage?”

After thinking about it, Chen Chen asked, “X, communicate with X-112 and find out — based on its current understanding of the E language, can it set a special pattern so that ordinary people will die immediately when they see this pattern, but people in our God’s Forbidden Zone can be immune?”

“It can be done.”

Little X’s voice paused for a moment and then replied, “Although X-112 has a very simple understanding of E language, it has zoomed in on the regularity of several patterns by observing the Filth mutation process in its entirety. With this regularity, X-112 can perform subtle programming to the Basilisk pattern ‘Eye’. After this programming, ‘Eye’ can still be effective on ordinary people, causing brain death in them, but if there is a ‘vaccine’, they can be easily immune.”

“Vaccine?”

Chen Chen asked.

“The so-called ‘vaccine’ is a Basilisk pattern, but its nature is not to kill people, but to prevent people from observing the ‘Eye’ and experiencing brain shutdown. It’s just like getting a vaccine in advance.”

X replied, “Moreover, X-112 can also shut down the mutation characteristic of the E language, so that the mutation probability of these Basilisk patterns will be minimized.”

Chen Chen’s eyes lit up upon hearing the words. “We can try this!”

After he had spoken, the printer beside him squeaked again and soon, a complex and abstract pattern started to emerge on the surface of the paper.

It was different from the Basilisk pattern that Chen Chen had seen before. It was a colorful pattern as if beams of light were flickering in front of one’s eyes, but when one looked carefully, these patterns had no meaning.

As the pattern became more and more complete, suddenly, a special kind of throbbing gripped Chen Chen’s mind. He seemed to experience an illusion, an illusion of being watched...

Chen Chen breathed a sigh of relief only when the entire picture was printed out. He picked up the picture. “Is this picture the so-called ‘vaccine’?”

“Yes, based on the ‘cognitohazard’ and ‘infohazard’ produced by the memetic effect, this ‘vaccine’ can generate a special ‘cognitive exemption’. You only need to see this pattern to avoid any cognitive harm caused by memetic attacks.”

Hearing these words, Chen Chen carefully observed the pattern again, only to see that there was a faint eye buried among a scribble of meaningless pen and ink. Even so, upon closer look, it seemed that there was nothing.

Yes, it was just a set of abstract images that did not make any sense. Maybe some performance artists could talk enough bull and sell it for one or two hundred yuan.

“Well, after seeing this picture, you have been implanted with the vaccine in advance. Next, please take a look at another pattern.”

Following Little X’s words, another pattern was printed out by the printer again. Chen Chen looked at it and found that the second picture was dozens of times more complicated than the first. Chen Chen just took a look and could not help frowning.

This pattern also depicted an eye, but this eye was formed by the overlapping of countless eyes. Some of them were laughing, some were crying, and some showed a touch of tenderness, just like a lover’s smile. Some were full of fear as if gazing into an infinite abyss...

Countless eyes layered on top of each other and finally formed such a peculiar pattern.

“This is ‘Eye’?”

Chen Chen could not refrain from gasping. He was sure that it was not the first time he saw this pattern. Once in the Gantz dimension, he saw this pattern out of carelessness. At that time, he had spent dozens of minutes just to get used to it.

Still, he never expected to see it again in reality this time.

Even so, it was different from before. At this time, although Chen Chen was still a little dizzy after seeing this pattern, this was completely unlike how he used to react, when he almost passed out.

Currently, he just felt an unusual nausea and this feeling was getting weaker and weaker as his brain continued to adapt.

“This is the ‘Eye’ that was modified by X-112.”

Little X replied, “But please rest assured. You’ve seen the vaccine in advance, so this new ‘Eye’ will not cause any damage to you.”

With that, the entire picture was completely discharged from the printer, but Chen Chen was no longer feeling any discomfort.

“So, that’s it.”

Chen Chen wore a look of realization. “Is this modified ‘Eye’ different from the previous one in other respects?”

“Apart from eliminating its variability, there’s not much difference, but it can be ‘mitigated’ by another pattern.”

Little X replied, “We only need to use this ‘Eye’ as a watermark for our confidential documents so that we can indiscriminately target all spies who have no authority, who still try to read confidential documents.”

“Very well. Then my question is this.”

Chen Chen snapped his fingers. “How long is the effect of this vaccine? After all, you have to understand that the human brain can forget. Will the implantation of this vaccine from too long ago cause the brain to forget it and result in the vaccine failing?”

“Please be rest assured, the effective period of the vaccine is permanent.”

Little X replied, “After all, it’s the language system of a god-level civilization. These concerns of yours will not arise at all.”

“Is that so?”

Chen Chen could not help but look pensive. After a while, he said again, “In that case, we’ll give this new confidentiality mechanism a name. After all, the label of ‘Basilisk pattern’ isn’t conducive to classification and generalization. We’ll call this kind of pattern ‘memetic obliteration catalyst’...”

chapters.title: Chapter 352: Tying Up Loose Ends
chapters.body:
Chapter 352: Tying Up Loose Ends

Chapter 352: Tying Up Loose Ends

“This is the international news channel. I’m your host, Reinhard.

“The Minister of Energy, Mr. Zurama, on behalf of the Namibian government, officially signed an aerospace and military contract with Blackwatch on 20 May 2024. This will be the eighteenth contract between Blackwatch and the Namibian government...

“From now on, Blackwatch will formally step into the aerospace industry and the military industry, becoming the first company under the umbrella of Namibia that balances both aerospace and military industries...

“At present, it’s known that the military factory will be set up in the south of the industrial park, in the western suburbs of the Eco Science City. This will be divided into two new areas, the military industrial area and the aerospace industrial park...

“Next is news from Europe:

“From 3 to 10 May, there was a large-scale disappearance case in Switzerland. The European regional government is following the matter closely. The specific details are still under investigation. The following is a reporter’s online report...”

At this moment, Chen Chen was sitting in the head chair in the office, watching all kinds of news constantly being broadcast on the TV on the wall. He suddenly said, “It seems that the Swiss authorities aren’t revealing much to the public. What they didn’t mention was that many of the missing persons were members of major consortia and there were also many like you who were... Entrepreneurs?

“Wouldn’t you say so, Mr. Wynn?”

As he spoke, Chen Chen put on a gentle smile, turned his head, and looked at a handsome middle-aged man in a suit, who was standing across the desk.

This man was about forty years old. He had the air and grace of a British aristocrat. Whoever saw him would imagine how handsome and charming he had been when he was young.

However, at this moment, the man’s face was extremely pale as if he had been badly frightened. Although he tried to keep calm, he still could not conceal the panic in his expression.

This man was the branch manager hired by Chen Chen when he bought over the biotechnology branch in Switzerland — Mr. Wynn.

Wynn was originally an old man who was over 60 years old, but after the treatment of the age reversal program, he had regained his forty-something appearance. At this time, he only wore an ingratiating smile and smiled humbly at Chen Chen. “Boss, is there a reason you have summoned me to the head office? If there is anything you need, I can help you unconditionally!”

“Unconditionally?”

Chen Chen tilted his head and tapped the desk. “Surely that’s too much to ask?”

“Not at all, not at all!”

Hearing this, Wynn looked overjoyed and quickly laughed. “Remember, you needed the ultra-precision machine tools, right? And I helped you obtain several of them. In addition, there were many medical equipment exports that I’ve also helped make possible. So now, whatever you need, I can help you do it unconditionally!”

Chen Chen suddenly clapped his hands and laughed loudly at the same time. “Mr. Wynn, you’re devoted to our company...”

“As I should, as I should!”

Wynn also laughed and the entire office was filled with a cheerful atmosphere for a while.

“Since this is the case, I’ll be frank.”

After laughing for a while, Chen Chen sighed. “Mr. Wynn, you’re still very wise. You haven’t involved your family from beginning to end. In that case, please be rest assured that we’ll not involve your family as well. We also will not revoke the benefits we gave you.”

Hearing this, Wynn’s expression finally darkened and he said bitterly, “I understand, this is indeed what I deserve... I won’t blame you.”

“Any last words?”

Chen Chen asked at last.

“...”

Wynn was silent for a while and after a moment, he said again, “There’s nothing much to say. If anything, I’d like to ask that, no matter what, please don’t tell my son about my death and please protect him. Let him successfully take over the inheritance of 5 billion Swiss francs in my bank.”

“Sure.”

Chen Chen nodded without hesitation. Then, two Black Knights outside the door immediately walked in, took Wynn by his arms, and dragged the despairing man out of the room...

What awaited him was the fate of being recycled as an experimental subject in the Spire’s laboratory.

The reason for this was that after being kidnapped by White Embers before, Wynn finally chose to call Chen Chen and thoroughly carried out the plan to lure Chen Chen to Switzerland.

The only unfortunate thing was that when he chose to call Chen Chen to betray him, the White Embers organization had been fully decapitated.

In other words, even if he had chosen to reject the White Embers organization at that time, his kidnapped son would have been fine. However, there were no ifs and buts in this world. Ultimately, he had chosen to betray Chen Chen.

Still, even betrayal was divided into levels. If it did not endanger Chen Chen’s life, Chen Chen would only discipline him at most, considering their existing partnership. Nonetheless, Wynn knew that the White Embers organization was going to assassinate Chen Chen, but he still decided to help White Embers.

In this case, as an accomplice in threatening Chen Chen’s life, Chen Chen naturally would not spare Wynn.

This explained the scene that just took place.

After dealing with Wynn, before Chen Chen’s office door could be closed, Samuel walked in anxiously.

“Boss...”

Samuel’s face was a little pale. As Chen Chen had not specifically sent him away just now, he had fully overheard the conversation between the two outside the door and understood that Wynn was completely done for.

Since Wynn was his former boss, he had been acquainted with that man for more than ten years and Wynn’s end inevitably made him feel sad for a kindred spirit. After all, he and Wynn had had the same experience.

The only difference between Samuel and Wynn was that before Samuel could even betray Chen Chen, Chen Chen had already wiped out White Embers.

Samuel had searched his own heart. He realized that, after his daughter was kidnapped by the other party, if the Black Knight had not come to him first, he might have done the same thing for his daughter as Wynn did.

Therefore, Samuel felt a pang of guilt after seeing Wynn’s end.

“Mr. Samuel, please sit down.”

As if nothing had happened, Chen Chen asked Samuel to sit down, then picked up the information on the table and said, “You did a great job with the experiment on Gantz Island. Is there any reason you’ve come to see me now?”

“Here’s the thing. I thought about it for a long time and ultimately I still want to come to Africa.”

Samuel said thoughtfully, “I don’t know if God’s Forbidden Zone is still willing to let me join.”

Chen Chen did not seem to be surprised but laughed. “Of course, we’re willing. Although your achievements in the biological field are barely adequate, the organization is still short of people. If you work hard, it’s not impossible that you’ll attain a higher position in the organization...”

“Yes, my thoughts exactly!”

Samuel nodded repeatedly.

“But I remember you didn’t say that at the beginning.”

Suddenly, Chen Chen’s tone changed and he put on a puzzled expression. “What caused you to change your mind?”

“It’s my daughter.”

Samuel sighed and smiled bitterly. “Since the Black Knights rescued my daughter, she has kept asking me to come here. Maybe she hopes that her father will make something of himself. She hopes that I can forge a new path of prospects for her.”

“So that’s it. No wonder your daughter also came here this time.”

Chen Chen nodded. “You haven’t revealed to your daughter that the group of people who kidnapped her has died?”

“No, my daughter is still young, I don’t want to scare her.”

Samuel shook his head. “If possible, I still hope she knows as little about our internal workings as possible.”

“I hope so too.”

Chen Chen gave a meaningful smile. “Okay, I agree with your proposal. You’ll live in a five-star hotel in the Eco Science City for the time being. I’ll arrange a place for you in the next few days and then you can come work at the Biotechnology Research Institute.”

“Yes, thank you, Boss!”

Seeing this, Samuel nodded and turned to leave.

It was not until Samuel had left that Chen Chen quietly dropped his smile. If anyone was watching Chen Chen at this time, they would find that there was a picture of a blonde European girl with bright eyes and white teeth on the computer screen facing Chen Chen.

The blonde girl in the photo had fair skin, sharp refined features, and a sweet smile, full of youth and vitality. If ten marks were a perfect score, the girl could score at least seven points.

The resolution of this photo was also extremely high. Chen Chen could even see some tiny freckles on the girl’s cheeks. These freckles not only did not diminish the girl’s attractiveness but instead acted as a contrast, making her even lovelier and friendlier, like an angel among men.

This girl was Samuel’s daughter, Annie.

At this time, Chen Chen looked at the girl’s image on the screen with a smile. “Initially, out of deference to Samuel, I planned to erase her memory of that past incident and let her forget everything about White Embers. In that manner, we can still get along with each other in peace. However, unfortunately, some people just keep asking for it.

“She’s head over heels in love with Fisher. To a woman in love, the reason doesn’t exist.”

Little X’s voice came from the computer. “But this time she has used her father’s name to try and break into God’s Forbidden Zone. Should we just let it go?”

“It’s enough to keep an eye on her.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “Don’t forget, everything is under our control. In that case, we can trace her footsteps, find the weak points of the company, and improve them. This is better than allowing spies to locate these weaknesses in the future, which would cause us greater losses.”

At this point, Chen Chen pressed the mouse and clicked Delete on this photo.

With a “swoosh”, the girl’s photo was thrown by the computer into the recycling bin. Chen Chen finally said, “Of course, when the trash is no longer useful, what must be recycled will still be recycled...”

chapters.title: Chapter 353: Musk
chapters.body:
Chapter 353: Musk

Chapter 353: Musk

At noon on 1 June, at Walvis Bay International Airport, a luxurious private jet slowly landed on the temporarily prepared runway.

A plain-looking middle-aged man got off the plane slowly under the protection of a bodyguard.

The man was wearing casual summer clothes and a pair of sunglasses that covered most of his face. Although his looks could not be seen, anyone could tell that he was of considerable status, based on the ceremonial treatment he received as well as the aura he exuded.

At this time, beneath the passage, three Rolls-Royce Phantoms had already parked in front of the plane. At the same time, several men in suits stood with their hands behind them, with a young man at their head who also wore sunglasses.

The instant they met, they shook hands with each other and then hugged gently as if they were old friends who were reunited.

If an International Weekly reporter saw this scene at this point, they would likely be shaking all over. Although the two men here were not stars in the entertainment circle, being unrelated to that industry, their influence was still unmatched by any international star.

One was the real-life version of Iron Man and the top entrepreneur across the three fields of new energy, aerospace, and electric vehicles — Elon Musk.

Another one was the newly emerging oligarch of the biological field, the pioneer who brought mankind into the era of anti-aging and the era of bionic transformation — Chen Chen.

This was the first time the two were meeting. It was a pity that no reporter was able to cover this historic scene.

“Mr. Musk, you’re my predecessor.”

At this time, Chen Chen wore a humble smile on his face and said enthusiastically, “I didn’t expect you to be so young in reality. If I didn’t know your age, I’d have thought you were a thirty-year-old young man.”

“If I can receive your company’s latest age-reversal treatment program now, maybe I can return to being thirty years old.”

Elon Musk laughed and patted Chen Chen on the shoulder. “Mr. Chen Chen, your achievements aren’t inferior to mine and you’re younger. Your future is far greater than mine.”

“You’re flattering me, Mr. Musk. You’re older than me, so you can just call me by name.”

Chen Chen said conversationally.

“Alright, Chen, I’ll just call you that then.”

Musk nodded and then looked past the airport, into the direction of the rows of tall buildings on the distant skyline. “If I’ve guessed correctly, that place ahead of us is the jewel of human life sciences — the Eco Science City, right?”

“That’s too high a praise. This city is just an ordinary sanatorium. I’m just trying to make a simple living.”

Chen Chen smiled and gestured. “Please get in the car, Mr. Musk.”

Following Chen Chen’s invitation, Musk and one of his personal bodyguards got into the backseat of the car. Seeing this, Chen Chen simply sat in the front passenger seat. Then, Cheng Cao drove the car in the direction of the Eco Science City.

Even in the car, Musk could not sit still. Being somewhat of a workaholic, he did not exchange pleasantries, but asked directly, “By the way, Mr. Chen Chen, your invitation this time did mention, besides our new cooperation, that there’s a new type of treatment technology that can cure a series of diseases such as hypertension, diabetes, and so on. Could I ask what that’s all about?”

“This is a brand-new technology whose real effect lies in repairing the body. It has just been tested successfully, but it hasn’t been made public.”

Although Musk’s bodyguard was present in the car, Chen Chen did not evade the topic in the slightest but smiled. “Furthermore, I will not disclose this technology for at least 30 years and only a few people will know the existence of this technology, and use it. I believe you’ve heard of it, right?”

“God’s Forbidden Zone?”

Musk’s expression sharpened and he blurted out immediately, “Only people from the ‘God’s Forbidden Zone’ organization are eligible to use it?”

“Yes, that’s true.”

Chen Chen nodded. It was no surprise that the other party knew about God’s Forbidden Zone. After all, the name “God’s Forbidden Zone” was almost spreading like wildfire in the upper-class society of Europe and America and only ordinary people were still kept in the dark.

“But I don’t mean to join any organization.”

Musk shook his head with a laugh, with a flash of disdain in his eyes. As an emerging titan who was striving to rise on his own, he did not think much of this kind of old-fashioned consortium that could only rely on existing capital and monopolize traditional industries.

“It doesn’t matter, we can talk about this later.”

Chen Chen did not shift his expression just because of the other man’s refusal and he still smiled. “I know you’re a technocrat and a workaholic, so I’ve brought this ‘Letter of Intent for Cooperation’ for you. You can take a look first.”

With that, Chen Chen waved one hand and a book of information appeared in his grasp out of thin air. By one side, Musk’s bodyguard seemed to turn blank when he saw this — he could not tell what sort of trick Chen Chen had used.

Conversely, Musk was not surprised by this. He raised his eyebrows, took the “Letter of Intent for Cooperation”, and read it directly in the car with relish.

However, as he read, his face darkened...

This was the second collaboration between Chen Chen and Musk.

A few months ago, Chen Chen spent huge sums of money to buy the lace electrode technology of Musk’s Neuralink company and applied it to his Xingtian bionic prosthetic project. Strictly speaking, this was the first collaboration between the two.

Although Chen Chen had paid a billion dollars for this, now it seemed to Musk that he had lost out.

After all, the technology usage rights of the lace electrodes were a one-off deal, but the speed by which Project Xingtian was making money was extremely ridiculous. It even appeared likely that Chen Chen would recover the cost in just two months.

After entering the era of anti-aging, human beings were further drawn into the age of bionic transformation before they could even react.

However, they were all powerful, influential figures. This amount of money was akin to a drop in the ocean after Musk’s Starlink project was launched. After all, the 12,000 Starlink satellites launched by his subsidiary, SpaceX, had covered the world and monopolized the world’s Internet broadband services except for Mainland China.

This was another network technology parallel to the 5G technology led by China. Although the speed was not as fast as 5G, it did not require any server base station technology after all, but employed ubiquitous low-altitude satellites to provide global network coverage. This included the north and south poles and the ocean.

The so-called Starlink project relied on a satellite network composed of tens of thousands of low-orbit satellites in space to blanket the world and provide omnipresent Internet services for the earth’s population.

It was precisely because of this plan proposed on 16 January 2015, that Musk had formally monopolized the global internet access service this year, raking in nearly 50 billion USD each year...

Therefore, Musk at this time could be described as brimming with confidence. This was the peak moment of his life.

Musk was not a rocket expert. His dream was not to launch rockets. His real dream was to emigrate to Mars — a dream that could change human civilization.

In early 2001, Musk, who was just in his early thirties, planned a project called “Mars Oasis” to be able to emigrate to Mars in the future. He planned to land a small experimental greenhouse on Mars.

However, when he found that the cost of launching was much higher than the R&D and engineering costs of this project, he reluctantly postponed the project and decided to set up a company to study how to reduce the launch cost.

Thus, the famous company SpaceX was born.

On 31 May 2012, the “Dragon” capsule of SpaceX, a company under Musk, successfully docked with the International Space Station and returned to Earth, ushering in the era of private space transportation.

On 7 February 2018, SpaceX’s “Falcon Heavy” carrier rocket was successfully launched for the first time at the Kennedy Space Center in the United States and successfully completed the full recovery of two first-stage booster rockets.

There were only three original spacecraft launch and recovery technologies in the world. They were: the North American Continent, the North Sea Continent, and Mainland China.

However, from that day on, there was also an Elon Musk.

Musk had also planned all this a long time ago. When the spacecraft launch and recovery technology was developed, which would save a lot of costs, he could start the Starlink project and monopolize the global Internet access services. Therefore, sufficient funds could be obtained for research on how to emigrate to Mars.

Hence, when he read the content of Chen Chen’s “Letter of Intent for Cooperation”, the more he looked at it, the more he was astonished.

In this “Letter of Intent for Cooperation”, Blackwatch Corporation began with a cooperation project worth 30 billion, asking SpaceX to help itself launch five geosynchronous orbit satellites.

Moreover, this was only the first cooperation and the second cooperation was a new type of spacecraft technology.

SpaceX began researching interstellar spacecraft in 2019 and by now it had produced a model of the test machine named SN7, but this could still only achieve a geosynchronous orbit. They were uncertain if it could land on Mars. Musk, who originally planned to land on Mars in 2023, had fallen into a technological bottleneck.

Therefore, when he saw the new spacecraft technology mentioned in the “Letter of Intent”, his inner surprise was beyond words.

“Are you joking?”

At this time, Musk slowly raised his head, revealing an incredulous look. “The interstellar spacecraft technology on which we’ve spent more than ten years in SpaceX already represents the forefront of manned spacecraft technology. Even the Earth Federation wouldn’t dare to claim that they have anything better than our technology. Why do you think your spacecraft technology can be more advanced than ours?”

“Unless... You stole the interstellar spacecraft technology from China?”

“Not at all, Mr. Musk.”

Chen Chen’s expression remained unchanged. “Please forgive me for keeping the source of the technology a secret. I know you will not believe whatever I say now. In that case, I can allow you and your engineers to refer to it until you’re sure that our technology is real. Then we’ll talk about any future cooperation. How about that?”

“... Great!”

Musk’s face no longer wore a relaxed smile. He took a deep look at Chen Chen, then lowered his head and continued to look at the lengthy file of information in his hand.

Nonetheless, one thing was certain — it was difficult for him to be as calm as before...

chapters.title: Chapter 354: Kidnap And A Tour
chapters.body:
Chapter 354: Kidnap And A Tour

Chapter 354: Kidnap and a Tour

Two hours later, Musk looked at the giant factories standing next to each other beneath the horizon and the truck clusters hurrying up and down the broad road. He was wearing an expression of admiration.

This was a fairly complete industrial system from the extraction of raw materials, the processing of heavy industry, the refining, the factories of various finished goods, and finally the assembly factory of 10,000 people. The entire industrial chain was carried out in this vast industrial park.

What was even more outrageous was that the entire industrial park here and even the resource mining company that provided raw materials, belonged to the young man next to him...

“I think the world has greatly underestimated your assets.”

Standing in front of the gate of the industrial park, Musk could not help sighing. “I think your actual assets are far beyond the outside world’s estimates and may even exceed the total assets of some established financial groups.”

The reason he said this was that in the Forbes World’s Billionaires List in 2023, Chen Chen was assessed by Forbes to have total assets worth 50 billion USD, based on his Eco Science City and various patents. He was ranked 20th in the world and was also the youngest member on the list.

“That’s what you say, but in Africa, everything is worthless, including human lives.”

Chen Chen smiled indifferently. “What’s more, I’m not a businessman. For people like you and me who have big dreams, money is just a tool to serve our dreams. If the dream cannot be realized, no matter how much money there is, what’s the use?”

“Hahahaha, Chen, well said indeed!”

Hearing this, Musk’s eyes suddenly lit up. He just remembered that the young man in front of him, like himself, was constantly working hard for a certain dream — his dream was to establish a permanent colony on Mars and turn humans into a trans-planetary species. Was this not the same for the anti-aging plan of this amazing young man in front of him?

Blackwatch had stated on countless public occasions that the company’s corporate vision was to enter the age of longevity and even move toward the dream of immortality...

He had to admit that the young man before him who had created a legend was indeed in line with his own ideas. After all, if he achieved his desire to emigrate to Mars in the future, he would probably be near death’s door. Must it be that just when he had fulfilled his dream, he must die of old age on Mars?

Although he had said repeatedly, “My dream is to scatter my ashes on Mars”, Musk knew that he did not want to die.

He had an endless pool of money and a talent that stood out in the world. This world was full of excitement for him.

Even so, today, the development of science and technology was changing with each passing day. In this biological company, Musk also saw his hope of obtaining a higher quality of life. Due to this, he especially gave the company many preferential treatments, including the lace electrode technology, and this friendly visit.

These were all manifestations of Musk’s goodwill.

Next, Chen Chen led Musk around the entire industrial park, and then came to a satellite manufacturing factory department.

“This is the communication satellite we designed.”

In the dust-free laboratory, Chen Chen led Musk to a gigantic object.

This was a spiral-shaped metallic object. Its entire body was pitch black, with a diameter of only one meter, but its height was more than four or five meters. The patterns on its surface were glimmering and it was also inlaid with a large number of scale-like lines.

It was not so much a satellite than an artistic creation of intense style, exuding an indescribable peculiar aesthetic.

“This is...”

Musk gaped at the strange thing in front of him, dumbfounded. As an expert in rockets and satellites, naturally, he would know that because there was no air in the vast outer space, satellites did not have to be fashioned into graceful streamlined structures like airplanes. Therefore, the shapes of satellites were varied.

There were spindles, spheres, barrels, and even panes...

Furthermore, many satellites had their own solar panels, so when they were deployed, the satellites looked like a sailing ship. Some even carried a receiving antenna shaped like an iron pan and their appearance was far less aesthetic than that of an airplane.

In any case, satellites had strong functional characteristics. Real experts could tell what functions a satellite had just by looking at it.

Nonetheless, the shape of this satellite in front of him had subverted Musk’s perception of satellites.

“I suspect that this knickknack you want to launch will hardly be approved by international aerospace regulatory agencies...”

Musk looked mystified as he joked, “And this thing that you want to launch, is it for contacting aliens?”

“I know it doesn’t look like a human creation, but don’t worry, I’m not launching it to contact aliens.”

Chen Chen shook his head and solemnly said, “This is a communications satellite developed by our Blacklight Biotechnology. My purpose for launching it is to provide my company with a unique encrypted communications technology.”

“Encrypted?”

“Yes.”

Chen Chen nodded. “After all, Mr. Musk, you have to understand that in our kind of high-tech companies, any technology leak represents billions or even tens of billions in losses. Therefore, technology leaks are very difficult for us to bear. However, if we have a unique communication channel, things are much simpler.”

As he said, Chen Chen patted Musk on the shoulder. “Moreover, as for the Earth Federation Space Regulatory Agency, naturally I have my ways to make it happen. Mr. Musk, you only need to launch it into a geosynchronous orbit.”

Hearing that, Musk could not refrain from shooting a deep look at Chen Chen, then slowly walked to the so-called satellite and touched it lightly.

Musk had an ominous feeling about this satellite, especially the totem-like patterns upon its surface. When he reached out and touched it, he even had the illusion that he was touching a wriggling maggot...

It was a revolting, spine-tingling sensation, like touching a living thing.

“The technology of this satellite is very advanced.”

Chen Chen walked to Musk’s side and appraised the satellite as if it was an artwork. “It weighs only about ten tons and your Falcon 9 can carry it easily.”

“What I read in the Letter of Intent for Cooperation just now is that you’re going to launch five such satellites in total?”

Musk asked, “With a satellite in a geosynchronous orbit, only three are needed to cover all areas on Earth except the north and south poles. So, you’re launching five satellites to cover even the north and south poles at the same time?”

“Yes, that’s what I had in mind.”

Chen Chen nodded and then said invitingly, “Now that we’ve finished observing the satellite, what else do you want to see next?”

“Since I’ve come to the world-renowned Eco Science City, naturally the next step is to look at the life science technology of your company.”

Musk did not want to probe too deeply into this satellite. He thought about it and took the initiative to switch the conversation to the longevity technology that was mentioned. “Didn’t you say that there’s a brand-new age-reversal project? If possible, I wish to take a look at that. I just hope this request doesn’t bother you.”

“Not at all, it’s a great honor.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Since you want to see it, I’ll naturally do my best as your host.”

While speaking, Chen Chen shot a meaningful glance at Cheng Cao, then turned around and walked out of the laboratory first.

...

The so-called satellite was nothing other than a part of the Gantz teleportation technology.

Chen Chen’s goal was to use five Gantz satellites to fully cover every corner of Earth. Then he would only need to build a few larger-sized Gantz servers to be able to use Gantz technology for teleportation anywhere globally without delay.

At that time, Chen Chen would have an initial edge in contending against the Earth Federation...

The reason Chen Chen borrowed Musk’s power to launch the satellites was very simple.

With the energy in Chen Chen’s current USB drive, he could extract something around the level of a small Bugatti Spaceship at most. However, although this type of aircraft was agile and could even navigate space at will, it simply could not take on the weight of the Gantz satellite.

Although Chen Chen had applied for aerospace qualification in Namibia and had the interstellar spacecraft technology acquired from the dimension of Elysium, after all, he had no foundation in the aerospace industry. It was impossible to develop everything from scratch in just a few months and launch a ten-ton satellite from the ground into the sky.

Therefore, Chen Chen would not be able to put the Gantz satellites into a geosynchronous orbit on his own for at least one or two years. If Chen Chen wanted to launch the satellites, he must entrust this to other space agencies.

As the world’s best private aerospace company, SpaceX, which was owned by Musk, was naturally a good choice.

In addition, it was not as if Chen Chen had not considered getting Musk to join him.

After everyone got into the vehicles again, the group of three cars turned around and drove toward the Eco Science City, but gradually, the bodyguard beside Musk suddenly felt that something was fishy.

This was because the bodyguards in the other two cars disappeared, along with their vehicles, when they passed a fork in the road.

“Boss, something’s wrong...”

The bodyguard whispered a warning in Musk’s ear and then he slowly put his hand into his pocket.

“If I were you, I wouldn’t do that.”

However, before the bodyguard could grab the gun in his pocket, Chen Chen, who was in the passenger’s seat, suddenly turned his head and glanced at the bodyguard with a smile.

The next second, the bodyguard suddenly felt a pain in the back of his neck. It seemed that something had pierced him lightly. He was shocked, but before he could respond, darkness washed over him and he immediately lost consciousness...

All this happened in the blink of an eye. Musk stared blankly as an anesthetic needle slowly slipped out of the bodyguard’s neck, flew on its own into Chen Chen’s palm, and was put away by Chen Chen.

“Mr, Mr. Chen, what’s the meaning of this?”

Musk’s expression darkened. “I haven’t seemed to offend you, right? Is it just because I don’t want to join God’s Forbidden Zone that you have to attack me?”

“You’ve misunderstood.”

Chen Chen shrugged and said innocently, “I have no intention of hurting you. The reason I’m doing this is just to ensure secrecy. After all, you’ll soon see the real secrets of our God’s Forbidden Zone...”

“The real secrets?”

Musk turned solemn. He looked at the busy traffic in the Eco Science City going by outside the window and was slightly relieved. “I hope that’s the case.”

Chen Chen smiled without explaining much.

Soon, Cheng Cao, as the driver, drove the Rolls Royce into the underground garage of a 5A office building.

At once, Musk’s pupils contracted involuntarily when he saw that they had arrived at such a secluded area.

Still, this time, after the car officially stopped in the parking space, Chen Chen and Cheng Cao got out of the car first while Cheng Cao also considerately helped to open the door for Musk in the back.

Musk got out of the car reluctantly and a damp, cold, musty smell invaded his nose, making him sneeze.

“Please follow me.”

Chen Chen nodded to the other party and walked to the elevator on the side first.

Musk hesitated slightly. He looked at Cheng Cao, who was staring at him, only to sigh secretly and slowly follow Chen Chen.

In this way, the three of them entered the elevator. However, when the elevator closed, no one pressed any floor button in the elevator, and the elevator moved automatically.

In addition, the elevator did not go up, it was going down...

As he sensed the declining elevator, Musk’s pupils shrank even more. After all, in just half a minute, the elevator had probably descended over dozens of meters.

When the elevator reached the bottom and opened its doors again with a “ding”, a 500-square-meter underground space was immediately revealed to Musk’s eyes.

This was an empty subway platform.

The subway platform was empty, with only two tunnel trams that looked like small trains that were stopped by the platform.

The length of these two tunnel trams was about 5 meters and their entire body was fully made of alloy. The front of the carriage adopted a triangular design and the head was also marked with the Blackwatch Corporation logo.

Seeing this special underground cave, Musk was even more surprised. He subconsciously looked into the distance along the track but saw that except for the platform, the tunnel stretching into the distance was completely dark.

“The Hive in Resident Evil?”

Musk could not refrain from cracking a joke in such a tense moment. “Now I finally understand why I’ve been getting a sense of déjà vu...”

“Stop kidding around, Mr. Musk.”

Chen Chen boarded the tunnel tram first. “The Hive isn’t as hidden and complex as this underground base I built.”

“I’ll like to see for myself too.”

Musk replied and immediately got on the tram.

After he found his footing, there was a sizzle of electric current. The tram started slowly by itself and began to move toward the end of the tunnel.

Unlike the tunnel tram in Resident Evil, Chen Chen’s tunnel tram was more user-friendly. It not only had three rows of seats but also had a large LCD monitor, which was constantly playing the Chinese version of Crayon Shin-chan.

Watching Crayon Shin-chan in the gloomy and depressing tunnel tram was a rather interesting experience.

The tunnel tram was extremely fast, only taking a few minutes to reach the end of the trip.

When Musk got off the train again, he was greeted by a subway platform that was almost the same as the previous one.

Unlike the previous platform, there was a circular corridor at the end of the platform. At the center of the corridor was the alloy gate of two elevators. Next to the corridor, three other passages were leading to three subway platforms in other directions.

In other words, across the surface of the Eco Science City, there were three other entrances in addition to the parking lot they were in.

Musk understood this at first glance.

Paying no mind to Musk’s observation, Chen Chen walked to the elevator gates in the corridor. The elevator gates opened in response, revealing a larger elevator space.

This time, without Cheng Cao’s coercion, Musk entered the elevator again. Now, Musk felt that the elevator was starting to descend at an even more alarming speed.

After several twists and turns, as Musk was increasingly gripped by intense shock, the three finally stepped into the interior of the Spire Experimental Base.

chapters.title: Chapter 355: A Thrilling Tour
chapters.body:
Chapter 355: A Thrilling Tour

Chapter 355: A Thrilling Tour

As he walked through the nightmarish corridor, Musk felt a shiver creeping up his neck.

One had to admit that Resident Evil was truly a significant cornerstone of Hollywood as even someone like Musk was familiar with it. He followed Chen Chen every step along the way, a lingering fear whispered in the back of his mind, afraid that a laser would suddenly appear in front and slice him in half.

After passing through the laser corridor, Musk saw a triple-layer reinforced explosion-proof gate, something that looked like it belonged in a nuclear bunker. After the three layers of gate shuffled open after the other, the escalator leading to the Spire Experimental Base was revealed.

The escalator went upward. By the looks of it, the escalator traveled at least fifty meters.

In other words, even if someone were to invade, they would be stalled by the fifty-meter-deep underground structure and would have no choice but to find a way to break through the nuclear bunker-like fortification.

“Such a reinforced defensive mechanism...”

Musk felt every inch of muscle on his cheeks pulsing uncontrollably. “To install such intricate defensive mechanisms even several hundred meters underground, you don’t think this is a case of paranoia?”

“Better safe than sorry.”

Chen Chen waved his hands. “To be honest, I still don’t think it’s secure enough. If possible, I’d like to further improve the fortification system.”

“Are there any other exits?”

Musk stepped onto the escalator and asked with mild curiosity, “If the government army were to break in here, there’s no point to having all these fortifications if you don’t have an exit strategy.”

“Of course.”

Chen Chen shrugged. “Which is why I carve a kilometer-long tunnel in the underground base which passes through the Atlantic Ocean.”

“What an absurd idea.” Musk could only note Chen Chen’s statement with a gentle nod. He was completely astounded by this underground base he was seeing.

At this point, there was very little that Musk could do so he only followed the two of them obediently. As the escalator brought them closer to the entrance, a brightly lit underground building complex quickly entered Musk’s view.

The place was brightly lit with some sort of projection lamp shining overhead, creating an illusion of a blue sky and white clouds. Without paying close attention, it could easily fool its beholder.

There was an array of large buildings in the distance. The buildings were constructed of metal, giving off a strong impression of a futuristic utopian city.

Amid the arrays of buildings was an iconic building the shape of a Mayan pyramid acting as a landmark. It was nearly twenty stories tall and was a magnificent sight to behold in the underground space.

Plenty of fauna that did not require sunlight to grow was planted in the midst of these buildings, forming a vast and lush underground garden.

Everything Musk saw along the way was getting increasingly astonishing. Musk could feel his senses growing numb with every step he took. He felt like he just had to ask again, “You constructed all of these without discussing with the local government at all?”

Chen Chen did not answer his question but instead only continued showing him around the collection of buildings. They went to the dock with the gates shut tight, the military camp used for training the Black Knights as well as the soilless greenhouse. After that, they went to the power station of the underground base with Chen Chen leading the way.

Musk could no longer contain his disbelief when he saw a configurable nuclear fusion reactor in all its glory before him. “Are you kidding me? This is a branch of technology that nobody in the world can utilize and yet you’re properly incorporating it here? Are you kidding me!”

“Calm down. As you can see, this is the real deal.”

Chen Chen was not bothered by Musks’ reaction. He continued staring ahead at the spherical device in front of him and informed, “It’s also worth mentioning that what you’re seeing is just an inertial confinement reactor, its output can barely compare to the tokamak reactor. A better implementation would be to install it on a spaceship to serve as the nuclear engine of an interstellar spacecraft.”

Musk took in a deep breath when he heard this. He immediately thought of the brand-new spacecraft technology mentioned in the previous Letter of Intent for Cooperation.

As if sensing what Musk was thinking, Chen Chen promptly explained, “Our nuclear is currently in its research stages, it’s essentially a miniaturized version of the international confinement reactor. We expect a breakthrough within two years. However, the new spacecraft technology mentioned in the letter isn’t this...”

“You mean to say that you have other spacecraft technology?” Musk asked curiously.

“Yes.”

Chen Chen nodded. “And I can guarantee that you’ll be greatly inspired when you see it...”

Musk spent nearly an hour near this reactor. After all, this was the only usable nuclear fusion reactor in the entire world. There was no such thing as spending too long investigating it.

Chen Chen displayed great patience as well, not once did he urge Musk to hurry. After an hour had passed when Musk had finished observing the scientific marvel, Chen Chen brought him to the entrance of the central building of the Spire Experimental Base.

“This building is the Spire laboratory.”

Chen Chen brought his guest into the Mayan pyramid-shaped building. He suddenly turned around. “Mr. Musk, would you like to look at our brand-new treatment technology right away or would you like to take a look at the other bioscience technology first?”

“Is there a difference?”

Musk became immediately intrigued.

“Of course, there’s a difference.”

Chen Chen flashed a smile. “In fact, we’ve mastered a branch of amnestic technique. Should you decide to not join our God’s Forbidden Zone after you’ve finished touring the base, we won’t force you to do so as well. In that case, we’ll be erasing your memory to ensure you don’t leak our secrets to the outside world.

“However, if you choose to join us, we’ll grant you access to our newest treatment technology at first notice. I understand that you have lingering trauma related to high blood pressure and diabetes, right? To the outside world, these diseases are extremely pesky to manage and may very well directly affect one’s lifespan and life quality. However, such diseases are almost effortless for us to treat...”

“Is that true?”

Musk took a deep breath. A part of him was astounded by this memory-erasing technique mentioned, but he still managed to collect himself and asked rationally, “There’s no such thing as a free lunch in the world. What do I have to contribute to become a part of God’s Forbidden Zone?”

“If you decide to join, I’ll grant you tier two qualifications as a member of the Disciple Council.”

Chen Chen said, “The Disciples Council membership shifts every decade, I’m sure you’re aware of the benefits. Your contribution is very simple. We only expect the obligation of mutual aid between the association.”

“It’s that simple?”

“Yes, it truly is just that simple.”

Chen Chen’s expression was sincere.

“Alright, please do show me your company’s new treatment technology first.”

Musk took another deep breath and stated decisively.

“Please come with me.”

Chen Chen smiled quickly and brought Musk to the laboratory where the Medpod 3000 device was hosted.

The Medpod 3000 no longer looked like its previous form as Little X had added another layer of outer shell to it, reiterating its original appearance.

Now this Medpod 3000 device was no different from a regular NMR machine.

The reason this was done was that Chen Chen thought that as he instantiated more items from different movies, there was a larger chance of members of God’s Forbidden Zone speculating theories of their own that they should not have.

After all, having one or two items from movies may be brushed off as a mere coincidence, but what if there were three or four of them? What if he had five or six of them altogether?

Having more of these so-called coincidences may give rise to unexpected scenarios.

With that in mind, the Medpod 3000 was given a layer of disguise and converted to a common NMR machine.

“This machine is our latest developed machine used to repair the human body.”

Chen Chen made an inviting gesture. “Mr. Musk, would you like a demonstration?”

“Demonstration?”

Musk was mildly surprised. “What kind of demonstration?’

Instead of answering the question, Chen Chen snapped his fingers slightly. Immediately after that, the side entrance of the laboratory opened and revealed two Black Knights bringing in a white man.

The white man wore a gray jumpsuit decorated with horizontal white stripes across it. There was a large letter “D” stitched on the jumpsuit. He was incredibly restless and continuously tried to wring himself free.

No matter how hard he resisted, the two men holding him by his arm remained still like iron walls, barely budging at all.

“This...”

Musk was immediately taken aback when he saw this. What happened next would prompt a more drastic reaction from him as he would turn to Chen Chen in utter disbelief.

This was because one of the Black Knights suddenly produced a dagger the length of a finger and started to viciously stab the man’s chest!

One stab, two stabs, three stabs...

The poor man shrieked in anguish as an incomprehensible amount of blood splattered in front of Musk. This caused Musk to stumble backward with his legs quivering uncontrollably.

At first, the white man kept up his endless howling and struggling. After a dozen stabs, blood started seeping out of the corner of his mouth as all the strength was drained out of his body, he could hardly cry anymore.

When he saw a living person dying in front of him, the insides of Musk’s mouth twitched uncontrollably. As a proper entrepreneur, it was the first time that he witnessed something like this. What he witnessed impacted him greatly, but the fact that he could still hold himself firmly in place was a representation of his extremely strong psychological quality.

After that, the two Black Knights propped the man back up and took off his jumpsuit before carrying him to the NMR machine.

Blood was oozing out of the dozens of knife wounds on the man’s chest. The parts of flesh and skin where the dagger hit was turned inside out. The horrific sight further reinforced the reality of the situation.

“Initializing scanning.”

When the machine was activated, a mechanical voice came from within the machine. What followed after was an alert. “Detected critical injury, initializing automated physiological repair function...”

“Repairing...”

After the short alert from the machine, Musk saw the man being pushed into the interior section of the machine. What came after were waves of repeated buzzing. The wounds on the man’s chest were rapidly recovering at a speed visible to the naked eye!

Musk quickly rubbed his eyes only to confirm that what he was seeing was not his eyes playing tricks on him. The depthless marks left behind by the dagger were rapidly recovering until all that was left was a fully-repaired chest covered by the previous pool of blood. The wounds carved by the dagger had completely disappeared without a trace.

On top of that, the man’s chest that had become motionless started to move to the pace of his breathing again. This implied that not only had the wounds healed, but the functionality of his lungs and heart had recovered as well.

“Repair complete. Reinitializing scanning procedures...

“Scanning complete. The user is in a stable physiological state. Lungs functionalities are stable. Heart functionalities are stable. Liver functionalities are stable...”

Before the machine could finish reporting, the two Black Knights rudely pulled the man out from the machine and proceeded to splash cold water on his face.

The rudely awakened man immediately shrieked — he was resurrected.

“Resurrection?”

Musk felt a dryness stemming from his throat. What he was seeing was so ridiculous to a point he wanted to find the fault in it to rebuke it. It had to be a ruse!

However, there was no way for him to prove it...

“No, this isn’t resurrection.”

Chen Chen corrected him. “This fully-automated medical platform cannot bring someone back to life. It should not be feasible for mankind to attain the ability to resurrect someone within the next millennia. The only reason this subject can be brought back to life is that he hadn’t died yet. He had at least a tiny breath of life left in him just now.”

There was nothing Musk could do except to smile bitterly and shake his head.

After that, the man was once again dragged out of the laboratory by the two Black Knights, like livestock being taken away.

“Please don’t label us as some kind of evil corporation, Mr. Musk.”

Chen Chen tapped Musk on the shoulder and stated in what sounded like an attempt to provide comfort, “These subjects are all felons that we acquired via legal means by purchasing them from the Namibian government. I can assure you that it’s legal.”

“...”

There was a slim chance of Musk believing Chen Chen’s claims, but it did serve him well enough to distract him from his upset mood and initial disgust. His eagerness slowly came back to him as he stated, “This machine is truly wondrous. I’d like to give it a shot, provided that it can truly treat the disease lurking inside of me.”

“Don’t worry.”

Chen Chen nodded. “If it can heal drastic wounds, diabetes and high blood pressure won’t be an issue at all. However, I believe I’ve also stated the implications of using this machine.”

Musk fell silent for a moment as if he was contemplating. He negotiated, “I’m willing to pay 100 million dollars to use it just once.”

“Mr. Musk, I’m afraid I don’t have any need for the 100 million dollars.”

Chen Chen waved the offer away. “Even if you offer a billion dollars, the answer is still no.”

“Why?”

Musk objected. “Judging by your casual demonstration, I can conclude that it doesn’t cost a lot to use this fully-automated medical platform. Why would you stubbornly insist on not letting anyone use it then?”

“This branch of technology is as close as it can get to dark technology, is it not?”

Chen Chen smiled and proceeded to explain patiently, “I’m sure you’re well aware of its insurmountable value as what it offers is leaps and bounds above the age-reversal treatment. Not only can it be used to return its user’s body to prime condition, but it can also alleviate tricky diseases. Do you think some kind of technology like this is supposed to exist in real life?

“What happens if the existence of such machinery was exposed to the outside world?”

As Chen Chen said this, he wagged a finger and said, “I cannot let the word of the existence of this machine get out there. Or at the very least, I won’t do it until I have a sure way to protect myself. 100 million dollars isn’t worth the potential risk it entails!”

Musk immediately became quiet when he heard this. However, his vivid blue eyes rapidly expanded and contracted consecutively, clearly hinting that he was fiercely contemplating his next move.

After spending some time ruminating, Musk finally spoke again. He asked with a lowered voice, “So you’re saying that I can only receive this treatment by joining God’s Forbidden Zone?”

“Exactly.”

Chen Chen nodded and looked right into Musk’s eyes. “By joining us, all sorts of diseases, whether it’s cancer, Alzheimer’s disease or diabetes and other terminal illnesses will no longer be a factor in your life. Your body will always remain in its prime and your youth will last until you’re 200-years-old, 300-years-old... Your life will be continuously sustained with mankind’s anti-aging project...”

Chen Chen dragged his voice lower and said with great sincerity, “And the only thing you need to do is to join us, Mr. Musk. With life comes limitless possibilities, don’t you agree?”

chapters.title: Chapter 356: Ultimate Collaboration
chapters.body:
Chapter 356: Ultimate Collaboration

Chapter 356: Ultimate Collaboration

Nobody could resist the allure of extending their lifespan.

Regardless if they were billionaires with more money than they could spend in their lifetimes or the bottom of the barrel stuck in poverty, high position officials possessing great influence or common people just trying to live another day...
