Chen Chen shook his head and when Little X breathed a sigh of relief, Chen Chen suddenly added. “I suspect this is an antimemetic effect.”

“Ah...”

Little X whispered, “It should be impossible, right?”

Chen Chen shook his head and said nothing more.

Dreamland, antimemetic...

Perhaps it was his subconscious that kept warning himself, which led to him figuring out the key factors — antimemes and dreams.

To ordinary people, this was of course a preposterous suggestion. How could the subconscious mind be so intelligent? Nonetheless, for Chen Chen who had taken NZT-48, all this was not impossible...

After all, this drug had never disappointed him.

Chen Chen thought silently.

Still, if this was the case, then all of this would be truly alarming. He had been infected with an antimeme without being aware of it?

However, where did this antimeme come from? The dimension of Gantz?

No, it was impossible!

If it was transmitted from the Gantz dimension, would this not mean that the USB drive did not have the power to shield any memetic effects? Then, it would have been impossible for him to get rid of the entanglement of the memetic effect “Thuds”.

Therefore, these two causal relationships were in conflict.

As such, since it did not originate from the world in Gantz, where did this antimeme come from?

The commercial vehicle drove fast on the flat road and soon left the Eco Science City and moved toward the industrial park.

Inside the vehicle, Chen Chen fell into deep thought.

chapters.title: Chapter 362: Hypnosis
chapters.body:
Chapter 362: Hypnosis

Chapter 362: Hypnosis

When Chen Chen returned to the industrial park where the metal Faraday cage was erected, he could not help but laugh at himself.

He originally thought that he had gotten free of the crisis, but he had never expected that in less than two months, he would have to revisit the place and re-enter this cramped room.

At this time, the metal Faraday cage isolation room was like a Russian doll, one layer after another, with a total of five layers in its structure. At this moment, the five layers were set out separately. The innermost isolation room was only about five meters in diameter, but the second layer next to it reached up to six meters. It was essentially a metal wall with a thickness of one meter that could enclose the innermost layer fully.

The subsequent walls had increasing diameters, with the fifth layer having a diameter of 15 meters.

“The computer, bed, toilet, and daily necessities are all prepared for you.”

Little X said, “Do you need me to help you get the USB flash drive in? If we can’t resolve this, you can try to use the USB flash drive again to shuttle across the different dimensions to avoid the infection of the memetic effect.”

“I don’t need it for the time being, just leave it charging.”

Chen Chen waved his hand. After the second inertial fusion reactor was built at the Spire Experimental Base, the USB drive was transferred from the Blacklight Nuclear Power Plant to the Spire Experimental Base.

The size of this second nuclear fusion reactor was only a little larger than the first but its power generation was more than ten times that of the first, reaching a staggering 2 million kWh. This was almost the same as the Blacklight Nuclear Power Plant.

The reason for this was that Little X had gained the experience of making it for the first time. After all, as the world’s smartest intelligent life, the learning speed of Little X was terrifying. Considering that the second reactor was comparable to the first in terms of size and fuel consumption, the stability of pressure and heat resistance was greatly improved and its Q value could reach more than 200.

At this point, Chen Chen finally got rid of the problem of having insufficient power. Half of the Blacklight Nuclear Power Plant was used to power the Eco Science City and the other half was directly allocated to the USB drive.

The same was true for the Spire Experimental Base. Of the two nuclear fusion reactors, nuclear reactor No. 1, with a power of only 200,000 kilowatts, was responsible for powering the Spire Experimental Base. The newly constructed one was fully used to charge the USB drive.

Therefore, the speed at which the USB drive was absorbing power had tripled, which was already extremely impressive.

“X, prepare a set of questionnaires and send them to all Blacklight Biotechnology employees. Tell them it’s just a psychological diagnosis and treatment, and find out if they have any unusual dreams recently.”

Before entering the isolation room, Chen Chen gave a final order. He was still not at ease. After all, if it was something antimemetic, the damage to human society would be on par with a memetic effect.

“Got it.”

Little X promised. “Please be rest assured. Not only that, but after you enter, I’ll also bring over the Gantz server I’m stationed in. If you’re infected with an antimeme, maybe I can suppress it within the Death Radius.”

“Okay.”

Chen Chen exhaled and walked into the all-metal isolation room.

The room was still powered on as before. Under the dim light, there were only two small metal beds, a table, a laptop on the table, and a toilet in the corner. It was the same layout as when he came back from Gantz’s dimension.

The reason there were two beds was that someone must help Chen Chen with his hypnosis task. Chen Chen could not possibly hypnotize himself.

However, after hypnosis, it was very likely that the antimeme would be triggered, resulting in various unknown effects, so the hypnotist must not leave.

Of course, under the control of Little X, they could use a Black Knight as the hypnotist, so there would be no problem.

At this time, Chen Chen closed the door of the isolation room and locked himself inside. Then, he sat directly on a bed to the right and lay down comfortably with his arm as a pillow.

The temperature and humidity in the isolation room had been kept at optimal conditio, and there were special ventilation facilities. Living in it was like staying in a hotel.

More than ten minutes later, Little X’s voice came from the computer on the desk, “Your Godfather, my body has been shipped over, and the Death Radius has enveloped the isolation room.”

“Then, let’s get ready to start.”

Chen Chen nodded and said calmly.

“Yes.”

After Little X agreed, the door of the isolation room was opened again and then a slight fragrance wafted in. Chen Chen instinctively turned his head and suddenly saw a girl, about 1.6 meters tall and wearing clothes for men that were several sizes larger than her, walked in from outside the door.

Chen Chen: ???

“Sir Godfather, it’s me...”

What surprised Chen Chen even more was that Little X’s voice came out directly from the mouth of the little girl in front of him. As this was a real person, there was a touch of softness in her voice. “Let me be the one to hypnotize you next!”

Chen Chen looked on with amazement and saw that the girl was about fourteen or fifteen years old. Her appearance could be described as stunning as her skin was astonishingly smooth as if even the lightest touch would shatter it. At the same time, she had long black silken hair, which even in the dim light continued to exude a fine luster.

In terms of dressing, the girl wore clothes that Chen Chen once wore. She wore a white T-shirt as her top. As the size was too large, the shirt fell directly on her knees and the sleeves completely covered the little girl’s hands.

Her lower body was clad in a pair of navy blue jeans. As this was oversized too, the pant legs had buried the little girl’s feet, revealing only a few pale and lovely toes.

“This is a Black Knight?”

Chen Chen constantly stared at her in surprise. No matter how he looked at it, the little girl in front of him was not as rigid as the group of Black Knights. Instead, she was alive, spirited, and full of health.

“This body is A6-853 from the sixth batch of Black Knights that just came out of the incubators.”

The little girl nodded and pointed to her head. “But unlike other Black Knights, I slightly improved the God chip implanted in this body so that it can receive more information from me and this body can also transmit more feedback, just as if my mind was completely attached to this body. Thus, it seems more real.”

Only then did Chen Chen understand.

To put it bluntly, with normal Black Knights, Little X did not directly control them. Instead, it was akin to having separated itself into non-thinking clones and then controlling them indirectly, so they looked extremely stiff, like a robot.

As for the little girl in front of Chen Chen, Little X had enlarged the channel of information flow and was commanding it with full attention, so she was more like a human being.

However, if one looked closely, one could still see traces of a Black Knight. After all, the little girl had been optimized to be too perfect. Whether it was her skin or hair or body, even the pheromones she emitted, it was beyond the extent of ordinary human health.

Just like Chen Chen today — when someone’s genes had been optimized by the T-virus, no matter where they went, even in a sea of people, they would have the most prominent presence.

However, this little girl, the Black Knight, was not made without Chen Chen’s knowledge because when the Black Knights were cultivated, they must be cultivated at all ages. Not only the combat Black Knights, but even the spying Black Knights would also require this.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen could not help feeling a little speechless. After all, the original plan was to lock himself and the hypnotist together, but Little X had specially selected a girl’s body. Did it have something impure in mind?

Nonetheless, on second thought, the gender and age of Little X were indeed those of a young girl. This was set when Little X was created. At this time, Little X chose this little girl as an avatar and even changed her voice to her original voice. Therefore, this could be justified.

Maybe he was just thinking too much...

Chen Chen shook his head and could not be bothered to think about this topic anymore. Instead, he lay down and accepted his fate.

“Don’t worry. Just leave everything to me.”

Little X asked in a light clear voice, “Sir Godfather, are you ready?”

“Okay, we can start.”

Chen Chen nodded.

Following Chen Chen’s answer, he immediately felt a sense of being suspended. He knew that the isolation room was being lifted by a crane. After being lifted, the entire isolation room was placed into the second-level isolation room.

Then the third layer, then the fourth...

It was like a Russian doll. In the end, Chen Chen was enclosed tightly by a five-layer alloy wall. It was more than ten meters thick. Unless a nuclear weapon was detonated at close range, there was really nothing in the world that could break open this wall by force.

This was what Chen Chen did to combat the “Thuds” memetic effect. This Faraday cage could not help trapping Chen Chen and also provide protection to Chen Chen.

After all the outer walls were completely locked, Little X began to move.

It seemed that all this was rather novel to her. At this time, Little X squatted and put a silver medicine box on the ground. Somewhat awkwardly, she took out the syringe and ampoule from the medicine box and drew the liquid into the syringe.

“First of all, we’ll give you an injection to put you in a completely relaxed state!”

Little X gave a reminder, then took out a rubber band and tied it around Chen Chen’s left arm. After finding the vein, she plunged the needle in.

These needles were all reinforced to ensure that they could penetrate the skin of a T-virus host.

Chen Chen was injected with diazepam, a commonly used tranquilizer with fewer side effects. However, due to his physical attributes, Little X finally injected Chen Chen with five times the normal dose.

At this time, the efficacy of NZT-48 had completely passed. After the injection, Chen Chen only felt a soothing sensation slowly spreading from his arm to his whole body.

Suddenly, Chen Chen only felt that his body became limp as if he was lying on a yacht while the yacht was floating in an endless sea. The sound of ethereal waves soothed his ears and the blue sky and ocean were in front of him...

At this time, Chen Chen’s eyes gradually changed from lively to vacant.

“Chen Chen...”

At this moment, the girl’s pure face suddenly appeared in Chen Chen’s field of vision but Chen Chen did not seem to notice anything at this time. He still looked forward blankly, his eyes staring past the girl’s cheek toward a certain point in the blank space.

“Chen Chen, look at me...”

The girl looked down at Chen Chen and said in a voice as faint as a mosquito, “Look into my eyes.”

At this point, Chen Chen’s eyes flickered slightly. His eyes focused a little and he stared closely at the girl’s deep pupils.

“Now you’ve returned to the original darkness. You’re slowly wandering in the ocean of memory, and in front of you, there’s an iceberg of memory...”

The girl whispered sweetly in Chen Chen’s ear, using a huskier tone of voice, “Listen to my voice... Don’t be afraid of being lost, don’t be afraid of the darkness because my voice will show you the way and my eyes will light up everything. You’ll go to that iceberg of memory under my guidance...

“When I count from one to ten, you’ll walk into the iceberg and return to the night when you merged with the T-virus...”

Little X took a deep breath and was just about to start counting when she suddenly thought of something.

In the next second, Little X took a deep breath and slowly lowered her head as if she was plucking up courage. In the next second, she gave Chen Chen a gentle peck on the lips.

Suddenly, her cheeks flamed red, almost to her ears.

At the same time, she whispered, “Chen Chen, please remember what you felt just now. If ‘it’ wants to hurt you, come back immediately through the anchor!”

After she finished speaking, Little X sighed in relief. “Okay, let’s get started! Ten, nine, eight, seven, six, five, four...

“Three.

“Two.

“One.”

“Clatter!”

Chen Chen’s hand loosened and he sat up, startled.

What happened? He was paying attention to class, why did he suddenly drift off?

Chen Chen frowned. He lowered his head and looked, but saw that his gel pen had fallen under the table because of his dazed state earlier.

With no alternative, Chen Chen had to bend down from his seat and pick up the gel pen.

At the moment Chen Chen bent over, he subconsciously glanced at the bottom of the table, only to see a dark glint flashing past.

It was a black USB flash drive.

After picking up his pen, Chen Chen picked at the USB drive. With a click, the USB drive embedded in the groove at the bottom of the table was easily plucked out.

He must get up quickly, otherwise, the professor would call his name...

Suddenly, this thought occurred to him. Chen Chen quickly straightened up, exhaled, and sat obediently on the seat.

“That student... Uh.”

On the podium, old professor Wang Xi opened his mouth and then changed his words, “The student in the last row, please answer...”

“Its name is the Hayflick limit!””It, it should be Hayflick limit...”

Two voices, one male and one female, rang out simultaneously behind Chen Chen.

After hearing this, Chen Chen subconsciously glanced back, only to see Wang Wei, the leader of his dormitory, standing there awkwardly. Behind him, a cute-looking girl had also stood up. Both seemed to have thought that the professor was calling them, so they stood up and answered at the same time.

Chen Chen instantly snickered to himself at their embarrassment.

What happened next had nothing to do with Chen Chen. Seeing that the professor had looked away from him, Chen Chen looked down at the prize in his hand.

Sure enough, it was a USB flash drive.

It was just different from an ordinary USB flash drive. The length of this USB flash drive was much longer. It measured twelve or thirteen centimeters and looked like a carbon rod.

In addition, the entire USB drive was completely integrated. It was black all over without even any logos or storage size.

Still, seeing how cool it appeared, Chen Chen ultimately put the USB flash drive into his trouser pocket, planning to look at it closer back in his dorm.

Time flew by.

Soon, it was time for the class to end. As Professor Wang Xi on the podium verbally dismissed the class, the entire classroom suddenly became “alive” and everyone crowded together noisily. Chen Chen even saw that many people were rushing toward the back row.

In the last row of the classroom, a sweet-looking young girl was surrounded by a group of perverts, including Chen Chen’s three dorm mates.

Chen Chen paid no mind to those sex fiends. He went straight back to Dorm 404, turned on the computer, and inserted the USB drive at the same time.

After waiting for a while, Chen Chen clicked on [My Computer], but found that there was nothing displayed in the column of removable storage devices except for the DVD drive.

The drive letter of the USB drive that should have been automatically recognized was missing.

The USB drive was broken?

Chen Chen could not believe this, so he inserted it again, but there was still no response.

Therefore, Chen Chen simply relied on a familiar computer operation. He merely pulled up the USB installation program on the computer, so that the drive letter of the USB drive appeared.

At this time, Chen Chen opened the USB flash drive, full of expectation. After all, perhaps hidden there was some secret code in the USB flash drive, who could say...

However, when the USB drive was opened, Chen Chen was stunned. Following that, he immediately felt disappointed.

There was nothing in the USB flash drive.

This USB flash drive was empty...

chapters.title: Chapter 363: Usb Drive
chapters.body:
Chapter 363: Usb Drive

Chapter 363: USB Drive

“What? After so much effort, I get nothing...”

Chen Chen silently closed the window and right-clicked to open the properties of the USB flash drive but saw that the total capacity of the USB flash drive was displayed as unknown.

“So, the USB drive is broken?”

Chen Chen thought for a while and was just about to transfer some files to test out the USB flash drive. However, he heard a flurry of footsteps approaching from a distance. Then, the dormitory door was kicked open with a “blam” and Li Bo walked in doing the latest viral dance.

“Lil Chen, why’d you come back so early today?”

Li Bo yawned and sat next to Chen Chen. “Why did you dash off after class just now? The class leader was distributing admission tickets to the campus evening party at the back of the classroom.”

“Isn’t the campus party organized by the school? What admission ticket do I need?”

Chen Chen said in objection.

“This time, the evening party is set up in the auditorium. It can only hold one-third of the school’s people. How can everyone possibly join?”

Li Bo shook his head and said, “Moreover, many celebrities have been invited to this party, so there are only ten tickets for a class.”

“You guys can go then.”

Chen Chen yawned and waved his hand. “I’m not going anymore. I might as well game a few rounds if I have this idle time.”

At this moment, Wang Wei and Zhou Jie also returned to the dormitory. Suddenly, there was a raucous atmosphere in the dormitory. The four of them kept chatting about the campus party a few days later, but Chen Chen found out that none of them had even grabbed an admission ticket to the campus party.

“Come on, we’d still do what we gotta do.”

The dorm leader, Wang Wei, shrugged, and suddenly let out a “Huh” sound. “Lil Chen, what is that black thing on your computer?”

“A USB flash drive that I picked up in the classroom.”

Chen Chen unplugged the USB flash drive and threw it to Wang Wei. “Take it if you like it.”

“It’s not like I don’t have one.”

Wang Wei handed the USB flash drive to Zhou Jie, who was beside him. “This USB flash drive looks pretty good.”

“Is there any good stuff in it?”

Zhou Jie’s eyes suddenly lit up. He quickly took out his computer and inserted the USB flash drive in.

“Nope, I’ve checked. There’s nothing inside and I can’t even see its capacity. It’s probably corrupted.”

Chen Chen gave a warning. Seeing that Zhou Jie was still enthusiastically studying the USB drive, Chen Chen did not continue to mind him.

Soon enough, there was a “ding” from Zhou Jie’s computer.

“Oh, is it really broken?”

Zhou Jie complained, “I tried to upload some files and a ‘Program Error’ window popped up directly! What a waste of its cool appearance.”

Hearing this, Chen Chen’s heart suddenly jolted. He seemed to have thought of something and said, without rhyme nor reason, “Maybe you can try a movie?”

“No files can be copied in, so of course, movies are impossible.”

Zhou Jie grumbled but he still followed Chen Chen’s words.

In a little while, Zhou Jie suddenly chuckled and said excitedly, “Who said it was broken? Isn’t it working just fine?”

Chen Chen reflexively raised his head. Wang Wei and Li Bo on the side also looked at Zhou Jie’s computer subconsciously, only to see that four avi-format movies had been put in the computer at this point.

[Ju-on.avi.], [Final Destination.avi.], [One Missed Call.avi], [Ring.avi.].

“D*mn, how come you put this kind of movie in, Nostril?”

Wang Wei said in puzzlement, “Do you like watching horror movies too?”

“They’re okay. Didn’t Lil Bo say he was looking for excitement before? So I downloaded them. It has been several days and I kept forgetting to watch them.”

Zhou Jie said.

Chen Chen just remembered that the reason Zhou Jie said this was because the school’s campus network was billed by time and the cost was not low. These guys in Chen Chen’s dormitory often ran out of time before the end of the month, so to reduce their online time and use less of the campus network, they all agreed to launch the “Night Cinema” activity.

After 11 o’clock at night every day, the four of them would only keep one computer connected to the Internet and everyone would sit in front of this computer to watch a movie, which was also considered team-building.

In terms of film selection, everyone in the dormitory had proclaimed the “three no’s” principle — no watching romantic dramas, no watching idol dramas, and no watching literary movies. After all, a group of grown men sitting together, watching those sort of sentimental, long-winded movies — well, if something happened, no one could bear the blame.

Apart from those, they could watch anything else. Nonetheless, Chen Chen preferred science fiction and disaster movies while dorm leader Wang Wei preferred horror and slasher movies. Zhou Jie’s taste was similar to Wang Wei’s while Li Bo prefers Hong Kong movies from the last century.

Still, fortunately, the four of them had a common movie category that they liked, that was, the X-rated genre.

However, this genre was more suitable for viewing alone. It was inevitable that one would lose the mood if they watched it together. Therefore, when they were together, they still watched Hollywood blockbusters most of the time.

At this time, Li Bo, who was more afraid of ghosts, said with a guilty conscience, “No way, Nostril, do you want everyone to watch these four movies?”

“Of course.”

Zhou Jie smugly put the USB flash drive on the table. “Now, we even have a shared USB flash drive. In the future, you can upload any movies you want to play for ‘Night Cinema’ so that we can even save the last bit of campus network time!”

“Not bad. That’s more like it.”

Wang Wei was also extremely excited. “These films are much better than European and American slasher films. Europe and the United States don’t understand horror. They either scare people using jump scares or fill the screen with blood and gore or make some furniture fly around. It’s getting so old. For real horror, you still need to go for Japanese horror!”

Everyone immediately started talking in full swing.

However, the enthusiastic group did not notice Chen Chen, who was sitting opposite, had turned pale at this time. He stared at the USB flash drive on the table but suddenly felt an inexplicable throbbing in his heart.

He felt like after moving the horror movies into this USB flash drive, something bad would happen...

This ominous premonition was getting heavier. Dorm leader Wang Wei finally noticed that something was wrong. He asked with concern, “Lil Chen, are you feeling sick?”

“I’m fine...”

Chen Chen shook his head. He was about to take the USB flash drive and throw it away but Zhou Jie, who was opposite him, picked up the USB flash drive again and started fighting with Li Bo.

Seeing this, Chen Chen had to give up.

Soon, it was noon and four of them went to the cafeteria to have lunch. Then, they returned to the dormitory, laughing and talking, and lay down in bed.

In this dormitory, Chen Chen and Wang Wei had always had the habit of taking a lunch break but Li Bo and Zhou Jie did not have this habit. However, during their lunch break, they would also tacitly lower the sound of the computer, so as not to affect the other two.

After having lunch, the foreboding in Chen Chen’s heart had gradually disappeared. Finally, he looked at Zhou Jie and the USB flash drive next to the computer. He gave up on thinking anymore and just closed his eyes.

Soon, Chen Chen fell asleep.

In the beginning, Chen Chen slept extremely comfortably because he found that today’s dormitory was exceptionally quiet as if he was the only one in the entire dormitory.

However, for some reason, after falling asleep, Chen Chen only felt that his whole body was getting colder. He had already covered himself with the quilt but there was still an inexplicable chill in the dormitory.

Not only that but as he drifted between sleep and wakefulness, Chen Chen seemed to see something stretching over the upper bunk and approaching his face bit by bit. However, when the thing was about to reach Chen Chen’s head, the dormitory’s broadcaster suddenly blared.

It was the wake-up call at two o’clock in the afternoon.

Chen Chen opened his eyes quickly but saw a flash of something white suddenly retracting into the upper bunk. It looked to be a hand. Chen Chen just sat up, yawned heavily, and subconsciously looked at the opposite side.

What Chen Chen saw was that on the opposite table, Zhou Jie was sitting in front of the computer with a slack expression. His face white as if he had been attacked by something...

“What’s wrong, Nostril?”

At this time, the rest also got up one after another, and suddenly noticed Zhou Jie who was slumped in front of the computer, so they started to inquire with concern.

Even so, Zhou Jie was like a wooden man. Even if he heard everyone’s inquiries, he did not respond.

“Nostril, Nostril!”

Wang Wei quickly got out of bed, walked in front of Zhou Jie, and waved his hand before Zhou Jie’s eyes. At this point, Zhou Jie opened a pair of dull eyes and slowly turned his head to look at Wang Wei.

“Nostril, what’s wrong with you?”

Chen Chen also got out of bed. He subconsciously walked around Zhou Jie, only to see that the computer in front of Zhou Jie was turned on and the computer was displaying a “Program Error” window. On the right side of the computer, the black USB flash drive was plugged in...

“Did you watch these movies?”

Chen Chen’s heart sank. He immediately grabbed the mouse and crossed out the program error window. Then, he selected all four movies on the USB drive and pressed Ctrl+D at the same time.

With a “swoosh”, all four movies were deleted!

After doing all this, Chen Chen secretly breathed a sigh of relief. Although he did not understand where his inner anxiety came from, for some reason, this USB flash drive always felt evil to him.

“I’m fine!”

Just then, Zhou Jie also stood up suddenly, smiled stiffly at everyone, then turned around and walked outside the dormitory.

After he had left the dormitory, Chen Chen, Wang Wei, and Li Bo looked at each other.

“What happened to Nostril?”

“Maybe he’s sick or is he just scared by those four horror films?”

“It shouldn’t be that. Nostril isn’t so timid...”

The three of them exchanged a few words casually without much thought. After that, they packed their things, left the dormitory, and walked toward the teaching building.

When the three of them entered the classroom and saw Zhou Jie sitting in his seat as usual, everyone felt completely relieved.

Soon, the class teacher hurried in and the class began.

Chen Chen listened to the class as usually did, but for some reason, as he sat in class, Chen Chen felt a tingling sensation at his back.

It was as if someone was staring at him...

Chen Chen looked back subconsciously but was immediately met with a pair of clear and deep pupils.

Was that the girl he saw in the morning?

At this time, the little girl was sitting alone in the back row of the classroom, in the same column as Chen Chen. As Chen Chen turned his head abruptly, the two looked at each other by coincidence.

Chen Chen saw a stunned look on the girl’s face before she widened her eyes and smiled at him.

Chen Chen reluctantly squeezed out an awkward smile and quickly turned his head back.

In the next class, Chen Chen distinctly felt that the cold tingle on his back had lessened significantly. This was because his head was filled with the girl’s smile. He did not know why, but her eyes seemed to have seared deeply into his heart.

It was nothing like a crush, but a feeling of pity.

Chen Chen did not know why he had to pity her. He just forced himself to finish the class. After the teacher had left, he stood up and started to pack his textbooks.

However, Zhou Jie in front of him moved faster. He picked up the textbooks and left the classroom right after class.

“Lil Chen, I feel something is wrong with Nostril. I’ll go take a look. Help me take my books back.”

The dorm leader Wang Wei quickly patted Chen Chen on the shoulder. Before Chen Chen could agree, he also walked off.

“I’ll go take a look too.” Li Bo also waved to Chen Chen, then turned and left.

Chen Chen thought nothing much of this. He took the notebooks and textbooks of the two of them and put them together with his own.

However, just as Chen Chen bowed his head to pack up the books, a burst of unknown fragrance came to him.

“Hello there!”

There was a clear voice by his ear. Chen Chen raised his head subconsciously, only to see the girl standing directly in front of him. She was looking at him with a smile on her face.

“Hey there...”

Chen Chen replied instinctively, “What’s up, classmate?”

“I’m a new transfer student. Can I be friends with you?” the girl asked enthusiastically.

“Be friends?”

Chen Chen was astounded. As a member of the loser gang, he knew his place in the social hierarchy. He was one of those who would become invisible in a crowd of people. In other words, he had a very common face. Thus, ever since a young age, he had never encountered a situation where a girl took the initiative to strike up a conversation with him.

Not only that, but because of his relatively introverted personality, although Chen Chen could be said to be experienced, it was all in theory. He had never touched a girl’s hand.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen glanced around subconsciously but saw several people also looking over in astonishment, seemingly wondering why he would be favored by the girl. He quickly lowered his head and smiled dryly. “Classmate, shouldn’t you first make friends with those of the same sex?”

“It’s all the same.”

The girl giggled and then bounced out of the classroom.

Seeing this, Chen Chen felt completely relieved. He was truly a little lost in the face of the girl’s sudden enthusiasm.

Holding a thick stack of books back to the dormitory building, Chen Chen panted and walked up to the fourth floor. When he opened the door of Dorm 404 with the key, Chen Chen suddenly froze.

He should have felt completely relieved but for some reason, when he stepped into the dormitory he was familiar with, his hairs suddenly stood up all over his body and he got goosebumps without warning!

He had never felt this way.

“What’s up with that?”

Chen Chen frowned and could only bite the bullet and walked into the dormitory. He threw the books directly on everyone’s beds and then sat back on his own bed.

He had gradually realized that ever since today or strictly speaking, since he picked up the black USB flash drive, he had been feeling rather peculiar.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen subconsciously looked at the location of Zhou Jie’s computer. Sure enough, the USB flash drive was still plugged into Zhou Jie’s laptop.

Could it be that something was wrong with this USB drive?

Chen Chen could not figure this out. He simply took advantage of the fact that everyone in the dormitory had not come back and walked directly to Zhou Jie’s computer, moving as if he was about to unplug the USB flash drive.

However, the instant Chen Chen touched the USB flash drive, a pale arm suddenly stretched out from beyond Chen Chen’s line of sight and grabbed Chen Chen’s hand...

Chen Chen’s pupils contracted quickly!

chapters.title: Chapter 364: Ghostly Encounter
chapters.body:
Chapter 364: Ghostly Encounter

Chapter 364: Ghostly Encounter

The moment that hand touched Chen Chen, he withdrew his hand as if he had gotten an electric shock. He raised his head, flabbergasted, but there was no one in front of him.

Even so, Chen Chen still felt numb all over because at the moment that hand touched him, a bone-piercing cold swept across his body. The muscles of his entire hand were still constantly trembling.

That was not a human hand!

Chen Chen was full of shock. While holding his right hand, he moved back step by step. By now, even the biggest idiot would understand that there was something wrong with this dormitory...

However, when he retreated to the door, he suddenly felt himself bumping against a person. Chen Chen’s heart was seized again. He jumped nervously and at the same time turned quickly, only to see Zhou Jie standing at the door of the dormitory.

“Ah, so it’s Chen Chen?”

Zhou Jie’s face was stupefied like a dead man’s expression with only one pair of eyes staring at Chen Chen. He asked mechanically, “Chen Chen, when did you come back?”

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes, still guarding against Zhou Jie, but then he suddenly thought of everything that happened before. His heart leaped and he moved directly back to Zhou Jie’s desk, then tried to pick up the USB flash drive again.

This time, Zhou Jie still maintained a sluggish expression. He just watched Chen Chen unplug the USB flash drive, but still did not move.

“Phew, my gamble paid off...”

Chen Chen secretly breathed a sigh of relief only when he had the USB flash drive firmly in his grasp.

“Chen Chen, when did you come back?”

At this moment, Zhou Jie asked again, still in the same tone as before.

It was already time for classes to end and Zhou Jie stood at the door of the dormitory, blocking the way. However, Chen Chen saw that behind Zhou Jie, there were other students in the dormitory passing by, talking and laughing. Thus, without hesitating, Chen Chen raised one leg suddenly and kicked Zhou Jie in the gut!

“Go ask your grandmother!”

In one move, Chen Chen had kicked Zhou Jie to the ground.

With this opportunity, Chen Chen fled from the dormitory and ran down the stairs.

What Chen Chen did not know was that, after he left, several students in the next class saw Zhou Jie being knocked down, and immediately took the initiative to help him up. They brought Zhou Jie, who still wore a slack expression, back into Dorm 404.

After they entered, there seemed to be a gust of wind blowing past the corridors and then with a “blam”, the door of Dorm 404 was slowly closed by the wind...

Chen Chen ran out of the dormitory building, out of breath. Only then did he look back while panting heavily.

Fortunately, Zhou Jie did not run after him.

Chen Chen tightly gripped the USB flash drive in his hand, barely aware of how disordered and forlorn he looked. This attracted the attention of students who were moving in and out of the dormitory.

“Lil Chen, what’s wrong?”

Still, after a while, Chen Chen heard the voices of Wang Wei and Li Bo. He looked up at this sound and saw that they were standing before him. “Lil Chen, have you seen Zhou Jie?”

“He... Went back to the dormitory.”

Chen Chen opened his mouth but the words on the tip of his tongue were swallowed abruptly, and then he said again, “Wang Wei, Li Bo, I have something very important to tell you. Come here.”

With that, Chen Chen turned and walked toward the school cafeteria.

“If he has something to say, can’t he say it in the dormitory? Why so mysterious?”

Wang Wei was a little curious. He and Li Bo looked at each other. After hesitating, the two followed Chen Chen.

Chen Chen secretly breathed a sigh of relief when he saw that they were willing to follow. He finally glanced toward the fourth floor, but he immediately stiffened. A chill surged directly to the top of his head and his entire scalp almost flew off the top...

This was because, in the glance just now, he saw that on the window of his dormitory, a woman with a pale body and disheveled hair was sprawled across the window, staring in his direction.

From that angle, Chen Chen knew, without even thinking about it, that she was staring at him!

What in the world was that thing? Was it the legendary...

Chen Chen took a deep breath, said nothing, but went straight to the cafeteria and sat down.

“Lil Chen, why do you look so agitated?”

Wang Wei asked with concern. He took out his campus card and handed it to Li Bo, who was aside. “Lil Bo, go buy some spicy rolls for Lil Chen to perk up his spirits.”

“...”

Chen Chen waved his hand, indicating that he did not need it. He walked to the shop on the side and bought three bottles of Sprite, then placed it before the two of them. He opened one of them before he said, “Didn’t you hunt down Zhou Jie after class? Why’d you let him come back alone?”

“As soon as we ran out, we found that Nostril had vanished without a trace.”

Speaking of this, Wang Wei was also a little depressed. “God knows how that guy could run so fast, but we thought about it again — since he can run so fast, it means nothing is wrong. So we just ignored him and went to the cafeteria to eat, before heading back.”

“Then we bumped into you downstairs.”

Li Bo added.

After listening, Chen Chen shook his head. He did not know where to start for a moment. He simply took out the USB flash drive and placed it in front of the two of them. “I’m going to say something that I know might be difficult for you to believe. You might even think that I’m crazy, but I hope that no matter how outrageous or absurd I sound, you’ll finish listening to what I said.”

Hearing this, Wang Wei and Li Bo turned slightly solemn. They solemnly looked at the USB drive on the table, only to observe that the USB flash drive had become extremely old-looking since some unknown time as if it was picked up from the trash. There was none of its noontime luster.

However, their attention was not on this matter at this time, so Wang Wei just said, “Say anything you want. We’re all brothers, of course, we trust you.”

Chen Chen nodded. He looked around and checked that no one was paying attention to them. Then he whispered, “Brother Wei, Lil Bo, there’s a problem with the USB drive I picked up today...”

The two did not speak but looked fixedly at Chen Chen.

“Did you feel something was off when you fell asleep at noon today?”

Chen Chen asked, “For example, did you feel that the air in the dormitory was getting cold?”

“Hmm?”

Both of them were taken aback. After that, Wang Wei said immediately, “Now that you’ve mentioned it, I did feel that. I was sleeping at the time, but it seemed to be getting colder in the dormitory. Although it’s now November, it shouldn’t be so cold. I even thought someone had turned on the air conditioner.”

“Me too.”

Li Bo nodded affirmatively. “Not only that, but I also dreamed of a head poking up from the lower bunk. At first, I thought it was Lil Chen playing a prank, but after I woke up, I realized that wasn’t right because that head had long hair and appeared to be a woman. It didn’t look like Lil Chen at all!”

Chen Chen was shaken to his core and immediately said, “I also felt what Lil Bo felt. When I was sleeping, I sensed something stretching down from the upper bunk, but when I woke up, there was nothing there...”

“Hold on.”

Wang Wei promptly pointed to Chen Chen and Li Bo in astonishment. “Lil Chen, you’re on the lower bunk and Lil Bo is on the upper bunk, but both of you feel, at the same time, that there’s something strange in the upper and lower bunks. So, what is it that can hide between the upper bunk and the lower bunk?”

“Some things can’t be explained with physics.”

Chen Chen took a sip of Sprite again. “Remember, Zhou Jie uploaded four movies on this USB flash drive and then that peculiar incident happened at noon. Plus, after we woke up, we saw Zhou Jie sitting in front of the computer...”

“So?”

Both of them asked immediately.

“...”

Chen Chen’s words suddenly halted. A series of images flashed in his mind, but they were not of his previous encounter. Rather, they seemed totally illogical.

In the scene, he seemed to be attempting something bit by bit, slowly starting to test the rules of the USB drive...

“Tss...”

Chen Chen only felt a sharp pain in his head. He quickly covered his head and cried out in pain.

“Lil Chen, Lil Chen?”

Their concerned voices rang by his ears, but Chen Chen could not answer anymore. Scenes were flashing in his mind, all related to the USB flash drive, but when he thought about it, these images and what they portrayed had no connection to reality as if they happened in another time and space...

This pain came and went quickly. When Chen Chen recovered from his dazed collapse, the sharp pain in his mind had disappeared as it came, without warning.

In Chen Chen’s mind, there were only a few strange memories...

“D*mn...”

Chen Chen panted heavily and his face darkened because these few memories were related to the USB flash drive. If the rules in the memory were true, would it not mean that...

Thinking of this, Chen Chen only felt his scalp numb. He said to the two of them, “Zhou Jie has played the four horror films. The four horror films are Ju-on, Final Destination, One Missed Call, and Ring, so our dormitory has now become a gathering place for those malevolent ghosts!”

Wang Wei and Li Bo both stared at each other with wide eyes for a moment, then Wang Wei forced a smile and hugged himself. “Lil Chen, don’t tell me that what you said is true.”

“I know it’s hard to believe, but when you were eating just now, I was the first to return. After returning to the dormitory, I saw something that’s difficult to describe in words.”

Chen Chen finally advised, “Anyway, I won’t step into Dorm 404 anymore. I’ve told you everything I should and I’ll stop here. If you still want to go back, I will not say a word to dissuade you.”

As he spoke, Chen Chen put away the USB flash drive. He no longer planned to hand it to the two of them.

If those few rules in his mind were true...

Chen Chen’s heart suddenly started to pound restlessly.

“You’re truly amazing, man.”

Li Bo said in awe, “Lil Chen, if someone else told me this, I’ll beat him up, but the way you said it was so real that I’m almost convinced by you.”

Chen Chen sighed secretly. He knew that since Li Bo said this, it showed that the other guy still did not believe Chen Chen.

Wang Wei suddenly thought of something. He slapped his thigh instantly, took out his cell phone and said, “Forget it, no matter what, I’ll call Nostril. Lil Chen, didn’t you say that Nostril has returned to the dormitory? I’m going to ask him to come out and ask him what’s going on.”

With that, he got ready to dial Zhou Jie’s phone number.

Chen Chen’s eyes turned completely cold. He stood up without hesitation, turned, and left the canteen without hesitation.

“Lil Chen? Lil Chen?”

Chen Chen ignored the calls from the two behind him. He knew that if they did not see those things with their own eyes, those two would never believe him.

However, after they encountered those things, would they still be lucky enough to survive like him?

Chen Chen did not know.

However, Chen Chen knew that he would not waste time on people who did not believe him. When humans wanted to die, no one could stop them. Even if one tried to hold back those people by force, they would drag one along before falling into the abyss, pulling one down into the abyss of death as well...

Thinking of this, the panicked tremor in Chen Chen’s eyes vanished completely, replaced by a kind of absolute reason and coldness. Even Chen Chen himself could not understand where this kind of reason and coldness came from.

It was as if he should have been this rational and aloof all along.

“According to the rules about the USB flash drive that appeared in my mind, the USB drive can extract many movie props. Then, what I need most now is...”

Chen Chen thought for a while and walked straight out of the campus. He looked at the sun gradually setting westward and his expression became slightly solemn. He knew that he must act quickly.

It was not until Chen Chen had left that Wang Wei and Li Bo looked at each other with a hint of helplessness in their eyes.

“Brother Wei, do you think what Lil Chen said is true?”

Li Bo hesitated and asked, “Although I don’t believe in all that, something felt wrong at noon today, plus Nostril and Lil Chen are now behaving like this...”

“Let me call Nostril and ask.”

Wang Wei sighed. He looked at Zhou Jie’s number on the phone, hesitated for a moment, and pressed the dial button.

However, when he was pressing the dial button, there was no call interface on the phone. Instead, the screen showed the caller ID interface and at the same time, a clear and lovely ringtone rang out.

“La la la la~ La la la la~ La la la la~ La la la la~ La la la la~ La la la la~ La~ La~ La~ La~ La la la~ La la la~ La la la~ La la la~...”

Wang Wei subconsciously looked at the phone but saw that Zhou Jie’s number was displayed on the screen.

“Huh...”

However, Wang Wei frowned and could not help wondering aloud. “This isn’t my ringtone...”

...

Ten minutes later, Chen Chen came to an Internet cafe.

After proficiently arranging for an hour of computer time at the front desk, Chen Chen found a computer in the corner and then inserted the USB drive.

Next, Chen Chen directly opened the movie inventory that came with the computer, searched for a ghost film from the Lam Ching Ying series, and dragged it to the USB drive.

“Ding!”

The sound of a program error came from the headset. The USB drive rejected the movie.

“Since I can’t put a movie about exorcism into the USB flash drive, then the rules in my memory are true...”

Chen Chen frowned. Although he had verified a rule, he did not feel any excitement. Instead, his heart sank.

Having thought of this, he tried a few movies that featured superpowers. In the end, a few such as Chronicle, Push, and The Boys were copied in while others like The Flash, Spider-Man, Batman, Iron Man, and Captain America were rejected by the USB drive.

“The Marvel series and the DC series can’t be placed into the USB flash drive, confirmed.”

Chen Chen verified a rule again and he immediately understood that there was no chance for him to gain superpowers in one step.

In the several movies that could be placed into the USB flash drive, the acquisition of superpowers was very demanding. Moreover, if the rules in his mind were not deceiving him, at this time, after the supernatural entities in those movies were released, the USB flash drive was left without the slightest energy...

The sign that the USB drive had run out of energy was that it would look extremely old and battered.

This was what the USB drive appeared to be now.

chapters.title: Chapter 365: Strategic Plan
chapters.body:
Chapter 365: Strategic Plan

Chapter 365: Strategic Plan

The USB drive had no energy.

Chen Chen sighed to himself, then plugged out the USB drive listlessly and tossed it back into his pocket.

Although the rules about the USB flash drive that appeared in his mind were extremely outrageous and even his knowledge about the USB drive itself was a very outrageous thing, the fact that the USB drive had no energy left caught Chen Chen unprepared.

He must not panic, there must be a solution...

Chen Chen took a deep breath and leaned back on the sofa. At the same time, his brain began to spin at an extremely fast pace.

“First of all, the reason why I have a lot of strange knowledge about the USB flash drive in my mind — maybe because I own the USB flash drive, so just like in fantasy novels, the USB flash drive recognizes its master?

“Of course, this explanation isn’t reliable. Even if it has to acknowledge its master, it should be Zhou Jie. After all, he was the one who copied the movies in and he also played the movies to release those eerie beings...”

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes and supported his forehead with one hand, and gradually fell into thought.

“Even putting this aside for now, according to the rules in my mind about the USB flash drive, once the movie you put in is played, you can extract substances from it... But strangely, among all the rules, there are no rules about ghost movies at all. That is to say, in terms of what happens when you play a ghost movie, even the USB flash drive itself doesn’t know...

“But the other rules and our encounters in the afternoon can tell us that — if you play ordinary movies, you can take out ordinary movie props. If you play sci-fi movies, you can take out the sci-fi props inside. Then, if you play ghost movies...”

Erm...

“Could it be that the ghosts themselves will come out of the movies?

“However, Final Destination isn’t a ghost film, but a special kind of...”

Chen Chen’s heart throbbed. He suddenly raised his head and stared at the screen in front of him.

“Rule?”

Chen Chen’s eyes suddenly lit up.

“If Final Destination features a kind of rule, then Ju-on, Ring, and One Missed Call, are they also manifestations of a certain rule?”

Thinking of this, Chen Chen turned on the computer again and watched the four horror movies in the cinema of the Internet cafe.

Final Destination was a movie that told of a classic Chinese fatalistic narrative —”If the Reaper wants you dead at midnight, who would keep you alive until dawn?” If one was on Death’s list, then no matter how one fled or hid, one would ultimately be unable to escape the fate of death. One might try various methods to escape death but the irony was that these methods seemed to stack up coincidentally and became the way that Death killed one.

If this rule appeared in reality, that would be truly interesting...

Chen Chen only felt a chill in his heart. He did not think there was an entity such as Death in this world. The so-called Death in the movie was a special “rule”. Strictly speaking, this was like the law of cause and effect —

As the group of main characters in the movie was set to experience the “effect” of dying, any action to save themselves would become the “cause” of death.

If this rule was unleashed, then the entire world was probably going to collapse...

At that time, besides Newton’s law, thermodynamics law, mass-energy conservation law, and other scientific laws, there would be another “Death’s law” in the world?

‘F*ck that sh*t...’

Chen Chen only felt a pain in his brain but he had to continue hypothesizing. In his mind, a brainstorm had gradually started...

The movie Ju-on told the story of a middle-aged woman Kayako with a grievance. She lived a difficult life since she was a child. When she grew up, she was mistaken for being unfaithful and killed by her husband, so everyone who entered her house would be killed by her and her son Toshio.

The core of this movie was very simple. As long as anyone stepped into this house, they would become entangled with Kayako and eventually die.

If it was also regarded as a rule, then “enter and die” was the rule of the entire movie.

If this was the case, the instant this rule was unleashed, the entire Dorm 404 was already contaminated by this rule.

Chen Chen felt a greater ache in his heart. He was not afraid that Death’s law was unleashed. After all, Death’s law only killed people who were supposed to die but did not. He would not be implicated. However, if it were the rule of Ju-on, he would be at a dead end.

This was because Ju-on was a completely unsolvable horror movie. What did unsolvable mean? That was, people who had entered the house must die and there was no solution!

It was not just Ju-on. In addition to this unsolvable horror film, Zhou Jie had also released One Missed Call and Ring.

One Missed Call was about a mental disorder called “Munchausen syndrome”. A girl named “Mimiko” suffered from asthma herself and because her mother suffered from Munchausen syndrome, she was implicated in further suffering and eventually died. At the same time, she was filled with incomparable resentment after her death and her spirit was attached to her phone.

Anyone targeted by Mimiko would receive their own death call a few days in advance. When the call came, those phones would ring with a special nursery rhyme ringtone.

Of the content in the incoming call, some would be their voices before their death and some would send an MMS with the scene of their death. Moreover, the date of death was the date displayed on the call.

What was more terrifying was that this kind of death was contagious. After being killed by Mimiko, their mobile phone would randomly select a person from the address book to continue this relay game of death...

This was the killing method in One Missed Call.

To put it simply, the ghostly call first informed one that one would die and told one the method and date of one’s death. However, the truly despairing factor was that, even after knowing all this, one would still experience the exact manner of death, at the exact time, as given by the call, for one reason or another. There would not even be the slightest discrepancy.

This was the same as Final Destination.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen suddenly recalled that he did not seem to have received any strange calls. That was to say, he might not have been targeted by Mimiko.

With this in mind, Chen Chen could finally breathe a sigh of relief.

However, he did not let down his guard but swiftly took out his mobile phone, pulled out the SIM card, and then turned off the phone directly. After that, he threw it to the ground and gave it a few good, heavy stamps.

He did not stop until he heard a crunch from the phone.

If the phone was broken, he should not receive the curse of One Missed Call, right?

As for the last movie, Ring, Chen Chen had not watched it directly, but he knew the rules of death in this movie. After all, this movie was too famous and the rules in it were simpler. If anyone watched a recording of the videotape, they would be haunted by a female ghost named Sadako.

Still, it was obvious that Chen Chen had not watched any videotapes. In terms of rules, perhaps Ring was the easiest one to avoid.

In other words, among the four horror films, the only one that threatened him at present was Ju-on.

Chen Chen did not know whether to cry or laugh for a while. Although to him, he would have ended up dead no matter how many female spirits were haunting him. Now that he knew he was only cursed by one, he was surprised to feel a little relieved...

Chen Chen smiled bitterly.

“But even so, this matter is still very serious.”

Chen Chen murmured, “Because this means that the rules of Ju-on will start with Dorm 404 and spread around little by little since Dorm 404 dormitory is often visited by people. As long as they enter, they’ll be on Kayako’s kill list. The room of anyone killed by Kayako will also become a new place of the grudge-curse. The people entering that room will also be hunted down by Kayako...”

Compared with the other killing rules, Kayako’s rules were like a kind of spiritual calamity, enough to result in a destruction that was thousands of times worse than that of other rules!

“At the end of the day, the world will only have one ending — that is, houses all over the world can no longer house people and the entire human civilization will be destroyed because of Kayako’s curse.”

Thinking of this, Chen Chen could not help inhaling sharply. At this moment, his entire back was almost drenched in a cold sweat.

“This is beyond the scope of what I can solve.”

Chen Chen slowly shut down the computer. If there was still energy in the USB flash drive, even if he had to take a gamble, he would still find a way to solve Kayako’s curse.

What a shame the odds were not in his favor. The USB drive had run out of energy.

In that case, if he wanted to survive, Chen Chen had only one path left...

With this in mind, the computer was shut down fully. Chen Chen was about to stand up when suddenly, through the reflection of the computer screen, he saw a pale boy passing behind him.

At this instant, Chen Chen only felt that his blood was about to freeze. He stiffened his neck and slowly turned around, but found that there was nothing behind him.

“Toshio...”

Chen Chen’s lips turned blue and he quickly stood up. Without even cashing in his remaining balance, he staggered out of the Internet cafe.

Chen Chen did not realize until he had left the Internet cafe that the sky was completely dark.

In the entire sky, only a small flaming cloud drifted in the western vista, like a bonfire about to go out, leaving only a final spark.

Darkness was coming.

Chen Chen’s heart was icy. At this point, he even felt a kind of confusion, an overwhelming confusion that he did not know how to face all of this.

However, at this moment, out of the corner of Chen Chen’s eyes, he saw a dark shape at the corner of the Internet cafe door.

Chen Chen, who had been nervous for a long while now, jumped in fright. He turned to look instinctively, only to see a dainty, dirty beggar curled up in front of the Internet cafe, looking at him pitifully.

Those eyes felt inexplicably familiar to Chen Chen.

“You are...”

Chen Chen subconsciously took two steps forward and then immediately realized that he did not even know this beggar.

Something was off about this.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen immediately turned around and left without looking back. When he learned that he had become a protagonist of a horror movie, he knew that he must not take even the slightest risk. He had all the more reason to stay far away from this weird incident!

What Chen Chen did not know was that, after he turned and left, the beggar still stared at his back and did not want to leave for a long time...

“If I go directly to the police, they’ll only treat me as a basket case. Therefore, I must produce strong evidence to get my voice heard by those at the top as quickly as possible and attract official attention...”

Along the way, while observing his surroundings, Chen Chen kept thinking about the next step. Chen Chen, who was at the end of his rope, understood that he had only one way to survive now and that was to cooperate with the authorities.

That was right, he had to rely on the authorities!

If there were enough energy in the USB flash drive, Chen Chen would never choose this path because he did not want to pin his destiny on the conscience of others. Only heavens knew how he would be dealt with after handing over the USB flash drive.

Imprisoned? Ignored? Exterminated?

Nonetheless, Chen Chen understood that it was impossible for him to charge the USB flash drive for now. There was one rule in his mind about the USB flash drive. The USB flash drive required 1200 kWh of electricity to extract one gram of material, so whether it was in terms of time or money, Chen Chen had no means to get this done.

The only way was to inform the authorities of the function of the USB flash drive. After verifying the authenticity of the USB flash drive and acknowledging the tragic situation that would result from the spread of Ju-on’s rules, the authorities would naturally use electricity from the whole country to charge the USB flash drive to protect themselves.

In this way, it would only take one night at most for the USB drive to receive a lot of energy and thus they could find a way to stop the grudge-curse!

Therefore, what Chen Chen had to do now was to check the situation in the dormitory building. He did not know whether Dorm 404’s grudge-curse had spread in the course of one afternoon...

However, when Chen Chen returned to campus in a nervous mood and came to the front of the dormitory, all of a sudden, the hairs on his body stood up.

He did not know when but the dormitory building that was supposed to be exceptionally lively at this time, appeared particularly eerie and quiet. It was clear that most of the dormitories were still lit up but he could not sense any human activity at all.

No, the entire dormitory building had been infected...

Chen Chen took a deep breath. His feet that had just stepped on the stairs were immediately drawn back. He understood that the dormitory building No. 1 of his school and the hundreds of students in the building, were essentially done for. Even if they did not die now, their time would eventually come.

“Chen Chen, so you’re here?”

Just as Chen Chen’s heart was shivering and he was about to leave, a middle-aged male voice suddenly came from behind him.

Chen Chen turned his head quickly and suddenly saw his tutor, Wang Yi, standing behind him, staring at him anxiously.

“Tutor Wang!”

The thoughts in Chen Chen’s heart flickered by. He even spoke before the other man spoke, “Tutor Wang, did something happen to our dormitory?”

“You know as well?”

At this time, the middle-aged man’s face was ashen and when he heard Chen Chen’s words, he suddenly spoke agitatedly, “How many times did I call you? Why didn’t you answer any of them?”

“Tutor Wang, my phone is broken...”

Chen Chen showed a look of embarrassment. “What the h*ll happened? I noticed Zhou Jie was acting weird today. Did something happen to him?”

“Zhou Jie, Zhou Jie...”

However, as Chen Chen said the name Zhou Jie, the middle-aged man’s face suddenly turned even bleaker. He took a deep breath and suppressed the rage in his heart. “When did you last see Zhou Jie today?”

“At the end of the first class in the afternoon.”

Chen Chen said immediately, “I had just returned to the dormitory at that time and Zhou Jie came back behind me. Then I went to the cafeteria with Wang Wei and Li Bo for dinner before I went out of campus and returned just now.”

As he spoke, Chen Chen suddenly felt a jolt in his heart. As he was reminded of the killing rules in Ju-on, he immediately asked, “Tutor Wang, is Zhou Jie missing?”

“Not only Zhou Jie.”

Tutor Wang looked extremely grave at this point. “In the entire dormitory building No. 1, a total of five people are missing. Someone saw that they all disappeared after entering Dorm 404!”

chapters.title: Chapter 366: Wang Wei’S Death
chapters.body:
Chapter 366: Wang Wei’S Death

Chapter 366: Wang Wei’s Death

“How is that possible?”

Chen Chen looked surprised. “How can they disappear after entering our dormitory? Tutor Wang, are you kidding me?”

“I...”

Hearing these words, Wang Yi suddenly lost interest in interrogating Chen Chen and said irritably, “The ones missing are all students in your class. Do you think I’m not puzzled myself? I even called the other people in your Dorm 404 specifically, but Wang Wei from your dormitory...”

When talking about Wang Wei, Wang Yi’s expression changed again.

Chen Chen felt a chill. Although he understood that Wang Wei was going to die sooner or later due to the grudge-curse, like himself, he was still a bit sad. Nonetheless, he still pretended to look mystified. “What happened to Wang Wei?”

“Wang Wei has passed away due to sudden epilepsy.”

Wang Yi waved his hand as if he did not want to say more, but Chen Chen certainly would not let this go. He continued to ask, “Tutor Wang, Wang Wei has no problems with his health. I know this very well...”

“That’s enough!”

However, Chen Chen only said one sentence before Wang Yi immediately interrupted him. Wang Yi adopted a stern expression and said strictly, “This matter doesn’t need to be discussed again. Wang Wei died of epilepsy-induced muscle twitches — that’s the forensic doctor’s diagnosis and that’s final! Chen Chen, don’t spread any rumors. If it affects the reputation of the school, no one will be able to take responsibility for it, understand?”

Hearing Wang Yi’s words, Chen Chen could not help feeling cold. He nodded. “Tutor Wang, I understand. I won’t speak without thinking.”

“Good...”

Hearing Chen Chen’s assurance, Wang Yi’s expression relaxed slightly and he waved his hand. “The police couldn’t wait for you just now, so they have already left. Now that you’ve returned, take a taxi to the police station to make a statement. Don’t worry, this is just a procedure. The rest of your class has done it.”

“Okay.”

Chen Chen nodded, then watched Wang Yi hurriedly leave again.

Staring at the other man’s anxious expression, Chen Chen sneered secretly in his heart.

Even with the state of things, the school was still trying to pretend they could suppress everything. Little did they know, today’s death and disappearances were just the beginning.

The next few days would be when the spiritual abnormality of the grudge-curse erupted. At that time, the school could forget about holding on to their official positions. The main question would be whether they could hold on to their very lives.

This sort of incident could be called a “spiritual abnormality”.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen turned around and walked toward the campus gate.

It was not that he wanted to deliberately conceal this and watch this group of students die in cold blood, but because Chen Chen understood that whatever he did now would be futile. It could be seen from the fact that the police did not block off the dormitory at all and still allowed these students to go in and out. The authorities had no experience in dealing with such cases.

As the largest commercial city in Mainland China, the importance of Shangdu in this country was second only to the capital city. If there were no relevant emergency measures for Shangdu, did it mean that everything Chen Chen had experienced now had never been experienced by the government?

Therefore, what could be done even if he headed to the police station, told them everything, and selflessly handed the USB drive over?

He would only be regarded as someone insane. Even if there were strange disappearances and Wang Wei died under peculiar circumstances, under the interference of the school, it would still be difficult for Chen Chen to be taken seriously by the higher-ups in Shangdu, let alone the core leaders of China.

If it was not a case where dozens or hundreds had died, this sort of matter would not reach their ears at all...

Therefore, Chen Chen had to wait until enough people had died at school and the entire issue was blown up enough before he would have a chance of being heard by the top dogs!

Of course, this was assuming he could survive to that point.

At this time, Chen Chen was already outside the campus. He hailed a taxi, gave an address, and headed to the nearest police station.

His tutor had asked Chen Chen to make a statement, which was in line with Chen Chen’s expectation because he knew the police’s usual procedure. After his statement was recorded, these statements would be sent to a higher level of authority.

In the next few days, this matter would become critical and Chen Chen’s statement would naturally be taken seriously. Then, Chen Chen would have the opportunity to speak to the ones at the top.

Considering this, Chen Chen thought to himself about how to phrase all this.

Soon, the vehicle stopped in front of the gate of the police station. As soon as Chen Chen entered the duty room, he explained his intentions to the people in the duty room. After a while, a policeman called Chen Chen into the office.

“Name?”

“Chen Chen.”

“Age?”

“19 years old.”

“Why did you come to the police station?”

“I was called to the police station by you guys because some people in our Dorm 404 are missing.”

After some standard inquiries, the statement officially entered the main subject.

However, as questions and answers were exchanged, the policeman lifted his head from the computer screen and his professional expression shifted. He looked at Chen Chen as if he were an idiot.

“Student, this is the police station and I’m recording your statement, not writing online novels for you,” the policeman said with annoyance.

“I can guarantee that what I said is true.”

Chen Chen looked serious. “The reason why a few students disappeared in Dorm 404 is that they were infected with the curse in the Japanese horror film Ju-on. Now they are probably dead. The death of my roommate Wang Wei is also related to this kind of curse. Mr. Policeman, please record the full statement as I dictated it, okay?”

Seeing this, the policeman reluctantly wrote it down and then grinned. “I say, you’re interesting and you seem to take this very earnestly. But do you know that you can be held legally responsible for saying nonsense?”

“I know, I’m willing to take full responsibility.”

Chen Chen nodded and suddenly asked, “By the way, I also have a roommate named Li Bo. You should know him. Where’s he?”

“Li Bo is making his statement in the office next door.”

The policeman pointed to the wall beside him.

“In that case, please ask the next room if, after he’s done, he could wait for me to go with him.” Chen Chen suggested.

“Okay, hold on.”

The policeman taking the statement readily agreed. He walked out and then walked back in again within ten seconds.

Then, he continued to ask Chen Chen various questions.

“In the next few days, this curse will continue to spread and even turn into a kind of spiritual abnormality. By that time, hundreds of people will die...”

Chen Chen said with a solemn expression, “And I have a way to stop all of this, so if you want to stop everything, come to me. I want to speak directly with the central government of China!”

...

When Chen Chen walked out of the office, it was late at night.

In the duty room of the police station, Li Bo had been sitting in a chair waiting for a long time.

Seeing Li Bo’s white face and dull eyes, Chen Chen suddenly understood that Li Bo had seen something.

“Lil Chen.”

Seeing Chen Chen coming out, Li Bo stood up with a blank look. “Lil Chen, Brother Wei is gone...”

Chen Chen came forward and patted Li Bo on the shoulder. “I know. Lil Bo, tell me the truth, what happened before?”

However, after hearing Chen Chen’s words, Li Bo fell silent. He just kept shaking his head, but he would not say anything.

“Lil Bo!”

Chen Chen looked around and instantly saw the vending machine outside the police station’s duty room. He immediately went out, bought two cans of Sprite, and helped Li Bo open one can.

“Lil Bo, sit down and tell me!”

Chen Chen asked Li Bo to take a seat and then asked in a low voice, “Lil Bo, this concerns our lives. You must tell me everything or we’ll probably die as Brother Wei did!”

Hearing Chen Chen’s words, Li Bo finally trembled. He raised his head in disbelief and stared at Chen Chen. “You mean, we’ll also hear that ringtone and die as Brother Wei did?”

“Ringtone?”

Chen Chen was startled. His voice suddenly leaped by an octave. “What ringtone are you speaking about?”

“It’s that, that...”

Li Bo frowned, deep in horrifying thought. “It’s that weird ringtone. You and I both know Brother Wei’s ringtone was set to be Wowkie Zhang’s ‘Sunshine, Rainbow, White Pony’. Whenever he gets a call, it goes ‘You’re that~ that~ that that that~ Sunshine rainbow white pony...

“But in the cafeteria today, as soon as you walked away, there was a ringtone on Brother Wei’s mobile phone that I had never heard before. It was an a cappella tune sung by a little girl and the entire song was just constantly going la la la la...”

Hearing this, Chen Chen could not help gasping, only feeling cold sweat trickling down his back.

The ringtone mentioned by Li Bo immediately reminded Chen Chen of the other movie that was as horrible as Ju-on — One Missed Call.

One Missed Call had three movies. In the first two, due to the limits of that era, the sign of getting cursed was that the phone would ring with a polyphonic ringtone. However, by the third movie, although the pitch had not changed, the original polyphonic ringtone was replaced by a little girl singing la la la.

In other words, whether it was a polyphonic ringtone or the clearer original ringtone, it meant one thing — Wang Wei was killed by the curse of One Missed Call!

Thinking of this, Chen Chen looked more solemn and he asked in a low voice, “Lil Bo, tell me! How did Brother Wei die!”

“Brother Wei... Wrung his neck with his own hands.”

It seemed that the tragic scene was flashing in his mind again and Li Bo’s eyes were full of terror. “I only heard a series of screams on the phone after he answered the phone and that voice was almost the same as Brother Wei’s. Brother Wei thought it was a prank but when he hung up and looked at the number of the call, it turned out to be his own number and the call time... Was five minutes later.”

Chen Chen’s pupils shrank slightly but he did not speak, only listened quietly.

Li Bo continued. “At the time, Brother Wei was a little scared. He told me that this situation was particularly like a movie named One Missed Call, which was one of the four horror films that Nostril had copied into the USB drive at noon. Then he told me the rules of the movie, but I was playing with my phone and didn’t listen carefully...

“Then, after about five minutes, I suddenly heard a groan from Brother Wei. I looked up and asked him what was wrong. He said that two of his arms were cramped...

“I was just about to soothe him, but his arms suddenly seemed to go out of control. They rose with a cracking sound and closed around his neck from both sides...”

Speaking of this, Li Bo was already shuddering. While shaking violently, he said intermittently, “Brother Wei seemed to have gone crazy. He kept twisting his neck with both hands, screaming and twisting, and even his tongue was bitten off... Just like that, Brother Wei took just a few minutes and forcefully broke his own neck...”

At the end of the narration, Li Bo’s face was already pale. He suddenly drank the Sprite in his hand, and his body seemed to slump.

Chen Chen patted Li Bo on the shoulder with a somber expression.

No wonder the school classified Wang Wei’s cause of death as epilepsy. Perhaps on a superficial level, Wang Wei’s symptoms before death were indeed similar to epilepsy but what epilepsy was able to cause someone to twist his head off?

Nonetheless, in the next second, Chen Chen shook his head. It did not make sense to bother about these things. After all, he had to save his own skin before saving others.

The most important thing now was to survive as far as possible during the time before the authorities confronted this matter. If he could not ride out the next few days, then everything would be for naught.

No matter how good his strategy, no matter how good his planning, it would all be meaningless.

He looked at the sky. It was late at night and the other students who made a statement seemed to have gone back but Chen Chen had no intention of returning to campus.

In the past, on Hong Kong and Taiwanese TV series, there was often a saying that ghosts dared not approach police stations as the place was filled with righteous energy. Although this saying was only a feudalistic superstition, staying here was better than going back to the cursed Dorm 404.

However, Chen Chen also understood that Kayako and Toshio from Ju-on were more like an incarnation of the rules. No matter where one stayed, whether there were many or few people, whether it was day or night, even if one stayed in a church or a temple, one would still die when one’s time was up.

Chen Chen only hoped that, by staying outside, he would be less likely to be killed by Kayako than by staying in Dorm 404.

Come to think of it, surely right now in the dormitory building, the slaughter under the grudge-curse had already begun?

Chen Chen took a deep breath. After comforting Li Bo, he walked out of the duty room again and bought two cups of instant noodles from the vending machine in front. The duty room of the police station had enough seats and automatic drinking fountains. Even if he stayed for one night, there would be no problem and Chen Chen planned to survive tonight before anything else.

Nevertheless, when Chen Chen bought the instant noodles, a girl who appeared to be only fifteen or sixteen years old came out of the duty room. Although the girl was not tall, being only over one and a half meters tall, the proportions of her body were extremely balanced.

Chen Chen immediately remembered her after seeing her face. This girl was the one who struck up a conversation with him at noon today.

At this time, after seeing Chen Chen, the girl also stepped forward in surprise.

“Chen Chen, we meet again!”

“You know my name?”

Chen Chen asked a little warily.

“I asked the other students about it.”

The girl beamed. “Oh right, you still don’t know, my name is Aixen!”

chapters.title: Chapter 367: Phone Call
chapters.body:
Chapter 367: Phone Call

Chapter 367: Phone Call

“Aixen? What kind of name is that?”

Chen Chen did not directly lower his guard. The girl in front of him was so friendly to him, which made Chen Chen feel like she was planning to rape him or rob him or something. He tentatively asked, “What are you doing at the police station?”

“I’m giving my statement just like you guys.”

The girl spread her hands innocently. “Several people in our class are missing and the whole class has to give a statement to assist in the investigation!”

Hearing this, Chen Chen’s expression slightly relaxed and he nodded. “In that case, you can go back now. It’s late at night and it’s not good for a girl to stay outside.”

“Oh...”

Upon hearing these words, the girl immediately nodded obediently. “Then I’ll head back to the dormitory, bye-bye...”

With that, she waved to Chen Chen, then turned and left.

Chen Chen was taken aback when he saw that the girl was so well-behaved and obedient. He felt that he had misjudged her.

As he watched her leave step by step, after thinking about it, Chen Chen simply shouted at her back, “Oh right, if you can, I hope you can leave school as soon as possible. It’s best if you can ask Tutor Wang for a long leave tomorrow.”

“Ask for leave?”

The girl turned her head and asked curiously, “Why must I leave the school?”

“Because if you wait too long, you might never be able to leave.”

Chen Chen smiled and just dropped such a sentence. Then he stopped paying any attention to her.

At this point, if the girl still did not want to leave, it had nothing to do with Chen Chen. As the saying went — good advice meant nothing to stubborn ears. Chen Chen could not always devote his time and narrate the whole story from beginning to end, right?

Now that there was still insufficient evidence, even if he explained everything, the only result was that he would be seen as an idiot.
