All this flashed through Chen Chen’s mind. At this time, Chen Chen walked into the duty room again and began to prepare instant noodles for himself and Li Bo.

Making instant noodles could be said to be the traditional art form practiced by the loser gang of Mainland China. One could be clueless about eating steak with a knife and fork or driving a Ferrari with one hand, but making instant noodles was an inevitable skill, one that they were all familiar with.

Chen Chen, being a veteran loser himself, was so used to this that when he used the plastic fork to fix the lid in place, he could insert it through the lid and the cup accurately on the first attempt, without making mistakes.

Moreover, for times when he could not even afford a cup, Chen Chen had also invented the skill of cooking instant noodles from a packet using the lid of a thermos.

After preparing the two cups of instant noodles with great ease, Chen Chen placed one of the cups in front of Li Bo. At this time, Li Bo’s expression was still a bit slack. It was obvious that Wang Wei’s death in the afternoon was too tragic and caused him a terrible fright.

Seeing this scene, Chen Chen suddenly realized that compared to ordinary people like Li Bo, his mentality seemed too calm.

There was no doubt that Chen Chen had encountered those supernatural beings two or three times, but he not only analyzed various clues but also calmly planned a countermeasure to stop the curse, even if this countermeasure was at the cost of hundreds of deaths.

This calmness could even be said to be a cold-blooded mentality, which had surpassed the mentality that ordinary people should have. Even Chen Chen himself could not understand why he still had such clear and meticulous thinking. He was unlike a person who had been targeted by Kayako, someone destined to die...

‘Perhaps this is due to the supernatural power of the USB flash drive?’

Chen Chen stared at the instant noodles in front of him and thought inwardly, ‘The USB drive not only gave me the rules about the USB drive, but it also gave me a calmness beyond that of ordinary people?’

Even so, in the next second, Chen Chen overruled this possibility himself. After all, he did not feel that he was being manipulated at all. On the contrary, this calmness was more like the result of long-term training — a habit or instinct born out of a special experience.

However, as to what that experience was, he had forgotten...

Chen Chen’s movements froze as fragmented images flashed in his mind again. Among those images, there were scenes of himself in control of a company, scenes of himself absorbing some kind of crystal shards, and scenes of himself floating within a glass cabin of a tall, high-tech building...

However, when Chen Chen wanted to step forward and grab those fragmentary memories, they seemed to disappear without a trace like fallen meteors.

A trick of his mind or was there something wrong with his memory?

Chen Chen took a deep breath. He was starting to get dizzy, so he stopped thinking about it, and started to chow down the noodles after they were cooked.

Seeing Chen Chen’s behavior, Li Bo seemed to have finally recovered. He asked in a low voice, “Lil Chen, didn’t you just say that we’re likely to die like Brother Wei? Why is that?”

“I said in the afternoon that Zhou Jie had unleashed four horror movies at that time, do you remember?”

After Li Bo nodded, Chen Chen continued. “And before Brother Wei died, didn’t he also say that the situation when he received a strange ringtone was like the movie called One Missed Call? And what I want to tell you now is that the manner in which he died was also similar to this movie!”

“...”

Li Bo fell silent after hearing the words and it took a long time before he could say in a soft voice, “Lil Chen, I understand what you mean. You’re saying that Zhou Jie has released the ghosts in the four movies and we’re the targets of these ghosts now?”

“You can say that.”

Chen Chen sighed. “But I still think that there are no ghosts in this world. We’re just implicated in various rules that can kill people. As long as we find a way to find the loopholes in the rule, we can make it out alive!”

After listening to Chen Chen’s words, Li Bo nodded steadily as if his hope for life was rekindled. He also picked up the instant noodles and began to eat ravenously.

Both of their actions caused the assistant police officer at the front desk to watch with mouth agape.

In this manner, an entire night had quickly passed by.

Fortunately, perhaps the police station did have some mysterious power or because Kayako had an entire dormitory building’s worth of targets to hunt down, ultimately, Chen Chen and Li Bo managed to survive the first night without Kayako trying to claim their lives.

However, this was also normal. After all, people who had entered Kayako’s house in the movie did not always die at once. Some of them died one day after leaving Kayako’s house, some died two days later, and the longest time span was six or seven days later.

In other words, during these six or seven days, Kayako might appear at any time and kill Chen Chen and Li Bo.

It could also be known from the movie that there was no distance limit for the curse’s pursuit. Even if they fled to the other side of the earth or even to the moon or Mars, it would not help.

Chen Chen sat up from the seat in the police duty room and looked at the clock on the wall. It was already seven in the morning, so Chen Chen woke Li Bo up, and the two took a taxi back to the school.

It might appear that Chen Chen was going back to school to attend classes, but in fact, it was just to check the situation on campus.

However, as soon as he returned to the campus, Chen Chen distinctly sensed a peculiar atmosphere. The campus that was once so quiet now had a few police cars parked next to building No. 1. In addition to the police, there was also a group of school leaders surrounding the place, causing the students passing by to look back frequently.

“What happened?”

Li Bo saw the situation at the school and turned to look at Chen Chen. “Is this also caused by those ghosts?”

“How would I know.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “But you just have to remember, don’t step into dormitory building No. 1. Now this dormitory building has become the source of the curse. Even the police officers who entered are dead meat...”

“...”

Li Bo fell silent immediately.

After having breakfast in the cafeteria, Chen Chen and Li Bo walked into the classroom according to the schedule. However, when he first entered the classroom, he saw a group of students in his class discussing something in full swing.

“Did you know? Something happened to Wang Wei in our class!”

“I know. Wang Wei died of epilepsy yesterday. It’s really terrible...”

“Not just that, but Zhou Jie and those in our class have also disappeared, and they haven’t been found yet!”

Chen Chen sat beside the crowd and listened silently. As expected, soon enough he heard about last night’s news. Zheng Jie, the monitor of Chen Chen’s class, also turned his head and said to the group next to Chen Chen, “Not only that, but when I was downstairs just now, there were police cars all around along with the tutors from the department next door. So I went to an acquaintance from the department next door and asked, and guess what I found out?”

The people near Chen Chen stopped talking and looked at the class monitor Zheng Jie solemnly.

“Just last night, two more students disappeared, and both of them lived in Dorm 304!”

“Why is it Dorm 304?”

Chen Chen frowned at once. “Is it related to Dorm 404?”

“Of course!”

Zheng Jie put on a mysterious expression. “I heard that acquaintance said that in the middle of the night, the people in Dorm 304 heard people crying for help from upstairs!”

Chen Chen’s expression sharpened upon hearing these words.

“Cries for help — is that real? Chen Chen and Li Bo weren’t in their dorm last night?” The classmate beside Chen Chen turned to him with a suspicious look.

“Because the police asked a lot of questions last night, Li Bo and I didn’t go back to the dormitory all night and we just spent the night at the police station.”

Chen Chen nodded. “My phone is broken, but the tutor did call Li Bo, and he can also prove that I’ve been in the police station the whole time.”

Seeing this, Zheng Jie hurriedly said, “You see, I told you, didn’t I? At that time, there was no one in Dorm 404, but the four people in Dorm 304 heard the cries for help from Dorm 404, and it sounded like Zhou Jie’s voice...

“So two of them went to check on Dorm 404 themselves, but after they went, they didn’t come back even until dawn.”

Zheng Jie looked at everyone’s faces one by one as if he wanted to see a trace of fear, but to his disappointment, everyone looked at him calmly, not at all frightened by the story.

“So, the two people in Dorm 304 called the police, thus resulting in the scene downstairs that we saw.”

Seeing this, Zheng Jie stopped trying to build suspense and finished his story lamely.

“So, did the police find anything?”

Chen Chen seemed to have thought of something and he asked again.

“Only the slippers of the two people from Dorm 304. However, apart from their slippers, the two of them seemed to have evaporated into thin air. There was nothing left.”

There was a glint of worry in Zheng Jie’s eyes and he spread his hands. “This whole incident is strange. Five people disappeared yesterday and two people disappeared just when today began. Heaven knows where those people went...”

When everyone was talking about this, the lecturer who was in charge of the class finally walked in. Everyone stopped talking and started to pay attention to class.

“Alright, settle down now, students. Let’s continue our previous lesson...”

As the lecturer started to teach, Chen Chen suddenly turned his head and looked around the classroom.

Nonetheless, after looking around, he did not see the petite figure.

“Did she ask for leave?”

Chen Chen shook his head. He did not expect that she would obey his request and asked for leave so promptly. He was a little dumbfounded at the level of trust she had in him.

Just then, a familiar cell phone ringtone came from in front of Chen Chen.

After hearing this ringtone, Chen Chen, who had been extremely composed, suddenly shuddered.

Chen Chen was no stranger to this sound. When he watched the movie One Missed Call, he had memorized this ringtone...

The class was going on now. It was very rude to have a mobile phone ringing in class. Generally, students would turn it off quickly, but this ringtone kept ringing for a long time without anyone addressing it.

The teacher who was giving the lecture finally could not stand it. She was originally writing something on the blackboard but now she turned around and said, “Whosever phone it is, turn it off, thank you!”

Suddenly, everyone looked at the monitor Zheng Jie.

“It’s not mine. This isn’t my ringtone...”

Class monitor Zheng Jie subconsciously picked up his mobile phone placed on the table, but he was taken aback at once. He did not expect that it was his mobile phone that was ringing.

“D*mn it, why is it this again...”

Zheng Jie cursed in a low voice. It was not the first time he had heard this ringtone. Still, after hearing this, Chen Chen abruptly raised his head and took a deep long look at the embarrassed Zheng Jie.

According to the rules of One Missed Call, there would occasionally be two or even multiple calls. The first call was always the moment the curse took root and the second call was often when one’s time was up...

Sure enough, Zheng Jie had just grumbled in a whisper, but by the time he raised his head, he suddenly let out a scream as if he saw a person standing in front of him that no one could see!

In the next second, Zheng Jie twitched and stood up while pulling on the desk in front of him with both hands. The books of the students beside him were sent crashing to the ground by his actions while Zheng Jie continued to open his mouth wide in agony, staggering and walking step by step toward the window of the classroom.

“Student, what are you doing?”

Seeing that this student was so insolent, the lecturer suddenly became stern. “Stop right there at once!”

However, at this time, Zheng Jie was destined to not listen to her words. In front of the whole class, Zheng Jie tipped over three desks and came to the window!

Although the teaching building was not very high, it had eight or nine floors. At this point, their classroom was on the seventh floor and Zheng Jie was sobbing as he kept looking back at everyone.

“Zheng Jie, what are you doing?”

Many people immediately realized something and they subconsciously wanted to rush forward, but before they could do so, Zheng Jie shrieked and jumped out of the window!

“Crash!”

“Ahhh!”

A violent collective scream erupted from the whole class and the face of the teacher who was giving the lecture changed drastically. She wanted to stop Zheng Jie, but it was a pity that all this happened too quickly, and there was almost no warning. When she came to her senses, Zheng Jie had already leaped out of the window...

Listening to the screams rising one after another in the class, Chen Chen’s expression was extremely somber. In his perspective, Zheng Jie did not jump down voluntarily but was controlled by an invisible and intangible force before getting thrown out of the building.

Whether it was Zheng Jie’s behavior before his death or his expression that was desperate for help, all this told Chen Chen one thing.

Zheng Jie was killed by One Missed Call...

chapters.title: Chapter 368: Suspicions
chapters.body:
Chapter 368: Suspicions

Chapter 368: Suspicions

“Zheng Jie fell!”

“Oh no! He’s not dead, is he?”

“Ah— !”

The class was in turmoil. Their screams even echoed across half of the school compound, prompting countless other students and teachers to turn to where the noise came from.

When Chen Chen saw the panicking crowd, he approached the window and leaned half his body outside the window to investigate.

On the base of the building, Zheng Jie was lying silently on the pavement. His face was twisted in horror and shock while a pool of blood formed under him.

At the same time, a red-colored round object slipped out of the corner of his lips and rolled onto the soft ground beside him.

Chen Chen felt another chill creeping into his heart when he saw this.

Inside the movie, all of Mimiko’s victims died of a large variety of different causes. The one thing they had in common was that red maltose would be deposited into their mouths. It was said that this was Mimiko’s favorite treat when she was alive.

When Chen Chen thought of this, he seemed to have suddenly recalled something. He immediately turned around and looked at the phone Zheng Jie had left behind.

The phone had automatically stopped ringing as if it understood its owner had passed on. However, its display was still left on. It shifted through the menus and opened the address books as if there was an invisible hand navigating through it.

Immediately after that, it dialed a new number...

“La la la la~ la la la la~ la la la la~ la la la la~ la ~ la~ la~ la~ la la la~ la la la~ la la la...”

A melodic rhyme resounded across the classroom. One of the girls who was curled up in her seat looked down at her phone when she heard her phone ringing.

She saw that the contact number of the caller was her phone number...

The death relay still went on...

Chen Chen shook his head and exited the classroom. He was much more immune to this curse compared to other people considering he did not even have a phone. Besides, the rate of killing in One Missed Call was mere child’s play compared to Ju-on.

Chen Chen suddenly halted the moment he stepped out of the classroom. A strange sensation immediately took over his body.

It was an extremely implacable feeling. An apt way to describe it would be a sudden shudder. He did not observe anything else unnatural but for some undistinguishable reason, he was extra sensitive to this strange sensation.

At this moment, he sensed what felt like a chilling breeze sweeping toward him from the end of the corridor. The stoic air was filled with a sense of dread and was absent of any warmth, like a frozen dead body.

Chen Chen’s first instinct was to back away. Just as he wanted to retreat into the classroom, he found out that the door to the classroom was locked. Chen Chen could not open the door no matter how hard he tried.

On top of that, the buzzing classroom had suddenly descended into a dead silence as if all the students inside had disappeared without a trace...

Something was off here.

Chen Chen drew a deep breath. He quickly decided to turn around and walk down the other end of the corridor. His gut told him that there was something hostile approaching and that he needed to get away from where the ominous wind came from.

It may be a futile attempt but Chen Chen did not have many options available to him at the moment...

Chen Chen felt a sourness rising up his throat. Should he consider this a case of poor luck or good luck? Of all the targets in the dormitory, Kayako just had to come for him.

The plot of the movie was evident enough that Kayako killed her victims in whatever order she liked at any given moment. There was no identifiable structure whatsoever in the order in which she claimed her victims.

These thoughts ran their course through Chen Chen’s mind as he sprinted down the end of the corridor. There was another emergency door down this way but when Chen Chen twisted the handle of the door, he found that it was the same as the classroom door. The handle would not budge no matter how hard he pulled. It felt as if someone was gripping the handle on the other end.

Meanwhile, the opposite end of the corridor had become shrouded in darkness without his noticing.

Even though it was still daytime, the world outside the windows had become as dark as the night. Even the sun had retreated into the darkness.

Chen Chen’s first thought was to look out at the window behind him. He saw that the bright daylight still poured through the window.

This was the strange predicament he found himself in. On one end of the corridor, the world outside was submerged in deep darkness while on the other end, it was nothing but daylight...

It was here!

Chen Chen felt a terrifying shiver. He began to hammer away at the door like a mad man. The corridor was filled with the hectic “bang, bang” sound of Chen Chen slamming at the door. During his constant barrage, there seemed to be another sound mixed into it...

“Hnnnghhhhhhhhhhh...”

It sounded like the sound a person made when their throat was dried up. Chen Chen was no stranger to this sound because he had heard it countless times in the movie before. Whenever one heard this sound, it could only mean one thing — Kayako was only inches away!

When Chen Chen considered this, he turned toward the dark end of the corridor yet again. This time, he noticed that the darkness seemed to have crept closer toward him.

If the total length of the corridor was fifty meters, only forty meters were separating Chen Chen from the desolate darkness — the foul darkness had claimed ten meters of the corridor...

Cold sweat rained down between Chen Chen’s brows. He continued smashing at the emergency door with every ounce of strength left in him and still, it did not budge even after his fists were dripping blood. Meanwhile, the mechanical “hnnghhh” was drawing closer to a point where it felt like it was drowning out the sound of him smashing the door. It felt like it was ringing in his ears!

“Is it over yet?”

Chen Chen stopped beating against the door and twisted his neck around with bloodshot eyes. The darkness had swept through four-fifth of the corridor. There was less than a ten-meter gap between the void and Chen Chen...

Chen Chen could also see the faint silhouette of a woman with brazen hair dragging herself across the floor on all fours within the suffocating, obscure darkness!

Five meters, four meters, three meters!

When Chen Chen drew in a deep breath and prepared to confront whatever was to come, he suddenly felt something pulling at his arm. He immediately turned around and saw a familiar pair of widened eyes staring back into his.

Chen Chen abruptly pulled himself up and sat on the floor.

It was only now when he realized that he had fainted on the corridor without having any recollection of it.

The corridor was as empty as they were. Where were the darkness and Kayako?

“Chen Chen, are you alright?”

A familiar voice rang in his ears. Chen Chen inspected his body, still visibly shocked. After ensuring that he was unharmed, he pulled the other person closer to him. “Aixen, what time is it now?”

“Class just started not too long ago.”

The girl took her phone out and presented it to Chen Chen. The clock showed an eight and a ten.

This meant that it had only been less than half a minute since he stepped into the corridor from the classroom!

Chen Chen quickly understood that he was cut off from reality from the moment he stepped out of the classroom. He drew this conclusion because although he spent several minutes in that dimension, it had only been a brief moment in the real world.

Upon realizing this, Chen Chen puffed out a giant sigh of relief. Although he was not sure how he managed to get himself out of that mess, it had something to do with this girl in front of him.

Chen Chen brushed the dust off of him and got up while asking, “Didn’t I tell you to apply for a leave from Tutor Wang? Why are you back here again?”

“I’ve already taken leave. I even bought train tickets for tomorrow. I just figure that since I didn’t have anything to do today, I’d finish one last class before leaving...”

The girl stole a glance at Chen Chen and informed somewhat embarrassingly.

Meanwhile, many people started to shuffle out of the classroom and quickly made their way to the ground floor of the building. The corridor quickly became crowded. When Chen Chen saw this, he took the girl by her hand and led her out of the school building as well.

“You can leave tomorrow, but don’t attend any classes today. Don’t even stay to stick around the dormitory for that matter, got it?”

Chen Chen waited until they left the vicinity of the school building before issuing the stern reminder. Since she was responsible for saving his life, he figured he should return the favor as well.

“You haven’t given me a proper reason!”

The girl complained a little.

“All I can say is that the longer you stay here, the higher the chances of you dropping dead is.”

Chen Chen said in a hushed tone while leading the girl outside the main gate of the school, “Yesterday was day one, the likelihood of death was one in a thousand. Today is day two and the likelihood of death is one in a hundred. By tomorrow, that number rises to one in ten, and that number will only climb higher by day four and five...”

After saying this, Chen Chen took a deep breath and concluded. “Point is, get as far away from here as possible. Actually, get out of the city if you can. Just do as I say!”

The girl was spooked when she heard this and immediately became quiet.

Chen Chen had walked out of the school compound with the girl by this point. He finally heaved a sigh of relief and said with a soft chuckle, “I know you might think that I’ve lost my mind, but since you’ve already taken leave anyway, might as well do as I say, right?”

“Based on my previous observation, it doesn’t fit your personality to leave behind that final remark.”

Surprisingly, the girl suddenly switched the topic and stared at Chen Chen seriously. “But you went out of your way to explain yourself with that final remark. Did you do it because you’re worried about me?”

“???”

Chen Chen looked back at her, all confused. “What goes on in that head of yours? It’s like you didn’t even register half of what I just told you?”

Without waiting for the girl to answer him, Chen Chen turned back around to enter the school.

The girl suddenly pulled him by his arm.

“What else do you want?” Chen Chen expressed with a frown.

“Are you going to die?”

The girl fixed her eyes on Chen Chen and asked in a lowered voice, “Are you going to die here?”

Chen Chen became silent for a moment before shaking his head and answered in a way that seemed like he was psyching himself up, “I’m not going to die in this place, I’m going to live!’

With that, Chen Chen patted her head before turning around and left.

For some reason, every time he saw that girl, he felt something funny inside him.

Maybe because Chen Chen had caused a small commotion, many people passing by turned to look in his direction. Chen Chen paid no mind to these trivial matters and quickly walked toward the school entrance. That was when he saw Li Bo sprinting toward him.

“Where did you go?”

Li Bo was gasping for air and asked with a tinge of horror in his voice, “When I saw you leaving the classroom, I quickly packed up my bag and chased after you. Then you suddenly disappeared. I searched through half of the school building before I finally saw you through the window.”

“I was just escorting that transfer student from our class out of the school.”

Chen Chen waved his hand nonchalantly and remarked while walking, “I told her to apply for a long holiday so she can get away from these things.”

“Transfer student?” Li Bo looked at Chen Chen, his eyes growing wide as he said this.

“You know, that Aixen girl. The one you and Brother Wei fussed about.”

Chen Chen proceeded to explain.

Li Bo’s expression quickly turned sour. He looked at Chen Chen with mild horror, a strange look quickly took over. “Lil Chen, there’s something I gotta tell you, don’t freak out...”

Chen Chen froze subtly when he heard this. He glanced at Li Bo. “Go ahead.”

Li Bo glanced in the direction of the school entrance briefly and held his voice down. “There’s never been any transfer student in our class!”

Li Bo’s expression was twisted with horror. “Well, there’s a transfer student over in the next class, but he’s a guy...”

Chen Chen stopped walking and subconsciously turned around and looked in the direction of the main gate. A large crowd had gathered around the gate by this point. He could not find the familiar figure anywhere.

It was as if the girl called Aixen had never existed in the first place...

“That’s impossible...”

Chen Chen became bewildered. He snapped his head around and grabbed Li Bo by his shoulder. “That’s impossible, she was in the class with us and even answering questions. There’s no way!”

“Lil Chen, you have to calm down!”

Li Bo panicked more when he saw Chen Chen’s reaction. He quickly explained, “What I’m telling you is true, there’s never been a transfer student in our class!”

“What about during recess? I swear I saw you and Wang Wei flocking to her table during recess last morning. How do you explain that?”

“Yesterday’s recess?”

Li Bo immediately recalled the events. “That’s not right, we gathered together during recess yesterday because the class rep Zheng Jie was handing out tickets for the campus party!”

Chen Chen stood in place as if struck by lightning. He slowly lifted his head, the memory zapped across his mind like a bolt of thunder.

...

“Hi, is there anything I can do for you?”

“I’m the new transfer student, can I become friends with you?”

“Making friends? Shouldn’t you be making friends of the same gender first usually?”

“This works too!”

...

“Chen Chen, we met again!”

“How do you know my name?”

“I asked the other students. Right, you don’t know my name yet, don’t you? The name’s Aixen!”

...

“Chen Chen, you alright?”

“Aixen, what time is it now?”

“Class just started not too long ago.”

...

Chen Chen felt his body shivering. He finally understood why people would stare at him strangely whenever he talked with that girl.

Back then when he was in the class, he thought they were wondering why such a cute girl would flirt with him. Back when he was at the school gate, he thought it was because he was causing a commotion. Looking back now, they were probably looking at him strangely because they saw him interacting with the air...

“Just now when I was upstairs, I also saw you yanking away at the air and walking out the school. You didn’t even respond when I was shouting at you.”

Li Bo lowered his voice, slightly trembling as he asked, “Chen Chen, did you see... Did you see... That?”

Chen Chen stumbled several paces backward. He felt his head twisting into a chaotic mess. He felt his sanity slowly trickling away bit by bit. Even the image of the girl’s appearance in his mind started to turn into a blur.

Despite all that, the pair of eyes she wore was deeply imprinted into the depths of his mind. It crept into the deepest crevice of his mind...

chapters.title: Chapter 369: Attracting Attention
chapters.body:
Chapter 369: Attracting Attention

Chapter 369: Attracting Attention

Inside a large and brightly lit conference room, a dozen men dressed in formal wear were seated uniformly on both sides of the conference table.

The head of the conference table was unoccupied. Behind the seat was a large poster that depicted the logo of a silver-white hollowed-out Earth. A halo of light entwined with green leaves and red stars surrounded the picture of Earth.

This logo was the union emblem of the Earth Federation.

Below the emblem, the words “Central Continent Division of the Federal Bureau of Investigation” was written.

Meanwhile, a middle-aged man with an imposing aura seated at the top right corner of the table said in a low voice while looking at the report in his hand:

“Following the six missing cases that occurred in dormitory building No. 1 of a school in Shangdu on 15 December, 110 additional missing cases were reported in the same building over the next two days. What do you guys think of this incident?”

The middle-aged man had a bed of gray hair but the roots of his hair stood upright. His temperament was as sharp as the tip of a blade. “The police report contains zero valuable information except for the fact that most of them disappeared in the dormitory.”

“Hold on.”

One of the members at the conference table suddenly interjected. “What do you mean when you said that most of them disappeared in the dormitory? You mean that not all of the students disappeared in the dormitory?”

“Of course not.”

The middle-aged man became grave as he said, “The six missing cases that occurred on the first day all occurred in the dormitory building. To be specific, they all occurred in Dorm 404 but two days later, the same incident occurred on a larger scale in the dormitory building. Some of the disappearances were in the toilets, some in other dormitories, the strangest cases are the ones who went missing in the school building during classes...”

“How is it that we’re now only hearing of such a shocking event?”

Seated opposite the middle-aged man was another elderly man who was in his mid-fifties. This man expressed with his brows heavily knitted together. “A grave event like this should’ve been reported to us as early as yesterday. Are the people in the lower organization a bunch of imbeciles?”

“That’s because their department still didn’t dare to pinpoint the exact nature of this incident due to how bizarre the entire event is.”

The middle-aged man answered with unchanging expression, “Only a handful of people were reported missing by last afternoon. Most of them were not written off as being missing yet since they were mostly students and could very well just be loitering around together.

“However, earlier this morning, most of the students in dormitory building No. 1 had disappeared from their beds. The number of cases also skyrocketed from double digits to triple digits. As we speak, this number is still continuously increasing and the cases aren’t restricted to the dormitory only.

“During the first day, it only happened in one specific dormitory. By the second day, the entire dormitory building was affected and it quickly spread into the entire school by the third day...”

Another man in his early forties with coarse skin suddenly thought of something and raised a point. “Is it just me or are these incidents slowly spreading outward like a virus?”

“It seems that way to me too.”

The middle-aged man tipped his head in agreement. “What’s even more worrisome is that the disappearing victims aren’t only students, even some of the police vanished...”

“What!”

“What!”

There was a sudden uproar in the conference room. The question was thrown around. “Is this information verified?”

“We’re very sure of it. So far, three police staff had gone missing. The one thing in common between the three of them is that they have all entered the dormitory at one point.”

The middle-aged man passed the report to his elder colleague. “Take a look and you’ll get what I mean...”

“Little Wang, print more copies at the office and hand them out to everyone here.”

The elderly man instructed before taking the report in hand and started going through it. When he started reading it, everyone immediately noticed sweat dripping down his forehead and along his back...

Was it cold sweat?

Everyone present was silently awed by this sight. This man had been second in command at the Federal Bureau of Investigation for many years. There was no spectacle that he had not seen before, but now he was reduced to such a state?

“I have to say, it doesn’t look good.”

After five minutes, the aged man struggled to lift his head back up. His expression had become incomprehensibly grave. “I’ve heard of contagious fever or pneumonia, but never such a thing as contagious disappearance. No matter how you look at this incident, all evidence points at the Dorm 404 as the source.”

“Have we properly investigated that dormitory?” one of them asked again.

“We have. Everything suspicious in the dormitory has been investigated. Even all of the folders in the four laptops inside this dormitory have been thoroughly investigated. We also went through the websites they frequented but there’s no notable discovery.”

The middle-aged man answered, “The only abnormal observation is probably a videotape we found under one of the beds.”

The young man they called Little Wang had returned to the conference room again. He lapped around the conference table and handed a copy of the report to each person before returning to his original seat.

“Videotape?”

Everyone was slightly surprised by this piece of information. Something like a videotape had been obsolete for at least two decades. Why would it show up in a dormitory housed by young university students?

“What are the contents of the videotape?” Some of the members could no longer bear the suspense and flipped to the end of the report. What they saw caught them off guard.

“Ring?”

“That’s right. Odd, isn’t it.”

The middle-aged man held up a report and tapped a paragraph written on the report. “The contents of the videotape is very similar to a Southeast Asian movie called Ring.

“After comparing them side by side, we found there are many similarities between the movie Ring and the videotape we discovered. They both contain elements such as ‘well’, ‘volcano explosion’, ‘combing lady’, it’s completely identical...”

“Maybe it’s just the graduation project of one of the students?’

Someone raised a valid point. “Considering the special effects technology these days, it shouldn’t be too hard for an academy student to recreate the same images.”

“The police have investigated the tape.”

The middle-aged man flipped to the next page. “Six members watched this movie and right after they finished the movie, their phones coincidentally rang all at the same time. The number of the caller was blank and when they picked up, the call was cut off.”

“Isn’t this the same as the plot of the movie?”

Some of them who were familiar with the movie suddenly murmured, “This is the plot of the movie. Anyone who watches this movie will receive a phone call and they’ll die in seven days, isn’t it?”

“That’s right.”

The middle-aged man nodded. “They even tried to track the source of this phone call but the local station reports that their phones didn’t receive any inbound call at that time.”

Everyone immediately descended into silence.

“Maybe we can try to contact the director of this movie.”

Suddenly, the elderly man suggested. “Not just the director, we should meet with the entire casting crew. They were the ones who filmed this movie, I believe that they should be able to contribute to this case.”

“I agree...”

“That’s right, that should be it.”

“Agreed!”

Many people vouched for the same idea.

“Hold on!”

It was then when the young man called Little Wang seated in the final row suddenly drew everyone’s attention and flipped to the final page. “Sudden disappearances aren’t the only abnormal activity reported in that school. There are also cases of people falling to their deaths and sudden deaths, all of them also originated from Dorm 404!”

As he said this, he shot up from his seat and pointed at the relevant article. “The first victim of these cases is a patient with epilepsy called Wang Wei. What’s odd is that he’s never had this syndrome in his medical history nor any of his family members! The method of his death warrants some suspicion as well. He snapped his neck on his own accord!”

Everyone was shocked by this revelation and flipped to the mentioned page.

“Our utmost priority now is to find the people involved. This report says that two people in Dorm 404 are still living. Even if they aren’t the killer, they must be the ones who can offer the most information!”

...

Chen Chen stood outside the school and observed the impeccably tranquil campus. He shook his head.

This was the third day since the incidents began occurring.

The curses began spreading on the campus two days ago. In this span, the number of people killed by Kayako had risen to the hundreds. Everyone on the campus decided that it was better to protect themselves and left the campus without notice. Some of them rented rooms in hotels while others bought tickets to return to their homes.

It was not so much a lack of action on the part of the Federal Bureau of Investigation. It was more so that this event was too bizarre.

Who would have expected an inconspicuous lot to be the spreader of a deadly curse?

Dormitory building No. 1 was no longer the only building that contained the strength of the curse. Some of the classrooms, toilets, even some of the offices on the campus...

All of these places had become the source of the curse.

Now, close to thousands of still-living people afflicted with the curse had evacuated from this place. Some of them came from the south, others from the north, some of them from the inland while others lived along the coast. There were even the occasional foreigners.

These people would speed the curse of Kayako across the globe. There was nothing that could stop the spread.

Even Li Bo chose to leave.

Chen Chen did not try to stop him. After all, the curse was already spreading. It made no difference even if he stopped Li Bo. Still, Chen Chen made sure to give him a stern warning: unless he wanted to bring ruin to his family, he better not return home. If he was going to die, at least die somewhere away from his family.

Although the time now was at the height of noon, the sky cast a depressing gray over the lands below. Thick dark clouds covered the sun and painted the sky with a gloomy haze. Not a trace of sunlight was allowed to shine through.

Chen Chen stood by the entrance of the school for a long time, watching both teachers and students alike fleeing the school.

Each one of them had fear written across their faces. They were afraid of disappearing if they even lingered in the school for a moment longer.

When a group of students passed by Chen Chen, he somehow managed to sense a faint, sinister aura stemming from them.

He knew that this could only mean that this group of people had been afflicted with the curse but were blissfully unaware of it...

If this supernatural event was portrayed as a viral infectious disease, this group of people would be at the height of the incubation period. The disease may not be infectious during the incubation period, but it would not be spread from the fate of the final stage of the curse.

The symptom of breakout would be when Kayako appeared to claim their lives.

The reason Chen Chen did not enter the school nor try to get away was that he was waiting for someone. Considering the dire state of the current situation, the ones above should be dispatching someone to look for him.

Chen Chen suddenly felt someone fixing their gaze on him. This was a queer sensation that could not be put into words. This would commonly be described as “instinct” or “sixth sense”.

However, as someone who was well versed in biology, Chen Chen was aware that the human body contained bioelectricity. The conduction of everything in the body from brain waves to body nerves was transmitted by electrical signals.

This was how some people with hypersensitivity could close their eyes and still sense someone approaching or the gaze of someone on them. This was not because they saw them, but that they could feel the bioelectricity of others.

This was the exact sensation Chen Chen was feeling at this moment.

This was no time to figure out where this acquired hypersensitivity came from. Chen Chen instinctively turned around and immediately saw a filthy beggar whose appearance he could not make out standing in front of a shop lot. This beggar was studying him silently...

Chen Chen felt an odd sense of familiarity when he looked at those eyes.

“You again?”

Chen Chen muttered to himself and turned into a frown. This was the second time he met this beggar. The first time was when he was leaving the Internet cafe on an evening two days ago.

For some unknown reason unknown, he felt a queer familiarity each time he looked at this beggar.

It was as if he was able to see the faint silhouette of another person — Aixen.

Impossible...

Chen Chen shook his head suddenly. There was no way that this beggar he was looking at would be the mysterious Aixen. They only carried a vaguely similar aura.

Arriving at this thought, Chen Chen decided to stop studying this person and turned the other way instead. He knew better than to get involved with any other strange phenomenon now.

After Chen Chen had walked only several paces, a black Chevrolet suddenly swept around the corner and screeched to a stop in front of Chen Chen. The screeching brakes even jolted Chen Chen a little.

The door quickly slid open and revealed two towering figures dressed in dark suits who hopped out of the vehicle.

“Are you student Chen Chen?”

One of them asked in a whisper as he took out his certification, “Zhang Qiang, Federal Bureau of Investigation, please come with us if you don’t mind.”

Chen Chen instinctively ducked backward. It only took a single large stride from one of the men in black to close off Chen Chen’s path of retreat.

“Finally here?”

To their surprise, Chen Chen heaved a sigh of relief before telling them, “Alright, I’ve been wanting to meet the higher-ups at FBI for some time now.”

Without waiting for an invitation, Chen Chen got into the car on his own.

The man in black chuckled softly when he saw this reaction coming from Chen Chen. The two men entered the car from both sides and kept Chen Chen seated between them.

The door shut swiftly after that as they drove off into the distance...

chapters.title: Chapter 370: The Usb Drive Is Broken?
chapters.body:
Chapter 370: The Usb Drive Is Broken?

Chapter 370: The USB Drive is Broken?

Several hours later, Chen Chen switched into another set of clothes after going through an X-ray. All metal objects were removed from him and he was taken to an empty room by the two men in black.

The design of the room was peculiar. It was approximately twenty square meters in size and was brightly illuminated by the dozens of lamps lined along the ceiling. In the middle of the room, there was a table and two chairs while floor mirrors that stood at waist length decorated the four walls.

Cameras were installed in each corner and recorded everything happening inside the room.

As Chen Chen entered, one of the security asked him to sit on one of the chairs.

Unlike the chairs on the opposite end of the table, the one Chen Chen sat on did not have a backrest.

It also did not have a horizontal bar that served as a footrest like regular chairs. Instead of calling it a chair, it would be more appropriate to describe it as a stool.

At one point, Chen Chen was deeply interested in studying criminal psychology. He immediately recognized at first glance that the room was intentionally designed to make his conversation with the FBI agents as uncomfortable as possible.

The chair without a backrest was designed this way to strip away his sense of security. Without a proper footrest, his sense of balance was taken away as well. The irritating brightness coupled with the cold color temperature in the room made it so that it was easy to become disassociated after staying in the room for a prolonged period.

When Chen Chen saw the mirrors, he immediately determined that there were probably at least ten psychologists hiding behind them, observing and dissecting every move he made...

This room was far better equipped than regular interrogation rooms seen in police stations.

After Chen Chen sat down on his uncomfortable chair, the two men in black stood on guard behind him with their arms behind their backs. Although Chen Chen was not strapped with handcuffs, this was essentially the same thing as being cuffed.

Within another ten seconds, the door opened again. A young lady in office attire that wore a stone-cold expression stepped into the room. She wore a pair of gold-framed glasses and as she strode across the room, her heels would click against the marble floor and produce striking noises.

Chen Chen only spared a curious glance before averting his sight away from her.

“Clack!”

When the lady came to the opposite end of the table, she suddenly pulled out a leather whip and slammed it on the table with all her might. Chen Chen was shocked by this. She immediately started. “Chen Chen, male, 19 years old, 20th-grade student in the Department of Biological Sciences of Shangdu Jiaotong University, a native of Handu. Third grade of elementary school, broke a window in the school and was punished by the class teacher and parents. Second grade of junior high school, got in trouble with the school for fighting. Second grade of high school...”

The lady began narrating the events that occurred to Chen Chen throughout his childhood, some of them Chen Chen had even almost completely forgotten about. After she finished her sequence, she asked icily, “Chen Chen, do you understand the situation you are in right now?”

Chen Chen could not help but scratch his head when he heard this and said in a slightly troubled voice, “Sorry, I have no clue. Anyway, why are you dressed so much like the actors in the adult movies I’ve saved on my computer? Are you going to seduce me now?”

“What did you say?” The lady’s eyebrows flared up and her voice grew harsher at this mention.

“I have to admit that you agents have done your homework well. You even know what kind of movies I’ve saved on my computer.”

Chen Chen shook his head and looked slightly disappointed as he said this, “But I think you guys have this mixed up because these adult movies line up better with my dorm leader Wang Wei’s taste. A few days ago, he had to borrow his girlfriend’s computer so he transferred his videos to my computer to hide them. I’m more of an innocent schoolgirl type if I’m being honest...”

“The audacity!”

Before Chen Chen could finish, the lady slammed the table. The sudden loud burst echoed across Chen Chen’s eardrums.

Chen Chen subconsciously craned his neck to the side and covered his ears. His expression grew several degrees colder as he did this. “Enough! Let’s not discuss these unsavory topics for now. Haven’t the FBI had enough of these cases of disappearing students? Let’s talk about how we’re going to solve this problem.”

“You...”

The lady flicked her eyebrows and grew even more agitated. Before she could go on another rampage, the voice of another man came from somewhere in the interrogation room. “Shen Qi, that’s enough.”

The sound traveled from all sides of the room in a three-dimensional stereo pattern. As Chen Chen started to scan his surroundings, the entrance to the interrogation room opened again. This time, a man with ashen gray hair and a stern expression walked in.

“Director, he...”

The lady quickly approached the man and tried to explain herself. The man quickly waved her away and she had no choice but to step to the side. This man came before the table.

Director?

The moment this person announced his presence, Chen Chen began sizing him up. This man appeared to be somewhere in his mid-fifties. His expression was like iron. The ends of his hair stood up like sharp needles in a flat top style. He undoubtedly had the most intimidating aura Chen Chen had ever witnessed in person.

While Chen Chen was sizing this man up, the same favor was being returned as well. The newly-arrived man only started speaking after he sat down. The tone in which he spoke suggested that this man was not to be questioned. “Chen Chen, we’ve gone through your notes. We can meet your stated conditions.”

“Your ranking isn’t high enough.”

However, Chen Chen only shook his head and answered formally, “Branch director still falls a little short of what I requested.”

“Insolent!”

The girl called Shen Qi quickly howled at Chen Chen angrily. Her superior promptly raised a hand to stop her while he addressed Chen Chen, seemingly unbothered. “Of course, I understand that my ranking doesn’t meet your stated requirement. We can see to it that you get to speak to the core officials of the Mainland. Unfortunately, due to the recent cases of disappearances, they are unable to come in person. However, I can arrange for you to speak with them in a video call.”

Chen Chen was relieved when he heard this. “Alright, I’m fine with that.”

The man seemed somewhat relieved when he heard this. Meanwhile, he moved the chair under him to the side of the table.

Chen Chen quickly sensed something happening as he raised his head and looked at the mirror in front of him. He saw the mirror flash before turning into a dark panel and heard muffled noise coming out of it.

What appeared to be a regular piece of mirror had been an electronic display all this time!

Following another flash, the image of a small conference room immediately came into view.

Chen Chen could not stop himself from tightening up and breathing in a drag of cold air when he saw the image displayed before him. What he saw before him were familiar faces that regularly appeared on television!

This was a meeting with the highest-level officials of the Central Continent branch!

“How’s this for satisfying your conditions?”

The man seated at the side of the table turned to him and asked.

Chen Chen inspected the people exchanging silent whispers among themselves who occasionally stole glances in his direction, he nodded. “Yes, if this is still not satisfactory, nothing else could cut it anymore. Judging by this, I take it that you guys already know about the USB drive?”

“How did you know?” The man perched an eyebrow.

“I doubt that the ongoing spiritual abnormality in Shangdu that hasn’t even got to its worst stages is enough to demand an audience with the council.”

Chen Chen smiled. “I’d wager that what warranted you guys’ attention has more to do with the USB drive I possess. I think it’s rather clear that you guys got to Li Bo before this and found out about the USB drive used to instantiate objects from movies from him. This is the primary reason you guys invested this much effort.”

“You’re a smart one. It’d be a waste to not become a detective.”

The man nodded when he heard Chen Chen’s remark and expressed. “From the very beginning until now, you’ve been trying to stake your place in the driver seat of this negotiation. Apart from this being a sign of your intelligence, it’s also a telltale sign of how nervous you’ve been feeling. There’s no need for any of this, it’s wiser to hand the USB drive to us because there’s no way you can control it.”

The man pointed at the screen to his side. “Now that there are so many witnesses, I suppose you won’t have to be worried about me killing you and taking it by force?”

“You don’t have to tell me that, I already know you weren’t going to do that anyway.”

Chen Chen frowned. “Otherwise I wouldn’t claim to know how to fix this abnormality in my notes.”

“Spiritual abnormality, so that’s what you’re naming this?”

The man held up the notes Chen Chen referred to and nodded amiably. “Not bad, so where did you put the USB drive?”

“From the back entrance of the Roman Holiday Hotel on Wuhe Street, take twenty-five steps to the left. On the bottom of the left wall, you should spot a crack in the wall. That’s where the USB flash drive is hidden.”

Chen Chen answered without hesitation, “As for the shape of the USB drive, I’m sure Li Bo has told you about it.”

“Excellent, let’s hope it’s still there.”

The man was silently relieved at how Chen Chen revealed its location without much trouble. He originally thought that Chen Chen would raise more conditions but it turned out this young man had surprised him with how cooperative he was.

At this moment, he could not help but take interest in this well-guarded while at the same time strangely calm young man.

“Chen Chen, according to your notes, your method of fixing this crisis is to use the power of the USB drive. In that case, why didn’t you do it yourself with the USB drive? Say, put an exorcist movie into the drive to instantiate the relevant tools for purging the ghost?”

The girl called Shen Qi raised the question again. “Is it because of certain limitations of the USB drive?”

“The problem is a lack of power.”

Chen Chen nodded amiably as a show of sincerity. He had to protect himself as well, which was why he gave away the location of the USB flash drive without any resistance. It would not make much sense now to be stubborn, so he laid everything on the table. “The USB drive needs to be powered to instantiate objects. After releasing the entities in four horror films, there’s no longer any power left in the USB drive.”

“What’s the energy source? How can we recharge it?”

It was neither Shen Qi nor the branch director who raised this question, but one of the men on the screen opposite Chen Chen.

Chen Chen let the silence linger for a moment. He considered for a bit and instead of telling them about the rules of the USB drive which he was well acquainted with, he pretended to be unsure. “I think it’s electricity.”

“How much electricity?” the man on the screen asked.

“A lot.”

Chen Chen smiled. “The specifics can be obtained by using Einstein’s mass-energy equation.”

Everyone’s faces turned when they heard this. Going by the energy conversion equation, for each gram of the item they planned to instantiate, at least an hour’s worth of electricity used to power an international metropolis would be required.

Chen Chen did not clarify that they did not need that much energy. The reason he did so was that he wanted to inject a sense of urgency into them. After all, he was still infected with the Kayako curse at the moment.

An eerie silence quickly descended on the conference room.

It was then when another man wearing a suit entered the interrogation room and came beside the branch director and whispered something into his ears. The branch director was immediately relieved upon receiving the message and sent the agent away with a nod before turning to Chen Chen. “We found the USB drive.”

With that, he immediately got up and bowed earnestly at Chen Chen. “I’d like to thank you on behalf of the Central Continent and the Earth Federation. If the USB drive’s properties are just as you described, it must be an extremely high-tech object. This will be greatly beneficial to mankind!”

“Don’t get too excited just yet.”

“Leave all that aside and worry about how you guys are going to charge the USB drive first. The top priority now is to deal with the Ju-on curse, otherwise, you may find that by tomorrow, the disappearance is going to spread beyond Shangdu and expand across the entire Mainland.”

The branch director answered gravely, “We’ve considered the possibility of this as well and have already quarantined all of the people who left the school. Even the ones who returned home by taking the train or planes will be quarantined by the officials at their destinations upon their arrival. Even the public officers that have entered the dormitory have been quarantined as well. There’s nothing to worry about.”

D*mn!

When Chen Chen heard this, he realized that he had been worrying too much over nothing. The fully unleashed strength of the country was certainly not to be underestimated. To think that tens of thousands of people could be quarantined without the outside world even catching wind of it, he was thoroughly impressed.

While Chen Chen silently marveled at this, the entrance to the interrogation room suddenly burst open. A man in black charged inside nervously while exclaiming, “This is bad, the USB drive broke when we tried to charge it just now!”

“What!”

The color on Chen Chen’s face was immediately drained away.

The USB drive already came with a set of rules on how it should be charged which clearly stated that it could not be damaged regardless of the voltage running through it. How did it manage to break?

Perhaps the FBI was trying to deceive the council and the branch director was a foreign spy trying to snatch away the USB drive?

Chen Chen debunked this theory as soon as he came up with it.

It was true that the Earth Federation was just a shell of its former self and the FBI that was originally meant to be under the jurisdiction of the Earth Federation had now become the manpower of each continent. Still, he heavily doubted that the situation had become hectic enough that they would try to snatch the USB drive right under the prying eyes of the head council. Besides, would the FBI have not some insurance in place to prevent such a scenario?

Unless there were other spies?

Countless speculations flashed through Chen Chen’s mind in an instant, but most of them were too much of a stretch to be believable.

It was not only Chen Chen who showed such a drastic reaction as the same reaction could be observed on all the people present at the scene. Inside the screen, many of the men glared daggers at the branch director seated beside Chen Chen. The branch director was sweating endlessly.

There was no question about it. They had also immediately suspected that this was the work of the branch director based on a logical assumption.

chapters.title: Chapter 371: Narrow Escape From Death
chapters.body:
Chapter 371: Narrow Escape From Death

Chapter 371: Narrow Escape from Death

“Hurry and take me there!”

The branch director bellowed before quickly darting across the interrogation and leaving in a frenzy.

Shen Qi and the other two men in black in charge of watching over Chen Chen did the same as well. Shen Qi shot one last glare at Chen Chen before chasing after her superior. Even the display in front of Chen Chen was shut off and reverted to an ordinary mirror.

In an instant, Chen Chen became the only one left in the room.

Even Chen Chen was at a loss for words. He had prepared himself for hundreds of thousands of scenarios, but this one had never crossed his mind.

By the looks of the current situation, there were only three explanations for the destruction of the USB drive.

One, somebody switched it out after Chen Chen hid it.

This was not out of the realm of possibility. Even though Chen Chen had been extremely cautious and hid the USB drive in an extremely secluded place that had no surveillance, he was not exactly what one would call an expert. There was a chance that someone had been spying on him when he hid the USB drive and swapped it out stealthily.

The second possibility, the USB drive was swapped out by a spy in the FBI with a fake.

This possibility seemed to be more likely than the first one, but it was not any less tricky. After all, there was no way for Chen Chen to prove whether the broken USB drive was the real thing or not. He suspected he would have a difficult time convincing them that the broken USB drive was a substituted fake.

The final possibility. Chen Chen had only told three people in his dorm about the existence of the USB drive. Now that both Wang Wei and Zhou Jie were dead, only Li Bo knew about the USB drive. He had known Li Bo for a very long time. Unless he had been hatching ideas about the USB drive...

Chen Chen became speechless upon considering this possibility.

If it was Li Bo who was the one behind this, then he certainly deserved the Oscars for the show he had put on. It would be a great waste of talent for him to not be an actor.

Chen Chen figured there was no point considering any other possibilities. Without the USB drive, they were just sitting ducks.

While Chen Chen was beginning to despair, the lights on the ceiling of the interrogation room began to rattle on and on.

The interrogation room was designed with the idea of imposing a negative psychological effect on the interrogated in mind. Dozens of tubes of lights were installed on the ceiling that cast a blinding sheet of harsh and cold beams on the room.

Seemingly due to the unstable voltage, they started to flicker without any signs of stopping anytime soon. The state of the room constantly swung between light and darkness.

Initially, Chen Chen paid no mind to it. After this constant flickering persisted for more than ten seconds, he started sensing that something was off.

Where was he again? This was not some sort of remote village or a unit in the borders of Africa. This was the FBI branch directly under the jurisdiction of the regional government. How could there possibly be electricity disturbance?

Chen Chen immediately felt a chill coursing through him when he considered this. He raised his head and looked at the huge mirror opposite him. Between the disorientating flashes of light, he caught the glimpse of a little boy standing behind him...

The little boy was not wearing any clothes. The color of his body was a terrible pale that looked like the skin of the deceased. The boy stood in the corner of the room and stared into Chen Chen’s eyes with terrible dark eyes without a trace of white in them.

It was Toshio, the son of Kayako.

Chen Chen immediately felt a harsh chill penetrating through his body from behind him and made the hair stand up on the back of his neck.

“Am I going to die?”

This thought invaded and occupied Chen Chen’s mind. He lost the strength to get up on his feet. All he could do was look into the innocent Toshio standing in the corner of the room.

“Hnnghhhh...”

He heard gurgling patrolling in his ears. Unlike the last time Chen Chen heard this voice, it came from behind him this time, almost inches away!

Chen Chen felt as if his blood was about to freeze. He became petrified, unsure if it was because of the pulsing fear inside him or because of the power of Kayako. Chen Chen remained unmoving and stared into his reflection in the mirror. From his reflection, he could see two slender, delicate arms slowly creeping behind his back...

The pairs of arms constantly bent at awkward angles as if they were performing some sort of ritualistic dance. Just like Toshio in the corner, they were the color of the dead. They slowly crept up from a short distance behind Chen Chen before latching themselves onto Chen Chen’s neck!

In an instant, an indescribable feeling assaulted his five senses. The harsh bite seemed to penetrate through his bones and sent across his body like an electric shock!

Just with the arms placed on his shoulder, Chen Chen felt like he was going to fall apart then and there!

“Hnnnghhhhh....”

The hoarse gurgling sound screaming at him was just inches away now, but Chen Chen could hardly muster the strength to turn around. All he could do was stare into his reflection and saw a black object rising from behind his right shoulder in between the flickering lights.

It was a head with disheveled hair!

Chen Chen’s expression twitched uncontrollably as he made a desperate effort to turn to his right. Suddenly, he saw his world turn dark. In a few seconds, the light was turned on again. When the light returned, he saw a pair of midnight black eyes only three centimeters away from his own, gazing right into him!

Such eyes they were, completely devoid of senses and lacking any ounce of humanity. They were two bottomless abysses that screamed hatred for life!

After that, the light in the room went out again!

In this split instant, Chen Chen felt the arms behind him dragging him backward. His stream of thoughts became dull and muddy, as if just mere touch delivered great pain to him. Inside the darkness, he felt he was dragged out of the chair and into the depthless, unknown darkness...

It was at this moment when Chen Chen suddenly heard the clicking sound of a door opening beside him...

The sound sounded almost godsend. Chen Chen simultaneously felt a ray of light beaming from the door. When the light was cast on Chen Chen, he felt his body winding down and the feeling of being dragged away quickly dissipated.

When Chen Chen came to his senses, he saw that the lights overhead had returned to a normal state. He was still seated in the chair in the same position as he previously did.

“Chen Chen! You... Hm?”

Shen Qi was furious when she stomped inside. For some reason, the light in the room was turned off when she walked in. Before she could respond accordingly, there was an abrupt flash followed by the lights turning back on again.

On top of that, she felt an inexplicable chill the moment she stepped inside the room.

It was not the sort of cold felt as a mere decrease of temperature but a kind of ringing cold that penetrated deep into the bones. The moment she sensed it, she felt goosebumps rising on her body like a pulse of electricity. Such a sensation could never be related by anyone who had not experienced it.

She did not make any remark about it but instead, approached Chen Chen right away. “Chen Chen. Tell me, why did the USB drive break? Are you trying to deceive us with a fake? I’m talking to you! Chen Chen? Chen Chen!”

Shen Qi threw question after question at him. She then noticed something was off. Although Chen Chen was looking at her, his expression was unbelievably pale. His green-ish veins faintly popped from under his skin as if he had been stricken with a horrible disease. It was slightly reminiscent of the color of a corpse.

“What’s going on?”

When Shen Qi was still perplexed by this sight, the FBI branch director stepped into the room again. This time, he no longer carried his previous poise but was instead ridden by anxiety and even a slight hint of anger. When he saw Chen Chen’s face, he became shocked. “Did he commit suicide by ingesting poison? Hurry, get the doctor!”

Fortunately, the FBI had designated doctors. Within five minutes, several doctors and nurses rushed inside with devices and first aid kits in tow. Just as Chen Chen was carried on the stretcher, he began to come back to his senses.

“I, I’m alright!”

Chen Chen struggled to get up while gasping for air.

“Subdue him!”

Shen Qi barked the order. When the other officers heard this and tried to step forward, Chen Chen suddenly grabbed at the collar of his shirt and yanked it.

Two black marks were immediately revealed under his collar.

“This is?”

The doctors were astounded by the sight of it. From their point of view, the mark was even clearer and distinctly resembled the shape of two palms. The outline of each finger could not be any more vivid. The left and right hand could be clearly identified.

“This is the curse of Ju-on...”

Chen Chen brushed the mark on his shoulder but felt numbness on it. He could hardly sense anything there, not even pain.

“This is a symptom of skin necrosis. It seems to have been this way for a long time, at least half a month.”

One of the doctors frowned. “But if it’s been that long, it should’ve been inflamed by now, unlike its current state.”

The branch director became perplexed. As the highest executive officer here, he was well aware that this young man had a full-body examination conducted when they brought him in. CT scan was conducted thoroughly to search for the USB drive.

There were no visible wounds on the young man back then.

“Of course, that’s because I was attacked by the spiritual abnormality.”

Chen Chen expressed with faint anger. “Where did the psychologists go? Shouldn’t they be observing me from behind the mirror? How is it that they didn’t notice me being attacked?”

The branch director quickly turned to Shen Qi for an answer.

“After finding out about the USB drive breaking, they came with us to inspect it...”

Shen Qi answered.

“Then, that means not a single person saw me just now?”

Chen Chen let out an exasperated sigh. He cursed at the fact that such a large department would fail to oversee just one person. If not for someone barging in to interrupt Kayako, his corpse would have become stiff by now.

When Chen Chen thought of this, he suddenly caught a stray thought passing through his mind. He tried to grab it but it seemed to escape him. He could not recall what exactly it was that he thought of.

“Hurry and inspect the wound on Chen Chen’s shoulder!”

The branch director knew that this was no time to point fingers at anyone. He hid the anger and agitation in his voice as he demanded. “Chen Chen, we’re still going to need you to confirm if the USB drive is real, we suspect that someone may have swapped it.”

“Of course, I’d like to see for myself too.”

Chen Chen nodded meekly.

After that, the host of doctors carted the stretcher out of the interrogation room and down the corridor.

It did not take long before Chen Chen arrived at an elevator at the end of the corridor. The door of the elevator was far larger and sturdier than common elevators. When the doors shuffled open, an enormous space behind it was immediately revealed.

There were two armed guards already posted inside the elevator.

“Step right in.”

Following the order of the branch director, the doctors entered. The doctors waited until they had entered the elevator before they started to tend to the marks on both sides of Chen Chen’s shoulders. Meanwhile, Chen Chen investigated the elevator curiously and noted that there were no buttons in the elevator. The elevator descended automatically after the door was closed.

It would appear that there was an underground structure under the FBI building.

Chen Chen ruminated over this. For some reason, the idea of an underground base felt vaguely familiar to him.

The elevator descended for a minute before coming to a halt. When the doors opened again, Chen Chen was greeted by the sight of a vast underground space.

The space was around the size of an underground parking lot except it was far taller than an underground parking lot would otherwise be. It was nearly ten meters tall and dozens of strange instruments could be seen in the plaza.

Large bundles of cables with diameter the size of an infant’s fist connected these array of devices. Some of the devices were repeatedly emitting blue flashes of light. The scenery reminded one of a futuristic steampunk film.

It would appear that this was where the USB drive was charged.

Although Chen Chen was unsure what these devices did, he first debunked one possibility by process of elimination — they were not generators.

While Chen Chen was puzzling over this, he saw six soldiers armed with live ammunition advancing toward him in a straight line. Positioned in their formation was a white-bearded elderly man dressed in a white coat.

The elderly man approached with a metal box in hand. He came in front of Chen Chen and presented the box to them before opening it.

The contents of the box were none other than the USB drive that had shattered into four irregular pieces.

“Old Yu, what’s the verdict?”

Though he was restless upon seeing the USB drive, the branch director still kept himself collected and asked courteously.

“There are no internal structures whatsoever. This USB drive is an entirely solid object.”

The professor named Old Yu picked up a piece of USB drive fragment with his hands wrapped in gloves. A hint of wonder and sorrow appeared in his eyes as he did this. “Even after magnifying its composition millions of times using the most powerful tunneling electron microscope, there’s still nothing that could be observed. This isn’t any ordinary material structure that should belong to our word. I’m sorry, with our current technology, we can hardly conduct any worthwhile analysis...”

The branch director first felt slightly relieved when he heard this but this was immediately followed by a sense of heaviness.

At the very least, this proved that the USB drive was the real deal and that it was not swapped at any point.

However, if the USB drive was broken, how were they going to deal with the ongoing disappearances occurring in the city?

“May I have a look?”

While the branch director was rifling through his mind, Chen Chen spoke suddenly. He held his hand out and asked the bearded elderly man, “Old Yu, I’d like to see if this is the fragment I obtained previously.”

“That won’t do. The USB drive is already ruined as is. What if you cause further damage to it?” Shen Qi immediately tried to stop Chen Chen.

“It’s alright, let him.”

It was the elderly man who handed the box to Chen Chen on his own accord. He also mentioned, “We’ve tested the toughness and resilience of these USB drive fragments. Even cut diamonds could not inflict the slightest scratch on it. We’re certain it surpasses the current scope of mankind’s technology.”

“Thank you.”

Chen Chen received the box and picked up a fragment to investigate it closely.

However, the moment Chen Chen touched its fragments, he suddenly felt a burning sensation. His grip loosened as he dropped the fragment into the box. What happened next made everyone’s eyes turn wide!

When the USB flash drive fell into the box, the fragments of the USB drive morphed into a puddle of pure black liquid. It quickly flowed toward other fragments inside the box and upon making contact with the second fragment, the second fragment melted as well. This happened subsequently to the third and fourth fragment!

A miracle happened when all the USB drive fragments melted and pooled together...

chapters.title: Chapter 372: Memory Restored
chapters.body:
Chapter 372: Memory Restored

Chapter 372: Memory Restored

A miracle happened when all the USB drive fragments melted and pooled together...

The black mass of liquid floated into the air. The tip of the USB drive soon emerged from the mass of liquid and slowly pushed outward. Then, the mass of liquid slowly disappeared as this happened.

With everyone beholding it, the puddle of liquid had become solid and morphed into a pitch-black USB drive that was completely undamaged!

The USB drive dropped onto Chen Chen’s palm.

“Chen Chen, after I count from one to ten, you’ll walk inside and return to the night when you merged with the USB-drive...”

Amid all this, Chen Chen heard an incredibly familiar whisper in his mind. The next second, he clutched at his head with both hands and began to scream horribly!

“Ahhhh!!!”

“What’s going on!”

The branch director froze. The first thing he did after was to snatch the USB drive away from Chen Chen before shouting, “Doctor, hurry and save him, this person is extremely vital to our cause!”

A host of doctors immediately swarmed toward Chen Chen and strapped him onto the stretcher before rushing him to the emergency room.

Fortunately, the emergency room was not too far away and they arrived in a matter of minutes. After that, the doctors swiftly turned on all sorts of devices and connected electrocardiography and electroencephalography devices to Chen Chen.

“He’s starting to foam at the mouth!”

One of the nurses declared urgently.

“The EEG shows that his cerebral cortex is abnormally active!”

A doctor looked at the reading on the EEG. “His cerebral cortex and hippocampus are undergoing extremely intense chemical reactions, it’s like a prophetic storm in there!”

“Brain pressure is skyrocketing and his brain temperature has exceeded 42 degrees. The patient is in critical condition!”

The doctor who was measuring his temperature suddenly turned pale. “A large amount of cerebral hemorrhage is detected, prepare for craniotomy immediately!”

...

In this state of daze, Chen Chen seemed to feel something pouring into his mind.

Strings of memories ever since he obtained the USB drive began to stream into the depths of his mind — NZT-48, Little X, God chip, God’s Forbidden Zone, GS Combat Suit, Mankind Anti-aging Project, Eco Science City...

In a matter of minutes, an enormous amount of what seemed to be unfamiliar memories swarmed into his mind!

These memories led to changes in the physical structure of Chen Chen’s brain. Such changes should usually occur over years, but it was now unraveling in the span of just a few minutes!

After Chen Chen’s brain structure continued to shift until a certain point, Chen Chen suddenly snapped open his eyes!

At this point, one of the doctors had just prepared a tranquilizer and was about to give Chen Chen the injection. Just as the doctor turned to his patient, he immediately met Chen Chen’s eyes.

“You?”

The doctor was caught off guard by this. Before he could enact a response, he felt a terrific impact on his chest. He barely had time to whimper before he was sent flying away!

“Old Yu!”

When the other doctors turned to Chen Chen with their faces contorted in shock, they saw Chen Chen unstrapping himself from the stretcher before he levitated into the air above!

“Ah, I see how it is now.”

Chen Chen chuckled. His eyes had turned blood red. It was a symptom caused by a large number of capillary ruptures. The blood pooled around the whites of his eyes, he looked like a demon risen from the underworld.

None of this mattered. What mattered was that Chen Chen had remembered everything.

“So, this is the dream dimension of my past memory?”

Chen Chen looked below him coldly and turned into a frown while he did so. “No, this isn’t just a dream, it’s... Something that even I have trouble figuring out. At last, I know that the USB drive had never done something like this in my past recollection...”

When Chen Chen murmured to himself, the doctors evacuated the emergency room as if they had seen a ghost. They did not even bother to save the doctor sent flying away by Chen Chen. They rushed out and locked the airlock on their way out, sealing Chen Chen inside.

Chen Chen paid no mind to them and instead, raised his hand and looked at his palm.

“This must be ‘Field’, but it’s stronger than it was in reality... Just how strong is it?”

Chen Chen jerked his head to the side and looked at the wall in front of him without emotion. The next second, a terrible screech was heard as the alloy-built wall became bent inside-out!
