Chen Chen was sprawled on the ground, his hands and feet convulsing uncontrollably. It felt like only seconds had passed, yet it also felt like centuries. He did not know how long it took. It was only after that desperate feeling of death swept over him like a wave that Chen Chen slowly recovered...

That kind of feeling was comparable to the first time he faced the Basilisk pattern. If his willpower was not strong enough, Chen Chen would have rather died than bore this kind of pain.

“That, that is... An antimeme!”

Chen Chen collapsed and sat up, panting heavily. Although he had experienced agony comparable to death, at this moment, his brain had never been so clear as if it had been washed by a clear spring.

“No wonder my memory had errors. So, it’s all thanks to an antimeme!”

Chen Chen gritted his teeth, resisting the subsiding pain, and at the same time, analyzed in a low voice, “It turns out that the night I was injected with the T-virus, I had this dream...’I Saw You’, is that the name of this antimeme?”

Chen Chen could not help frowning. “However, how did this antimeme find me? Could it be...”

Chen Chen’s pupils suddenly shrank!

At this moment, the quantum computer of the Earth Federation flashed through his mind.

He still remembered that after he had gained power by relying on the USB flash drive, he had wanted to find out its origin. After all, the USB drive came from Shangdu Jiao Tong University, where he was located. If he began with the school, it would not be difficult to find the source of the USB flash drive.

Due to this, he used Little X to investigate in many ways, but the origin of the USB drive was always like a black hole and not a single clue could be unearthed...

However, if he linked this to the nature of this antimeme, where all traces of a person’s existence would disappear after it had swallowed a person, then was it possible that this antimeme existed a long time ago?

Following this line of thinking, Chen Chen even arrived at a terrifying hypothesis —

If this antimeme had existed a long time ago, would it not be possible that the previous owner of the USB drive was swallowed by it?

In other words, the USB drive owner before Chen Chen was not non-existent. They might have used the USB drive to stir up some major events affecting the world and might even have rewritten the historical process of the world. No clues could be found by Little X because they were also swallowed by the antimeme!

This conjecture was not unfounded because the quantum computer of the Earth Federation was the strongest piece of evidence!

This thought flashed across Chen Chen’s mind but it made him shudder — if it was true, would it not mean that when he got the USB flash drive, the antimeme had already followed him, only it had been lurking in the dark. Was it only activated when certain conditions were fulfilled?

“Heh heh!”

Chen Chen laughed lowly. At this time, the death-like pain had completely disappeared. He stood up a little dazedly, a dark glint flashing in his eyes.

This antimeme was silently devouring this world...

The disappearance of Li Bo was the best proof.

Not only Li Bo, Chen Chen clearly remembered that there were more than 30,000 students in the entire school, but in the interrogation room at that time, the director’s memory and the information found showed that there were only more than 20,000 people.

It was not that Chen Chen’s memory was erroneous, but that the remaining 10,000 people were swallowed by the antimeme.

The reason Chen Chen could still remember the real number without being affected by the antimeme was not that he was special, but that he had memories from realities.

However, this was the limit.

In the subsequent spiritual abnormality that erupted across the entire city, it was unknown how many people died at the hands of this antimeme. It silently hid in the shadow of Ju-on, devouring lives, yet no one noticed...

This antimeme was not only devouring this parallel world, but also Chen Chen’s dream.

In other words, the goal of this antimeme, from beginning to end, had always been Chen Chen. Only by swallowing this parallel dimension that was mixed up with Chen Chen’s dream, would it completely swallow Chen Chen...

This would explain why Shen Qi suddenly appeared to save him when he was about to be killed by the grudge-curse — because he had been possessed by this antimeme. If he was killed by the grudge-curse in advance, it would have ruined the antimeme’s plan to devour him.

Even Zhou Jie’s unleashing of the four horror films was probably all caused by this antimeme, so that it could hide in the shadow of the spiritual abnormality and prevent Chen Chen from detecting it...

However, would an abstract existence like an antimeme, which was more like a law of nature, have its own thinking?

A dark look flitted across Chen Chen’s eyes, as a more terrifying conjecture gradually came to mind —

Perhaps there was another possibility...

chapters.title: Chapter 378: Eyes
chapters.body:
Chapter 378: Eyes

Chapter 378: Eyes

Perhaps the reason the antimeme could do everything it had done up until now was not that the antimeme had a human mind, but that someone was behind it.

“Could it be ‘that person’?”

Chen Chen muttered in a low voice and a theory emerged vaguely in his mind.

Still, in the next second, Chen Chen frowned again. “If it’s really ‘that person’, then how did ‘he’ do it?

“I saw you...”

Chen Chen kept chewing on this sentence, trying to find some clues, but he still did not get anything.

Thus, Chen Chen simply put aside this question for the time being and turned to look at the environment of this Ju-on’s dimension.

Only then did he realize that his current location was on a hill.

It was already noon. Looking down from the top of the hill, Chen Chen could see a small town. The streets were full of bustling traffic. He could even hear the hustle and bustle of the city and the noise of the market.

“What’s this?”

Chen Chen looked over in amazement. This was the world of Ju-on. According to the inherent nature of the USB flash drive, it should have been a world that was already destroyed. How could there be people?

Besides Gantz, this was the second time Chen Chen had encountered a dimension with humans.

Could it be that the Basilisk pattern vaccine provided immunity to memes?

Chen Chen quickly took out the material from his pocket and looked at the image recorded in it, but when he saw the image, Chen Chen suddenly felt himself becoming lighter.

It was as if a weight was taken off his heart. Chen Chen felt himself quivering, overcome by a sense of vigor and vitality...

It truly worked!

Chen Chen was overjoyed as if something had been removed from him. It was like being relieved of a burden, which he knew must have been the Ju-on meme.

“Sure enough, I was right.”

Chen Chen whispered. The reason this occurred was that the Basilisk pattern was invalid in reality and it was only effective after one had entered the movie’s world, probably because the Basilisk pattern had limitations.

After all, the Basilisk pattern was not a complete meme but was more similar to an artificially processed inactivated vaccine, which had lost its effectiveness due to the environment. The dimension of the new Ju-on and the dream world where Chen Chen was located were essentially distinct.

If it were a complete meme, a little difference was not a big deal and it could still spread, and even actively mutate and evolve like a virus. However, for the Basilisk pattern, it was difficult to do this.

Therefore, this type of Basilisk pattern was extracted, yet it was invalid. Chen Chen needed to come to Ju-on’s dimension to suit it.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen looked at the cave-like passage behind him. At that time, the situation was extremely urgent. Chen Chen had no other choice, so he came through here desperately, but he was indeed too lucky, now that he thought about it.

He had no idea what was the situation on the other side of the passage now. After entering the passage, the laptop must have fallen from a high altitude. If the laptop was broken, would it mean that the passage was closed and he could not go back again?

Still, now it appeared that the channel had not disappeared.

It seemed that there were indeed many undiscovered rules of the USB drive and this was likely one of them.

This thought flashed by in Chen Chen’s mind. Now that the passage had not disappeared, there was no rush to return. Therefore, Chen Chen took a big stride forward and rose into the air as if breaking free of gravity, and flew toward the town at the foot of the mountain.

The closer Chen Chen was, the more he could see that this town was obviously a typical Japanese town. The surrounding area was full of wooden Japanese-style self-built houses, and the commercial area in the center of the town was filled with the sleazy, gaudy atmosphere of shops and pubs. There were even ramen restaurants.

As he flew closer and closer, the noises of the town got louder and louder. Countless pedestrians and vehicles passed by on the street and in a park, he could even see hundreds of children playing raucously in large crowds. The children were all wearing kindergarten uniforms, which were dark blue.

However, when Chen Chen saw this scene, his expression gradually stiffened.

He hovered halfway up the hill and did not fly down.

Indescribable anxiety gripped Chen Chen’s mind. He gradually realized that this city seemed to be too lively.

From the top of the hill, the area of this town indicated it was a small town that could accommodate tens of thousands of people, but if he put together all the densely packed pedestrians on the street, there seemed to be something inexplicably fishy.

Surely the streets of this small town were a little too busy.

Not only that, but Chen Chen was also looking at the only park in the town. The park was crowded with thousands of children chasing and playing, creating an extremely festive atmosphere.

However, in a small town with ten thousand people, where would more than a thousand kindergarten children come from?

Chen Chen stood in the high distance and drifted over the town, but only found one kindergarten. No matter how one looked at it, the kindergarten was of average size and could only take in over a hundred children at most.

Even so, the number of kids in the park was more than ten times this number.

Something was wrong, very wrong...

This town seemed to have an evil aura.

Chen Chen narrowed his eyes. After noticing these unreasonable scenes, he finally stopped hesitating, turned, and flew toward the sky.

However, the moment he turned around, the hustle and bustle of the town behind Chen Chen fell silent!

It was as if a TV had been blaring in a room, but someone had suddenly switched it off without any warning. In an instant, it was so quiet, one could hear a pin drop.

This peculiar development sent a chill over Chen Chen’s scalp. As he flew further up, he turned to look back...

Chen Chen could see all the pedestrians walking on the streets and the children playing in the park across the town. At this moment, they all stopped their activities. They stood stiffly in place while raising their heads, staring fixedly at Chen Chen.

This sight caused Chen Chen’s heart to shiver. He blinked involuntarily.

However, when he looked again, he found that the entire town was empty. There were no pedestrians on the streets or children playing in the park, none at all.

The entire town was empty without a single soul as if the scene just now was an illusion in his mind...

“Sure enough, I was too optimistic.”

Chen Chen did not look back and rushed straight to the top of the hill, returning to the passage of the black hole.

Just when Chen Chen was about to head back, he suddenly thought of something and the movement of his feet suddenly froze.

He suddenly realized that if he went back right now, everything he had inferred just now would be forgotten again. This was the most basic ability of antimemes and it was impossible to prevent it anyway.

If he wanted to defeat the antimeme, the only way was to find “that person”...

Nonetheless, even if he saw that person, how was he supposed to remember everything under the influence of the antimeme?

Just when Chen Chen was stalled by uncertainty, a weird cry suddenly came from behind him.

It was a baffling crying noise. The voice stretched on forever as if there was no need to breathe. Every cry was heart-piercing and Chen Chen’s whole body shuddered in terror...

Moreover, this cry was just one voice at first, but as time passed, one after another, more voices joined the crying. In an instant, it seemed that thousands of people were crying together, rushing from the foot of the hill in the direction of Chen Chen...

What kind of people could cry ceaselessly without gasping for air?

In Chen Chen’s mind, the answer faintly emerged.

“D*mn it!”

Chen Chen cursed. Now he must return to the passage immediately, otherwise, he would only end up dying here. Still, if he returned, everything he had remembered just now would be forgotten again.

At once, Chen Chen was caught in a dilemma.

“Wait, hold on!”

The sound of crying behind him was getting closer and closer. When he was about to reach the top of the hill, his mind suddenly made a connection and he thought of a movie —

Event Horizon!

“So, is that it?”

At this moment, Chen Chen seemed to understand something and it all suddenly became clear in his mind. The next second, Chen Chen gritted his teeth and promptly raised his hands to clasp his eyes!

“Win or lose, it all comes down to this!”

As Chen Chen muttered in a low voice, his fingers directly gripped his eyes while he exerted a violent force. Suddenly, his index and middle fingers were inserted straight into the gap between the eye socket and the eyeball!

“Agh agh agh agh agh!!”

Strips of blue veins protruded from Chen Chen’s forehead and an extreme pain seized him. Chen Chen could only sense countless gold stars twinkling in front of him and he could not help but let out a spine-chilling scream!

Even so, he still did not let go, but clenched his fingers and tugged at the same time!

“Splat!”

With two sharp sounds of nerves and muscle fiber snapping, Chen Chen’s vision that was dazzled with golden stars now disappeared totally. His eyes were fully plunged into endless darkness!

Chen Chen let out a stifled wail and the intense pain caused him to tremble like a leaf, but at this time Chen Chen had no time to recover at all. He immediately stepped forward and entered the passage!

This was because, at this time, the crying behind him was already close at hand...

The moment he entered, Chen Chen seemed to hear an angry roar behind him but before he could hear it clearly, his body suddenly turned weightless, and he tumbled out of the passage!

The moment he returned from the passage, Chen Chen collapsed directly to the ground. He covered his eyes and rolled over in pain, unable to recover for a while.

About half an hour later, Chen Chen sat up again. His eyes were closed tightly and the two lines of blood stained his cheeks, flowing from the eye sockets.

At this time, Chen Chen’s hands were tightly gripping two spherical items...

Those were the two eyeballs removed by Chen Chen himself.

“What happened?”

Chen Chen kept his eyes closed and murmured in a low voice, “What happened across the passage? Why did I make up my mind to blind myself?”

At this moment, Chen Chen had once again forgotten everything about the antimeme on the other side of the portal. The only thing he could remember now was that he had blinded himself for a certain purpose.

“The channel has the ability to cut off memes. Doesn’t this mean that after entering the channel, I’ve recalled what the antimeme is?”

Chen Chen looked thoughtful.

However, now was not the time to think about this. Chen Chen, who had lost his eyes, must instead try to find something to replace his vision.

Obviously, “Field” would be a very good method.

Thus, Chen Chen began to try observing the surroundings with the power of “Field”.

He did not know why, in this dream world, his Field energy was more than ten times stronger than in reality. This also meant that the range he could explore by relying on Field energy had expanded, reaching a range of 20 to 30 meters.

At this time, under the sensing of Chen Chen’s Field energy, he had “seen” everything in a radius of more than 40 square meters.

This was the middle of a wide road. Chen Chen realized that the exit of the passage was still on the computer screen, but the Alienware notebook he was holding had fallen to the ground from a high altitude and broken to pieces.

On the smashed wreck of the computer, there was also a USB drive flickering with stars.

Chen Chen slowly squatted and pulled out the USB flash drive from the wreckage of the computer. Only then, the dark portal on the computer screen vanished completely...

“As long as the USB drive was not unplugged, the channel will always be retained and will not even disappear due to equipment damage?”

Seeing this scene, Chen Chen could not help sighing silently.

This rule had not always been the same. Chen Chen once tested it. When he opened the channel of the USB drive by himself before, if he moved the computer, the channel on the computer screen would also move.

Nonetheless, now, even if the equipment was damaged, the formed channel had not disappeared due to the destruction of the equipment. What this indicated only raised more hypotheses for Chen Chen.

Still, now was not the time to think of this. Chen Chen put away the USB drive and thought for a while. Then, he took off again and flew shakily in a certain direction.

“According to the layout of the towns in my memory, this direction should be where the city of Shangdu lies...”

Chen Chen murmured in a soft voice. He adjusted his bearings and flew in that direction unsteadily.

The reason he was wobbling was that after lift-off, he had no surrounding reference objects, so it was difficult to pin down his own position.

After all, the distance that he could survey was no more than a radius of about 20 meters.

However, as Chen Chen began to adapt to the condition of having lost his vision, he became more stable and faster while flying.

He simply rose directly into the clouds. At this distance, he could avoid being spotted by the army on the ground or by the satellites above him. The water vapor in the clouds was denser, so there was no mistaking it.

Relying on the sense of direction in his mind and the memory from before, Chen Chen flew at subsonic speed for about half an hour. After arriving here, Chen Chen gradually stopped in the clouds.

He held his breath, suppressed his heartbeat to the lowest volume, and tried to listen to the sounds below.

Below the clouds, there were the faint noises of honking cars and the babble of a crowd.

“It seems that I’m back.”

Chen Chen murmured in a low voice then he dived and dropped directly toward the ground...

The wind whistled past his ears. Chen Chen calculated his current height while counting the seconds. When he felt it was almost time, he suddenly started to slow down.

chapters.title: Chapter 379: Aixen And The Beggar
chapters.body:
Chapter 379: Aixen And The Beggar

Chapter 379: Aixen and the Beggar

Standing on the top of this building, Chen Chen silently listened to the sounds of the entire city. The blood from his eyes had formed scabs, trailing down his cheek. It gave him a mystifying, tragic air.

Although Chen Chen had forgotten his purpose in digging out his eyes before returning to this dimension, due to the existence of the antimeme, thinking about it, there must be a very important clue in this.

Plus, perhaps because of this loss of vision, Chen Chen felt that his senses had become more acute.

Just by listening carefully, he could hear sounds that were difficult to discern in the past such as the sound of the wind blowing over obstacles, the deathly silence of the tall building under his feet, and even his own breathing and heartbeat...

It was not just his hearing. Chen Chen could smell a never-ending rotting stench in the air. Even though he was on the coast, he could not avoid this smell as it came from the buildings themselves.

The walls of these buildings had long been filled with the bodies of missing persons and these walls were the source of the decaying smell...

Through the previous information, Chen Chen had learned that now the Ju-on meme was not limited to the room, but would even infect the surrounding area. Due to this, the Ju-on meme would completely spiral out of control and caused the downfall of the entire Shangdu.

If they did not find a way to stop it, Shangdu would be just the beginning. After this, the entire world, with Shangdu as the origin, would decay little by little, until the entire world had lost its last piece of pure land.

Humans would be helpless against this meme.

“If my guess is correct, the number of deaths should be a very important factor.”

Chen Chen murmured in a low voice. According to the data, more than ten thousand students were quarantined in that original large hospital. According to official estimates, less than one-tenth of the students were infected with the Ju-on meme.

In other words, among these students, about a thousand people would eventually be killed by the meme. At that time, after the incubation period, the remaining people could be sure that they were not infected, so that any suspected infection could be ruled out.

Although it was a little cruel, to the powerless authorities, it was the easiest way to stop further losses.

This set of prescriptions did have a certain effect at the beginning. As more time passed, the number of missing persons would be very high at first, but after reaching a peak, it quickly decreased.

That was because almost everyone infected with the meme would have died out.

If one plotted this data into a graph in one’s mind, it would be clear at a glance — it was an arched curve. The numbers were not high at first, but as time passed, the curve started to ascend rapidly until it reached a peak, and then began to decrease rapidly...

As long as this number finally dropped to zero, it was equivalent to controlling the spread of the meme, curbing something that could have terrorized the entire planet at a price that was neither too low nor too high.

It was a pitiful reality which had proven that China and the FBI had underestimated the meme.

According to the subsequent data, the graph did not completely drop to zero as they expected, but after falling to a low point, it rose again abruptly, and its peak began to gain new highs continuously, each one being two times, three times or even ten times higher than before!

The reason for this was an answer that Chen Chen had already gotten from the data — in a large building, when the people who had died of the grudge-curse reached a certain number, these grudge-curses would link together and become one, widening the scope from a single room to the entire large building!

At this point, as long as one entered this building, one would be infected by the Ju-on meme.

Then, when more and more people were killed by Ju-on in this building and when it reached a critical point again, the Ju-on meme would explode a second time. The infection area would spread out of the building, enlarging the area that had become cursed.

This then descended fully into the endless loop of continuous proliferation.

This was the deadliest rule of the Ju-on meme.

It was because the Chinese officials had gathered all the infected people together that resulted in a self-defeating outcome, allowing the meme to spread in a more rampant manner.

“In other words, there are at least several areas of this indiscriminate infection in Shangdu at this time. As long as you enter this area, you’ll be infected by the Ju-on meme, and more people will die in these areas. The area would then grow wider...”

Chen Chen murmured, “In this case, this kind of area can be called a ‘cursed dead zone’. Anyone who enters this zone will die.”

He silently analyzed everything in his mind. All this was probably another rule of the Ju-on meme. Compared with the first one — that there could be no third-person observer — this law was undoubtedly more deadly. Its threat was at the level of the entire human civilization.

Now it seemed that the international metropolis of Shangdu had been abandoned.

Furthermore, if they did not find ways to reduce the number of people dying in the cursed dead zone, there was a greater hidden danger — when the number of deaths in the cursed dead zone reached the millions, it was likely that all of Shangdu would become a massive cursed dead zone. This might even expand further, infecting nearby cities.

The only way to suppress the expansion of the dead zone was probably if the authorities cut off the metaphorical diseased limb and evacuated people thousands of kilometers away within a few days. Then, an uninhabited region would be created to prevent the cursed dead zone from spreading.

However, did the authorities have such daring and capability?

Chen Chen secretly sighed. About 30% of the population of the entire continent lived near this region around Shangdu and it was one of the most densely populated areas in the world. If all were evacuated, the manpower and material resources required would be an astronomical figure.

Moreover, even if the authorities successfully suppressed it at a high cost, this area spanning several thousand kilometers would be completely lost to them, like an ugly indelible scar on the surface of the earth. Even after centuries or millennia, it would remain here.

Moreover, in these areas, the Earth Federation would need to spend tens of billions of dollars each year for troops to be stationed on the borders, so that no one was allowed to enter. After all, these cursed dead zones were stagnant only because of their surrounding lack of resources, but even after a million years, the so-called curse dead zone would still be infectious.

As a meme was a concept, it would not disappear.

This place would be like a big time-bomb of human civilization. It might be detonated again at any time and no one could guarantee that there would be a chance of prevention the next time it was detonated.

These thoughts flowed through Chen Chen’s mind but Chen Chen did not need to worry about them. After all, he had already used the Basilisk pattern for immunity in the Ju-on dimension. It was as if he had been vaccinated. Antibodies had been produced in his body, forever immunizing him to the infection of the Ju-on meme.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen jumped up and leaped straight down from the top of the tall building.

Following this was a magical scene. Chen Chen displayed a uniform declining speed, jumping directly from the 100-meter high building, and then gently landing on the ground.

Still, at this time, the streets were already extremely barren and there was not even a single person, so this shocking scene was not witnessed by any audience.

Chen Chen tilted his head. Based on his inference, he was on a familiar street at this time. This road was called Renmin Road, which was a kind of commercial street.

Even so, the shops on both sides of the commercial street were either completely closed down or forcibly opened and the products inside were cleaned out.

The people in this city were not extinct and there was still a large number of people trapped in the city. They hid in some shops and homes, hoarding enough food, thinking that they could escape the eerie white-clothed woman.

However, all this was just wishful thinking. Memes did not play hide-and-seek games with humans.

“I’m just wondering if I was looking for something by returning to Shangdu?”

Chen Chen was rather confused and a little depressed. After all, after returning from the opposite side of the passage, he found that he had plucked out his eyes and made himself blind for the first time.

Not only that, but his subconscious mind was still urging him to return to Shangdu. Although Chen Chen was immune to the Ju-on meme, the One Missed Call meme and the Ring meme were still spreading in this city, and the more frightening Final Destination meme too.

Only Chen Chen still had not heard of anyone who had experienced the Final Destination meme.

Still, that was to be expected because anyone who had experienced the Final Destination meme would be dead.

Next, Chen Chen simply wandered aimlessly on the streets of this city. He believed that he had planned something when he was on the other side of the passage. At this time, he only had to follow along with his own plan.

...

Following this, Chen Chen constantly used Field to scan both sides of the street.

With the advantage of having no blind spots in Field, Chen Chen quickly found several living people in the gaps on both sides of the road.

These living people were very vigilant. They hid in the general merchandise store. There was enough food for them to eat for several months without worrying about starving to death.

Moreover, because Chen Chen did not soften his footsteps when he walked, many people who had heard his movements even stared at him from the crack of the door.

It was just that, when they saw the blood on Chen Chen’s cheeks, those who had wanted to question Chen Chen fell silent. In their opinion, Chen Chen’s bizarre appearance was not much different from that of Kayako.

Still, when Chen Chen turned at the end of a road, his footsteps suddenly stopped.

In his perception, in a corner of the street, a familiar figure had flashed by.

Therefore, Chen Chen stopped. He turned around, with his eyes still closed, walking in the direction where the figure had appeared.

Then, Chen Chen “sensed” a familiar figure.

It was that beggar.

This beggar looked very young, only about fourteen or fifteen years old. Her skinny, rickety body was curled up in a dark corner and her skin had festered, emitting a weird stench.

Her hair had congealed into flakes like wool felt. Chen Chen could not even make out her appearance. The only thing that was certain was that the beggar was a girl.

After sensing this scene, Chen Chen slowly stopped his steps. He closed his eyes and turned his face to her. At the same time, he frowned slightly, revealing a solemn expression.

A beggar who could survive a memetic disaster, could she even be an ordinary beggar?

Chen Chen would not buy that.

Even so, at this moment, the beggar finally saw Chen Chen, and suddenly, she sat up slowly. At the same time, she stretched out her thin and dark arms, crawling toward Chen Chen in a strange posture...

Unlike vision, his Field could not make out many details. Chen Chen only sensed that the other party was staring at him with a pair of wide godless eyes.

Had she reached him?

Chen Chen only felt his body stiffening. He took a step back subconsciously, but at this moment, he suddenly heard a clear exclamation behind him, “Chen Chen, run!”

Then came the sound of sharp and rapid footsteps.

That voice was Aixen’s!

As he realized this, the owner of the voice had also entered Chen Chen’s range of Field. At this point, Chen Chen relaxed fully.

As detected by his Field, there was no mistake that it was Aixen indeed.

“Chen Chen, hurry up! Run!”

Aixen grabbed Chen Chen’s right hand and dragged him back and at the same time panicked aloud. “Don’t touch that person, no matter what!”

Chen Chen only felt that he was caught by a soft slender hand and then he was pulled by the other girl in the opposite direction...

“Why are you here?”

Chen Chen was still a little on his guard and asked as he ran. Aixen had appeared after he was facing the beggar, which seemed too coincidental.

“I, I don’t know either...”

However, Aixen was stunned as well. She shook her head and said in a puzzled manner, “I don’t know why I am here, I only know that you were in danger!”

Chen Chen’s heart leaped when he heard this. If Aixen was the incarnation of Little X in reality, then it was indeed a reasonable thing for her to appear here. After all, she was the guide of his own hypnosis.

However, was it that simple?

Chen Chen only felt that the doubts in his heart had grown heavier. The person in front of him was indeed Aixen, but he did not know why, he gradually began to feel somewhat uncomfortable.

It was as if he had forgotten something very important.

At this moment, the sweat from his running suddenly dripped from Chen Chen’s forehead and when it trickled over his eyes, a piercing ache suddenly struck him.

“Agh!”

Chen Chen could not help covering his eyes and knelt on the ground and cried out in pain.

“Chen Chen, run! Don’t stop!”

Aixen once again grabbed Chen Chen by the collar, trying to pull Chen Chen forward. Meanwhile, she sounded like she was near tears, her voice becoming more and more urgent.

Even so, in the dwindling twinge of agony, Chen Chen was hit by a realization, like Buddha achieving enlightenment!

He finally figured out what was wrong — Aixen did not ask about his eyes!

Thinking of this, Chen Chen abruptly raised his head. He directly faced Aixen’s direction while constantly trying to open his eyes!

Nonetheless, in reality, what he opened was a pair of black, empty sockets, which contained nothing but a bloody pulp.

chapters.title: Chapter 380: The End
chapters.body:
Chapter 380: The End

Chapter 380: The End

Due to Aixen’s tugging and the recurring severe pain from the wound triggered by sweat, Chen Chen’s eyelids that had been stuck together due to blood coagulation suddenly loosened and a large volume of blood flowed from Chen Chen’s eye sockets again.

Therefore, Chen Chen suddenly discovered that while his eyes were hurting, he seemed to see something between the gaps of his eyelids...

This was not an illusion because even if Chen Chen had lost his eyesight, he could still use the “Field” energy to explore the world around him. Therefore, when light shone upon his blinded eyes and merged with the perception of Field, Chen Chen was immediately sure that he did see some kind of super mysterious existence.

However, what was it really?

Although the dark hollow eye sockets could no longer be called eyes, at this moment, Chen Chen still resisted the anguish and opened his eyes resolutely!

At once, an indescribable image appeared in Chen Chen’s mind. At this moment, Chen Chen saw it with his eyes...

There was endless darkness in front of him. It was not even darkness, it should be called nothingness.

However, in the boundless nothingness, a being that seemed to be composed of liquid and smoke appeared where Aixen was standing!

Why did he call it a being? Although it did not have a fixed shape, it was blood-red, and the only concrete thing on its body was a crammed pile of eyeballs!

These eyeballs covered Aixen’s entire body. What was even more frightening was that these eyeballs were still constantly shifting. Even as Chen Chen stared at the pile of eyeballs, they seemed to notice him and started to glare back at Chen Chen!

“So that’s the case?”

Seeing this scene, Chen Chen seemed to be enlightened and countless clues were finally connected. He suddenly raised his hand and pointed with one finger!

“Bang!”

Aixen’s thin and slender body flew back and hit the wall of the shop across the street!

Chen Chen stood up slowly, opened his pair of eyes that seemed to come from hell, and looked at Aixen. In his line of sight, the world was a canvas of nothingness and the monster in front of him was the only solid existence.

“Chen Chen, what are you doing? I’m Aixen! Wake up, I’m X!”

Even though she was knocked back, Aixen in front of him still tried her best to justify herself in a tearful voice.

“Drop the act. There’s no point now. What should I call you?”

Drop by drop, the vapor around the monster that had collapsed on the ground slowly trickled away. The parasitic eyeballs on the vapor began to spin around frantically. Even in Chen Chen’s mind, there were innumerable screeches, which were the agonized cries of the eyeballs.

“Should I call you the previous owner of the USB flash drive? Or an antimeme? Or...”

Chen Chen walked to his opponent and knelt gently. “Event Horizon?”

“Impossible, you cheated! How could you find me!”

At this point, Aixen finally displayed a vicious cruel look. It no longer had the same bright voice as before. At this time, its voice was more like the sound of metal rasping against glass and it was also like a wailing from the abyss, full of silence and gloom!

“If your brain is sh*t, everything you do is sh*t. Why don’t you go and ask the devil himself!”

Chen Chen pointed to his head, then placed his palms directly on the face of the monster in front of him, and smiled wickedly. “Remember, in your next reincarnation, no matter what you do, never go against Chen Chen, understand?”

With that said, Chen Chen’s Field energy was immediately activated and it began to close in on the body of the monster before him, like a shredder!

In his line of sight, the gas and liquid that made up the monster were constantly dripping away and evaporating under the attack of Chen Chen’s Field energy. The countless eyeballs on the monster’s body were even more miserable as they were directly exploded by the Field energy!

“No!”

The monster just kept struggling and screaming, but in any case, it was unable to break free from Chen Chen’s grip. It could only watch as the eyeballs in its body continued to shatter and the vapors continued to dissipate and dispersed.

“No, you can’t kill me because I’m a concept and a concept cannot die! Absolutely not!”

In the end, the monster yelled in despair.

“No, a concept is a concept. You are yourself.”

Chen Chen said coldly, “You’re just a poor bug that has attached its consciousness to a meme. You must’ve led a very hard life. In that case, let me do you a favor!”

With that, the last few eyeballs finally broke apart. At the moment when the last few eyeballs shattered, the remaining gas finally evaporated and dissipated fully, vanishing at once into the vast void!

“Arghh—!”

There was only the final lingering wail of that monster...

In the end, everything was wiped out.

In the same moment that Aixen in front of him was annihilated, Chen Chen only felt his mind clear up and all the memories rushed into his mind again!

This time, Chen Chen was no longer in danger of forgetting.

It was not only Chen Chen. At this instant, a middle-aged man had come to the isolated edge of Shangdu, looking at the body of Shen Qi, who no longer looked human. He wore an anguished expression but at this moment, his gaze suddenly sharpened as if something had appeared in his mind...

The relatives of those who were swallowed by the antimeme invariably stopped their movements at this point and their memories began to flicker back in their minds...

At this moment, the world affected by the antimeme had returned to normal again.

“Phew!”

Currently, Chen Chen exhaled silently, finally looking relieved.

At the same time, Chen Chen suddenly felt warmth in his hand. Chen Chen looked down and saw that the dirty little beggar had crawled to his side somehow. She stood up weakly and took Chen Chen’s hand as if it was the most natural thing in the world.

“Thank you, X.”

Chen Chen nodded, carried the little beggar in front of him, and flew away in the direction from which he came.

He wanted to fly out of this continent and meet the group of officials one last time to solve the final issue.

While flying, Chen Chen gradually sorted out his thoughts. The source of all this was the night he was injected with the T-virus and he had a terrible nightmare.

On the second day after the dream, when Chen Chen woke up, he had forgotten everything as if nothing had happened. He continued to live his life according to its original trajectory.

As he had been taking NZT-48 all year round, his intelligence was constantly improving, but this kind of improvement took a long time. Inevitably it would easily lead to brain failure and even the sequelae of brain damage, but Chen Chen had extracted to Medpod 3000 to repair himself and even used the T-virus to strengthen his physique.

Therefore, even if he was not taking the drug now, his brain had already begun to show some special changes such as having an eidetic memory, an efficiency of mental arithmetic comparable to that of a computer, and even an intelligence exceeding that of ordinary people’s.

Therefore, although he had forgotten something, his subconscious mind detected this danger, so it pointed out key clues such as “antimeme” and “dreams.”

This led to Little X hypnotizing Chen Chen and his coming here.

However, what Chen Chen did not expect was that, though it was only hypnosis to awaken the memories of the past, his consciousness was drawn into a parallel universe, even resulting in a certain mingling between his dream and this world.

In other words, this was indeed a new world and the timeline of this world happened to be the moment Chen Chen just obtained the USB drive four years ago in the original dimension where Chen Chen was located.

Nonetheless, this was simultaneously also Chen Chen’s dreamscape.

How should one explain this phenomenon? Wave-particle duality of light? Or quantum mechanics?

Chen Chen was still clueless for the time being but the cause of this might be due to the USB flash drive or more likely, it should be all thanks to Aixen!

Namely, the monster killed by Chen Chen earlier.

This monster was the previous owner of the USB flash drive.

The reason Chen Chen was sure that the USB drive had a previous owner was that he gradually became aware of the abnormal phenomena in the Earth Federation such as the quantum computer and even the formation of the Earth Federation itself.

Nevertheless, Chen Chen could only locate the problem, but could not find the cause of the problem or any clues. It was as if, at the end of all the anomalies, there was an invisible black hole of information, which swallowed all information about anomalies and devoured everything, leaving not a single trace.

It was because of this black hole of information that Chen Chen perceived the existence of a concept such as the antimeme. Then, he was reminded of the anomaly of the Earth Federation and inferred that the USB drive was likely to have a previous owner.

In the beginning, Chen Chen thought that the previous owner was swallowed by the antimeme, but after all, an antimeme was just a concept, like a meme. Since it was a concept, it was impossible for it to have its own thoughts like a human.

Nonetheless, everything that Chen Chen had encountered in this dream world showed severe indications of human interference.

Whether it was by hiding behind the Ju-on meme and silently devouring this world or the behavior of Shen Qi, Zhou Jie, and the others after being possessed, all of this demonstrated that the antimeme was capable of thought.

However, how could an antimeme have human thinking?

Therefore, after entering Ju-on’s dimension, Chen Chen had a hypothesis in his mind. “Perhaps the previous owner used the technology of a certain movie to turn himself into an antimeme? Or attached himself to the antimeme? So that all information about him would disappear?”

Among the countless impossibilities, this was the most likely possibility to explain everything.

Therefore, he continued inferring — the Earth Federation was established in 1992 and the first four movies in the USB flash drive were mostly around 2000. That was to say, it was very likely that, from the previous owner getting the USB flash drive to losing it again, it all took place approximately around this timeframe, with a possible discrepancy of fewer than ten years.

In that case, before 2010, which movie could transform people into an antimeme?

Only one answer appeared in Chen Chen’s mind — Event Horizon!

This movie was released in 1997. It was a sci-fi horror movie. It told the story of the future year 2040 when humans had developed a jump engine and built a starship, “Event Horizon”, that could jump through spacetime.

To test the ability of the jump engine, humans allowed the Event Horizon to start traveling, but two months after the Event Horizon set sail, it disappeared completely, along with hundreds of astronauts, after opening a portal and making a jump near Neptune.

It was only until 2047 that the starship, which had been missing for 7 years, appeared again, and at the same time it even sent a distress signal to Earth.

The last sound of the distress signal was a very obscure sentence —”Save me!”

As the earth received the distress signal from Event Horizon, the designer and rescuers of the spacecraft rushed to see what happened to it in the past seven years, but they were met by a terrifying journey...

This was the background of Event Horizon.

The “jump” concept in the movie was an ability that allowed the spacecraft to jump from one point in the universe to another in an instant. The principle was that the spacecraft would open a portal during the jump and the other side of the portal was another place in the universe.

A metaphor was also used in the movie. The designer of the spacecraft drew two points on a piece of paper and asked the crew members on the ship what was the shortest distance between the two points. The hired crew naturally said that the straight line was the shortest, but he said that answer was wrong. Then he folded the paper directly, putting the two points together, and pierced the paper with a pen, after which he explained that the shortest distance was achieved by placing the two points together. The jump engine worked by joining the two points together.

However, who would have expected that after the spacecraft made a jump, although it successfully disappeared from this point, it did not appear at another point? It completely disappeared in this vast universe and went to an unknown place outside the universe.

In the film, all the supernatural phenomena on the spacecraft seemed to Chen Chen to be caused by the spacecraft being infected with some kind of meme. In addition, there was a line that made an impression on Chen Chen: “This ship has been beyond the boundaries of our universe, of known scientific reality. Who knows where it’s been, what it’s seen or what it’s brought back with it...”

The biggest clue in the plot was probably the part where a character gouged out his own eyes because in the movie’s design, Event Horizon was heading to a terrifying world, which was like hell. In that world, there was no need for an organ such as the eyes. Only by gouging out one’s eyes could one see the truth of that world.

Therefore, under the lure of that world, the designer of the spaceship dug out his eyes and actively embraced darkness. Doing so, he turned into a spokesperson for that world, instantly possessing terrifying power.

Therefore, Chen Chen surmised that if the owner of the USB drive wanted to assimilate himself into a conceptual existence such as a meme or antimeme, it could only be done with Event Horizon.

As such, in the instant before he returned from Ju-on’s dimension, Chen Chen had gouged out his eyes first because after gouging out his eyes, he would be able to see the previous master of the USB flash drive.

In fact, at that time, Chen Chen had already begun to doubt Aixen because the objective of the USB drive’s previous owner was to swallow Chen Chen and replace him. To replace Chen Chen, it would not be enough to just kill Chen Chen in the dream. Rather, he had to devour Chen Chen’s dream world before Chen Chen woke up. Therefore, he would spare no effort to protect Chen Chen and prevent Chen Chen from being prematurely killed by Ju-on.

It was Aixen who had once saved Chen Chen before Chen Chen was killed by Ju-on.

The rest happened as a matter of course.

As for the little beggar, she was the back door that Little X had implanted to ensure Chen Chen’s safety. When he was hypnotized, Little X had kissed Chen Chen, and this kiss was an “anchor”. As long as the little beggar came into contact with him in his dream and kissed him, Chen Chen could easily wake up, causing all of Aixen’s efforts to go up in a puff of smoke.

Therefore, strictly speaking, Aixen was not capable of killing Chen Chen. On the contrary, he had not completely integrated with the antimeme. His actual body was the blood-red eyes and the antimeme was that substance that looked like both liquid and gas. As long as the eyes were crushed, it was akin to killing him.

After his death, the antimeme naturally lost its power against Chen Chen and became dormant again.

As a result, Chen Chen’s memories had fully returned.

As for why the previous owner of the USB flash drive turned himself into a meme, Chen Chen did not know.

Perhaps this would forever be a mystery.

Nevertheless, it now appeared that all this was probably planned by the previous owner of the USB flash drive because even the four horror movies in the USB flash drive were placed there by him, with the objective of devouring the next owner of the USB drive.

It was a pity that he had encountered Chen Chen.

chapters.title: Chapter 381: Nanomite Bombs And Returning
chapters.body:
Chapter 381: Nanomite Bombs And Returning

Chapter 381: Nanomite Bombs and Returning

At last, everything was over...

Chen Chen carried the little beggar and kept rising, and finally broke through the dense layer of clouds. Suddenly, a glimmering golden ball of light emerged in front of them!

The golden brilliance poured upon the clouds, dyeing the boundless clouds a deep golden yellow, like a vast magnificent postcard picture. The little beggar’s eyes grew wider, showing an expression of wonder.

“It’s beautiful, isn’t it?”

Chen Chen suddenly spoke. He lowered his head and said softly, “This scene must be beautiful, but it’s too bad I can’t see it anymore.”

The little beggar tilted her head in puzzlement. She suddenly stretched out her hand and carefully touched Chen Chen’s eyes.

Chen Chen smiled.

Strictly speaking, this little beggar was not Little X but a person in Chen Chen’s self-consciousness. She did not have Little X’s thoughts and even ordinary people could not see her.

There was only one purpose for her existence — that was to get Chen Chen out of the dream.

Therefore, the little beggar was fake. Strictly speaking, the world in front of Chen Chen was also fake.

“But then what is true?”

Chen Chen suddenly muttered in a low voice. Must the world seen by one’s own eyes necessarily be real?

Science taught humans that everything humans saw with their eyes was nothing more than the world portrayed by the eyes and brain.

The sky as an example, sunlight was divided into seven types of visible light — red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet. Moreover, as the wavelengths of blue and violet lights were relatively short, when they encountered small objects such as dust in the sky, blue and purple lights would be “scattered” everywhere due to being blocked.

Therefore, the color of the sky was blue.

Other objects in the world were the same. Some objects would more easily reflect the red in sunlight, so humans would see them as being red. Others would appear blue or green...

All this was what the eyes allowed humans to “see”, but there were also infrared and ultraviolet rays in the sunlight, which were invisible to humans. If a person could see these two kinds of light or even electromagnetic waves and radiation, then his world must be very different from the world that humankind saw.

Moreover, everything that a person saw had to be processed by not just the eyes, but also by the brain for a second time. As an example, objects that were projected on the human eyeballs were originally upside down but after the processing of the brain, these images gained an upright appearance.

Another example was human eyes. Human eyes could not receive red light and the red that humans saw was the color obtained by the brain’s mixing of green and yellow. Even the peripheral field of vision received by the eye was in blurry black and white and the details were later filled in by the brain...

All of these undoubtedly demonstrated that everything that people saw was limited by their physiological structure or that everything that humans perceived, including sight, hearing, touch, and taste, was perceived by the brain. Therefore, what evidence was there to prove that everything the brain perceived must be real?

Just like the concept of “the brain in a vat” that had always existed in science fiction novels — the brain of a person, who had lost his body due to an accident, was first extracted. Then, advanced technology was used to maintain the activity of brain cells and instruments would stimulate the brain to create a fictitious electronic world. As a result, the person would think he was still alive and it would be almost impossible for him to notice any abnormalities.

He would never know that he had lost his freedom and his entire world, and he only lived under God’s observation...

At this time, was it not true that Chen Chen had fallen into a “brain in a vat”? It was just that this “brain in a vat” was an entire parallel universe.

After leaving Shangdu, Chen Chen flew to the town where he had stayed before, found a new computer, and inserted the USB drive.

Shangdu had long since lost all power and connectivity and only these surrounding towns still had a network supply, but they too, were on the verge of collapse.

At this time, after turning on the computer, Chen Chen hesitated a little and finally opened a web page and started downloading a movie.

This movie was called G.I. Joe: The Rise of Cobra.

When the download was completed, Chen Chen dragged the movie into the USB drive, then opened the file from that location and skipped to a particular scene.

In this scene, an open suitcase was shown, which held three missiles with warheads made of some green transparent material. They also seemed to contain some sort of liquid.

“Nanomite missiles...”

Chen Chen murmured. These three warheads could be said to be the core technology of this movie and the whole plot took off because of this sort of missile.

To put it simply, this nanomite missile contained seven million nanomites. Once launched, it could destroy and engulf all materials on the surface of the earth whether it was humans, plants or reinforced concrete. Everything would become its destruction target and it would never stop.

The only way to stop them was to use the kill switch device matched to these nanorobots. As long as the kill switch was activated, the nanorobots would completely short-circuit and self-destruct.

These kill switch devices were also housed in this suitcase. They consisted of just an LCD screen with the word MARS on it.

After thinking for a while, Chen Chen finally reached into the screen and tugged.

At once, an entire suitcase was taken out of the screen by Chen Chen.

At this point, Chen Chen heaved a sigh of relief. He locked the suitcase, then carried it in one hand and the little beggar in the other, and lifted off again, flying into the distance...

...

“Yes, yes, yes...

“Okay, I understand...

“I understand... I’ll resign and give the higher-ups an explanation.”

In front of Shen Qi’s corpse, the middle-aged man seemed to have aged ten years. He was talking on the phone with a dull expression and replied in a low voice.

However, at this moment, there was a sudden boom in the sky as if someone had set off a firecracker. Only the sound was dozens of times louder than a firecracker.

The middle-aged man was shocked. He knew this sound. It was the sound of something breaking through the sound barrier. Whenever a supersonic plane flew overhead, people would hear this sound. Sometimes it was so loud it could even shatter glass.

Nonetheless, how could a plane possibly pass by here now?

The middle-aged man turned his head subconsciously, but he was astonished to find that the source of the sound was not an airplane at all but a person!

At this time, the person was standing at a high altitude and slowly descending. In one hand he was holding a silver-white suitcase.

“It’s Chen Chen!”

There was a surprised exclamation from the soldiers beside him, following which came the sound of gun bolts being pulled. All the soldiers instinctively prepared for combat.

“Wait, don’t shoot!”

The middle-aged man hurriedly stopped them. Although he knew Chen Chen was the murderer of Shen Qi, it did not matter whom Chen Chen had killed at this time. The key was that he was the owner of the USB flash drive. Without the owner, the USB drive could not be used. This had been verified before.

“Chen Chen, what’s wrong with your eyes?”

However, when he saw Chen Chen’s eyes clearly, he was shocked. He noticed that Chen Chen’s cheeks were covered with blood scabs and both eyes were tightly shut. Chen Chen’s eyelids were also red, swollen, and even a little caved in.

“Mr. Director, what happened to my eyes is a rather long story.”

Chen Chen completely ignored the ruthless stares of the soldiers and walked straight toward the middle-aged man. “But I’ve finished my business here, so I’m going to leave.”

“You’re leaving?”

Hearing Chen Chen’s words, the middle-aged man suddenly became anxious and moved forward in agitation. “Chen Chen, you can’t leave. What will happen to this city if you leave?”

“This city is a goner.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “I’m not your savior and there are no saviors in this world. Now what you have to consider isn’t the question of whether Shangdu can be preserved, but whether human civilization will be destroyed due to the memetic effect.”

“What do you mean?”

The middle-aged man was promptly taken aback. He looked down at the suitcase in Chen Chen’s hand and seemed to understand something.

“Give it up. You can’t cure the Ju-on meme by shooting your own movie.”

Chen Chen tossed the suitcase to a soldier beside the middle-aged man. Once the soldier caught it, he took two steps back and then looked at the middle-aged man helplessly.

“Open it!”

The middle-aged man nodded and ordered.

Upon hearing this, the soldier clenched his jaw and opened the suitcase directly.

At once, everyone saw the three green warhead-like devices in the suitcase, and three palm-sized devices similar to remote controls.

“This is... A nanomite warhead?”

The middle-aged man said, astounded, “Did you extract this?”

“I did.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Since you know what it is, it means you have considered using it before, so my advice to you is — just cut the Gordian knot. Before the memetic effect kills enough people and spreads fully, destroy everything with the nanomites first! This is the only way to stop the spread of memetic effects from the source.”

The middle-aged man fell silent after hearing this.

“This is all I have to say. What you should do is up to you.”

Chen Chen said that and turned to leave.

“Hold on!”

The middle-aged man looked more anxious, but he did not seem to want to use coercive means, so he quickly scampered forward and ran to Chen Chen’s side. “Little Chen, where are you going?”

“Of course I’m going back to where I should be.”

Chen Chen walked forward slowly step by step and said calmly, “I’ve been in the dream for a long time and it’s time to wake up now. Plus, I’ve also found a way to wake up.”

“Dream?” The middle-aged man was mystified.

“Mr. Director.”

Chen Chen stopped and turned to look at the other man while a smile crept up the corners of his mouth. “Have you ever thought that the world in which you’ve spent a lifetime is actually just someone’s dream?

“When that person dreams, the world is born and when he wakes up from the dream, the world disappears again. Along with the world, the countless lives in the dream disappear as well. Will they realize that their long human lives were just part of someone’s dream?”

“Impossible!”

The middle-aged man narrowed his eyes. He thoroughly understood the implication of Chen Chen’s words, but still said emphatically, “This world cannot be a dream of some other person because everything I’ve experienced and encountered is telling me that I am who I am.

“Even if I’ll die and the world will be destroyed, nothing can erase the evidence of my existence, just like Descartes’s famous philosophical saying — I think, therefore, I am. I don’t need anyone to prove whether I’m true or false because I’m my own proof.”

“Very good.”

Chen Chen applauded reflexively. “I like your attitude, so please stick to it.”

With that, Chen Chen lifted off suddenly and was about to fly into the heights.

“Wait!”

The middle-aged man was seized by panic. He raised his head and shouted, “Chen Chen, even if you want to leave, leave the USB drive behind. This kind of thing shouldn’t be left in the hands of an individual. It will threaten the entire human race!”

“Even if I give it to you, you won’t obtain it.”

Chen Chen smiled contemptuously. “Don’t believe me? Then take it!”

As he spoke, Chen Chen took out the USB drive and threw it at the middle-aged man, then rushed into the sky without looking back!

The middle-aged man reacted swiftly and reached for the USB flash drive. The moment he grabbed it, there was a flicker of ecstasy in his eyes, but then he thought of something, quickly held it to his mouth, and bit it.

The USB drive did not budge at all.

“It’s real!”

The middle-aged man looked even more gleeful. He quickly called someone to put the USB drive away. It was placed in a thick solid safe, to be transported that very night.

At this time, Chen Chen had flown into the air again. He kept carrying the little beggar as he rushed to the sky and once again came to the top of the golden clouds.

Under the endless rays of the sun, Chen Chen suddenly looked powerless.

“I say, X, if you can hear me in reality, I suggest that if there’s another time, let’s change the anchor point.”

As he spoke, he looked at the little beggar, and finally, reluctantly, kissed her on the lips.

At once, a miraculous scene took place — in the golden radiance, the two figures melted instantly as if melding into the golden sunlight. In just the blink of an eye, they had vanished...

It was as if Chen Chen had never existed in this world.

At the same moment that Chen Chen disappeared from this world, the USB flash drive that had been sealed shut in the safe, and was being shipped to the capital city, also began to melt rapidly. The surveillance feed in the safe showed that it directly turned into a pool of black liquid, which squirmed ceaselessly. Then it began to dwindle as if by evaporation and finally melted into the air.

There was no trace left of it...

...

When Chen Chen opened his eyes again, he found that he was lying on a hard cramped bed. There was a dazzling light over his face and he could not help but squint.

Chen Chen subconsciously raised his hand to block his eyes, but immediately realized that he could see again.

“Have I returned?”

Chen Chen sat up thoughtfully but there seemed to be an unusual weight on his chest. Chen Chen subconsciously glanced down, only to find that the Black Knight clone of Little X was lying on his chest, deep in quiet sleep.

Still, after Chen Chen’s movements, Little X was awakened. She yawned and then said in delighted surprise, “Sir Godfather, you’re awake?”

Upon seeing Little X’s deliberately anthropomorphic behavior, Chen Chen was a little speechless. After all, intelligent life did not need to sleep.

“I don’t need to sleep, but this body wants it.”

As if she understood Chen Chen’s facial expression, Little X suddenly became angry. “Besides, I also have a hibernation mode. Of course I can sleep, it’s just that it isn’t necessary!”

Chen Chen burst into laughter when he heard Little X’s sophistry. He sighed and everything in the dream flashed through his mind.

However, at this moment, Chen Chen suddenly felt something in his hand. He looked down, only to find that the star-speckled USB drive was resting quietly in his hand.

“What happened?”

Seeing this scene, Little X was completely dumbfounded. “I just saw the USB flash drive charging outside. How can it suddenly appear in the isolation room?”

With that, she directly pulled up the surveillance feed of the power distribution room. Indeed, the slot of the USB flash drive was empty and the USB flash drive that was inserted there had been gone for some time.

Fortunately, this instrument for charging the USB drive had been prepared for two things. A Galaxy pendant was also put into the charging system. Namely, if the USB drive was suddenly removed by someone, there was still the Galaxy pendant to absorb the majestic surge of electricity.

Nonetheless, the USB drive’s teleportation through thin air prompted a pensive reaction from Chen Chen.

chapters.title: Chapter 382: The Plan Of Four Sub-Eras
chapters.body:
Chapter 382: The Plan Of Four Sub-Eras

Chapter 382: The Plan of Four Sub-Eras

“In other words, I’ve slept for a total of 68 hours, which is less than three days?”

Looking at the calendar displayed on the phone, Chen Chen wore a look of wonder. “I spent more than a month in another world but only three days have passed in reality?”

As if something had occurred to him, Chen Chen turned his head again and asked, “For the past three days, has everything been normal with the USB flash drive?”

“Yes, except for its sudden appearance here after you woke up, there has been no change in the USB flash drive at other times.”

Little X nodded her little head and said with joy, “So, have you solved the hidden danger?”

“That’s right. It was a close call, but I finally found the other party and killed him. It turns out that the antimeme was my predecessor...”

“Do you mean... The previous holder of the USB drive?” Little X asked in bewilderment.

When Chen Chen replied, he could not help sweating a little. The antimeme itself had the ability to swallow all relevant information, so it was almost impossible to comprehend. Nonetheless, even in this case, he found a way to ruin its plans.

He was able to do this not just because of the calm thinking he had developed in reality, but also because he had been very lucky...

“But even now, I still don’t know who that person was, what his name was or what job he did.”

Thinking of this, Chen Chen could not help frowning. “X, check the Internet to see if there’s any information. The effect of the antimeme has been nullified, so the Earth Federation is very likely to have recalled ‘his’ existence. Maybe we can find out about his past on the Internet.”

“Let me try.”

Little X agreed, then she closed her eyes immediately, and began maniacally searching the entire Internet for any suspicious information.

These snippets of information were fished out one by one, after which they were quickly analyzed and calculated by Little X using extremely terrifying computing power. Then, any excess data was discarded. It was not until half an hour later that Little X had roughly scanned through certain websites.

“No, I still can’t find any clues.”

Little X shook his head. “Should I turn on the Prophet System to reconstruct our target’s past?”

“There’s no need for that.”

Chen Chen waved one hand. “Two possibilities: First, my predecessor is not yet dead and the antimeme is still working, so no information can be found online; second, the movies on the USB flash drive were all released from 1998 to 2003, that is to say, the owner of the USB flash drive might have vanished more than 20 years ago, so there’s nothing on the Internet either.

“If it’s the first one, then it’s useless to turn on the Prophet System. If it’s the second one, it won’t be necessary because the Internet may not remember his existence, but it’s impossible that the high-level members of the Earth Federation wouldn’t know. After all, the emergence of the Earth Federation was advocated by the previous owner himself. Now it has only been 20 years since then. I believe that many of the high-level members are still alive. If they suddenly remember everything, they will naturally start to investigate. Then, we’ll be able to discover the traces of their activities.”

“So we’ll just see how things play out?”

Little X pouted and sat on one side, a little bored. She started to toy with a water glass.

Chen Chen laughed. “Well, since there’s no threat now, let me go out.”

“Okay,” Little X agreed. The crane and the other equipment that had been waiting for a long time began to dismantle the outer layers and armor of the isolation room.

As this went on, Chen Chen’s heart suddenly leaped. He stood up, then sat down in front of the laptop, and directly inserted the USB flash drive he was holding.

When Chen Chen opened the USB flash drive on the computer, he noticed that the four very familiar movies on the USB flash drive were now completely gone...

Those four movies were Ju-on, Final Destination, One Missed Call, and Ring.

In addition, there were many more movies on the USB flash drive. These movies were diverse, including science-fiction, historical dramas, and even modern romances.

These were all placed here by the FBI in the other dimension when Chen Chen was having his dream.

“Sure enough, the movies in the USB flash drive have changed...”

Chen Chen could not help feeling a little stupefied. When he first got the USB drive, a question had occurred to him: if the USB drive could only accommodate movies that were in line with reality, why were the four horror movies there?

Could it be that the four horror movies were not unrealistic?

This idea had occupied Chen Chen’s mind for a long, long time, causing him constant apprehension, for fear that one day he would slip up and play the four horror films by mistake.

However, now, this worry was finally gone because these four films had been opened in another world, and then deleted by Chen Chen himself.

Still, now it seemed that Chen Chen’s concerns and caution were not groundless. If he had carelessly opened Ju-on when he first got the USB flash drive, well, he only had to look at what happened in the dream world to know the outcome.

“But then again, what was the reason the previous owner would resort to fusing himself with the antimeme? And he also placed four horror films in the USB flash drive, waiting for someone to take the bait?”

Chen Chen had a vague theory, but if he wanted to verify it, he would have to head to the movie, Event Horizon...

Event Horizon was jokingly called the prequel of Warhammer 40,000 by many netizens because the setting was somewhat similar to the backstory of the tabletop game Warhammer 40,000. In particular, the unknown space that the spaceship entered was just like the subspace in the Warhammer world. It was also meant to represent a certain kind of madness, chaos, despair, and so on.

Of course, currently, Chen Chen would not enter that movie just to die. Even if he were to go, he would have to wait until his own technology was sufficiently advanced. Now, he still had not even made it to space, let alone explore the antithesis of the human world — the subspace.

This was comparable to opening Ju-on without sufficient strength — barging rashly into Event Horizon might very well kill him too.

Thinking of this, Chen Chen understood the importance of having a steady foundation. Otherwise, he would end up like his predecessor. Even though the entire world had formed into a federation, that guy still ended up having to fuse himself with an antimeme, becoming something that was neither human nor spirit.

At this time, with a creaking sound from outside the isolation room, the door was finally opened fully.

“Sir Godfather, the armor has been disassembled. You can come out now.”

Little X interrupted Chen Chen’s pondering and notified him from behind.

“Coming.”

Chen Chen plugged out the USB drive and walked out of the isolation room.

Nonetheless, this time, Little X’s Black Knight clone was following Chen Chen closely. As Chen Chen was a rather fast walker, Little X had to hop and skip to keep up with Chen Chen’s pace. It looked a little funny from a distance.

“SpaceX has signed a contract of intent with us during the three days of your absence. If everything goes smoothly, we’ll be able to buy 10% of SpaceX’s shares in just a few weeks.”

Little X conscientiously reported as she walked. “However, if we want to invest in SpaceX, we must first report to the committee on foreign investment, CFIUS. Now CFIUS has actively contacted SpaceX to confirm this. We can only invest after obtaining their approval.”

Chen Chen could not refrain from frowning when he heard this. As all this was related to the aerospace industry, it was indeed difficult to acquire a company like SpaceX. Moreover, the West itself was a bunch of rogues, especially North America, the first continent of the Earth Federation. What happened years ago was still vivid in everyone’s mind and Chen Chen was reluctant to end up like that company.

Therefore, when he founded his company, Chen Chen had not considered North America at all.

“Then let’s offer a reward.”

Chen Chen thought for a while and commanded, “Announce a reward on the God’s Forbidden Zone forum. As long as a family is willing to help us lobby the authorities, we’ll pay them 10,000 information points and the family that’s successful in their lobbying will get around 50,000 to 100,000 information points.”

“Very well. But recently Rodriguez, Edwards, Munster, your servants Blanc, and several other consortia have provided some updates.”

Little X continued. “Dozens of North American council members simultaneously called for investigations into our company at the district council meeting. Because we hold a large part of Osmond Biotech’s shares, they suspect that we’re stealing the technology for Alzheimer’s drugs...”

Chen Chen’s steps halted and a sullen look came into his eyes.

“This is why I hate opening a company in the West.”

Chen Chen snorted coldly. “Are our members from the plutocratic families useless? They’d better do their best to suppress this proposal. I don’t want to see this proposal implemented.”

“Understood.” Little X nodded.

It must be said that, with the growing influence of Chen Chen’s company, it had fully attracted the attention of Western regimes. If Chen Chen had not roped in a large number of European and American consortia with the “mankind anti-aging project”, the ax would have fallen long ago in the form of North American sanctions. This was what happened to the company which developed 5G back then.

At this time, it was clear that the ax of sanctions was still hanging over the head of Blacklight Biotechnology. It was up to the financial groups in God’s Forbidden Zone to lobby the North American officials and continue to pressure the district council to prevent the sanctions from being issued.

However, in this matter, it was obvious that these consortia could not hold on for long.

“I used to think that we could reach the point where we would be unstoppable before Western regimes such as North America and Europe could react, but now it seems that they still can’t take this sitting down.”

Chen Chen sighed in a low voice. “Since the other party has already noticed me, then we should do our best to expand. There’s no need to keep a low profile for the sake of hiding ourselves. Just let them see that we’re not something they can keep under their thumb!”

“Are we going to go all out?”

Little X was taken aback. “We have a lot of technology now in our hands. What technology should we release first?”

Chen Chen thought for a while. “Naturally, AD-001 won’t do. I still plan to wait until May next year to see if the plot in the movie will come true by then. Moreover, we have already sold various anti-cancer targeted drugs, and there’s no recent biomedicine that is truly essential. In that case, we don’t have to stick to the biomedicine industry.”

“Then, let’s try VR.”

After thinking about it, Chen Chen sneered. “Didn’t we use Musk’s lace electrode technology? In this case, we can go with the flow and claim that we’ve developed a new electrode technology. Using what we got from the Elysium’s dimension as a sample, the new electrode technology will be released and made into a VR device. The VR device’s technology can be obtained from Ready Player One.”

Chen Chen said as he walked, “In this way, we can quickly earn a lot of money and not be restricted by finances.”

“Are you going to change the online landscape?”

Little X was shocked when she heard this. Of course, she had watched the movie Ready Player One. According to the movie, the world was devastated due to a world war. Due to a scarcity of resources and a low quality of life, people’s attention shifted to virtual worlds, hoping to find some comfort there. As such, the game Oasis came into being and quickly unified the entire Internet.

From 80-year-olds to 18-year-olds, everyone regarded Oasis as an indispensable part of their lives. Simply put, Oasis was the Internet and the Internet was Oasis.

However, according to current trends, the online world was changing with each passing day. It was probable that once a game like Oasis appeared, it would quickly sweep the entire Internet and completely reshuffle the online landscape without any major changes in reality.

“Even if it changes, what can they do?”

Chen Chen’s mouth curled up in a smirk. “I want to change not only the Internet’s landscape, but also the structure of reality itself. I’ve thought a lot during this period. Biomedicine, the aerospace industry, and the Internet are the three major fields of human society that can lead us to the future. Biomedicine can prolong human life and rejuvenate human beings.

“The aerospace industry can lead humankind beyond the earth and colonize outer space. This is also one of the areas that human history can never give up on.

“The Internet, corresponding to reality, represents the second world of human society. If the real world represents human needs on the material level, then the Internet represents human needs on the spiritual level. The Internet will only transform, but it will never be eliminated.”

After hearing Chen Chen’s analysis, Little X couldn’t help nodding. She was just about to say something when Chen Chen’s next words stunned her completely...

“The release of VR and the reshuffle of the Internet landscape are just the first step in my plan.”

Chen Chen added. “I’ve divided the future Internet landscape into four sub-eras — the first era: the VR era, namely, the era in which the VR field breaks through the traditional shackles and upgrades from audiovisual to audio, visual, smell, taste, and touch, the five senses!

“This era will inevitably bring about a change comparable to the industrial revolution because it represents the embryonic stage of the virtual world and may even represent the birth of a certain branch of human evolution...

“Next is the second sub-era — the era of artificial intelligence. Artificial intelligence becomes common in civilian use and robot butlers enter millions of households.

“I’ve also laid the foundation for this era, and that’s Project Xingtian! Next, I’ll let Project Xingtian continue to evolve and allow the androids to be born...

“After that, the third era — the Skynet era, when I release new powerful artificial intelligence and let it be formally used by the Earth Federation. At that point, the court will hand over all sentencing and trial work to AI, attaining true fairness and justice!
