Although it’d be unfair that this makes him a despicable person, you still have to admit that to get to such a level, a certain degree of scheming and plotting is required. You can’t say this he’s evil, but he’s not exactly a saint either.”

Chen Chen elaborated, “But once the protagonist realizes the true nature of the body he’s residing in, he’s willing to risk his life to charge into the camp to rescue a pair of mother and daughter he hardly acquainted with. He also turned on the scientists because they lied to him... That’s where it got problematic.

“In the end, the protagonist decides to stop taking his medicine, which led to the death of his consciousness and the body being returned to the original host. This is where the entire story becomes perplexing because if the protagonist is such a noble man willing to sacrifice himself, why would he turn to an illegal and ethically questionable operation in the first place?

“Even if the protagonist is doing it out of goodwill, nothing is stopping him from transferring his consciousness again to another willing body. This way, he didn’t have to die and the body can be returned to its original owner. Wouldn’t it be the best of both worlds?

“To summarize this, the protagonist spends a fortune on this to swap his consciousness into the body of a stranger he has zero knowledge about. The protagonist also had his very own family that he’s reluctant to leave as well. Despite all that, he decides to sacrifice his consciousness along with his life for a group of strangers he doesn’t know anything about...”

Chen Chen shrugged and expressed frustratingly, “I admire his noble spirit, but I can’t understand why he would choose a self-destructive path when there were other completely viable options present as well.”

“This problem you stated is valid. To add to your point, I think there’s a problem with the technology itself as well.”

Little X contemplated on the subject. “I remember you once mentioned a subject concerning the continuity of consciousness. The reason a person can be defined as a singular entity is largely attributed to the continuity of his or her consciousness. This is where there’s a problem in the technology shown in the movie.

“First of all, the consciousness transfer technology shown in the movie doesn’t involve any physical replacement. This means no brain transfer operation of any sort conducted, only direct consciousness replacement where the protagonist’s original conscience was directly swapped into a new body. In this case, is this new ‘person’ still the protagonist?”

Little X raised a question. “What are the chances of these devices only copying the protagonist’s lifelong accumulated memories to another body, wherein the new body inherits all these memories and is placed under the false impression that it’s the protagonist?”

Chen Chen applauded Little X when he heard this. “You raise a great point and I have to say, there aren’t suitable ways for us to verify this fact. Is this new person the protagonist himself or just a brand new persona that inherited all of the protagonists’ memories?

“I find myself agreeing with the point you’ve made — what are the chances that the consciousness and spirit of the operated person perish in the process of the consciousness transferring procedure? The new body only presumes that it’s the new ‘person’ due to these inherited memories when in truth, the real ‘person’ had died in the process of the experiment.

“Due to the complexity of the human brain, I find it difficult to believe that consciousness can be individually separated from the brain itself as an independent entity. The way I see it, the so-called consciousness transfer in the movie is merely a memory duplication. It might as well be finding a new body and injecting a collection of memory into it. This may further explain why the protagonist has to regularly take drugs to fortify his persona to avoid his new body from losing these memories of his and slowly morphing into another person entirely...”

“In this case, are you still planning on using this consciousness transfer technique on James Watson?”

Little X asked.

“This is why I consider Self/less as a secondary option.”

Chen Chen nodded. “To be fair, we’re only looking for an elite world-class scientist. It doesn’t matter whether the person surviving is the real James Watson, isn’t it?”

“...”

Little X became speechless at this notion. It was true, all Chen Chen was looking for was a knowledgeable biological science scholar. It was inconsequential whether this person was authentic...

When Chen Chen thought of this, he got up from his seat and stepped right past the virtual display. He went to the elevator positioned at the end of his office.

Half an hour later, Chen Chen had arrived at the Spire Experimental Base.

After extracting the USB drive from the superconducting charging device, Chen Chen walked to the next room and inserted the USB drive into the computer set up there.

Little X already had the two movies downloaded and ready to go. Chen Chen used the keyboard shortcut [ctrl+c] to duplicate the movie and pasted it inside the USB drive storage with the keyboard shortcut [ctrl+v].

“Ding!”

To his surprise, the output was an obnoxious error notification.

Chen Chen quickly investigated the error. It appeared that Robocop 2014 was successfully inserted into the USB drive storage but the other movie, Self/less was rejected by the USB drive...

“I knew it, something like consciousness transfer is an inherently flawed theory!”

Little X exclaimed.

“No, the reason this movie was rejected by the USB drive is not a sign that ‘consciousness transfer’ technology doesn’t exist.”

Chen Chen shook his head. “The problem lies in how the movie expresses the concept. The method shown in the movie shouldn’t feasibly achieve a consciousness transfer effect, duplication of consciousness is a wrong approach. This is why the USB drive didn’t accept the movie.”

Chen Chen’s tone switched as he said this, “Since the second movie didn’t work out, we’re only left with one option and that’s to acquire the brain restoration technology in this world.

“The movie never expressed much detail concerning the technology. I’m afraid we’re going to have to dive into the world of the movie to extract the relevant technology.”

Little X immediately understood what Chen Chen was suggesting.

“Got it.”

Chen Chen nodded. “Apart from the three microchips shown in the protagonist Alex’s brain, there are no verifiable implementations concerning this brain restoration technology for us to extract. We’re going to need to enter the movie dimension to extract it, just like we did with Elysium previously.”

“Understood, I’ll gather the Black Knights.”

Little X left as she said this.

Only Chen Chen was left in the room. His expression was heavy with thoughts as he looked at the Robocop 2014 file inside the USB drive.

“I wonder what sort of catastrophic disaster would have come to mankind inside this movie dimension?”

Chen Chen muttered to himself. So far, each movie’s dimension accessed from the USB drive was left in destruction. This was an indisputable fact. It started with Chronicle, then Resident Evil, Elysium, Alien, and finally the most devastating of them all, Gantz. All of these worlds were left in ruins, each more devastating than its predecessor, each one of them more perplexing than the other.

Chen Chen was about to step into his sixth movie dimension so far.

While these thoughts glided across his mind, he suddenly heard urgent rounds of footsteps quickly approaching from behind him. Chen Chen did not turn around to inspect but instead use his Field to expand his visual range and saw everything behind him.

Using Field, he saw more than ten Black Knights fully-fitted with GS Combat Suit and PK Armor, each one of them holding pulse rifles. They filed into the room and formed a straight line formation, awaiting their next orders.

These Black Knights were nurtured using T-virus infected zygotes and boasted combat prowess greatly surpassing ordinary humans. With the additional GS Combat Suit, Predator Armor from Predator 2018, and superconductor thermal pulse rifles, they were easily Chen Chen’s most powerful combat force.

They were the only ones qualified enough to enter the movie dimension and face whatever threat lied behind it.

Seeing that Little X had completed the appropriate preparations, Chen Chen activated the movie. In an instant, a familiar black hole appeared on the display...

The passage to the world in Robocop was formed.

chapters.title: Chapter 400: Extraction
chapters.body:
Chapter 400: Extraction

Chapter 400: Extraction

“Reporting to you live here at Daily News, I’m your host, Adam. Just this afternoon, the artificial intelligence developed by Omni Corp — God’s Blade officially connected to the North American law enforcement network. At the same time, the Robocop, EM-208, and ED-209 robots have also fully taken over the law enforcement agencies, but have triggered opposing demonstrations of an unprecedented scale...

“There have been multiple clashes between the protestors and Omni Corp in countless states all over the country. More than a hundred thousand people have been arrested so far. The arrests have only further escalated things and inspired larger protests and riots.

“There’s an underlying issue with politicians and capitalists of this country. Why should a law enforcement agency be ruled by a profit-motivated company? Why is a company allowed to freely formulate law enforcement rules and why are the police force reduced to mere tools of these companies? Will there ever be justice and fairness in this country so long as this corporation is allowed to freely reign above the law?

“We have to address this issue, address... What?

“Oh d*mn, there’s been a recent development. The robots are now rebelling. They’re now massacring people on the streets. Oh my god, tens of thousands of people are dying, what is Omni Corp trying to do!

“Run, everyone! Hurry up!

“...”

A week later, Chen Chen sat beside the tunnel opened by the USB drive. He silently inspected the footage and other documents brought back by the Black Knights from the other end of the tunnel.

“These are the footage we discovered that’s the closest to the time of doomsday.”

Little X notified, “What we currently know is that — after Omni Corps’s developed artificial intelligence connected to the global law enforcement network, the robot started to rebel. Some of them hypothesized that the cause is a bug stemming from the artificial intelligence thought process protocol. This makes it so that the robots see humanity as a whole as a crime and decides the right move is to destroy human civilization.”

“Sounds like the typical artificial intelligence thought process...”

Chen Chen nodded. “But I agree with this host named Adam. If a corporation is allowed to ride above the law, will there ever be anything resembling true justice in the world? He made a good point.”

“This is an ongoing debate between the Namibians as well.”

Little X reminded. “Outside of the media outlets that we have a stake in, several private media outlets are publishing these opinion pieces. They claimed that our Blacklight Biotechnology is riding above the law and dominated a majority of the country’s sectors which includes military affairs, airlines, electronics, and biopharmaceutical industries.”

“Don’t bother with these media outlets. If they do something out of line, I’ll have the Namibian government take care of them. You only need to control the narrative among the people, that’s what you’re good at.”

Chen Chen remarked, “In that case, what about the technology obtained from the Robocop 2014 dimension? Will it be able to help James Watson recover?

“We’ve acquired the technology. The brain restoration technology is very elaborate. It’s derived from a nano-level electrode technology that replaces the damaged area of the brain. Essentially, it’s an AI chip with a customized algorithm.”

A figure serving as visual-aid appeared on a holographic projection as Little X explained, “The chip is implanted into the defective area of the brain where hundreds of thousands of nano-scale electrodes connect to the brain’s nervous system. This chip then receives the brain’s nerve impulse signals to override control of the brain’s damaged region.

“However, there are three potential issues with reenacting this technology in the real world. The first is the nano-scale electrode and chip, these are extremely difficult to replicate in the real world.

“The second issue is the surgery itself. The practice of implanting a hundred thousand nano-scale electrodes into the brain greatly surpassed the average accuracy of human beings. This can only be done with specialized equipment.

“Finally, there’s the issue of AI calculation. Depending on the damaged region of the brain, the AI will have to write customized programming sequences while also avoiding affecting the user’s ability to formulate thoughts. This will be the hardest condition to achieve in usual circumstances, but it will be a non-issue for me.”

Little X explained, “Which means that realistically, there are only two problems we need to address.

“The first problem will not be difficult to address. We can just directly extract the required chip from the movie.”

Chen Chen considered. “So what we need is the medical equipment for implanting this chipset.”

“The problem is that it has been several centuries since the destruction of human civilization in the movie dimension. The post-nuclear aftermath has mostly subsided. The city ruins are nearly completely erased by overgrown plants and weather conditions over the years. The only remnant of human civilization is the handful of military bases. It took the Black Knights an entire week to find sparse clues.”

Little X became frustrated as she said this, “Forget about humans, even the rebelling robots have long since perished and rusted away.”

“That’s problematic.”

Chen Chen began to look for alternatives. “If we cannot find the appropriate technology for implanting the chip, then our only option is to design a specialized nano-scale surgical equipment ourselves. X, will the manufacturing of this equipment be feasible?”

“It’s worth a shot.”

Little X answered, “Not only that, do you remember the surgical robot developed by Neuralink? This machination is also known as a tailoring machine that has been developed to its third generation and is now capable of implanting micron electrodes. If I modify some parts and operate it myself, implantation of nano-scale electrodes will be possible.”

“In that case, get one of those as soon as possible. I’ll put you in charge of this.”

Chen Chen said confidently, “In that case, we have most of the preparations underway.”

As he said this, he closed the passage to the Robocop dimension and deleted the movie. After that, he inserted another copy of Robocop and booted it up in the first mode of the USB drive.

After that, Chen Chen switched to the frame where the protagonist Alex was looking at his reflection in the mirror. Inside the frame, three chips the size of fingernails could be seen exposed in his skull.

Chen Chen gently poked his hand into the screen and picked up the three chips...

When Chen Chen retracted his hands, three chips appeared in his hand. Their surface was glossy like glass. Countless tiny probes could be spotted protruding from its rear, they seem almost unnoticeable...

“These nano-scale electrodes are usually invisible to the naked eye, but due to the sheer volume they become slightly noticeable and achieve this unique visual effect.”

Little X commented on the nature of the extracted chip. After that, a Black Knight approached Chen Chen with a container filled with disinfectant and handed it over.

Chen Chen placed the instantiated chips into the disinfectant solution. This was an effective way to prevent the electrodes from being damaged or contaminated.

Chen Chen decided three chips were not enough. After brief consideration, he deleted the movie before replacing it with yet another new copy from the desktop. He opened it again and extracted the same chips again.

Chen Chen repeated this process until he had instantiated nine chips in total when he finally decided it was enough. He unplugged the USB drive and put it back into the charging room.

After that, Chen Chen left the Spire Experimental Base and called Felix.

Within a week, Felix had everything arranged and a private jet chartered to relocate James Watson to Namibia and into the central hospital of Eco Science City.

On the other end of things, Musk also delivered the surgical robots along with the blueprints. Since these surgical machines designed to implant electrodes were very similar to sewing machines, they were also known as tailor robots.

To follow up, Chen Chen had a large host of architects in the industrial park committed to the project and modified the surgical robot along with Little X.
